gmw_00008731.name: NGC 6500
gmw_00008731.overview: NGC 6500 is classified as a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region (LINER) galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0209607]. It likely hosts an obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0209607, arXiv:1001.2924], though its extended, non-variable ultraviolet emission is dominated by hot stars [arXiv:1001.2924].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6500 as an X-ray-faint LINER 2 whose steep spectrum, UV energy budget, radio core, and VLBA jet support an obscured LLAGN interpretation.

quotes.quote: The spectral slope of NGC 6500 ( =3.1^ +1.1 _ 1.7 ) is somewhat steeper than is typical of LLAGNs. The four objects NGC 2787, NGC 4550, NGC 5866, and NGC 6500, however, have much lower / ratios ( / 0) than expected from the correlation ( / 1-2 ), and their X-ray luminosities are insufficient to power the H emission (Terashima et al. 2000a).

The presence of hot stars in the nuclear region of NGC 6500 is suggested by UV spectroscopy (Maoz et al. 1998). Maoz et al. (1998) studied the energy budget for NGC 6500 by using the H and UV luminosity at 1300 A and showed that the observed UV luminosity is insufficient to power the H luminosity even if a stellar population with the Salpeter initial mass function and a high mass cutoff of 120 M_ are assumed. This result indicates that a power source in addition to hot stars must contribute significantly, and supports the obscured AGN interpretation discussed above.

Additional lines of evidence which support the presence of an AGN include the fact that all three of these galaxies (NGC 2787, NGC 5866, and NGC 6500) have VLBI-detected, sub-pc scale, nuclear radio core sources (Falcke et al. 2000), a broad H component (in NGC 2787, and an ambiguous detection in NGC 5866; Ho et al. 1997b), a variable radio core in NGC 2787, and a jet-like linear structure in a high-resolution radio map of NGC 6500 with the VLBA (Falcke et al. 2000).

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 6500 likely contains an obscured AGN, but its UV emission is extended, non-variable, and probably dominated by hot stars.

quotes.quote: NGC 6500. -- The UV spectrum of the nucleus of NGC 6500 maoz98 has a relatively low S/N, but still shows absorption features resembling lines from hot stars. Moreover, the nuclear UV source is extended, with no clear ``knot'' that could be identified with the nucleus maoz95,barth98 and no variability maoz05 . The X-ray spectrum terashima03 is indicative of an obscured AGN with E(B-V)=0.37 , implying an attenuation of its 2500 \ flux by a factor of 7. Thus this object is very similar to NGC 5055; an AGN is probably present, but is not the source of the observed UV flux.

gmw_00008732.name: DwB
gmw_00008732.overview: DwB is a dwarf galaxy that contains detectable neutral hydrogen gas [arXiv:1911.09694]. It has a kinematic distance similar to the background group containing NGC 5485, suggesting a physical association with this group [arXiv:1911.09694].

sources.summary: The paper detects HI in DwB, estimates a kinematic distance near the NGC 5485 group, and suggests it may be infalling along the line of sight despite gas retention.

quotes.quote: We estimate kinematic distances, D_ HI , for DF-5, DwB, dw1408+56, and Dw26 using of our detections and assuming H_ 0 =70 Mpc ^ 1 . The values of that we derive for DwB and dw1408+56 are similar to those of the background group containing NGC 5485, implying an association. The SBF lower limit on D_ opt from C19 for DwB is consistent with our distance estimate, while their D_ opt estimate for dw1408+56 is not.

On the other hand, DwB and dw1408+56 have consistent with that of the NGC 5485 group. Using their kinematic distances and projected separations, we estimate that the physical separations of DwB and dw1408+56 from NGC 5485 are 450 kpc and 940 kpc, respectively. By contrast, DwB projects near the group center and has a similar ; if it is indeed near the group center it should be gas-poor. This can be reconciled with our scenario if DwB is separated from the NGC 5485 group along the line-of-sight.

gmw_00008733.name: dw1408+56
gmw_00008733.overview: dw1408+56 is a dwarf galaxy located on the outskirts of the NGC 5485 group [arXiv:1911.09694]. Its physical properties and kinematic distance resemble those of a gas-rich ultra-diffuse galaxy progenitor [arXiv:1911.09694].

sources.summary: The paper detects HI in dw1408+56, associates it with the NGC 5485 group outskirts, and says its kinematic properties resemble a gas-rich ultra-diffuse progenitor.

quotes.quote: The values of that we derive for DwB and dw1408+56 are similar to those of the background group containing NGC 5485, implying an association. C19 note that their distance for this latter object may be unreliable due to its unusual morphology, and they conclude that it likely lies in the background of M101 as we find here.

On the other hand, DwB and dw1408+56 have consistent with that of the NGC 5485 group. Using their kinematic distances and projected separations, we estimate that the physical separations of DwB and dw1408+56 from NGC 5485 are 450 kpc and 940 kpc, respectively. Given its kinematic distance, the properties of dw1408+56 resemble those of a gas-rich ultra-diffuse galaxy UDG; R_ eff > 1.5 kpc , _0 > 24 mag arcsec^ 2 ; UDGsvandokkum progenitor with R_ eff = 1.45 0.17 kpc and _ ,0 = 23.28 0.06 mag arcsec^ 2 HCGUDGs .
The large projected separation between dw1408+56 and the group center as well as their correspondence in suggest that this object is indeed on the group outskirts.

gmw_00008734.name: Dw26
gmw_00008734.overview: Dw26 is a star-forming galaxy located at a distance of approximately 156 Mpc [arXiv:1911.09694]. This system lies far in the background of both the M101 galaxy and the NGC 5485 group [arXiv:1911.09694].

sources.summary: The paper reports Dw26 as an HI detection with the first distance estimate for the system, placing it far behind both M101 and the NGC 5485 group.

quotes.quote: Our measurement of D_ HI for Dw26 is the first distance estimate for this system (see also B17). Dw26 is our most distant HI detection with D_ HI = 156 Mpc . At such a distance, Dw26 is a star-forming galaxy well in the background of both M101 and the NGC 5485 group.

The vast range of distances that we derive from our HI detections in the M101 region demonstrates the need for follow up observations of optical LSB features to determine their physical properties and associations. We note that the only HI follow-up target plotted in Fig. \ fig:m101dist that is known not to be associated We note that the C19 SBF distance for Dw33 places it in the background of M101 but also in the foreground of NGC 5485. However, since C19 caution that the uncertainties on their Dw33 distance measurement may be underestimated, we deem it plausible that Dw33 is in fact associated with NGC 5485. with the NGC 5485 group is Dw26, which our HI detection places far in the background ( D 156 Mpc).

gmw_00008735.name: ID15371
gmw_00008735.overview: ID15371 is a high-redshift disk galaxy that features a dust-obscured center that becomes brighter at longer near-infrared wavelengths [arXiv:2307.07599]. The galaxy contains four identified clumps and shows no evidence of a hidden far-infrared component [arXiv:2307.07599].

sources.summary: The paper uses ID15371 as its worked example, showing a disk visible in all bands, a dust-obscured center brightening in NIRCam, four clumps, and no hidden FIR component.

quotes.quote: In this Section, we detail the methods used to analyze each galaxy, taking one of the objects (ID15371) as an example, to better clarify the procedure that we applied to all galaxies. For each galaxy, we started by creating cutouts in each band ( HST /ACS F606W, F814W and JWST /NIRCam F115W, F150W, F200W, F277W, F356W, F410M, F444W). We show the cutouts of a DSFG in Fig. fig:cut_id15371 where one can already see by eye a difference between the disk visible in all bands and the center of the galaxy invisible in the HST images but getting brighter at longer wavelengths, justifying the need to study each component individually rather than the galaxy as a whole. One of the first steps was to see if we could identify a bulge and a disk in each galaxies just like for ID15371, as discussed below.

For ID15371, we identify 4 clumps, there are shown by the white ellipses in the left panel of Fig. fig:id15371 . In Fig. fig:sed_fir , we show the comparison between the total SED of the galaxy ID15371 and the FIR flux densities. For this galaxy, the flux densities are consistent with the predicted FIR SED meaning that there is no hidden component.

gmw_00008736.name: ID13107
gmw_00008736.overview: ID13107 is a quiescent, clumpy disk galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2.21 [arXiv:2307.07599]. It may host an obscured active galactic nucleus at its core, suggested by an excess of far-infrared flux and a central green patch [arXiv:2307.07599].

sources.summary: The paper flags ID13107 as a Type II system whose excess FIR flux and central green patch suggest a possible obscured AGN within a quiescent, clumpy disk.

quotes.quote: This is the case for all the galaxies in our sample except one (ID13107 for which we have a FIR detection brighter than the SED model, pointing toward either a deeply attenuated component or an AGN even though there is no AGN signature in X-ray or radio). For the ID13107 galaxy ( z = 2.21 0.02 ), the green patch is close to the center of the galaxy, it is then possible that the H_ line is produced by the accretion disk of an AGN sitting at the center of the galaxy that becomes bright in this region because of a much weaker dust attenuation than in the core. Even though we have no radio or X-ray signature of an AGN in this galaxy, as mentioned before, the predicted SFR from the SED fitting is not enough to explain the FIR flux density observed with Herschel for this galaxy. This convinced us that there could be an AGN at the core of this galaxy.

When looking at Fig. fig:cutouts_II , one can clearly identify clumps in the Type II galaxies. The blue clumps of these quiescent disks (ID13107, ID18278 and ID13776 in Fig. fig:cutouts_II ) are due to a low dust attenuation and not a high sSFR. In our case, in the four remaining galaxies, two have clumpy heterogeneous disks (ID13107 and ID18278, see Fig. fig:cutouts_II ), the different properties of the patches, either linked to dust or sSFR (see Sect. subsec:uvj ) favors the idea of asymmetric accretion streams and minor mergers as the source of lopsidedness for these two galaxies.

gmw_00008737.name: ID18278
gmw_00008737.overview: ID18278 is a lopsided disk galaxy at a redshift of z = 1.805 [arXiv:2307.07599]. It features a clumpy, heterogeneous disk with a partly quenched outer region, likely shaped by asymmetric accretion streams or minor mergers [arXiv:2307.07599].

sources.summary: The paper classifies ID18278 as Type II with a partly quenched disk, old low-sSFR outer clumps, and heterogeneous lopsided structure likely tied to accretion or minor mergers.

quotes.quote: There was only one galaxy (ID18278) where only a fraction of the disk was quiescent (green region in Fig. fig:cutouts_II ), we decided to include it to the Type II as the quiescent part encompasses 16 of the disk stellar mass and could be considered as an early stage of quenching. For the ID18278 galaxy ( z = 1.805 ), the situation is different, the green patch is in the outer region and composed of clumps. These clumps could have actually been ionized by the hot evolved low-mass stars ( cid_fernandes_comprehensive_2011,belli_kmos-3d_2017 ) with an enhanced H_ line due to shocks from the minor merger. Indeed, these clumps are old (age of oldest stars = 2.5 0.5Gyr ) and have a very low sSFR, consistent with the ex-situ clumps defined in .

Among the lopsided galaxies showed in Figs. fig:cutouts_I and fig:cutouts_II , some have a clear compact central core and a rather homogeneously colored disks (e.g. ID11887, ID13776), others are mostly clumpy galaxies with a less compact core (e.g. ID18694, ID18278). In our case, in the four remaining galaxies, two have clumpy heterogeneous disks (ID13107 and ID18278, see Fig. fig:cutouts_II ), the different properties of the patches, either linked to dust or sSFR (see Sect. subsec:uvj ) favors the idea of asymmetric accretion streams and minor mergers as the source of lopsidedness for these two galaxies.

gmw_00008738.name: ID13776
gmw_00008738.overview: ID13776 is a Type II galaxy that hosts an X-ray active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2307.07599]. The galaxy features a bright, compact core and a highly off-centered disk with blue clumps caused by low dust attenuation [arXiv:2307.07599].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID13776 as a Type II galaxy with a compact core, X-ray AGN, low-attenuation blue disk clumps, possible compact-SMG counterpart status, and off-centered disk.

quotes.quote: 4 galaxies hosts X-ray AGNs that do not dominate the FIR emissions; 1 is a Type I galaxy (ID30186), 2 are Type II galaxies (ID13098 and ID13776) and the last one is a Type III galaxy (ID23205) ( nandra_aegis-x_2015 ). For some galaxies, the core/bulge has a clear clump-like morphology, is much brighter than the surroundings and is clearly delimited (e.g. ID13776 and ID23205 in Fig. fig:red_im ).

The blue clumps of these quiescent disks (ID13107, ID18278 and ID13776 in Fig. fig:cutouts_II ) are due to a low dust attenuation and not a high sSFR. A total of 5 galaxies have either a 3 detection in SCUBA2/ 850 m or a prediction > 1.1 mJy at (ID13776 and ID21190 from the Type II class and ID16544, ID29608 and ID30186 from the Type I class). They correspond to the 5 galaxies measured with a SFR 100 M _ yr ^ 1 in their core.

The ID13776 has a clumpy but more homogeneous disk, but highly off-centered. The eccentricity of this galaxy can both originate from asymmetric accretion making the disk grow on one side or from a major merger strong enough to shift the disk.

gmw_00008739.name: SDSS J091312.36+610554.2
gmw_00008739.overview: SDSS J091312.36+610554.2 is a bright, face-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:1706.00175]. It has a spectroscopic redshift of z = 0.19900, which corresponds to a luminosity distance of approximately 990 Mpc [arXiv:1706.00175].

sources.summary: The paper considers SDSS J091312.36+610554.2 as a nearby face-on spiral candidate environment for ATLAS17aeu, giving its redshift, luminosity distance, and projected separation if related.

quotes.quote: We originally noted in that ATLAS17aeu is 23 arcsec from the face-on, bright, spiral galaxy SDSS J091312.36+610554.2 which has a spectroscopic redshift of z = 0.19900 0.00004 . This implies a luminosity distance of 990 Mpc so if ATLAS17aeu were related it would be at a projected distance of 75 kpc from this galaxy (see Fig. fig:image ). This luminosity distance is consistent with the LIGO distance to GW170104 which suggests a 10-90 percentile probability of 520-1010 Mpc in this direction from the analysis of 2016ApJ...829L..15S .

gmw_00008740.name: Galaxy A
gmw_00008740.overview: Galaxy A is a very faint galaxy that is a possible host to the transient ATLAS17aeu [arXiv:1706.00024, arXiv:1706.00175]. It may be physically linked to Galaxy B in a merging or disturbed system, though its redshift remains unconstrained due to a lack of detected emission lines [arXiv:1706.00175].

sources.summary: The paper treats Galaxy A as a possible host at the transient position, reporting very faint optical magnitudes, marginal late-time DCT detection, and a Keck J-band non-detection.

quotes.quote: sts+17 detect a fainter source (``Galaxy A'', = 25.59 0.16 ) at the exact location of ATLAS17aeu, which may be the host galaxy. Our late time DCT imaging provides a marginal detection of this source, with magnitudes ^ = 25.54 0.39, i^ = 25.15 0.38 , and z^ = 24.40 0.41 . However, the measurements are low confidence (2.5 ), and we do not consider it a firm detection.

Deep imaging of the afterglow location was obtained with the J-band filter on the NIRC2 camera on Keck II, utilizing laser guide star adaptive optics corrections and obtaining 12 dithered 300 s exposures. We do not detect the galaxy and measure a 5 point source limiting magnitude of J > 22.8 (Vega mag) or J > 23.7 (AB mag).

sources.summary: The paper identifies Galaxy A as a very faint galaxy coincident with ATLAS17aeu, possibly linked to Galaxy B; spectroscopy found no obvious emission line, leaving its redshift unconstrained.

quotes.quote: There is a faint galaxy exactly at the position of ATLAS17aeu in the Gemini image of 2017 Apr 01, which we label "Galaxy A" in Fig. fig:image . With aperture photometry, we measure =25.59 0.16 .

It is possible that Galaxy A and B are physically linked and could either be a merging or disturbed system and a redshift for both is desirable. We placed the slit of GMOS and DEIMOS (see Sect. sec:obs for details of these deep spectra) across these two galaxies as illustrated in Fig. fig:image in search of any emission lines of either which would provide a redshift for ATLAS17aeu. There is no obvious emission line detected at the spatial positions of Galaxy A or B.

gmw_00008741.name: Galaxy B
gmw_00008741.overview: Galaxy B is a bright, extended galaxy located 1.8 arcseconds southeast of the transient ATLAS17aeu [arXiv:1706.00175]. Optical observations show that the galaxy exhibits a faint continuum, though it lacks a reliable redshift measurement [arXiv:1706.00175].

sources.summary: The paper describes Galaxy B as a brighter source 1.8 arcsec southeast of ATLAS17aeu, resolved from the transient, once proposed as a host, with faint continuum but no reliable redshift.

quotes.quote: There is also a brighter extended source which is 1 8 south east of the position of ATLAS17aeu (RA=9:13:14.120, DEC=+61:05:32.60), for which we measure =24.44 0.09 . We label this "Galaxy B", and it is clearly visible in the earlier Gemini image (from 2017 Jan 08) resolved from ATLAS17aeu. In this image we measure =24.11 .09 , with the difference between the two arising from the choice of aperture required to minimise contamination from the bright flux of ATLAS17aeu. Galaxy B is almost certainly the object report by at =24.23 0.2 , which they proposed as a host. Our GMOS images now show that this is offset and resolved from the position of ATLAS17aeu.

In the Gemini GMOS spectrum (for which nod and shuffle was used), there is faint continuum present for Galaxy B, whereas no apparent signal is at the position of Galaxy A (see Fig. fig:spec2D ). We are confident in the location of Galaxy B on the 2D spectral images from the spatial consistency of its position and measured offsets from the z=0.3487 and z=0.2765 bright sources located in the slit.

gmw_00008742.name: C-1-10
gmw_00008742.overview: C-1-10 is a spectroscopically confirmed member of the Centaurus cluster [arXiv:0901.4339]. It is morphologically classified as an E0(M32) galaxy, representing a small elliptical galaxy rather than a true compact elliptical [arXiv:0901.4339].

sources.summary: The paper describes C-1-10 as a confirmed Centaurus member, also CCC 70, with E0(M32) morphology and the smallest effective radius among similar-luminosity sample galaxies, but not a true cE.

quotes.quote: C-1-10 is a spectroscopically confirmed member of the Centaurus cluster. It is listed as CCC 70 in the Centaurus Cluster Catalogue and it is morphologically classified as an E0(M32) galaxy 1997A&AS..124....1J . The isophote fitting was performed on the 30s exposure, since the long-exposure image was saturated at the object centre. Due to the projected proximity of C-1-10 to the giant galaxy NGC 4696, we created and subtracted a model of the latter before modelling the dwarf galaxy. With an effective radius of 1.90'' (418 pc) C-1-10 is the most compact object among the galaxies with similar magnitude in our sample.

Moreover, C-1-10 does not have a particular high central surface brightness, but it falls exactly on the sequence of regular cluster dwarf galaxies (see upper panel of Fig. fig:sersic ). Also its colour is consistent with the cluster CMR (Fig. fig:cmd ). Given these properties, C-1-10 is rather a small elliptical galaxy than an exemplar of a cE galaxy.

gmw_00008743.name: C-1-21
gmw_00008743.overview: C-1-21 is a confirmed member galaxy of the Centaurus cluster [arXiv:0901.4339]. It is classified as a compact elliptical galaxy, characterized by a small effective radius of approximately 279 parsecs and a projected location close to a brighter galaxy [arXiv:0901.4339].

sources.summary: The paper identifies C-1-21 as a confirmed Centaurus member with a very small effective radius comparable to known compact ellipticals and concludes that it is a true cE galaxy.

quotes.quote: C-1-21 is a confirmed Centaurus member 2007ApJS..170...95C . The best model for C-1-21 was obtained with fixed centre coordinates and position angle, while the ellipticity was allowed to vary ( 0.0< <0.2 ). Its effective radius of 1.27'' , or 279 pc, is at least three times smaller than the ones of other cluster galaxies of the same luminosity (Fig. fig:sizelumidiag ). This is comparable to the size of the two M32 twins in Abell 1689 2005A&A...430L..25M , the cE galaxy A496cE 2007A&A...466L..21C and the cE candidate FS90 192 in the Antlia cluster 2008MNRAS.tmp.1243S .

Since three common characteristics of cE galaxies, namely the small effective radius, the high central surface brightness and the projected location close to a brighter galaxy, are given, we consider C-1-21 a true cE galaxy.

gmw_00008744.name: C-2-20
gmw_00008744.overview: C-2-20 is an unconfirmed candidate member of the Centaurus cluster with a very compact morphology [arXiv:0901.4339]. Although its physical properties resemble those of a compact elliptical galaxy, it is located in a relatively isolated position far from giant galaxies [arXiv:0901.4339].

sources.summary: The paper treats C-2-20 as an unconfirmed Centaurus candidate with properties like C-1-21, compact morphology, unusual isolation, and a need for spectroscopy to confirm membership.

quotes.quote: C-2-20 has no available spectroscopic redshift. Whether it is a cluster galaxy or a background galaxy can at this point only be determined by an educated guess on the basis of its morphological and photometric properties. Absolute magnitude, colour and central surface brightness are very similar to those of C-1-21 (see Table tab:cEs ).

Its very compact morphology ( R_ eff =363 pc) suggests that it is indeed a cluster cE. However, its relatively isolated position (see Fig. fig:fields ), far away from the giant galaxies, is unusual for a cE galaxy. We conclude that C-2-20 is definitely a good candidate for a cE galaxy, but whether it really belongs to the Centaurus cluster has to be confirmed by spectroscopic redshift measurements.

gmw_00008745.name: GRB 130606A sub-DLA host galaxy
gmw_00008745.overview: The GRB 130606A sub-DLA host galaxy is a high-redshift galaxy located at z = 5.91 [arXiv:1312.5631]. It features a compact and dense environment with a low metallicity content ranging from 1/7 to 1/60 of solar [arXiv:1312.5631].

sources.summary: The paper studies the GRB 130606A host at z=5.91, finding a sub-DLA with low metallicity, high-ionization species, and unusually rare high-redshift GRB-DLA properties.

quotes.quote: Events such as GRB 130606A at z = 5.91, offer an exciting new window into pre-galactic metal enrichment in these very high redshift host galaxies. We study the environment and host galaxy of GRB 130606A, a high-z event, in the context of a high redshift population of GRBs.

With an initial Lorentz bulk factor in the range _0 65-220, the X-ray afterglow evolution can be explained by a time-dependent photoionization of the local circumburst medium, within a compact and dense environment. The host galaxy is a sub-DLA (log N (H I) = 19.85 0.15), with a metallicity content in the range from 1/7 to 1/60 of solar. Highly ionized species (N V and Si IV) are also detected.
The GRB 130606A sub-DLA is a rare find: the second highest redshift burst with a measured GRB-DLA metallicity and only the third GRB absorber with sub-DLA HI column density.

gmw_00008746.name: Pox 120
gmw_00008746.overview: Pox 120 is classified as a Wolf-Rayet galaxy [arXiv:1510.02542]. It is identified as one of the local galaxies exhibiting the most extreme C III] emission [arXiv:1510.02542].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Pox 120 as one of the local galaxies with the most extreme C III] emission, classifies it as a Wolf-Rayet galaxy, and notes missing H-equivalent-width data.

quotes.quote: The three galaxies from the z 0 samples with the most extreme C III ] , with equivalent widths exceeding 14 , are Tololo 1214-277 , Markarian 71 (a star-forming region within NGC 2366), Many studies incorrectly name this object; see http://cdsarc.u-strasbg.fr/viz-bin/vizExec/.getpuz?7239 N 202363 and Pox 120. We now explore what is known about these three galaxies, and plot their ultraviolet spectra in Figure fig:spectra . Tololo 1214-277 is classified as a Wolf-Rayet galaxy; Mrk 71 is classified as a star cluster containing Wolf-Rayet stars within the irregular galaxy NGC 2366, and Pox 120 is classified as a Wolf-Rayet galaxy Kunth:1985vk,Schaerer:1999ku .
We could not find a published W_r(H ) for Pox 120.

gmw_00008747.name: SDSS J133443.05 + 331305.7
gmw_00008747.overview: SDSS J133443.05 + 331305.7 is a star-forming galaxy located at a photometric redshift of approximately 0.4 [arXiv:2211.16537]. It resides in the infall region of the galaxy group WHL J133453.9 + 331004 [arXiv:2211.16537].

sources.summary: The paper evaluates SDSS J133443.05 + 331305.7 as a foreground star-forming galaxy whose modest lensing magnification cannot explain the transient's high luminosity.

quotes.quote: AT c is located 5.6^ from the galaxy SDSS J133443.05 + 331305.7, at a photometric redshift of z=0.4 0.1 , and 3.7^ from the galaxy group WHL J133453.9 + 331004 at a spectroscopic redshift of z=0.4 . The optical luminosity of the group, and the sky location and colours of this galaxy are consistent with our line of sight to passing adjacent to a star-forming galaxy located in the infall region of ( R r_ 200 ) of a galaxy group with a mass M_ 200 3 10^ 13 M_ , where the mass estimate is obtained by combining the optical luminosity from with the mass-observable scaling relations from .

To estimate magnification by the galaxy, we compare its apparent magnitude in red pass-bands (i.e., relatively insensitive to any ongoing star formation) with a model for a passively evolving stellar population formed in a burst at a redshift of z>2 . This yields an estimated luminosity relative to the luminosity function of cluster and group galaxies of 0.3L^ . Combining this estimate with the scaling relations between mass and luminosity commonly used to estimate galaxy masses in gravitational lens models (e.g., ) we obtain a velocity dispersion estimate for the bulge of the galaxy of 120 km s^ 1 . Then, adopting a singular isothermal sphere (SIS) model of the galaxy mass distribution, and using the standard expressions for the lensing properties of an SIS (e.g., ), we derive an estimated Einstein radius of _ E 0.25^ and lens magnification of 1.05 , based on the lens redshift of z_ L =0.4 and source redshift of z_ S =1.193 .

gmw_00008748.name: NGC6034
gmw_00008748.overview: NGC 6034 is a lenticular brightest cluster galaxy located in the Hercules cluster A2151 [arXiv:1411.0361, arXiv:0808.1521]. It hosts an extended radio source with two jets [arXiv:1411.0361], exhibits rapid major-axis rotation [arXiv:0808.1521], and contains neutral hydrogen that likely forms a rotating disk [arXiv:1411.0361].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6034 as an S0-hosted extended radio source in Hercules cluster A2151, with two jets, flat spectrum, narrow systemic H I forming a rotating disk, and prior VLA detection.

quotes.quote: NGC 6034 This radio source is hosted by a S0 optical galaxy, which belongs to cluster A2151 of the Hercules Supercluster. The radio source is extended, with two jets emerging toward the north and the south Mack1993 . The spectrum is flat with no variation of the spectral index ( =-0.65 ). The line is very narrow and it is centred at the systemic velocity. This suggests that the \ may form a rotating disk in the host galaxy.
The neutral hydrogen in NGC 6034 has been first detected in absorption by VLA observations Dickey1997 .

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC6034 as a rapidly rotating BCG, with major-axis rotation of about 134 km/s, arguing against formation through purely dissipationless mergers.

quotes.quote: In particular, we find rapid rotation ( > 100 km s ^ 1 ) for two BCGs, NGC6034 and NGC7768, indicating that it is unlikely that they formed through dissipationless mergers.
NGC6034: The surface brightness profile was published by Schombert (1986). The radial velocity profile of this galaxy shows significant rotation of the order of 134 15 km s ^ 1 along the major axis.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 6034 was not recovered as a fossil group because its central galaxy lies in a larger group and lacks the required magnitude gap.

quotes.quote: Finally, NGC 6034 was not detected since we did not identify a massive and numerous halo in its position. Instead, we identified the central galaxy to be part of the outskirts of a larger group. A closer inspection also indicated that this galaxy also had a close bright neighbour in the same large group, causing the magnitude gap to be M_ 12 =1.07 and not 2, as the strict definition of fossil groups requires.

gmw_00008749.name: NGC7649
gmw_00008749.overview: NGC7649 is a brightest cluster galaxy that exhibits clear velocity substructure in its profile [arXiv:0808.1521]. A kinematically decoupled core is also clearly visible in the centre of this galaxy [arXiv:0808.1521].

sources.summary: The paper cites published imaging for NGC7649, finds a clearly visible central KDC, and includes it among BCGs with clear velocity substructure.

quotes.quote: NGC7649: The surface brightness profile was published by Schombert (1986), and HST imaging by Laine et al. \ (2003). A KDC is clearly visible in the centre of this galaxy.
At least 12 BCGs (NGC3842, NGC4889, NGC7647, ESO488-027, IC5358, MCG-02-12-039, NGC1713, NGC2832, NGC4839, NGC6269, NGC7649 and UGC05515) show clear velocity substructure in their profiles.

gmw_00008750.name: UGC02232
gmw_00008750.overview: UGC02232 is characterized by an extended envelope and a large number of objects in its vicinity [arXiv:0808.1521]. Observations of the galaxy detect no rotation or clear internal substructure [arXiv:0808.1521].

sources.summary: The paper says UGC02232 has an extended envelope and many nearby objects, cites HST imaging, and finds no significant rotation or clear substructure.

quotes.quote: UGC02232: This galaxy has an extended envelope and a large number of objects in the vicinity. HST imaging was published by Laine et al. \ (2003). No significant rotation or clear substructure is detected.

gmw_00008751.name: UGC05515
gmw_00008751.overview: UGC05515 is a brightest cluster galaxy that exhibits measurable rotation along its intermediate axis [arXiv:0808.1521]. The galaxy's radial velocity profile reveals a kinematically decoupled core in its center, as well as clear velocity substructure [arXiv:0808.1521].

sources.summary: The paper cites UGC05515 surface-brightness work, finds a central KDC, and includes it among BCGs with clear velocity substructure and measurable rotation.

quotes.quote: UGC05515: The surface brightness profile was published by Schombert (1986). The radial velocity profile shows a KDC in the centre of this galaxy.
At least 12 BCGs (NGC3842, NGC4889, NGC7647, ESO488-027, IC5358, MCG-02-12-039, NGC1713, NGC2832, NGC4839, NGC6269, NGC7649 and UGC05515) show clear velocity substructure in their profiles.

Several other BCGs show rotation that is > 40 km s ^ 1 and more than three times the standard error: ESO346-003, GSC555700266 and NGC4839 (major axis spectra); ESO488-027, IC5358 and UGC05515 (intermediate axis spectra), as do the two elliptical galaxies NGC4946 (major axis spectra) and NGC6047 (intermediate axis spectra).

gmw_00008752.name: UGC10143
gmw_00008752.overview: UGC 10143 is a radio-quiet giant galaxy located at the center of the Abell 2147 cluster [arXiv:1612.08089]. It features a smooth, featureless optical core with no active star formation [arXiv:1612.08089], and it is accompanied by a chain of companion galaxies [arXiv:0808.1521].

sources.summary: UGC 10143 is described as A2147's central giant, radio-quiet with a smooth core, whose globular cluster system contains about 12,500 clusters and a specific frequency of 8.2.

quotes.quote: UGC 10143 is the central giant in A2147, at d = 154 Mpc; and A2147 is part of the Hercules Supercluster along with A2151 and A2152 barmby_huchra1998 .

The BCG is radio-quiet and has a deduced central star formation rate consistent with zero from its lack of UV or infrared signature hoffer_etal2012 . Close inspection of our images shows a smooth, featureless optical core with no indications of peculiarities. Detection of the GC system around UGC 10143 was first done by blakeslee1999 through ground-based imaging and surface brightness fluctuation (SBF) analysis.

For UGC 10143, if again we restrict the calculation conservatively to the ACS field of view ( R < 155'' ), the total number of GCs with I < 27.0 is n = 6450 100 , which translates to N_ tot = 12500 200 over all magnitudes. Integration of the halo light donzelli_etal2011 to the same radius gives R_ tot = 12.43 , M_V^T 22.96 . Finally then, S_N = 8.2 0.1 to that radius.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC10143 as having a chain of companions, published surface-brightness and HST imaging, and no significant inferred rotation or substructure.

quotes.quote: UGC10143: This galaxy has a chain of companions. The surface brightness profile was published by Schombert (1986), and HST imaging by Laine et al. \ (2003). No significant rotation or substructure can be inferred from the radial kinematic profiles of this galaxy.

gmw_00008753.name: LARS 4
gmw_00008753.overview: LARS 4 is a morphologically irregular galaxy [arXiv:1511.05406] that features an apparent bar and an extended western tail [arXiv:1308.6578]. The system is undergoing a merger of two main photometric components [arXiv:1511.05406] and exhibits net Lyman-alpha absorption at all radii [arXiv:1308.6578].

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 4 as Ly-absorbing, morphologically irregular, and decomposable into two components whose shearing likely traces merging rather than disk rotation.

quotes.quote: LARS 4 ( SDSS J130728.45+542652.3 ) Although LARS 4 is similar to LARS 1 in terms of dust content and star formation rate, globally this galaxy shows Ly in absorption. The galaxy can be photometrically decomposed into two main components: an elongated lower surface brightness structure in the west and a more puffed up and luminous companion in the east. The eastern structure is tilted at 40^ with respect to the western one. Compared to the highly irregular morphology in UV and H , the radial velocity field appears rather regular, with a moderate amplitude of v_ shear 75 km s ^ 1 along a well defined axis from west to east. The kinematic centre appears to be right between the two photometric components, indicating that the observed shearing is the velocity difference between the merging components and not a consequence of rotation.

sources.summary: LARS 04 is described as highly irregular, with an apparent bar and western tail, small-scale emission but net absorption at all radii and no apparent large-scale emission component.

quotes.quote: LARS 04 LARS 04 shows a highly irregular UV morphology comprising an apparent bar and extended tail to the west. \ is emitted on small scales throughout all these regions, but in complete annuli a net absorption is found at all radii. Maps show that even on small scales the \ EW never exceeds expectations and no large scale component of emission is apparent -- between the UV bright regions, where diffuse emission should easily be detectable, the continuum-subtracted \ flux negative, suggesting much neutral gas absorption is ongoing and if \ is emitted it must be on very large scales indeed. In terms of SFR and dust content it is quite similar to LARS 01, which emits copious .

gmw_00008754.name: LARS 8
gmw_00008754.overview: LARS 8 is a face-on disk galaxy with a highly dust-obscured nucleus and high nebular metallicity [arXiv:1308.6578, arXiv:1511.05406]. It features patchy ultraviolet-bright star formation in its disk [arXiv:1308.6578] and shell-like structures that suggest a recent merger event [arXiv:1511.05406].

sources.summary: LARS 08 is described as a face-on disk-like galaxy with an obscured nucleus, high nebular metallicity, patchy UV-bright disk star formation, weak disk-surface emission, and no nuclear emission.

quotes.quote: LARS 08 This is a face-on disk-like galaxy with a heavily obscured nucleus, and shows the highest nebular metallicity in the sample according to strong-line diagnostics (Paper I). It shows numerous patches of UV bright star-forming regions in the disk, that appears to be more extended to the west. \ emission is seen coming from the surface of this disk, but it is not strong compared to the FUV or , with no significant super-recombination ratios visible at any position. Notably there is no sign of \ emission emanating from the nuclear regions, but instead \ is seen only from the regions that are brighter in the FUV, possibly as a correlation with the dust content. LARS 08 does show an ever increasing photometric curve-of-growth in its luminosity, but this does not translate into its EW or , which both flatten at smaller radii of about 2 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 8 as a dusty face-on disk with shell-like structures hinting at a recent merger, spatially distinct Ly emission, and disk-like velocity structure.

quotes.quote: LARS 8 ( SDSS-J125013.50+073441.5 ) LARS 8 appears a face-on disk with the highest metalicity of the sample. The apparent disk also possesses a highly dust obscured nucleus. Nevertheless, there is a high degree of irregularity compared to classical disks and spiral arms are not well defined. As traces of shell-like structures are visible in the inner parts and in the outskirts of the galaxy (cf. Fig. 6 in Ostlin2014 ), LARS 8 can be classified as a shell system hinting at a recent merger event e.g. Bettoni2011 . The Ly light distribution of LARS 8 does not resemble that seen in H .

gmw_00008755.name: BZB J0035+1515
gmw_00008755.overview: BZB J0035+1515 is a BL Lacertae object and blazar [arXiv:1701.04305]. Optical spectra of the source are featureless, establishing a redshift lower limit of z > 0.55 [arXiv:1701.04305].

sources.summary: The paper reports that previous and new spectra of BZB J0035+1515 are featureless, using the high-SNR GTC spectrum to set a redshift lower limit.

quotes.quote: BZB J0035+1515 : The source was first discovered by fischer1998 and catalogued as BLL on the basis of its featureless optical spectrum. A more recent optical spectrum, obtained as part of the SDSS survey, exhibits no features (although the automatic procedure suggests some tentative values, also included in NED). Also shaw2013a found a featureless spectrum. We confirm the featureless nature of the spectrum from 4200 to 9000 and from our high SNR we obtain an EW _ min of 0.09 - 0.18 , which correspond to a redshift lower limit of z > 0.55

gmw_00008756.name: MS 1221.8+2452
gmw_00008756.overview: MS 1221.8+2452 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1501.02765] located at a redshift of z = 0.218 [arXiv:1501.02765, arXiv:1701.04305]. The galaxy exhibits variable gamma-ray emission with week-scale flares [arXiv:1501.02765], and its resolved host galaxy displays stellar absorption lines [arXiv:1701.04305].

sources.summary: The paper identifies FBQS J122424.1+243623 as one of eight previously unreported variable gamma-ray sources, a BL Lac at z=0.218 with week-scale flares, hard spectra, and GeV-peaked SED behavior.

quotes.quote: Close inspection of the detected variable regions result in identification of 8 sources without previous known variability. Two of them are included in the second Fermi LAT source catalogue (FBQS J122424.1+243623 and GB6 J0043+3426) and one (3EG J1424+3734) was reported by EGRET and also was included in the First Fermi LAT source catalogue (1FGL), but is missing in the 2FGL. Possible identifications of five other sources are obtained using NED and SIMBAD databases (1RXS J161939.9+765515, PMN J2320-6447, PKS 0226-559, PKS J0030-0211, PMN J0225-2603). These new variable gamma-ray sources demonstrate recurring flaring activity with time scale weeks and have hard spectra. Their spectral energy distributions deviate significantly from a simple power-law shape and often peak around GeV.

Variability in these pixels was caused by 2FGL sources FBQS J122424.1+243623 (MS 1221.8+2452) and GB6 J0043+3426 correspondingly. The first one is BL Lac type object at the redshift z=0.218 that was recently observed also in the TeV energy range by the MAGIC collaboration MS1221 , the latter one is a blazar at the redshift z=0.966 .

sources.summary: The paper supports the low redshift of MS 1221.8+2452 by noting resolved-host imaging and detecting stellar absorption plus possible emission lines.

quotes.quote: MS 1221.8+2452 : A tentative redshift of z = 0.218 was proposed by morris1991 and rector2000 . Imaging studies of this source were able to resolve the host galaxy and are consistent with the low redshift of the target falomo1999, scarpa2000 . We detect the Ca II doublet and G-band 4305 absorption lines at z= 0.218 and we find emission lines at 7995 and 8020 AA that if confirmed could be attribuited to H _ and N II 6583 .

gmw_00008757.name: BZB J1243+3627
gmw_00008757.overview: BZB J1243+3627 is a BL Lacertae object and a blazar [arXiv:1701.04305, arXiv:1307.8113]. It has a featureless optical spectrum with an intervening Mg II absorption system that establishes a spectroscopic redshift lower limit of z > 0.483 [arXiv:1701.04305].

sources.summary: The paper confirms an intervening Mg II absorption system toward BZB J1243+3627 and sets a spectroscopic redshift lower limit while finding no intrinsic features.

quotes.quote: BZB J1243+3627 : white2000 reported a featureless spectrum for this source. An absorption feature of Mg II 2800 at 4160 was detected in the SDSS spectrum suggesting a redshift of z 0.485 plotkin2010 . This redshift limit appears consistent with the marginal detection of the host galaxy by meisner2010 who estimated z 0.50. From our spectrum (SNR 330), we confirm the intervening absorption system due to Mg II 2800 and the remaining part of the spectrum is completely featureless. The spectroscopic redshift lower limit is thus z > 0.483.

sources.summary: The paper identifies BZB J1243+3627 as a selected TeV BL Lac candidate observed by VERITAS, lacking a redshift estimate, with TeV non-detection possibly affected by background-light absorption.

quotes.quote: We note that VERITAS observations have been performed for 3 of our TBCs selected within the : BZB J0136+3905, BZB J1058+5628 and BZB J1243+3627 aliu12 . However, as discussed in Aliu et al. (2012), both BZB J0136+3905 and BZB J1243+3627 do not have a redshift estimate, indicating that their non TeV-detection could be due the absorption of high energy photons by the extragalactic background light franceschini08 . Thus, the non-detection at TeV energies of these three sources does not affect our selection that can only be verified with additional TeV observations.

gmw_00008758.name: BZB J1540+8155
gmw_00008758.overview: BZB J1540+8155 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1701.04305] with a spectroscopic redshift lower limit of z > 0.673 [arXiv:1701.04305]. Its optical spectrum lacks intrinsic emission or absorption features [arXiv:1701.04305], and its host galaxy remains undetected in Hubble Space Telescope images [arXiv:1701.04305].

sources.summary: The paper identifies an intervening Mg II absorption doublet toward BZB J1540+8155, setting z>0.673 and finding no intrinsic spectral features.

quotes.quote: BZB J1540+8155 : The source was identified as BLL by schachter1993 . The optical spectra obtained by perlman1996 failed to detect emission or absorption features. The host galaxy was not detected by HST images scarpa2000 posing the source at relatively high redshift. In our GTC spectrum we detect an intervening absorption doublet at 4680 that we identify as MgII 2800 absorption yielding a spectroscopic redshift lower limit of z > 0.673. No intrinsic emission or absorption lines are found.

gmw_00008759.name: Q2131-G1
gmw_00008759.overview: Q2131-G1 is a galaxy at a redshift of z=0.42974 with a star formation rate of 2.00 solar masses per year [arXiv:2105.07280]. Its strongly coupled molecular and ionized gas phases are directionally aligned within the disk, which is surrounded by an infalling gas component [arXiv:2105.07280].

sources.summary: The paper gives an in-depth analysis of Q2131-G1, measuring its CO-detected molecular gas, star formation, dark matter fraction, depletion time, coupled gas phases, and infalling gas component.

quotes.quote: Here, we give an in-depth analysis of the newly CO-detected galaxy Q2131-G1 ( z=0.42974 ), while providing stringent mass and depletion time limits for the non-detected galaxies. Q2131-G1 is associated with an absorber with column densities of log (N_ HI / cm ^ 2 ) 19.5 and log (N_ H _2 / cm ^ 2 ) 16.5 , has a star formation rate of SFR = 2.00 0.20 M _ yr ^ 1 , a dark matter fraction of f_ DM (r_ 1/2 ) = 0.24 - 0.54 and a molecular gas mass of M_ mol = 3.52 ^ +3.95 _ 0.31 10^9 M _ resulting in a depletion time of _ dep < 4.15 Gyr . Kinematic modelling of both the CO (3--2) and [ OIII ] 5008 emission lines of Q2131-G1 shows that the molecular and ionised gas phases are well aligned directionally and that the maximum rotation velocities closely match. These two gas phases within the disk are strongly coupled. The metallicity, kinematics and orientation of the atomic and molecular gas traced by a two-component absorption feature is consistent with being part of the extended rotating disk with a well-separated additional component associated with infalling gas.

gmw_00008760.name: Object 3
gmw_00008760.overview: Object 3 is an under-luminous irregular galaxy with an estimated stellar mass of approximately 5 billion solar masses [arXiv:1103.3663]. Photometric-redshift fitting indicates that the galaxy is located at a redshift of z=0.31 [arXiv:1103.3663].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Object 3 as the probable GRB host, using de-reddened near-infrared colors and photometric-redshift fitting to classify it as an under-luminous irregular galaxy at z=0.31 with about 5e9 solar masses.

quotes.quote: Figure figcolours shows that the de-reddened colours of Object 3 are compatible with moderate redshift solutions. We have indeed used the photometric redshift code by fernandez99 to estimate the redshift and basic properties of the host galaxy, finding that the best fit solution corresponds to an irregular galaxy template at redshift z=0.31_ 0.26 ^ +0.54 . We must remark, however, that there is a high degeneracy of the models in that region of colour space, which renders possible a large subset of galaxy models, spanning from early-type galaxies at low redshifts ( z 0.1 to z 0.5 ) to late-type models at moderate redshifts ( z 0.2 to z 1.8 ). Indeed by taking all the possible galaxy types into account the best fit redshift is z=0.3 , with a bimodal 1 confidence interval in the ranges 0.1--1.1 and 1.3--1.7 (secondary minimum).

Taking all this information into account, we have adopted the best-fit z=0.31 as reference value for the redshift, and an irregular template as the spectral choice. With these values, we show in Fig. fig:spec the intrinsic and observed spectrum of such an object. As can be seen, the photometry fits the observations perfectly, and we can derive an absolute rest-frame K band magnitude K=-21.0 \ AB , which corresponds approximately to a 0.1 \ L^* fontana06 galaxy, and, using a simple relation e.g. savaglio09 , to a mass of 5 10^9 M_ .

gmw_00008761.name: AzTEC4
gmw_00008761.overview: AzTEC4 is a high-redshift galaxy with an estimated redshift in the range of z = 4.5 to 5 [arXiv:1312.1173]. High-resolution observations resolve the galaxy into two distinct clumps separated by 1.5 kiloparsecs, suggesting that it is a mid-stage merger [arXiv:1609.02365].

sources.summary: The paper resolves AzTEC4 into two north-south clumps separated by 1.5 kpc, interprets it as a mid-stage merger, and finds most of its submillimeter flux missing in extended structure.

quotes.quote: AzTEC4 - At 0 05 resolution, the emission in AzTEC4 is divided into two distinct clumps with a separation of 1.5 kpc in the north-south direction. The large separation between the two clumps suggest that AzTEC4 is a mid stage merger. The peaks of the northern and southern sources are 536 61 Jy beam ^ 1 and 435 61 Jy beam ^ 1 , and the deconvolved source sizes from a two dimensional Gaussian fit are (0 130 0 023) (0 086 0 017) and (0 132 0 030) (0 056 0 018) , respectively. We have further inspected the residuals after subtracting both sources from the image, and found that the image is dominated by noise. The total integrated flux of the northern and southern sources are 1.98 0.31 mJy and 1.28 0.25 mJy, respectively, yielding a total of 3.26 0.40 mJy.
The total flux derived from the 0 86 0 77 SMA beam is 13.1 1.8 mJy for a Gaussian model 2010MNRAS.407.1268Y . Therefore, 75 of the total flux is missing in the extended structure.

sources.summary: The paper describes AzTEC4 as a robust SMA-position counterpart with photometric and long-wavelength redshifts near z = 4.5-5, identifiable from the original AzTEC position.

quotes.quote: AzTEC4. A robust single identification 0.78 arcsec from the SMA position. z_ phot and z_ LW are in good agreement that the source has a redshift in the range z = 4.5 - 5 . This source would have been successfully identified on the basis of the original AzTEC position by both the i-K and 8 m methods.

gmw_00008762.name: AzTEC8
gmw_00008762.overview: AzTEC8 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 3.18 [arXiv:1312.1173]. Its central region is resolved into two compact clumps separated by 200 parsecs, with 90 percent of its flux residing in an extended structure [arXiv:1609.02365].

sources.summary: The paper resolves AzTEC8 into two compact central clumps, finds 90 percent of its flux in extended structure, and argues its high star-formation surface density may be near or above the Eddington limit.

quotes.quote: AzTEC8 - The central region is clearly resolved into two clumps separated by 200 pc in the east-west direction. The peak of the eastern and western clumps are 273 34 Jy beam ^ 1 and 296 34 Jy beam ^ 1 , respectively. The total integrated flux in the central kpc (region within the red contour in Fig fig1 ) is 1.63 0.16 mJy. The total flux derived from the 0 86 0 55 SMA beam is 17.7 2.3 mJy for a Gaussian model 2010MNRAS.407.1268Y , and thus 90 of the flux is in the extended structure.

In contrast to AzTEC1 and AzTEC4, the average _ SFR in AzTEC8 is close to the upper end of the model predictions. The beam averaged peak values are even higher ( 3000 M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 ). The extreme values derived in AzTEC8 may suggest that it is forming stars near or above the Eddington limit.

sources.summary: The paper measures AzTEC8 as marginally resolved at 3 GHz, compares that radio size with SMA far-infrared and NIR sizes, and separates a lower-redshift radio source in the field.

quotes.quote: AzTEC8 . For this source, the FWHM size at 3 GHz is determined to be 0 41^ +0.10 _ 0.17 < 0 38 . The major axis is only marginally resolved, while the minor axis is unresolved. The 0 86 0 55 resolution SMA 870 m observations of AzTEC8 by Younger et al. (2010) showed the source size to be _ maj _ min =(0 6 0 2) (0 5 0 3) [ (1 0 0 5) (0 4 0 8) ] when modelled as a Gaussian (elliptical disk). This Gaussian major axis FWHM at _ rest =208 m is 1.5^ +1.8 _ 0.7 times larger than the radio size at _ rest =2.4 cm we have derived (see Fig. figure:sizecomparison ).

The observed-frame NIR diameter of AzTEC8 derived by Toft et al. (2014), <6.0 kpc ( <0 78 ), is consistent with our 3 GHz size and the Gaussian rest-frame FIR size determined by Younger et al. (2010).

sources.summary: The paper re-images AS2COS0028.1 to separate companions and adopts single-Gaussian line models because double-Gaussian fits are inconsistent and noise-sensitive.

quotes.quote: For AS2COS0001.1, AS2COS0008.1, and AS2COS0028.1, where the nearby SMGs are also detected in a few cases and close enough to potentially affect the spectra, we re-image the cubes using the Briggs baseline weighting with a robust parameter of -1.5 We have also tried uniform weighting but some spectra become too noisy. As stated in sec:spec and seen in fig:fig8 , the spatial resolutions of the naturally weighted cubes are too coarse to separate the SMG pairs of AS2COS0001.1/1.2, AS2COS0008.1/8.2, and AS2COS0028.1/28.2. For each line, our final selection of the preferred model then goes to the one that has a higher probability, except for AS2COS0001.1, AS2COS0023.1, and AS2COS0028.1, where our simulations yield inconclusive results either due to the comparable probabilities, or conflicting results from the two lines on the same target.

Finally for AS2COS0028.1, although our simulations yield inconclusive results for both CO lines, the fact that the best-fit double Gaussian models have significantly different components suggest that the best fits are unavoidably subject to noise structures, especially for CO(4-3) We have tried changing the boundaries for CO(4-3) but the broad component nevertheless appears to be in favor. Given their acceptable ^2 we therefore adopt single Gaussian models for AS2COS0028.1 for their consistency and simplicity.

sources.summary: The paper describes AzTEC8 as a robust SMA-position counterpart whose photometric and long-wavelength redshifts match z = 3.18, recoverable by i-K and 8 micron methods.

quotes.quote: AzTEC8. A robust single identification 0.16 arcsec from the SMA position. Both z_ phot and z_ LW are in good agreement with the spectroscopic redshift of z = 3.18 . This source would have been successfully identified on the basis of the original AzTEC position by both the i-K and 8 m methods.

gmw_00008763.name: A370-ID03
gmw_00008763.overview: A370-ID03 is a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:2203.06195] with a secure spectroscopic redshift of 1.258 [arXiv:2203.06195]. It has been detected by ALMA and possesses a counterpart in the Herschel catalog [arXiv:2203.06195].

sources.summary: The paper reports A370-ID03 as an ALMA detection with a secure MUSE spectroscopic counterpart at z=1.258, a reliable redshift flag, a Herschel counterpart, and an infrared SED redshift estimate.

quotes.quote: Two sources in A370 and two in AS1063 have spectroscopic redshifts, these are A370-ID01, A370-ID03, AS1063-ID02 and AS1063-ID04. A370-ID01 has a z_ spec =1.06 (secure) counterpart within 0 4 from GLASS, identifying multiple emission lines. A370-ID03 has a z_ spec =1.258 (secure) counterpart within 0 2 from Lagattuta2022 using MUSE at VLT. They give a redshift confidence flag of 3 to this source, indicating high reliability; this means that the redshift is either based on multiple clear spectral features, or on a single high-significance emission line with additional information.

We note that one detection in A370 (A370-ID03) and three in AS1063 (AS1063-ID01, AS1063-ID02 and AS1063-ID04) have counterparts in the Herschel catalog within 1'' (source coordinates given by either VLA or HST position references). A370-ID03 has a IR-estimated redshift of 0.9 from SED fitting using five Herschel bands (see Rawle2016 ).

gmw_00008764.name: AS1063-ID04
gmw_00008764.overview: AS1063-ID04 is a galaxy with a secure spectroscopic redshift of 0.611 [arXiv:2203.06195]. It features eight identified emission lines and contains a luminous, kiloparsec-sized H II region located at one edge of its disk [arXiv:2203.06195].

sources.summary: The paper reports AS1063-ID04 as an ALMA detection with multiple spectroscopic counterparts near z=0.61, adopting the secure CLASH value and noting eight emission lines and a luminous disk-edge HII region.

quotes.quote: AS1063-ID04 has a z_ spec =0.61 counterpart within 1 1 from Gomez2012 , obtained from H and [ O III ] emission lines using GMOS at Gemini-South. It has a z_ spec =0.609 (possible) counterpart within 0 9 from GLASS, having a single line detection of marginal quality but strong [ S III ] 9069 and [ S III ] 9531 emission lines using the G141 grism. It also has a z_ spec =0.611 (secure) counterpart within 0 9 from CLASH using MUSE at VLT. We adopt the latter value for this work. This counterpart was listed in a follow-up study to 24 m selected galaxies by Walth2019 .
They identified eight emission lines using LDSS-3 at Magellan-Clay in this galaxy, and reported the discovery of a luminous kpc-sized HII region at one edge of its disk.

gmw_00008765.name: 3C324
gmw_00008765.overview: The radio galaxy 3C324 is an extended object with an asymmetric east-west morphology and a dark optical center [arXiv:1508.04343, arXiv:1605.03196]. Its emission-line gas displays two distinct velocity components, which may indicate a merger or acceleration by radio jet shocks [arXiv:astro-ph/0401002].

sources.summary: The paper models 3C324 as a radio galaxy with an SED unlike quasars, fit by a pure AGN model plus old-stellar host contribution and small starburst fraction.

quotes.quote: Fits to the radio galaxies 3C324 and 3C356 The first thing that is striking about the two radio galaxies we studied here (3C324 and 3C356) is that their SEDs are distinctly different from those of the quasars. They show a much weaker near- to mid IR continuum relative to the far IR, a completely different slope in the 1 - 10 m range and evidence of shallow absorption features at 10 m. All of these differences are understood in terms of the optically thick torus model and are strongly supportive of the idea of unification. For the radio galaxies we add to the pure AGN torus or the AGN+SB models contributions from black bodies at temperature of 4000 - 5000 K, which model the emission from old stars in the host galaxies in these objects.

The SED of both galaxies are fit by pure AGN models with parameters as given in Table para.tab . In the combined models the starburst component is 8 for 3C324 and 19 for 3C356 of the intrinsic AGN luminosity (Table paragnsb.tab ).

sources.summary: 3C 324 is an extended radio galaxy with asymmetric east-west morphology, nearby sources, dark optical center, and bright eastern optical emission.

quotes.quote: 3C 324 (z=1.206) The radio galaxy 3C 324 appears in our IR image as an extended object stretching primarily east-west, with a smooth appearance to the west, and a more irregular, blobby appearance on the eastern side. In addition, there are 3 sources within 3 of the galactic center, including an area immediately to the west and in-line with the galaxy's semi-major axis, as well as two sources to the north and east. The bright galactic center in the IR image has almost no signal in the corresponding optical image. The blobby appearance of the eastern half of the galaxy in the IR image is also present in the optical image, with a semi-circle of bright emission just to the east of the dark galactic center. The western half of the galaxy appears in the optical image with the brightest signal just to the west of the galactic center and a decreasing brightness to the west as the distance from the galactic center increases.

sources.summary: The paper maps 3C324 emission-line gas, finding two velocity-separated components, possible merger or jet-shock acceleration, broad outflows, and centrally concentrated higher-ionization emission.

quotes.quote: We observed the z=1.206 radio galaxy 3C324, which has emission line regions aligned with the radio structure and extending over >100 kpc (several arcsec, well-matched to the size of the IFUs on Gemini). Our deep 2 hour spectrum targeted the [ OII ] 3727 \ emission line using GMOS (Smith et al. \ 2003), and mapped the spatial variation and velocity structure of the emission line gas (Fig. figlabel:3D3C324 ). The [ OII ] emission shows two distinct components of emission line gas with velocities separated by 800 km/s (as noted by Best et al. \ 2000). The two emission line components may indicate two separate physical systems undergoing a merger or radial acceleration by radio jet shocks. We see broad [ OII ] emission (FWHM 1000 km/s) indicating large-scale velocity outflows as well as narrower line emission (FWHM 300 km/s) in some regions.

gmw_00008766.name: J1247+6723
gmw_00008766.overview: J1247+6723 is a double-double giant radio galaxy [arXiv:2208.02130] with a Fanaroff-Riley type II morphology [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620]. It features a compact peaked-spectrum inner double [arXiv:2208.02130], core-dominated radio emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0309620], and detected neutral atomic hydrogen [arXiv:2208.02130].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1245 + 676 as the giant FR II radio galaxy VII Zw 485, a GPS source with core-dominated radio emission and tentative LINER classification needing better data.

quotes.quote: 1245 + 676 is a giant radio-galaxy with FR II type morphology (de Vries et al. 1997); it is also a known GPS source. At 1.4 GHz, its radio emission is core-dominated (Lara et al. 2001). This source has been identified with the galaxy VII Zw 485 at z= 0.107 . It displays a high contrast value and little radio polarization (Dennett-Thorpe March a 2000); in consequence, this does not seem to be a good beamed object candidate.

Spectroscopic observations published by de Vries et al. (2000) show the presence of , , 6584 and 6717, 6731 in emission; our data, corrected for the stellar contribution and modelled with a powerful line-deblending method, confirm these detections as well as the detection of 6548 in emission. However, the poor signal-to-noise ratio of our spectrum does not allow a firm detection of the 6300, 6363 lines; unfortunately, the spectrum published by de Vries et al. was not corrected for the atmospheric absorption, and displays a deep H _ 2 O absorption at the wavelengths of interest, making it impossible to check for the presence of the \ emission lines. Our LINER classification, based on one diagnostic diagram alone, is therefore to be checked through the analysis of better quality data. The presence of an AGN in this source needs still to be confirmed.

sources.summary: The review describes J1247+6723 as a double-double giant radio galaxy with a compact peaked-spectrum inner double and neutral hydrogen absorption components.

quotes.quote: Another example is the GRG J1247+6723, the bright core with a peaked spectrum is resolved into a compact double on VLBI scales Marecki03,Bondi04 . Hence, it is possible that some GRGs or RGs with bright unresolved radio cores (e.g. gigahertz-peaked spectrum core or GPS) may be harbouring an unresolved inner double. For RLAGN to be rejuvenated one of the ingredients would have to be the availability of fuel, and there have been searches for atomic and molecular gas. Among the GRGs exhibiting a DDRG morphology, both 3C236 and J1247+6723 have been detected in neutral atomic hydrogen Conway00,Saikia07 .

The third source J1247+6723 is again a DDRG where the inner double which has a peaked spectrum is only about 14 pc in size, compared to the overall size of 1200 kpc. The absorption profile obtained with the GMRT shows four components on either side of the systemic velocity Saikia07 . VLBI spectroscopic observations would be useful to determine the relationship between the different spectral components and the inner double.

gmw_00008767.name: B3 2247+381
gmw_00008767.overview: B3 2247+381 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1606.06431, arXiv:1201.2634] located at a redshift of z=0.119 [arXiv:1201.2634]. The active galaxy features a non-thermal jet [arXiv:1201.2634] with a well-defined fast-moving component [arXiv:1801.00817] and exhibits variable optical, radio, and very-high-energy gamma-ray emission [arXiv:1606.06431, arXiv:1201.2634].

sources.summary: The paper describes B3 2247+381 as a VHE BL Lac source in its monitoring sample and reports its optical/radio flux level, variability trends, and correlation behavior.

quotes.quote: B3 2247+381 : One of the faintest sources in the sample both in radio and optical band. There is a clear optical high state between MJD 55380 and 55600, during which the source was discovered in VHE rays Alek12b , but there is no clear high state in radio during this period. Polynomial subtraction suggests that at least 15 of the optical emission originates from the common optical-radio emitting region. The DCF suggests a correlation with optical leading by 90 days (see Fig. Fig:lc31 ).

sources.summary: The paper studies non-thermal jet emission from the BL Lac B3 2247+381 in a high optical state, reports its first VHE detection, and gives MAGIC-position coordinates with excess events.

quotes.quote: We study the non-thermal jet emission of the BL Lac object B3 2247+381 during a high optical state.
The BL Lac object B3 2247+381 (z=0.119) was detected, for the first time, at VHE rays at a statistical significance of 5.6 .

In the MAGIC data the distribution of the square of the angle between the reconstructed direction of the events after cuts and the position of B3 2247+381 (RA: 22.83472 ^ , DEC: 38.41028 ^ , J2000 as in Ficarra et al. \ 1985) shows an excess of 159 28 events above a threshold of 200 GeV.

sources.summary: The paper reports a well-defined fast jet component in 2247+381, observed at four epochs with a precise proper motion and assigned an Excellent quality code.

quotes.quote: 2247+381 Component 1 is observed at four epochs, with a proper motion of 0.470 0.047 mas yr ^ 1 . It is a well-defined feature, and has an `Excellent' quality code. Thus, four of the six relatively fast components have quality codes of `Excellent' or `Good' in this system, and four of the six motions are easily visible in either the full resolution or the tapered images.

gmw_00008768.name: Dragonfly 42
gmw_00008768.overview: Dragonfly 42 is a very faint ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the Coma Cluster [arXiv:1705.08513]. It is likely gravitationally bound to the nearby ultra-diffuse galaxy Dragonfly 44, as the two objects share very similar radial velocities [arXiv:1705.08513].

sources.summary: Dragonfly 42 is treated as a faint Coma UDG with a measured redshift, confirmed cluster membership, and likely bound association with Dragonfly 44.

quotes.quote: We also obtained a spectrum of Dragonfly 42, a very faint UDG. The instrumental resolution ( _ instr =32 ) and exposure time (120,600 s) were sufficient for measuring the central stellar velocity dispersions of both Dragonfly 44 and DFX1.

DFX1 and Dragonfly 42 contribute to the steadily growing sample of UDGs with confirmed distances (see Kadowaki , Zaritsky , Donnerstein 2017), and our redshifts confirm that Dragonfly 44, DFX1, and Dragonfly 42 are all members of the Coma cluster.
Dragonfly 42 and Dragonfly 44 are likely bound, as their radial velocities are less than 50 \ apart.

gmw_00008769.name: PKS 1829 718
gmw_00008769.overview: PKS 1829 718 is a radio active galactic nucleus host galaxy and a quasar [arXiv:1909.00813]. Neutral hydrogen absorption detected at a redshift of 0.536 is intrinsic to the host, where the associated gas and dust cause quasar reddening [arXiv:1909.00813].

sources.summary: The survey detects previously unknown H I absorption toward PKS 1829 718, models the absorption profile, and concludes it is intrinsic to the radio AGN host.

quotes.quote: Individual H i absorption detections PKS 1829 718 We detected H i absorption towards PKS 1829 718 centred at redshift z_ abs = 0.536.

We model the H i absorption features detected towards PKS 1829 718 as a superposition of Gaussian profiles and determine the best fitting line parameters (number of components and marginal distributions) through a Bayesian approach by comparing against other models, including a no absorption model Allison2012b , as done in .
We concluded that the H i absorption seen towards PKS 1829 718 is intrinsic to the host galaxy of the radio AGN, and that the associated gas and dust is the source of quasar reddening.

gmw_00008770.overview: The galaxy 8444-12703 is a barred disk galaxy classified as a Milky Way analogue [arXiv:2305.13366]. Its central bar structure has been analyzed using MaNGA integral field unit data to determine its physical length and pattern speed ratio [arXiv:2305.13366].

sources.summary: Galaxy 8444-12703 is used as a MaNGA proof of concept where the x_1 velocity method shortens the inferred bar and raises the bar-speed ratio to a physical value.

quotes.quote: Utility for IFU surveys As a proof of concept example, we apply our measurement technique to a Milky-Way- like galaxy measured in the MaNGA survey Bundy..MaNGA..2015 . We select galaxy 8444-12703, a part of a Milky-Way-analogue subsample GarmaOehmichen..MWanalogues..2022 . We perform the analysis purely on the MaNGA data cube.

This bar is particularly interesting, as both a Milky Way analogue, but also for the unphysically-low measurement of the dimensionless bar pattern speed ratio R =0.9 0.2 GarmaOehmichen..MWanalogues..2022 .

We find that our x_1 velocity indicator suggests a maximal x_1 length of 4.1 0.3 arcseconds, in contrast to the 5.8 arcseconds of the maximum ellipse. If we take the corotation radius at face value from GarmaOehmichen..MWanalogues..2022 , this change in bar length immediately raises the ratio R to a more physical value, 1.3.

gmw_00008771.name: VCS4 J0035+6130
gmw_00008771.overview: VCS4 J0035+6130 is an active galaxy located behind the Galactic disk [arXiv:1810.05676]. It exhibits strong, compact radio emission with apparent flux variability [arXiv:1810.05676], and its properties suggest it may be a blazar [arXiv:1801.05502].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VCS4 J0035+6130 as the strongest counterpart candidate to Fermi J0035+6131, citing hard X-rays, variable compact radio emission, and likely active-galaxy nature.

quotes.quote: VCS4 J0035+6130 The brightest X-ray source we detect inside the Fermi LAT confidence region is positionally coincident with the radio source VCS4 J0035+6130 2006AJ....131.1872P whose offset of 0 7 is comparable to the error of the X-ray source location ( 0 2 statistical, 0 5 systematic). VCS4 J0035+6130 (NVSS J003524+613030) has been observed in various radio surveys. 1998AJ....115.1693C measured a radio flux density of 292 mJy at 1.4 GHz , 1990A&AS...85..805F a flux density of 230 mJy at 2.7 GHz , and 1991ApJS...75.1011G a flux density of 180 mJy at 4.85 GHz . atel8718 performed radio observations of the object 6--23 days after the gamma-ray flare and measured mean flux densities of 230, 210, and 350 mJy , respectively, at 4.8, 11.2, and 21.7 GHz .

The radio properties of VCS4 J0035+6130 are similar to those of the active galactic nuclei investigated by 2012A&A...544A..34P , which generally exhibit milliarcsecond sizes and spectral indices above 0.5 . The strong, compact radio emission, the flat radio spectrum, and the apparent radio flux variability suggest that VCS4 J0035+6130 is an active galaxy serendipitously located behind the Galactic disk.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 87GB 003232.7+611352 as an X-ray-associated radio source with no optical counterpart, whose radio emission and optical faintness suggest a possible blazar behind the Galactic disk.

quotes.quote: One of these X-ray sources is associated with the unidentified radio source 87GB 003232.7+611352 (NVSS J003524+613030). This source currently does not have a known optical counterpart at visual wavelengths, although a detection in the near infrared has been reported . The radio emission and the low optical brightness suggest that the source may be a blazar serendipitously located behind the Galactic disk.

gmw_00008772.name: IC 267
gmw_00008772.overview: IC 267 is a borderline blue-bulge galaxy system [arXiv:astro-ph/0512346]. The galaxy exhibits strong Balmer emission, which suggests its red color is likely caused by dust from recent star formation rather than an old stellar population [arXiv:astro-ph/0512346].

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 267 as a borderline blue-bulge system: strong Balmer emission makes its red color likely dust-related from recent star formation rather than an old population.

quotes.quote: The exception is IC 267 (the red filled square with [ MgFe ] '=1.65). This bulge has strong Balmer emission which suggests that its red color is due to dust from recent star formation as opposed to an old SP. Hereafter, when we discuss blue bulges, we include IC 267 since it is similar, in most respects, to blue bulges and since its B-K color puts it right on the borderline between blue and red classes anyway.

gmw_00008773.name: NGC 5577
gmw_00008773.overview: NGC 5577 is an undisturbed spiral galaxy [arXiv:1802.09495] with a bulge characterized by positive metallicity and central color gradients [arXiv:astro-ph/0512346]. The galaxy is deficient in neutral hydrogen [arXiv:1802.09495] and its bulge exhibits a negative age gradient [arXiv:astro-ph/0512346].

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 5577 as an undisturbed spiral with H I that may have supplied stripped gas to AGC 249460 and appears H I deficient.

quotes.quote: Thus, we consider whether AGC 249460 could be gas stripped from NGC 5577, the only other galaxy in the vicinity that still has HI. Any presumed interaction must have been weak or happened a significant time ago, so that the \ in NGC 5577 should currently appear undisturbed. For comparison, the orbital time at the outer edge of the \ disc in NGC 5577 is 300 Myr. The projected distance between the objects is 16 \ or 110 kpc, and the time required to travel that distance at 300 \ is 360 Myr. The observed velocity difference between the two is only 10 , though, so that if we presume a strong interaction then AGC 249460 must be at very nearly the same distance as NGC 5577.

(If it is significantly in front of or behind then the crossing time in the radial direction at 10 \ becomes very long.) NGC 5577 itself shows possible signs of being stripped; boselli2014 give it an \ deficiency of 0.52 dex compared to its ``normal" analogs. Furthermore, its \ mass and B luminosity give M( )/ L_B 0.16 / , which is unusually low for spirals e.g. janowiecki .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5577 as an MP bulge with positive metallicity and central color gradients, a matching positive [ MgFe ] ' gradient, and a negative age gradient.

quotes.quote: IC 267 and NGC 266, 2487, 2916, 3728, and 5577) show a positive color gradient in the central 5 arcsec; of these, only IC 267 and NGC 5577 have a corresponding positive [ MgFe ] ' gradient.
Three of the five MP bulges (IC 267 and NGC 5577 and 6246A) have positive metallicity gradients.
At least five galaxies (NGCs 3728, 5577, 6246A, 7311, and the major axis of NGC 5793) have a negative age gradient in the bulge.

gmw_00008774.name: NGC 6246A
gmw_00008774.overview: NGC 6246A is characterized by a blue bulge with a flat color profile [arXiv:astro-ph/0512346]. This bulge exhibits a large single stellar population age, low metallicity, super-solar alpha-to-iron enhancement, and a positive central gradient in its magnesium-iron index [arXiv:astro-ph/0512346].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6246A as a blue bulge with super-solar /Fe, large SSP age, low metallicity, a flat color profile, and a positive central [ MgFe ] ' gradient.

quotes.quote: One of the blue bulges, NGC 6246A, has super-solar /Fe , large age, and small metallicity ( [ Z/H ] 0.8 ) like MW halo stars.
On the other hand, the central region of NGC 6246A has a positive [ MgFe ] ' gradient but its color profile is flat.
They have similar stellar populations as Prugniel et al.'s emission-line galaxies except that one of them (NGC 6246A) has a large SSP age, again possibly due to errors in emission correction.

gmw_00008775.name: F2M0825 + 4716
gmw_00008775.overview: F2M0825 + 4716 is a quasar that appears as a Type-2 object in the optical spectrum but exhibits broad lines in the infrared [arXiv:0709.2805]. Its host galaxy is linked to a nearby companion by a tidal bridge and displays double-peaked narrow emission lines [arXiv:0709.2805].

sources.summary: The paper describes F2M0825 + 4716 as optically Type-2-like but broad-lined in the infrared, with host-dominated continuum, a tidal bridge, and double-peaked narrow lines.

quotes.quote: F2M0825 + 4716 This looks like a Type-2 quasar in the optical spectrum. The color of the quasar (PSF) would suggest that also. However, the Spex IR spectrum shows broad quasar lines, however and a much redder spectral slope than implied by the optical continuum,which is probably dominated by host galaxy emission. The image shows that while the central component is fit well by an elliptical galaxy, there is a tidal bridge linking the central galaxy to a companion nearby.

The optical spectrum shows double-peaked narrow emission lines, separated by about 600km s^ 1 . The higher redshift set has relatively strong Balmer lines and [ O ii ] 3727 emission at z=0.803 , and probably corresponds to the systemic redshift of the host galaxy. The blueshifted component at z=0.800 is of higher ionization and probably corresponds to a high ionization outflow.

gmw_00008776.name: F2M0830 + 3759
gmw_00008776.overview: F2M0830 + 3759 is a dust-reddened, moderately obscured broad-line quasar [arXiv:1001.2404, arXiv:1602.03532] located at a redshift of z = 0.413 [arXiv:1001.2404]. Its host galaxy is highly disturbed [arXiv:0709.2805, arXiv:1001.2404], featuring nuclear shells [arXiv:0709.2805] and an ionization-cone-like structure [arXiv:1001.2404].

sources.summary: The authors find a highly disturbed low-redshift quasar host with nuclear shells, strong X-ray flux, a broad Fe K line, moderate absorption, and elevated gas-to-dust ratio.

quotes.quote: F2M0830 + 3759 This quasar has the lowest redshift in our sample, therefore features close to the nucleus will be more easily observable. The Hubble image in fact shows a lot of irregularity near the nucleus with shells of material along the major axis, either side of the nucleus. They almost cancel each other out in the radial profile plot, resulting in a deceptively smooth radial profile, while in reality the host galaxy is very disturbed.

F2M0830 + 3759 was also observed with Chandra and has a very high X-ray flux urrutia . The X-ray spectrum shows a very broad Iron K line, hinting that we are looking into the broad line region of the quasar. It has a moderate absorption, but a higher than usual spectral slope ( 2.9 ). This is also the only object for which we have a gas:dust ratio, which is about 5 times Galactic value. While dusty, it is well within the 3-100 times Galactic value range deduced by .
