sources.summary: FTM 0830 + 3759 is a dust-reddened z=0.413 broad-line quasar with a complex absorbed X-ray spectrum, soft excess, and disturbed host with ionization-cone-like structure.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: We assume throughout this paper H_0 = 70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _ = 0.73 and _M = 0.27 spergel07 . ccccccc Multiwavelength properties of R.A. & Dec. & z^a & Flux 1.4 GHz ^a & J - K^ b & E(B-V)^ & M ^ _B ( h m s ) & ( ' '' ) & & (mJy) & (mag) & (mag) & (mag) 08 30 11.12 & + 37 59 51.8 & 0.413 & 6.4 & 2.28 &1.29 0.12 & 23.07 All magnitudes are in the Vega system. a Data from urrutia05 . b Data from glikman07 .

quotes.quote: We report results from a 50 ks observation of the dust-reddened broad-line quasar ( z = 0.413) selected from the FIRST/2MASS Red Quasar survey. For this AGN, a very short 9 ks exposure had suggested a feature-rich X-ray spectrum and HST images revealed a very disturbed host galaxy morphology. Contrary to classical, optically-selected quasars, the X-ray properties of red (i.e. with J-K_s> 1.7 and R-K_s> 4.0) broad line quasars are still quite unexplored, although there is a growing consensus that, due to moderate obscuration, these objects can offer a unique view of spectral components typically swamped by the AGN light in normal, blue quasars. The observation discussed here has definitely confirmed the complexity of the X-ray spectrum revealing the presence of a cold (or mildly-ionized) absorber with 10 ^ 22 2 along the line of sight to the nucleus and a Compton reflection component accompanied by an intense emission line in this quasar with a 5 10 ^ 44 . A soft-excess component is also required by the data. The match between the column density derived by our spectral analysis and that expected on the basis of reddening due to the dust suggests the possibility that both absorptions occur in the same medium. \ is characterized by an extinction/absorption-corrected X-ray-to-optical flux ratio \ = 2.3, that is steeper than expected on the basis of its UV luminosity.

HST ACS Wide Field Camera images reveal that the host galaxy of shows a lot of irregularities near the nucleus along the major axis. A prominent structure with a ionization cone morphology is present: it can be interpreted as due to merger-induced star formation regions or to the presence of outflowing gas. After an accurate PSF fitting and host galaxy subtraction, urrutia08 measured a M _B = 23.07 mag and a reddening of E(B-V) = 1.29 for the quasar in .

sources.summary: NuSTAR and XMM-Newton data show F2M 0830+3759 is a moderately obscured red quasar with soft X-ray excess, Fe K emission, and global obscuration exceeding its line-of-sight column.

quotes.quote: Here, we present NuSTAR and XMM-Newton / Chandra observations of FIRST-2MASS selected red quasars F2M 0830+3759 and F2M 1227+3214. We find that though F2M 0830+3759 is moderately obscured ( N_ H,Z = 2.1 0.2 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) and F2M 1227+3214 is mildly absorbed ( N_ H,Z = 3.4^ +0.8 _ 0.7 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 ) along the line-of-sight, heavier global obscuration may be present in both sources, with N_ H,S = 3.7^ +4.1 _ 2.6 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and < 5.5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , for F2M 0830+3759 and F2M 1227+3214, respectively. F2M 0830+3759 also has an excess of soft X-ray emission below 1 keV which is well accommodated by a model where 7 of the intrinsic AGN X-ray emission is scattered into the line-of-sight.

This simple phenomenological model indicates at least moderate absorption ( N_ H > 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ). Additionally, 6.4 keV (rest-frame) Fe K emission is also clearly evident, which is a signature of X-rays reflecting off either distant matter or the accretion disk e.g., krolik,george,shu,ricci .
Though the spectra of F2M 1227+3214 has hints that the global column density may be much higher than that along the line-of-sight, the spectrum of F2M 0830+3759 requires that this be the case.

gmw_00008777.name: F2M0834 + 3506
gmw_00008777.overview: F2M0834 + 3506 is a red quasar system that shows almost no evidence of interaction [arXiv:0709.2805]. Its host galaxy profile may fit a combination of elliptical profiles or represent merger remnant shells, lacking any extended tidal tails [arXiv:0709.2805].

sources.summary: F2M0834 + 3506 is presented as a mostly undisturbed red quasar system whose profile may be elliptical or remnant-shell-like, with no extended tidal features.

quotes.quote: F2M0834 + 3506 The red quasar system has almost no evidence for interaction. The profile could fit an addition of elliptical profiles, but the morphology could also represent merger remnant shells. However, there are no extended features or tidal tails. It is unclear whether the blue component just to the North of the quasar is associated with it, or whether it is an unrelated object close to the line of sight. There are broad H and H emission lines 5000 km s ^ 1 blueward of the quasar's redshift, which could be associated with that component and/or with the quasar.

gmw_00008778.name: F2M0915 + 2418
gmw_00008778.overview: F2M0915 + 2418 is a quasar host galaxy that closely fits an elliptical profile [arXiv:0709.2805]. It is in the final stage of a merger, featuring a single nucleus and a clear tidal tail extending to the east [arXiv:0709.2805].

sources.summary: The authors describe F2M0915 + 2418 as the best elliptical-profile quasar host in the sample, but with an eastern tail marking a final-stage interaction.

quotes.quote: Note that none of the galaxies are a perfect fit to an elliptical, however, there were some which came quite close, like F2M0915 + 2418.
F2M0915 + 2418 F2M0915 + 2418 is the quasar which fits an elliptical profile best. However, a clear tail is seen extending to th East indication a recent interaction.

There is a wide range in merger phase, with some of our quasar hosts in an apparently early part of the merger sequence where the merging galaxies still retain their identity (F2M0841 + 3604), through to later stages with multiple nuclei (F2M1113 + 1244; F2M1012 + 2825), and objects at the final merger stage with only a single nucleus and a tidal tail remaining (F2M0915 + 2418).

gmw_00008779.name: F2M1113 + 1244
gmw_00008779.overview: F2M1113 + 1244 is a highly luminous, dust-reddened quasar [arXiv:1708.08914] in a late-stage merger phase [arXiv:0709.2805]. Its host galaxy exhibits an asymmetric, disturbed morphology [arXiv:1708.08914] characterized by multiple nuclei, several bright knots, and a 30 to 35 kiloparsec tidal tail [arXiv:0709.2805].

sources.summary: The paper studies F2M1113+1244 as a luminous dust-reddened quasar with disturbed host morphology; XMM fits favor absorbed power-law emission and a small black hole relative to its host.

quotes.quote: We obtained XMM-Newton observations of two highly luminous dust-reddened quasars, F2M1113+1244 and F2M1656+3821, that appear to be in the early, transitional phase predicted by merger-driven models of quasar/galaxy co-evolution. These sources have been well-studied at optical through mid-infrared wavelengths and are growing relatively rapidly, with Eddington ratios >30 . Their black hole masses are relatively small compared to their host galaxies placing them below the M_ BH L_ bulge relation. We find that for both sources, an absorbed power-law model with 1-3 of the intrinsic continuum scattered or leaked back into the line-of-sight best fits their X-ray spectra.

F2M1113+1244 and F2M1656+3821 are two well-studied red quasars with photometry spanning 3500 \ through >70  m, spectroscopy at optical and near-infrared wavelengths, and high-resolution optical imaging with HST . Figure fig:hst shows their color-combined HST images, which clearly show their asymmetric and disturbed morphologies. In Figure fig:ebv we plot their optical through near-infrared spectra overlaying a fit to a reddened quasar template, from which we derive their extinction properties, E(B-V) , listed in Table tab:objects .

sources.summary: The paper treats F2M1113 + 1244 as an irregular, multi-component quasar host with bright knots, a 30-35 kpc tidal tail, and a later-stage multiple-nucleus merger morphology.

quotes.quote: We did perform the subtraction for the most irregular merging systems in our sample (F2M0841 + 3604, F2M1113 + 1244, F2M1532 + 2415, F2M1656 + 3821); however, these sources have multiple components or are extremely irregular, so the host galaxy fitting will likely be wrong.

F2M1113 + 1244 While the position of the quasar is quite certain, because of the clear stellar-like feature, it is noteworthy that the rest of the host galaxy consists of several bright knots, with a low surface brightness tidal tail extending to 30-35 kpc from the galaxy.

There is a wide range in merger phase, with some of our quasar hosts in an apparently early part of the merger sequence where the merging galaxies still retain their identity (F2M0841 + 3604), through to later stages with multiple nuclei (F2M1113 + 1244; F2M1012 + 2825), and objects at the final merger stage with only a single nucleus and a tidal tail remaining (F2M0915 + 2418).

gmw_00008780.name: F2M1118 0033
gmw_00008780.overview: F2M1118 0033 is a merging system featuring a nucleus, two compact components, and two trailing tidal tails [arXiv:0709.2805]. The galaxy's optical spectrum is dominated by its host galaxy, while its infrared spectrum shows a rise in the K-band that likely originates from quasar light [arXiv:0709.2805].

sources.summary: F2M1118 0033 is described as a clear merger with compact components and tidal tails, an exponential near-nuclear profile, host-dominated optical spectrum, and K-band quasar light.

quotes.quote: F2M1118 0033 The image for this red quasar shows it to be a clear merger with a nucleus and two compact components, one near the nucleus and the other one at the end of the two tidal tails trailing the system. This is the only system for which an exponential profile fit the host galaxy better than an elliptical (but only near the nucleus). The optical spectrum for F2M1118 0033 shows a 4000 Balmer break, and a red spectral slope which appears to be dominated by the host galaxy. The infrared spectrum also fails to show obvious broad emission lines, but does show a significant rise in K-band, probably from quasar light.

gmw_00008781.name: F2M1151 + 5359
gmw_00008781.overview: F2M1151 + 5359 is a quasar hosted by a comparatively normal, undisturbed elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0709.2805]. The galaxy's spectrum displays asymmetric emission line profiles and narrow magnesium II absorption lines near the quasar's redshift [arXiv:0709.2805].

sources.summary: F2M1151 + 5359 is presented as a comparatively normal, undisturbed elliptical quasar host with good blue-band fit, minor I-band residuals, and asymmetric emission lines.

quotes.quote: F2M1151 + 5359 F2M1151 + 5359 is the quasar also fits an elliptical profile quite well, but there is still some residual in the host galaxy after model subtraction in the I_c band. The blue band fits almost perfectly. So this would be comparable to other ``normal'', bright, blue quasars, which have an undisturbed elliptical galaxy as a host. The spectrum shows asymmetric profiles in the emission lines and narrow MgII absorption lines very close to the quasar redshift.

gmw_00008782.name: F2M1507 + 3129
gmw_00008782.overview: F2M1507 + 3129 is a red quasar at a redshift of approximately 1 [arXiv:0709.2805]. Its host galaxy features an elliptical-like central component that is connected to a nearby red companion by a low surface brightness tidal bridge, indicating a large merger [arXiv:0709.2805].

sources.summary: F2M1507 + 3129 is the highest-redshift ACS target, with dust reddening, an elliptical-like central component, a tidal bridge to a red companion, and a large merger at z near 1.

quotes.quote: F2M1507 + 3129 This is the highest redshift red quasar imaged with ACS. The spectrum fits dust reddening quite well. Also here the central component can be well fit by an elliptical galaxy, but there is a low surface brightness tidal bridge connecting it to the red companion nearby. F2M1507 + 3129 is thus a very large (35 kpc) and spectacular merger at z 1.

gmw_00008783.name: F2M1532 + 2415
gmw_00008783.overview: F2M1532 + 2415 is an extremely disturbed quasar host galaxy undergoing extreme interaction [arXiv:0709.2805]. It features multiple components with an unclear nuclear location and is classified as a classical Fanaroff-Riley Type II radio double [arXiv:0709.2805].

sources.summary: The HST and radio data show F2M1532 + 2415 as an extremely disturbed quasar host with many components, uncertain nucleus location, and FRII radio-double emission.

quotes.quote: F2M1532 + 2415 HST imaging of F2M1532 + 2415 displays a truly disturbed host, with many components and no clear evidence of where the quasar is located among the constituents of the host galaxy. Radio maps show the synchrotron radiation coming from the central red component. They also show that this quasar is actually a classical Fanaroff-Riley Type II radio double (FRII, ), with the outer components being over one arminute away from the central source. The optical spectrum shows a very narrow H line and no broad MgII line, but in the infrared Pa is broad. The quasar host shows extreme interaction.

gmw_00008784.name: F2M1656 + 3821
gmw_00008784.overview: F2M1656 + 3821 is a highly luminous, dust-reddened quasar [arXiv:1708.08914] that displays an asymmetric and disturbed host galaxy morphology [arXiv:1708.08914]. High-resolution imaging reveals three distinct components [arXiv:0709.2805], and its radio emission originates from the brightest optical point source [arXiv:0709.2805].

sources.summary: The paper studies F2M1656+3821 as a luminous dust-reddened quasar with disturbed host morphology; XMM fits favor absorbed power-law emission and a small black hole relative to its host.

quotes.quote: We obtained XMM-Newton observations of two highly luminous dust-reddened quasars, F2M1113+1244 and F2M1656+3821, that appear to be in the early, transitional phase predicted by merger-driven models of quasar/galaxy co-evolution. These sources have been well-studied at optical through mid-infrared wavelengths and are growing relatively rapidly, with Eddington ratios >30 . Their black hole masses are relatively small compared to their host galaxies placing them below the M_ BH L_ bulge relation. We find that for both sources, an absorbed power-law model with 1-3 of the intrinsic continuum scattered or leaked back into the line-of-sight best fits their X-ray spectra.

F2M1113+1244 and F2M1656+3821 are two well-studied red quasars with photometry spanning 3500 \ through >70  m, spectroscopy at optical and near-infrared wavelengths, and high-resolution optical imaging with HST . Figure fig:hst shows their color-combined HST images, which clearly show their asymmetric and disturbed morphologies. In Figure fig:ebv we plot their optical through near-infrared spectra overlaying a fit to a reddened quasar template, from which we derive their extinction properties, E(B-V) , listed in Table tab:objects .

sources.summary: F2M1656 + 3821 is optically host-dominated but near-infrared quasar-like, with three HST components, radio emission from the brightest point source, and an argued-against lensing scenario.

quotes.quote: F2M1656 + 3821 This optically-faint object shows almost only host galaxy emission in the optical part of the spectrum, but in the near-infrared there is a very red continuum and a broad Pa line. There are three components in the HST image, two point-like and one elliptical. The radio flux points to the emission coming from the brightest (SW) optical point source.

The morphology of F2M1656 + 3821 could suggest that the system is a gravitational lens with the lensing galaxy lying between the two point sources. The reddening for this system might stem from the lensing galaxy and not the quasar itself, as is implied by the host galaxy's very red colors. However, the lack of any arc-like morphology in the outer optical components and the lack of spectral features at lower redshift in this object's spectrum argues against the lensing hypothesis.

gmw_00008785.name: Yagi012
gmw_00008785.overview: Yagi012, also cataloged as DFX2, is an ultra-diffuse dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1801.09686]. It is a confirmed member of the Coma Cluster and may belong to a compact, bound group of ultra-diffuse galaxies in the cluster's southeast region [arXiv:1801.09686].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Yagi012 as DFX2, confirms its Coma-cluster membership from the reduced D16 mask, and notes it may belong to a compact southeast UDG group.

quotes.quote: Yagi012 is the ultra-diffuse galaxy from the D16 mask with radial velocity not published in the literature.
Note that DFX2 is also cataloged in Y16 Y16 as Yagi012. Apart from Yagi012, these UDGs all have their radial velocities published in vanDokkum_2017 .

In addition, we confirm the cluster membership of Yagi012, from the D16 mask we have reduced. We have also obtained radial velocities for 35 non-UDG Coma cluster galaxies. Also, there are 3 UDGs (DF42, DF44, and Yagi012) in the South-East direction of the cluster (see Figure fig:spatial_distr ) that are within 320 kpc and 130  of each other, and thus may represent abound group of UDGs.

gmw_00008786.name: DF08
gmw_00008786.overview: DF08 is a ultra-diffuse galaxy confirmed as a member of the Coma Cluster [arXiv:1703.02042]. It is classified as an ancient infall into the cluster [arXiv:1801.09686], yet it features weak oxygen emission lines that indicate it is not entirely gas-depleted [arXiv:1703.02042, arXiv:1801.09686].

sources.summary: The paper measures DF08's redshift, confirms it as a Coma UDG member, and highlights its weak oxygen emission lines as evidence that it is not fully gas-depleted.

quotes.quote: We compare emission and absorption redshifts for DF08 to estimate the external uncertainties of the stellar cross correlation analysis. Four of our UDGs (DF07, DF08, DF30, and DF40), plus the previously measured DF44, fall within the caustics and we conclude these are bona fide members.

In Figure fig:OII_OIII we show a spectrum of DF08, which features weak [ OII ] and [ OIII ] emission lines at a redshift consistent with the absorption line redshift. The existence of these lines indicates that DF08 is not entirely gas-depleted nor devoid of ionizing sources. We present the first cluster UDG (DF08) spectrum with emission lines.

sources.summary: The paper classifies DF08 among ancient-infall UDGs, notes reported weak oxygen emission, and suggests it may be massive enough to retain gas for 8 Gyr.

quotes.quote: A few UDGs (Yagi285, Yagi364, DF08, Yagi093) unfortunately, we do not yet have the stellar populations of these ``primordial'' UDGs apart from Yagi093, which is 8  Gyr old (Paper II) and may be undergoing some disruption are ``primordial'' in the sense that they have been in the cluster since early epochs, with accretion epochs comparable to NGC 4874 and NGC 4889. The detection of weak [ O II ] and [ O III ] emission lines was reported in Kadowaki_2017 for DF08, although our analysis here suggests it is an ancient infall into the cluster. This could imply that DF08 is massive enough to have retained its gas for 8  Gyr.

gmw_00008787.name: LBQS 2212-1759
gmw_00008787.overview: LBQS 2212-1759 is a broad-absorption line quasar that exhibits extreme X-ray weakness [arXiv:astro-ph/0509432]. Simultaneous ultraviolet and optical observations reveal a blue spectrum, suggesting that intrinsic absorbing gas completely covers the compact X-ray source but only partially covers the UV-emitting region [arXiv:astro-ph/0509432].

sources.summary: XMM-Newton observations found LBQS 2212-1759 extremely X-ray weak, with blue UV/optical emission and evidence that intrinsic absorption partially covers different emitting regions.

quotes.quote: Very long (172 ks effective exposure time) observations of the BALQSO LBQS 2212-1759 with XMM-Newton yield a stringent upper-limit on its 0.2--10 keV (rest- frame 0.64--32.2 keV) flux, F_ 0.64-32.2 6 10^ 17 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 , while simultaneous UV and optical observations reveal a rather blue spectrum extending to 650 in the source rest frame. These results are used to set a tight upper-limit on its optical to X-ray spectral index _ ox 2.56 . Given the HI-BAL nature of LBQS 212-1759 , its X-ray weakness is most likely due to intrinsic absorption. If this is the case, and assuming that the intrinsic _ ox of LBQS 2212-1759 is -1.63 -- a value appropriate for a radio-quiet quasar of this luminosity -- one can set a lower limit on the X-ray absorbing column N_ H 3.4 10^ 25 cm^ 2 . Such a large column has a Thomson optical depth to electron scattering _ Th 23 , sufficient to extinguish the optical and UV emission.

This apparent contradiction probably means that our lines-of-sight to the X-ray and to the UV emitting regions are different, such that the gas covers completely the compact X-ray source but only partially the more extended source of ultraviolet photons.

gmw_00008788.name: B2 1005+28
gmw_00008788.overview: The radio galaxy B2 1005+28 has an optical spectrum that is completely devoid of emission features [arXiv:1508.03287]. Although its photometric fit is improved by including a young stellar component, the galaxy shows no trace of a ultraviolet excess [arXiv:1508.03287].

sources.summary: B2 1005+28 is described as needing a young stellar component for a better fit, while lacking UV excess and optical emission features.

quotes.quote: B2 1005+28. A young stellar component improves the fit, but there is no trace of a UV excess. The optical spectrum is devoid of emission features. The WISE data follow the old stellar component very well.

gmw_00008789.name: B2 1430+25
gmw_00008789.overview: The radio galaxy B2 1430+25 exhibits a ultraviolet excess [arXiv:1508.03287]. This excess is compatible with either a young stellar population or a power-law distribution, and the galaxy's spectrum displays moderately strong Balmer absorption lines [arXiv:1508.03287].

sources.summary: B2 1430+25 has WISE mismatch from point-source modeling, a significant UV excess compatible with YSP or power law, and Balmer absorption.

quotes.quote: B2 1430+25. The mismatch of the WISE data is due to their being based on a point source model. The UV excess ( XUV= 2.08 ) and the distribution the SDSS and GALEX points allow both a YSP or a power law as a plausible candidate. We note, however, that the spectrum shows some moderately strong Balmer lines in absorption.

gmw_00008790.name: B2 1455+28
gmw_00008790.overview: B2 1455+28 is classified as a high-excitation radio galaxy and a strong Fanaroff-Riley class II radio source [arXiv:1508.03287]. Although it lacks ultraviolet information, the galaxy is likely to possess an ultraviolet excess [arXiv:1508.03287].

sources.summary: The paper reports that B2 1455+28 lacks UV information but is a HEG, a strong FRII source, and likely has significant UV excess.

quotes.quote: B2 1455+28. Only a one-component fit was possible because UV information is lacking. However, the source is a HEG and also a strong FRII radio source. It is therefore likely that this source has a significant UV excess.

gmw_00008791.name: B2 1613+27
gmw_00008791.overview: The radio galaxy B2 1613+27 formally lacks a ultraviolet excess [arXiv:1508.03287]. However, it exhibits a strong increase towards far-ultraviolet wavelengths, which requires either a very young stellar component or a power law to fit the emission [arXiv:1508.03287].

sources.summary: B2 1613+27 formally lacks UV excess but rises toward FUV; the paper says only a young component or power law can fit it.

quotes.quote: B2 1613+27. Although there formally is no UV excess ( XUV=0 ), there appears to be a strong increase towards FUV. The reason for this is that XUV , which refers to the emitted luminosity at 2000 , is in this case mostly determined by NUV, which still follows the old stellar population (see Fig. fig1 ). Therefore a fit that takes the increase into account can only be achieved with a very young stellar component or a power law. There are no obvious signs of either in the optical spectrum (there is only H emission).

gmw_00008792.name: B2 0908+37
gmw_00008792.overview: B2 0908+37 is a radio galaxy with available multiwavelength photometry [arXiv:1508.03287]. Its WISE infrared luminosities are anomalously low compared to extrapolated values from GALEX, SDSS, and 2MASS data, despite the use of an extended source model for the WISE measurements [arXiv:1508.03287].

sources.summary: B2 0908+37 has anomalously low WISE luminosities relative to GALEX, SDSS, and 2MASS, despite an extended-source model and a consistent slope.

quotes.quote: B2 0908+37. The WISE data fall well below the values expected from GALEX, SDSS, and 2MASS data, although the slope appears to be consistent. This is one of the few cases in which the WISE flux determination has failed to account correctly for the extendedness of the object.

Indeed, there are a few cases where the WISE luminosities appear to be discrepant (B2 0908+37, B2 0924+30, B2 1243+26, B2 1430+25 and B2 1553+24) and significantly lower than the extrapolated luminosities from the SDSS and 2MASS. For three of these five sources, the WISE luminosities were determined using a point source fit, but not B2 0908+37 and B2 1553+25, which were fitted with an extended source model. The discrepancy of the WISE luminosities remains unaccounted for only in these two sources.

gmw_00008793.name: GS IRS20
gmw_00008793.overview: GS IRS20 is a high-redshift galaxy at z ~ 1.9 [arXiv:2002.08371, arXiv:2108.05596] that is classified as an irregular, merger-like starburst [arXiv:2002.08371] or a possibly interacting galaxy [arXiv:0910.3642]. It exhibits dusty star formation [arXiv:2108.05596] and features faint, extended emission to the northeast that suggests a tidally disrupted stellar population [arXiv:2002.08371].

sources.summary: The paper detects [C II] in GS IRS20 at high significance, finds marginally resolved emission, and classifies it as a merger-like starburst above the z=2 main sequence.

quotes.quote: Figure spec20 shows the detection of [ C II ] in GS IRS20's ALMA Band 9 spectrum. Gaps in spectral coverage are the result of limitations when configuring ALMA's spectral windows. We integrated the line over the frequency range where emission rose above the continuum level and measured a flux density of S_ v=9.95 0.07 Jy km s ^ 1 at a SNR of 34.3 and line velocity width of 330 km s ^ 1 . Next, we calculated the \ line luminosity \ in solar units following : = 1.04 10^ 3 S_ v D_L^2 _ obs \ [ L _ ] where D_L is the luminosity distance in Mpc, and _ obs is the observed frequency of the line in GHz. From the Band 9 spectrum, we calculate / L _ =9.169 0.003 in GS IRS20, the highest SNR detection of \ emission in a z 2 galaxy to date.
From a collapsed ALMA data cube containing only line emission, we find that \ in GS IRS20 is marginally resolved with a spatial FWHM of 0.56'' , corresponding to 4.7 kpc at z=1.9239 .

GS IRS20 is considered to be a merger by 80 of classifiers, and irregular by 100 , consistent with its position >5 above the z=2 galaxy main sequence (Fig. sfms ), and the presence of faint extended \ emission to the North-East, reminiscent of a tidally disrupted stellar population. Dust continuum and \ emission in GS IRS20 are co-spatial and coincide with the \ maximum.

sources.summary: The paper describes AzTEC.GS21 as a z=1.910 DSFG with compact unresolved ALMA emission tracing dusty star formation, radio flux from host star formation, and main-sequence SED properties.

quotes.quote: AzTEC.GS21 (J033247.59-274452.3) AzTEC.GS21 is a DSFG galaxy at z _ spec = 1.910 0.001 grism redshift by measured with the FORS2 spectrograph (ESO/VLT), ESO/GOODS spectroscopic campaign program in the GOODS-S field Vanzella2008:2008A&A...478...83V .

We found a >5 continuum detection for this source in the ALMA B9 ( =400-500 m), measuring a flux S _ B9 =12 1 mJy (Tab. tab:continuum ). AzTEC.GS21 is not resolved in the ALMA maps. For this reason we chose the map with the best spatial resolution available in the ALMA Archive in order to give at least an upper limit on its size, i.e. _ ALMA <0.7 kpc, that we use to trace the bulk of dusty star formation occurring in the galaxy. The AzTEC source has a radio (unresolved) counterpart (GS21a) in the deep ( 8 Jy) VLA map at 1.4 GHz by Kellermann2008:2008ApJS..179...71K and Miller2013:2013ApJS..205...13M . The corresponding radio flux is S _ 1.4 GHz =43.6 6.3 Jy, that we found to be consistent with the radio emission coming from the host galaxy star formation Pantoni2021:2021MNRAS.504..928P .
The SED analysis by Pantoni2021:2021MNRAS.504..928P presents AzTEC.GS21 as a main-sequence galaxy. The galaxy is forming stars at a rate SFR 350 M _ yr ^ 1 , with a sSFR 2 Gyr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: BLAST 861 is tied to a faint red possibly interacting galaxy with a unique z about 2 photometric solution, despite a marginal radio identification.

quotes.quote: BLAST 861: z_ est = 1.95 This source does not have a counterpart at 350 m or 500 m , but the 250 m flux-density is too faint for this to offer a useful redshift constraint. The radio indentification, at a radius of 11.9 arcsec, only just passes the adopted significance threshold P < 0.1 . This identifies the BLAST source with a faint, red, possibly interacting galaxy, for which the available photometry delivers a unique and well-constrained estimated redshift at z 2 . As with BLAST 66, the published COMBO17 photometric redshift z_ phot = 0.881 is very poorly constrained, and can be safely rejected.

gmw_00008794.name: GS IRS61
gmw_00008794.overview: GS IRS61 is a compact spheroid galaxy with a spatial dust-continuum extent of five kiloparsecs [arXiv:2002.08371]. Its X-ray and photometric properties are consistent with star-formation activity rather than an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1204.1152], and it shows evidence of high PAH ionization [arXiv:2002.08371].

sources.summary: The paper gives GS IRS61 a secure [C II] upper limit, describes compact dust-continuum emission, and interprets its low [C II]/PAH ratio as high PAH ionization.

quotes.quote: Amongst the non-detections, only GS IRS61 has p(l| ALMA , z)>90 . For this galaxy, we first calculate the rms over a spectrum at 50 km s ^ 1 resolution (rms _ 50 ), extracted from an aperture centered on the dust continuum. Then, we calculate the 3 upper limit on the line luminosity using Equation eqn:lcii with S_ v = 3 v( 6 rms _ 50 ), assuming v=300 km s ^ 1 as is observed in GS IRS20. Our upper limits for GS IRS61 on \ are summarized in Table almacont , and could be a factor 1.8 (0.25 dex) larger than what is reported if we assume a more extreme v=600 km s ^ 1 , greater than the noise-weighted average of 430 km s ^ 1 as observed in [ C II ] emitters at z 2-3 (e.g., the sample of Gullberg2015 ).

The rest of the sample was not classified as mergers, and GS IRS61 is classified as a spheroid by the full set of classifiers. The spatial extent of band and dust continuum in GS IRS61 is 5 kpc (FWHM), making this galaxy extremely compact.
GS IRS61, the galaxy with the lowest /PAH, shows evidence for high PAH ionization, consistent with inefficient gas heating in PDR regions.

sources.summary: U428 is the lone source whose photometry did not support an AGN component; X-ray classification treats it as a galaxy, with hard-band limits consistent with star formation.

quotes.quote: The SED-fitting procedure found that for all but one source (U428) the presence of an AGN is consistent with the photometry, although for only nine sources ( 35 of the sample: U4639, U4950, U4958, U5150, U5152, U5153, U5652, U5805, and U5877) its presence is significant at the 3 confidence level (i.e., solutions with no torus emission have ^ 2 ^ 2 _ min +28.5).
According to such classification, only two sources of the present sample are classified as galaxies, U428 and U4639, the remaining six being AGN.

Four sources have hard-band (2--8 keV) upper limits: U428, U4639, U5632, and U5775, all of which being characterized by limited counting statistics (12--26 counts in the full band). Their \ luminosity (see Table xray_properties ) For the four sources with 2--8 keV upper limits, the 2--10 keV luminosities are reported in Table xray_properties as these sources were detected in the hard band. We note, in fact, that the values reported in the table are derived from the 0.5--8 keV fluxes, where all of these sources are detected. is consistent with star-formation activity, although the presence of a low-luminosity AGN cannot be excluded.

gmw_00008795.name: ESO 374 G044
gmw_00008795.overview: ESO 374 G044 is a nearby barred spiral galaxy located in the Antlia galaxy cluster [arXiv:1610.06243]. The galaxy hosts a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus, which exhibits narrow, intermediate, and broad permitted-line components in its near-infrared spectra [arXiv:1610.06243].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 374 G044 as a nearby Antlia-cluster SBa Seyfert 2 galaxy whose NIR spectra require narrow, intermediate, and broad permitted-line components.

quotes.quote: ESO 374 G044 is a nearby SBa galaxy in the Antlia galaxy cluster hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus veron10 . The best fit of the most relevant emission lines of ESO 374 G044 are shown in Figure fig:ESO374G44 . The MR spectrum in the region of the Pa line has been fitted by three components: the narrow (FWHM = 450 ) Pa , the broad (1413 ) Pa and an intermediate (FWHM = 652 ) [ Fe II ] 12570 \ . The LR spectrum of the He I region has been fitted by three components: the narrow (FWHM = 632 ; 463 if deconvolved for the instrumental resolution) and broad (1202 ; 1122 if deconvolved for the instrumental resolution) He I and the narrow Pa . The optical spectrum does not show evidence of broad (or intermediate) line components, confirming the Seyfert 2 optical classification of .

gmw_00008796.name: MCG 01 24 012
gmw_00008796.overview: MCG 01 24 012 is a nearby spiral galaxy hosting a Compton-thin Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:1610.06243]. The galaxy features an extended narrow-line region [arXiv:1610.06243], and its near-infrared spectrum requires a broad Paschen-alpha component [arXiv:1610.06243].

sources.summary: The paper describes MCG 01 24 012 as a Compton-thin Seyfert 2 spiral with extended narrow-line emission, X-ray absorption features, and a required broad Pa component.

quotes.quote: MCG 01 24 012 is a nearby spiral galaxy hosting a Compton-thin ( N _ H 7 10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) Seyfert 2 nucleus veron06 . It was detected in the hard X-rays by with Beppo SAX/PDS observations, and it was identified as the counterpart of the X-ray source H0917 074, detected by . Interestingly, the X-ray spectrum shows the presence of a Fe K emission line and an absorption feature at 8.7 keV which cannot be explained with the presence of a warm absorber. From HST images, found a resolved emission in the [ O III ] which was explained as an extended (1.15 arcsec 2.3 arcsec; 460 pc 910 pc) NLR with the major axis along PA = 75 ^ .

Similarly, in the NIR spectrum an intermediate component having a blueshift of v = 806 \ is present in the LR He I and Pa lines and in the MR Pa line. Moreover the inclusion of a broad (FWHM = 2070 ) Pa component is necessary. In Figure fig:ResMCG012412 the fit with and without the inclusion of this broad Pa component is shown. The F test gives a probability of 2 10 ^ 15 that the improvement of the fit obtained with the inclusion of a broad Pa component is due to statistical fluctuations.

gmw_00008797.name: 2MASX J18305065+0928414
gmw_00008797.overview: 2MASX J18305065+0928414 is a nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1610.06243]. Its near-infrared spectrum displays a broad helium I emission line component, but lacks evidence of iron II or Paschen beta emission lines [arXiv:1610.06243].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 2MASX J18305065+0928414 as a nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy and finds a broad He I component in its X-shooter NIR spectrum.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J18305065+0928414 is a nearby galaxy, classified as a Seyfert 2 on the basis of the detection of pure narrow H and H in the optical spectra by . The X-shooter NIR spectrum of 2MASX J18305065+0928414 shows a faint He I with the signature of a broad component which falls near a telluric absorption band (see Figure fig:2MASXJ18+09 ). Moreover there is no evidence of the [ Fe II ] 12570 \ and Pa emission lines. The He I broad component has a FWHM of 3513 \ while the narrow component is associated with the FWHM = 216 \ component found in the optical [ N II ] lines. In Figure fig:Res2masxj1809 the fit with and without the inclusion of this broad He I component is shown.

gmw_00008798.name: ESO 234 G050
gmw_00008798.overview: ESO 234 G050 is a blue compact dwarf elliptical galaxy that hosts a Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:1610.06243]. Recent observations have detected a faint broad-line region component in its hydrogen emission lines, alongside evidence of a starburst component [arXiv:1610.06243].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 234 G050 as a blue compact dwarf elliptical Seyfert 2 with newly detected faint broad H and Pa BLR emission plus a starburst component.

quotes.quote: ESO 234 G050 is a blue compact dwarf (BCD) elliptical galaxy hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus aguero93 . No detection of broad components in the permitted lines was reported so far. In our optical X-shooter spectra we have found no evidence for broad components in the H , while a faint broad H having FWHM 970 \ has been detected. The Pa has been fitted by three components: the NLR component having FHWM = 167 , an intermediate component having FWHM = 343 and blueshifted by 35 \ with respect to the NLR (similarly as observed in the optical band) and a BLR component having FWHM = 1305 \ , with the same center of the NLR component. In Figure fig:ResESO234G050 the fit with and without the inclusion of the broad Pa component is shown.

The F test gives a probability of 1 10 ^ 60 that the improvement of the fit is due to statistical fluctuations. We therefore attribute it to the BLR emission. Finally, using line ratio diagnostic diagrams (see Section sec:classAGN ), we found some indication of a starburst component beside the AGN emission.

gmw_00008799.name: SDSSJ123714.60+064759.5
gmw_00008799.overview: SDSSJ123714.60+064759.5 is a quasar [arXiv:1508.07419] that exhibits a near-infrared excess in its spectral energy distribution [arXiv:1706.04214]. The host galaxy of this active nucleus has an estimated star formation rate of approximately 2200 solar masses per year [arXiv:1706.04214].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J123714.60+064759.5 (J1237+0647) as the background quasar for z=2.69 H2 absorption and describes 2013/2014 UVES observations totaling 11.5 hours with attached ThAr calibrations.

quotes.quote: In the present study H _ 2 absorption at z =2.69 is investigated in the line of sight toward quasar SDSS J123714.60+064759.5 , hereafter J1237+0647 , for further constraining a variation of the proton-to-electron mass ratio.

The quasar J1237+0647 was observed in visitor mode in May 2013, program 091.A-0124(A), and in service mode in the period March-June 2014, program 093.A-0373(A). The total integration time amounted to 11.5 hrs . Each exposure was nominally 4800 s in duration and was expected to deliver a S/N of 13 per 2.5 per pixel at 400 nm and a resolving power R of 40 000 , with a seeing of 0.8 arcsec , a slit of 1.0 arcsec , an airmass of 1.2 and 2 2 binning. Due to bad weather conditions, four exposures were shorter than scheduled and, therefore, the predicted S/N level could not be reached in those cases. Each of the quasar exposures had an `attached' standard ThAr calibration, which is a lamp exposure taken immediately after the science exposure leaving all the instrument parameters unchanged, and was immediately followed by a `supercalibration' exposure without allowing any grating reset.

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSSJ123714.60+064759.5 as a WISSH source where adding a NIR excess improves the SED fit and estimates SFR near 2200 M/yr.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:bbNIRsino shows as an example, one of the sources for which the additional component has been added (SDSSJ123714.60+064759.5). As visible even by eye, the inclusion of the NIR excess component improves the fit, i.e. looking at the WISE 3 photometric point, we found that including the NIR excess component brings the fit a factor of 5 closer to the observed point. For SDSSJ123714.60+064759.5, the result of the F-test is F=16.67, meaning that the addition of the third component produces an improvement in the resulting fit statistic significant at 99 confidence level.

For three objects in our sample, in common with Netzer2016 , we can directly compare the values of SFR, i.e., SDSS J020950.71-000506.4 (2) with a SFR of 1700 M_ /yr from Netzer2016 and of 2000 M_ /yr from our analysis, SDSS J123714.60+064759.5 (11) having 2700 M_ /yr and 2200 M_ /yr in the two works, and SDSS J212329.46-005052.9 (15) with 300 M_ /yr and 490 M_ /yr , respectively.

gmw_00008800.name: SDSS J074521.78+473436.1
gmw_00008800.overview: SDSS J074521.78+473436.1 is a highly luminous quasar [arXiv:1706.04214]. Radiative transfer simulations indicate that heating from the central quasar contributes approximately 60 percent of the galaxy's far-infrared flux [arXiv:1706.04214].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J074521.78+473436.1 as the most luminous extreme WISSH source and estimates quasar heating contributes about 60 percent of its FIR flux.

quotes.quote: We repeated the same procedure here, for two extreme sources in our sample: the least (SDSS J123714.60+064759.5) and the most (SDSS J074521.78+473436.1) luminous ones. Given the importance of such an estimate, we will apply the same procedure for the whole sample. For the two sources in this work, we produced TRADING radiative transfer simulations, choosing as the central source the nuclear and torus emission best-fit templates obtained from the optical/NIR data as described in Section sec:3 .

By comparing the modelled and observed SED, we found that the quasar contribution to the FIR fluxes is about 43 in the least luminous source raising to 60 for the most luminous one. This points towards a mild trend with luminosity.

gmw_00008801.name: IGR J18027 1455
gmw_00008801.overview: IGR J18027 1455 is a Compton-thin, active galactic nucleus-dominated Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0606695]. It features an unresolved radio counterpart with a spectral index of -0.75, and its optical and near-infrared spectra show redshifted emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0606695].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IGR J18027 1455 as a bright type 1 Seyfert/AGN, with unresolved radio emission, redshifted optical/NIR lines, hard X-ray luminosity, and Compton-thin AGN-dominated emission.

quotes.quote: Here we report new multifrequency radio, infrared and optical observations of the source IGR J18027 1455 , as well as a multi-wavelength study from radio to hard X-rays. The radio counterpart of IGR J18027 1455 is not resolved at any observing frequency. The radio flux density is well fitted by a simple power law with a spectral index =-0.75 0.02 .

The NIR and optical spectra show redshifted emission lines with z=0.034 , and a broad H line profile with FWHM 3400 km s ^ 1 . This suggests an Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN) of type 1 as the optical counterpart of IGR J18027 1455 . We confirm the Seyfert 1 nature of the source, which is intrinsically bright at high energies both in absolute terms and when scaled to a normalized 6 cm luminosity.
Finally, comparing its X-ray luminosity with isotropic indicators, we find that the source is Compton thin and AGN dominated.

gmw_00008802.name: CXOU J174437.3-323222
gmw_00008802.overview: CXOU J174437.3-323222 is a point source with a hard X-ray spectrum that is identified as a blazar candidate [arXiv:1411.1677]. Its spectral energy distribution suggests it is either a flat-spectrum radio quasar or a low-frequency-peaked BL Lac object [arXiv:1411.1677].

sources.summary: The paper identifies CXOU J174437.3-323222/source 1 as a blazar candidate, fits its X-ray spectrum, finds no significant light-curve variability, and proposes either an FSRQ or low-frequency-peaked BL Lac nature.

quotes.quote: Two possible counterparts were uncovered: an extended source, CXOU J174453.4-323254, and a point source with a hard spectrum, CXOU J174437.3-323222.

In this section we focus on source 1, which has been tentatively identified by curran.2011fk as a blazar. We start by comparing the spectra measured by in 2008 and in 2012. The spectra from the two instruments are independently fitted with an absorbed power-law model. Table tab:s0_cxo-xmm shows that the two spectra are similar within error, but we notice that the 2008 flux is 1.6 times higher than the one measured in 2012.

Source 1's light curve (LC) does not reveal any significant variability (Figure fig:blazarLC ): the LC can be fit with a constant rate of 0.20 ct/s, returning ^2_ =0.76 (83 dof). Figure fig:blazarSED shows the spectral energy distribution (SED) of source 1. Although we are missing a critical part of the spectrum in the optical and UV, this SED matches that of either a flat spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) or that of a low-frequency peaked BL Lac (LBL) padovani.1995uq , which are both described by a synchrotron peak in the 10^ 13 - 10^ 14 Hz range and an inverse Compton peak around 10^ 22 - 10^ 23 Hz.

gmw_00008803.name: NGC 4464
gmw_00008803.overview: NGC 4464 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1507.01336, arXiv:1105.4809] classified as a power-law galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0609762]. It features disky isophotes [arXiv:1507.01336, arXiv:1105.4809], a flat age profile of approximately 10 billion years [arXiv:1105.4809], and hints of substructure [arXiv:1507.01336].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4464 as an E3 system with deep surface-brightness profiles, flat inner color, disky isophotes, hints of substructure, and evidence for multiple components.

quotes.quote: NGC 4464 NGC 4464 (VCC 1178) is an E3 system. Figure prof4464 shows the azimuthal SB profiles reaching 30 mag/arcsec ^2 in g band and 29 mag/arcsec ^2 in i band at about 100 '' ( a/a_ e 12.8 ). In this case as well, the color distribution outside 1'' is very flat over a wide radial range: (g-i) 1.2 for a/a_ e < 3 .

Outside, the color profile bends toward a minimum in correspondence of a rapid variation of the ellipticity, P.A., and shape parameters (Fig. ell4464 ). In particular, a_4 indicates ``disky'' isophotes both in g and i bands, although outside a 20'' , the shape parameters are again rather noisy. The SB profiles in both bands also show for this galaxy some hints of a substructure as light excess with respect to the ^ 1/4 fit (Table tab:devauc ) shown in Fig. prof4464 (in this case around a/a_ e 1.7 ; see the (O-C) profile).

sources.summary: NGC 4464 is an E2 galaxy with discy isophotes, rapid central rotation, declining velocity dispersion, flat age profile, and slowly decreasing metallicity.

quotes.quote: NGC 4464 = VCC 1178 It is an E2 galaxy ( = 0.25) with a S e rsic index n = 2.31 and discy isophotes. The central region rotates fast, reaching V _ max = 80 at 1.5 arcsec, while in the outskirts the rotation slowly decreases to 40 . The central velocity dispersion is _0 = 165 and it slowly decreases to 70 . The age profile of the galaxy is flat (around 10 Gyr) and its metallicity slowly decreases from -0.2 to -0.4 dex at 1 (7.5 arcsec).

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 4464 as a power-law galaxy and says the VCS pure model falls below the central data, producing a lower inferred cusp slope.

quotes.quote: VCS Models of Power-law Galaxies NGC 4434, 4464, 4621, and 4660 are galaxies that we have classified as power-laws that lf fitted with pure laws. The VCS models for all four galaxies fall below the data as 0 (Figure fig:acscomp2 ). Again, this behavior is echoed in VCS Figure 103, which shows that the fits fall below the observed domain ACS VCS data for <0 3 in NGC 4464, 4621, and 4660, and <0 15 in NGC 4434.

gmw_00008804.name: VCC 1199
gmw_00008804.overview: VCC 1199 is a close companion galaxy of NGC 4472 that exhibits tidal truncation in its outer regions [arXiv:1507.01336]. It features a multicomponent structure, including a very thin edge-on disk, a large-scale spiral pattern, and a red color profile [arXiv:1507.01336].

sources.summary: The paper presents VCC 1199 as a close NGC 4472 companion with tidal truncation, a thin edge-on disk, a spiral pattern, red color profile, and multicomponent structure.

quotes.quote: VCC 1199 VCC 1199 is another close companion of NGC 4472, located at 4.5' from its center. F+06 found that it has a surface brightness brighter than galaxies of similar luminosity and is tidally truncated in the outer regions. They also found a very thin edge-on disk aligned with the galaxy major axis, extending less than 1 '' , and a large-scale spiral pattern.

The ELLIPSE azimuthal SB profiles are shown in Fig. prof1199 . They provide a ( g-i ) color profile with almost no gradient outside 1'' , which is the reddest in our sample. The shape parameters (see Fig. ell1199 ) show a structure very similar to NGC 4467, with a rapid and significant variation of ellipticity and position angle, and the transition from inner boxy isophotes to outer disky ones (although less pronounced than in NGC 4667).
This confirms the multicomponent nature of the object and the presence of an outer disk.

gmw_00008805.name: 1E 0117.2-2837
gmw_00008805.overview: 1E 0117.2-2837 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/9810105] located at a redshift of z = 0.347 [arXiv:astro-ph/9810105]. Its X-ray spectrum is characterized by a very steep power law or, alternatively, a warm-absorbed flat power law [arXiv:astro-ph/9810105].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the NLSy1 1E 0117.2-2837, describing its steep X-ray spectrum as either a very steep power law or warm-absorbed flat power law and predicting UV-EUV lines.

quotes.quote: The X-ray spectrum of the NLSy1 1E 0117.2-2837 is analyzed. It can be successfully described by a very steep powerlaw of photon index 4 , or alternatively by a warm-absorbed flat powerlaw. UV-EUV emission lines expected to arise from the warm material are predicted.

This NLSy1 was discovered as an X-ray source by and is at a redshift of z =0.347 (Stocke et al. 1991). It is serendipituously located in one of the PSPC pointings; the steep X-ray spectrum was briefly noted by Schwartz et al. (1993). When described by a single powerlaw continuum with Galactic cold column, the photon index is 3.6 (--4.3, if N_ H is a free parameter).

gmw_00008806.name: J103027.98+052459.51
gmw_00008806.overview: J103027.98+052459.51 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z = 5.676 [arXiv:1104.4194]. It has a star formation rate of 1.6 solar masses per year and shows no reported carbon IV absorption [arXiv:1104.4194].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J103027.98+052459.51 as a z = 5.676 Ly-alpha emitter with measured line flux, luminosity, star formation rate, and no reported C IV absorption.

quotes.quote: Target 2: J103027.98+052459.51 As can be seen from Fig. fig:target2 , we detect a weak and narrow emission line which we interpret as \ emission at z = 5.676 0.002 . With a line flux F_ Ly =4.9 1.1 10^ 18 erg s ^ 1 , the detection is significant at the 4.5 level. Although the line is weak, it does fall in a relatively clean region of the spectrum, free from obvious residuals from the subtraction of sky emission lines (see top panel of Fig. fig:target2 ).

The corresponding luminosity and star formation rate are, respectively, L_ Ly =1.7 0.4 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 and SFR _ Ly = 1.6 0.4 M _ yr ^ 1 (see Table mag ). The line has FWHM 150 \ after correcting for the instrumental resolution. Although this galaxy is relatively close to the QSO sight-line, at a projected distance of 83 kpc, no \ absorption at its redshift was reported by either rw6 nor si+11 .

gmw_00008807.name: J103026.49+052505.14
gmw_00008807.overview: J103026.49+052505.14 is a Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z = 5.719 [arXiv:1104.4194]. It has an estimated star formation rate of 1.4 solar masses per year based on its Lyman-alpha luminosity, and it appears to drive a galaxy-scale outflow [arXiv:1104.4194].

sources.summary: The paper presents J103026.49+052505.14 as the closest and brightest Ly-alpha galaxy to the QSO sight-line, likely associated with metal absorption from a galaxy-wide outflow.

quotes.quote: Target 3: J103026.49+052505.14 At a projected distance of 79 kpc, this galaxy is the closest to the QSO sight-line among the three galaxies targeted by our observations. We detect a weak emission line centred at _ obs = 8168 , in a clean region of the spectrum, free from strong sky line residuals (see Fig. fig:target3 ). We identify the feature as \ emission at z = 5.719 0.002 , with a flux F_ Ly =4.3 0.9 10^ 18 erg s ^ 1 \ ( 4.8 level detection).

This corresponds to a luminosity L_ Ly =1.5 0.3 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 and star formation rate SFR _ Ly = 1.4 0.3 M _ yr ^ 1 . The emission line redshift differs by v = -214 \ from the redshift z_ abs = 5.7238 0.0001 of the strongest \ absorption system in the spectrum of the QSO, with column density = 2.3 10^ 14 \ rw6 .

The first point to note is that, with a SFR _ UV = 17.0 M _ yr ^ 1 , J103026.49+052505.14 is typical of the galaxies driving outflows at redshifts z = 2 -3 in the sample of s10 . For comparison, the 13.5 arcsec separation on the sky between the galaxy J103026.49+052505.14 and the QSO SDSS J1030+0524 corresponds to an impact parameter b = 79 kpc at z_ abs = 5.7238 . Given the uncertainties in the interpolation and the likely spread of the average relationship between W_0( 1548) and b when applied to any individual galaxy, we conclude that the strength of the \ absorption seen 79 kpc from the galaxy J103026.49+052505.14 is typical of the galaxy-scale outflows operating at redshifts z = 2 - 3 .

gmw_00008808.name: SWIFT J1553.6+2606
gmw_00008808.overview: SWIFT J1553.6+2606 is an active galactic nucleus identified as a quasar at a redshift of 0.1664 [arXiv:1307.2761]. The source exhibits a hard X-ray luminosity of 4e43 erg/s in the 2-10 keV energy band [arXiv:1307.2761].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SWIFT J1553.6+2606 with the quasar SDSS J155334.73+261441.4, rejecting a softer nearby X-ray source and measuring its redshift and X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: In the Swift 70-month catalog (Baumgartner et al. 2013), the object SDSS J155334.73+261441.4, which is a quasar at redshift z=0.1664 0.0013 , is specified as an optical counterpart of SWIFT J1553.6+2606. However, the separation between the BAT position of SWIFT J1553.6+2606 and the quasar's position in the sky is 9 , which exceeds the BAT positional accuracy (Tueller et al. 2010). At the same time, within the Swift/BAT error circle there is another X-ray source with coordinates RA=15 ^h 53 ^m 38.12 ^s , Dec=26 04 38.8 \ that can also be associated with SWIFT J1553.6+2606.

The spectrum of the first of them is typical of quasars and can be fitted by a power-law dependence of the photon flux density on energy, dN/dE E^ , with a photon index =1.7 0.4 . There is absorption in the source's spectrum that exceeds the interstellar one in this direction ( 4 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ; Dickey and Lockman 1990), but the significance of this measurement is not very high, N_ H =0.32^ +0.26 _ 0.16 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 . Thus, this source (within the BAT error circle) cannot provide the hard X-ray emission from SWIFT J1553.6+2606 and the latter is a quasar at redshift 0.1664 with a luminosity of 4 10^ 43 \ in the 2-10 keV energy band.

gmw_00008809.name: SWIFT J1852.2+8424A
gmw_00008809.overview: SWIFT J1852.2+8424A is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 0.1828 [arXiv:1307.2761]. It is classified as a Seyfert 1 galaxy with an optical magnitude of 17.4 and a typical AGN-like X-ray spectrum [arXiv:1307.2761].

sources.summary: The paper treats SWIFT J1852.2+8424A as one of two Seyfert 1 counterparts, measuring its optical magnitude, redshift, AGN-like X-ray spectrum, and 2-10 keV luminosity.

quotes.quote: SWIFT J1852.2+8424 is another hard X-ray source from the Swift survey with two possible soft X-ray counterparts located within its BAT error circle (Fig. 2a). In contrast to the preceding object, the intensities of both soft X-ray sources (following Baumgartner et al. 2013, they are designated by letters A and B here) are essentially identical in the 2-10 keV energy band ( F_ X,A 7.6 10^ 13 and F_ X,B 9.1 10^ 13 , respectively). In the optical wavelength range, they correspond to objects with magnitudes m_ ,A 17.4 and m_ ,B 15.6 and the coordinates given in Table 3 (see also Fig. 2b).

They exhibit sets of emission lines typical of Seyfert 1 galaxies -- broad Balmer hydrogen lines, narrow O [ III ] , 4959, 5007 oxygen lines, etc. The redshifts of the galaxies measured from narrow lines are z = 0.1828 and z = 0.2249 for sources A and B, respectively. The XRT X-ray spectra are typical of active galactic nuclei -- they are well fitted by a simple power law with photon indices _A=1.75 0.07 and _B=1.67 0.07 (Fig. 3). The luminosities of the galaxies in the 2-10 keV energy band are L_ X,A 0.7 10^ 44 and L_ X,B 1.4 10^ 44 , according to their redshifts.

gmw_00008810.name: SWIFT J1852.2+8424B
gmw_00008810.overview: SWIFT J1852.2+8424B is a Seyfert 1 galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.2249 [arXiv:1307.2761]. It is an active galactic nucleus with an X-ray spectrum well fitted by a simple power law [arXiv:1307.2761].

sources.summary: The paper treats SWIFT J1852.2+8424B as the second Seyfert 1 counterpart, giving its optical magnitude, redshift, active-galaxy X-ray spectrum, and comparable hard-X-ray contribution.

quotes.quote: SWIFT J1852.2+8424 is another hard X-ray source from the Swift survey with two possible soft X-ray counterparts located within its BAT error circle (Fig. 2a). In contrast to the preceding object, the intensities of both soft X-ray sources (following Baumgartner et al. 2013, they are designated by letters A and B here) are essentially identical in the 2-10 keV energy band ( F_ X,A 7.6 10^ 13 and F_ X,B 9.1 10^ 13 , respectively). In the optical wavelength range, they correspond to objects with magnitudes m_ ,A 17.4 and m_ ,B 15.6 and the coordinates given in Table 3 (see also Fig. 2b).

They exhibit sets of emission lines typical of Seyfert 1 galaxies -- broad Balmer hydrogen lines, narrow O [ III ] , 4959, 5007 oxygen lines, etc. The redshifts of the galaxies measured from narrow lines are z = 0.1828 and z = 0.2249 for sources A and B, respectively. The XRT X-ray spectra are typical of active galactic nuclei -- they are well fitted by a simple power law with photon indices _A=1.75 0.07 and _B=1.67 0.07 (Fig. 3). Thus, the X-ray emission from SWIFT J1852.2+8424 detected at energies >15 keV is the sum of the emissions from two Seyfert 1 galaxies, with the contribution from each galaxy being approximately the same.

gmw_00008811.name: NGC 4981
gmw_00008811.overview: NGC 4981 is a barred spiral galaxy that hosts a faint stellar stream, indicating past tidal interactions [arXiv:2104.06071]. The galaxy exhibits ongoing star formation along its spiral arms and at the edges and ends of its bar [arXiv:2003.08946].

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 4981's spiral-arm and bar-edge star formation, low-metallicity bar regions, and SFH evidence for very young disc populations plus a bar-age V-shape.

quotes.quote: This figure shows that most galaxies have ongoing star formation either along spiral arms (NGC 4303, NGC 4981, NGC 5248), at the ends of the bar (IC 1438) or in a ring-like feature (NGC 6902, NGC 7755). Some galaxies show star formation at the edges of the bar (NGC 4303 and a few blobs in IC 1438, NGC 4981, NGC 6902, NGC 7755) while others show none at all. Interestingly, for three galaxies (NGC 4371, NGC 4981, NGC 4984), we see low metallicity regions in the bar between the centre and the end of the bar.

The SFHs are shown as an example for NGC 4981 along the four cuts in four different panels in Fig. fig:SFH_NGC4981_mass . In the H maps in Fig. fig:sample_Ha , in fact, we see ongoing star formation at the end of the bar in NGC 4981, in agreement with the very young populations seen in the SFH. Additionally, we recognise a `V-shape' in the ages above 2 Gyr, where stars at intermediate ages between 2-8 Gyr are more concentrated close to the major axis, while the oldest population ( > 8 Gyr) is spread across the whole spatial range.

sources.summary: The survey uses NGC 4981 as an example stream host, masking unrelated pixels and measuring a faint aperture on the thinnest, faintest end of its detected stream.

quotes.quote: All pixels that do not belong to NGC 4981 and its stream have been masked in panel (b), as well as all foreground and/or background sources overlapping the stream. Line-of-sight contamination was identified as clumps over Noise Chisel 's detections, using Segment To better separate the extended emission of background galaxies or foreground stars as clumps (to be masked), Segment was run with a Gaussian kernel of FWHM = (default 1.5 pixels), the default values were used for all other parameters. also part of Gnuastro Akhlaghi20 and masked in Figure fig-detection (b). Figure fig-detection () shows the host and the stream with all masked clumps interpolated.

The arcsec radius aperture shown in (a) is positioned along the thinnest and faintest end of the detected stream at a point with no background or foreground source. The flux within this aperture has a surface brightness of mag arcsec^ 2 which has a significance of compared to random apertures of the same size placed over undetected regions, see panel (d).

gmw_00008812.name: NGC 6902
gmw_00008812.overview: NGC 6902 is a barred spiral galaxy that exhibits a characteristic bar-age V-shape [arXiv:2003.08946]. The galaxy features ring-like star formation as well as active star formation at the edges of its bar [arXiv:2003.08946].

sources.summary: The paper reports ring-like star formation and bar-edge star formation in NGC 6902, and includes it among galaxies showing the bar-age V-shape.

quotes.quote: This figure shows that most galaxies have ongoing star formation either along spiral arms (NGC 4303, NGC 4981, NGC 5248), at the ends of the bar (IC 1438) or in a ring-like feature (NGC 6902, NGC 7755). Some galaxies show star formation at the edges of the bar (NGC 4303 and a few blobs in IC 1438, NGC 4981, NGC 6902, NGC 7755) while others show none at all.
This feature is not exclusive for this galaxy but can be seen in at least 5 out of 9 galaxies in our sample (IC 1438, NGC 4643, NGC 4981, NGC 6902, NGC 7755).

gmw_00008813.name: NGC 7755
gmw_00008813.overview: NGC 7755 is a nearby Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:2312.02389] that exhibits a ring-like star formation feature and a star-formation-desert region [arXiv:2003.08946]. The galaxy hosted the supernova SN 2004cx [arXiv:2312.02389] and contains older stellar populations along the edges of its central bar [arXiv:2003.08946].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7755 as a nearby Seyfert source hosting SN 2004cx, with no significant gamma-ray detection and an H.E.S.S.-based upper limit for UHECR luminosity.

quotes.quote: In this respect, for example, the sources NGC 1068, NGC 7755, NGC 175, and Arp 220 are nearby ( D_ s < 80 \ Mpc ) starburst and Seyferts Galaxies capable of producing substantial fluxes of multimessenger particles. Even though these sources are not high-energy neutrino generators , they can foster the emission of charged particles, contributing to the GeV-TeV extragalactic gamma-ray and neutrino emission.

For the Seyferts NGC 175 and NGC 7755 hosting supernova remnants (SN), significant gamma-ray emissions have not been observed for any of the objects. Therefore, upper limits on the > 1 TeV gamma-ray flux of the order of 10^ 13 \ cm^ 2 \ s^ 1 were obtained assuming the confidence level of 95 . Supernovae (SNe) emit TeV gamma rays at different stages of their evolution. In addition, core-collapse (cc-)SNe stemming from stellar progenitors can create a rich environment for UHECR acceleration .

The upper limit on the integral gamma-ray flux of NGC 175 hosting SN 2009hf obtained by H.E.S.S , at 95 CL, is I_ ^ UL (E_ ^ th > 1 TeV ) = 5.3 10^ 13 \ cm^ 2 \ s^ 1 and for NGC 7755 hosting SN 2004cx is I_ ^ UL (E_ ^ th > 1 TeV ) = 1.9 10^ 13 \ cm^ 2 \ s^ 1 . The plots in Fig. fig:upper (b) and () depict our upper limits for UHECR luminosity concerning the hosts of SNe SN 2009hf and SN 2004cx in Seyfert sources, utilizing values obtained from a more restrictive upper limit on the integral gamma-ray.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7755's ring-like and bar-edge star formation, uses it as a star-formation-desert example, and notes older bar-edge populations and V-shaped ages.

quotes.quote: This figure shows that most galaxies have ongoing star formation either along spiral arms (NGC 4303, NGC 4981, NGC 5248), at the ends of the bar (IC 1438) or in a ring-like feature (NGC 6902, NGC 7755). Some galaxies show star formation at the edges of the bar (NGC 4303 and a few blobs in IC 1438, NGC 4981, NGC 6902, NGC 7755) while others show none at all.
In our sample, NGC 7755 in Fig. fig:sample_Ha is a nice example of a SFD between the nuclear and the inner ring of H . It seems that star formation is being suppressed by the bar in the SFD.

This feature is not exclusive for this galaxy but can be seen in at least 5 out of 9 galaxies in our sample (IC 1438, NGC 4643, NGC 4981, NGC 6902, NGC 7755). In this case, the mass-weighted maps, since they highlight older stellar populations better, show at least in IC 1438 and NGC 7755 older populations on the edges of the bar (although, admittedly, this effect is not as clear in the other galaxies).

gmw_00008814.name: NGC 3095
gmw_00008814.overview: NGC 3095 is a barred spiral galaxy that contains neutral hydrogen gas associated with its disk [arXiv:2305.01075]. It forms a pair with the galaxy NGC 3100, which is located at a projected linear distance of 95 kpc [arXiv:2305.01075].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3095 as NGC 3100's barred spiral companion, detects H I in its disk, and links its velocity and proximity to possible weak group interactions.

quotes.quote: It is the brightest galaxy of a loose group and forms a pair with the barred spiral galaxy NGC 3095 , located at a projected linear distance of 95 kpc (Fig. fig:vegasHI ).

In particular, we detect \ gas associated to the disk of NGC 3095, NGC 3108, ESO 435-28 , EQ J100-3124 , and - for the very first time - in two dwarf galaxies ( DwN , DwW ), and in a cloud located in the sky area between \ and its companion, NGC 3095 ( Cl ).
The velocity field in Fig. fig:mom1 shows that NGC 3095 and DwN have velocities very similar to \ (within 60 ), while the diffuse cloud is blue-shifted by 200 .

gmw_00008815.name: EQ J100-3124
gmw_00008815.overview: EQ J100-3124 is a galaxy that was previously referred to as the anonymous galaxy A1000--31 [arXiv:2305.01075]. Recent observations have confirmed both its systemic velocity and its neutral hydrogen mass [arXiv:2305.01075].

sources.summary: The paper detects H I in EQ J100-3124, notes earlier observations called it anonymous galaxy A1000--31, and confirms its H I mass and systemic velocity.

quotes.quote: In particular, we detect \ gas associated to the disk of NGC 3095, NGC 3108, ESO 435-28 , EQ J100-3124 , and - for the very first time - in two dwarf galaxies ( DwN , DwW ), and in a cloud located in the sky area between \ and its companion, NGC 3095 ( Cl ).

ATCA observations a factor 1.4 less sensitive than the one presented in this paper, at comparable resolutions, previously observed the \ in NGC 3108 Oosterloo:2002,hau:2008,serra:2008 . The same observations also discovered \ in EQ J100-3124 , referring to this source as `anonymous galaxy' A1000--31 . Our results confirm the \ mass and systemic velocity of the latter, while we recover only partially the \ mass of NGC 3108 reported by Oosterloo:2002 ( 4.6 10^9 , at the distance of 53 Mpc).

gmw_00008816.name: DwN
gmw_00008816.overview: DwN is a newly detected dwarf galaxy located in the NGC 3100 group [arXiv:2305.01075]. It is rich in neutral hydrogen gas and features an H I tail pointing toward NGC 3100, which suggests a past interaction between the two galaxies [arXiv:2305.01075].

sources.summary: The paper identifies DwN as a newly detected H I-rich dwarf in the NGC 3100 group, with velocity like NGC 3100 and a tail suggesting interaction.

quotes.quote: In particular, we detect \ gas associated to the disk of NGC 3095, NGC 3108, ESO 435-28 , EQ J100-3124 , and - for the very first time - in two dwarf galaxies ( DwN , DwW ), and in a cloud located in the sky area between \ and its companion, NGC 3095 ( Cl ).
The velocity field in Fig. fig:mom1 shows that NGC 3095 and DwN have velocities very similar to \ (within 60 ), while the diffuse cloud is blue-shifted by 200 .

The diagram also suggests that not only \ may have interacted with , but also DwN, since it lies very close to \ in the projected phase-space diagram and has an \ tail pointing towards it (see Fig. fig:vegasHI ).

gmw_00008817.name: HATLASJ083601.5+002617
gmw_00008817.overview: HATLASJ083601.5+002617 is a galaxy that exhibits a compact carbon monoxide morphology concentrated in its center [arXiv:2006.04812]. Observations of its neutral hydrogen spectra reveal consistent line profiles, with an integrated flux of 1.68 mJy km/s and a full width at half maximum of 390 km/s [arXiv:2006.04812].

sources.summary: The paper uses HATLASJ083601.5+002617 to compare FAST and Arecibo H I spectra, finding consistent line profiles, flux, and width, plus compact central CO morphology.

quotes.quote: For HATLASJ083601.5+002617, in upper left panel of Fig. HIspec we show the results from both FAST and Arecibo telescopes. The ALFALFA survey is in the drift scan mode, and the effective on the target integration time for the ALFALFA spectrum is about 48 s Haynes2011 . The observed spectra show consistent line profiles. The integrated flux of the Arecibo spectrum given in Haynes2018 is 1.68 0.14 mJy km s ^ 1 , and the FWHM is 390 19 km s ^ 1 , both values consistent with our results (see Table table:1 ).

We show the CO contour and the optical images of our targets in Fig. img . we can see that the CO morphology is compact in the galaxy center for HATLASJ085346.4+001252, HATLASJ083601.5+002617, and HATLASJ085112.9+010342.

gmw_00008818.name: HATLASJ085340.7+013348
gmw_00008818.overview: HATLASJ085340.7+013348 is a galaxy observed by the Five-hundred-meter Aperture Spherical radio Telescope [arXiv:2006.04812]. It exhibits a clumpy CO morphology and a relatively high molecular-to-atomic hydrogen flux ratio, which may indicate efficient molecular gas formation in its nuclear region [arXiv:2006.04812].

sources.summary: The paper describes HATLASJ085340.7+013348 as a FAST-observed VALES galaxy with clumpy CO morphology and a comparatively high H2-to-H I flux ratio, suggesting efficient nuclear molecular-gas formation.

quotes.quote: The target HATLASJ085340.7+013348 has a clumpy CO morphology, which might be the reason of the relatively higher CO to flux ratio in Fig. HIspec .

And for the galaxy HATLASJ085340.7+013348, the CO morphology is clumpy. We also see that the H _2 to flux ratio for HATLASJ085340.7+013348 is about 15, while the rest two targets have CO to flux ratios about 10. This might suggest a more efficient formation of H _2 molecules in a nuclear region, and a possible connection between CO morphology and the CO to flux ratio.

gmw_00008819.name: WGA J1320 + 0140
gmw_00008819.overview: WGA J1320 + 0140 is a radio source featuring an elongated core region [arXiv:0809.3100]. It exhibits a broad jet-like structure embedded in a lobe, alongside one-sided diffuse radio emission and low-level extended emission [arXiv:0809.3100].

sources.summary: The paper describes WGA J1320 + 0140 as having an elongated core, one-sided diffuse radio emission, a broad jet-like structure embedded in a lobe, and additional low-level emission.

quotes.quote: The VLA A array map (Fig. 4e) shows the core region to be elongated to the west. Most of the diffuse radio emission is observed perpendicular to the axis of the core elongation and extends to one side only. It has a broad jet-like structure, which appears to be embedded in a lobe. Some low-level extended radio emission is found also along the axis of the core elongation.

gmw_00008820.name: WGA 1012 + 06
gmw_00008820.overview: WGA 1012 + 06 is a radio source featuring a core-jet structure embedded in a diffuse, extended radio halo [arXiv:0809.3100]. This structure includes a one-sided jet that bends toward the south-east and south, along with a possible counterjet hot spot [arXiv:0809.3100].

sources.summary: The paper describes WGA 1012 + 06 as showing a bent one-sided jet, a possible counterjet hot spot, and a core-jet structure embedded in diffuse extended halo emission.

quotes.quote: The VLA A array map (Fig. 8c) shows a one-sided jet emanating from the core, which first bends to the south-east and then to the south. A possible hot spot of a counterjet is seen on the opposite side of the core. The core-jet structure appears to be embedded in a diffuse extended radio halo.

gmw_00008821.name: WGA J1231 + 2848
gmw_00008821.overview: The radio source WGA J1231 + 2848 features a short, one-sided jet that emerges from its core and terminates in a hot spot [arXiv:0809.3100]. This core-jet structure is embedded within large-scale diffuse lobe emission, and a low-level counterjet appears to be present on the opposite side [arXiv:0809.3100].

sources.summary: The paper describes WGA J1231 + 2848 as having a short one-sided jet ending in a hot spot, embedded in larger lobe emission, with a possible low-level counterjet.

quotes.quote: The VLA A array map (Fig. 4b) shows a short ( 5'' long) one-sided jet emerging from the core and ending in a hot spot. The combined VLA A + C array map (Fig. 4c) shows that this core-jet structure is embedded in a large-scale diffuse lobe emission. A low-level counterjet seems to be present on the opposite side of the core.

gmw_00008822.name: TEX 0836 + 182
gmw_00008822.overview: TEX 0836 + 182 is a radio source that exhibits low-level extended emission around its core [arXiv:0809.3100]. It features a diffuse jet-like structure stretching to the southeast, as well as a well-collimated jet extending to the southwest [arXiv:0809.3100].

sources.summary: The paper describes TEX 0836 + 182 as having diffuse and well-collimated jet-like radio structures on different VLA scales, with low-level extended emission around the core.

quotes.quote: The VLA A array map (Fig. 8a) shows a diffuse jet-like structure emerging from the core and stretching to the south-east. Low-level extended emission on the scales of the jet appears to be present everywhere around the core. The VLA C array map (Fig. 8b) shows a well-collimated jet emerging from the core, which extends to the south-west and therefore almost perpendicular to the jet-like structure seen on smaller scales.

gmw_00008823.name: WGA 1202 + 44
gmw_00008823.overview: WGA 1202 + 44 is a radio galaxy with large-scale emission indicative of a Fanaroff-Riley Class II source [arXiv:0809.3100]. Its core is surrounded by a small diffuse halo, and it features two lobes, with a possible embedded one-sided jet in the western lobe [arXiv:0809.3100].

sources.summary: The paper describes WGA 1202 + 44 as having a small diffuse halo, larger resolved-out radio structure, FR II-like large-scale emission, two lobes, and a possible embedded one-sided jet.

quotes.quote: The VLA A array map (Fig. 8d) shows the core to be surrounded by a small ( 10'' ) diffuse halo. As the VLA C array map (Fig. 8e) indicates, this halo is most likely the remainder of an extended radio structure that is resolved out on these small scales. The observed large-scale radio emission is indicative of an FR II. Two lobes to each side of the nucleus are visible with the eastern lobe containing a hot spot. The western lobe appears to have an embedded one-sided jet, but a VLA B array map would be necessary to confirm this.

gmw_00008824.name: WGA J0245 + 1047
gmw_00008824.overview: WGA J0245 + 1047 is an X-shaped radio galaxy [arXiv:0809.3100]. High-resolution observations reveal a classical Fanaroff-Riley II structure with a one-sided jet and lobes containing hot spots, while larger-scale emission extends almost orthogonally to create the characteristic X-shape [arXiv:0809.3100].

sources.summary: The paper describes WGA J0245 + 1047 as a classical FR II structure on one map but an X-shaped radio galaxy when A and C array data are combined.

quotes.quote: The VLA A array map (Fig. 1f) shows a classical FR II structure, a one-sided jet emerges from the core in direction south-east and a lobe with a hot spot is on each side of the core. However, the VLA A + C array map (Fig. 2a) shows that this source is rather an X-shaped radio galaxy e.g., Cheung07 . Large-scale extended lobe emission is visible to both sides of the core and almost orthogonal to the small-scale lobes, thus giving the appearance of an X-shape.

gmw_00008825.name: WGA J1311 0521
gmw_00008825.overview: WGA J1311 0521 is a radio source that exhibits extended emission on both sides of its core [arXiv:0809.3100]. Its western side features an irregular lobe that may contain a one-sided jet, while the eastern structure is highly irregular and difficult to classify [arXiv:0809.3100].

sources.summary: The paper describes WGA J1311 0521 as having extended emission on both sides, an irregular western lobe possibly containing a one-sided jet, and an ambiguous eastern structure.

quotes.quote: The VLA C array map (Fig. 4d) shows extended emission on both sides of the core. A lobe with an irregular structure is present on the western side. Since the core appears to be elongated in its direction, this lobe might contain a one-sided jet. A VLA B array map would be necessary to confirm this.
The structure on the eastern side of the core is difficult to classify. It might be either a highly irregular lobe with a hot spot or a strongly bended large-scale jet.

gmw_00008826.name: Leo IV
gmw_00008826.overview: Leo IV is a round dwarf spheroidal galaxy with a half-light radius of approximately 130 pc [arXiv:0911.5352]. Its stellar population is predominantly old and metal-poor, though it contains a small young component comprising about two percent of its stellar mass [arXiv:0911.5352].

sources.summary: The paper finds Leo IV round with a roughly 130 pc half-light radius, no detected stellar bridge to Leo V, an old metal-poor population, and a small young component.

quotes.quote: Based on parameterized fits, we find that Leo IV is round, with < 0.23 (at the 68 confidence limit) and a half-light radius of r_ h 130 pc. Additionally, we perform a thorough search for extended structures in the plane of the sky and along the line of sight. We derive our surface brightness detection limit by implanting fake structures into our catalog with stellar populations identical to that of Leo IV.
