We show that we are sensitive to stream-like structures with surface brightness _ 29.6 mag arcsec ^ 2 , and at this limit, we find no stellar bridge between Leo IV (out to a radius of 0.5 kpc) and the recently discovered, nearby satellite Leo V.

Using the color magnitude fitting package StarFISH, we determine that Leo IV is consistent with a single age ( 14 Gyr), single metallicity ( [ Fe/H ] 2.3 ) stellar population, although we can not rule out a significant spread in these value. We derive a luminosity of M_ V =-5.5 0.3 . Studying both the spatial distribution and frequency of Leo IV's 'blue plume' stars reveals evidence for a young ( 2 Gyr) stellar population which makes up 2 of its stellar mass.

gmw_00008827.name: SDSS J130402.36 + 293840.6
gmw_00008827.overview: SDSS J130402.36 + 293840.6 is a galaxy located at a redshift of 0.341 [arXiv:2204.10580]. It is the candidate host galaxy of the gamma-ray burst GRB 200716C and exhibits a high star-formation rate driven by star-forming regions rather than an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2204.10580].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J130402.36 + 293840.6 as a candidate GRB 200716C host, detected across surveys, with high radio luminosity, redshift 0.341, and no compact VLBI core.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Its coordinates are (J2000) =13^ h 04^ m 02.371^ s , = +29^ 38^ 40.66^ Adelman2008 .

quotes.quote: We present the discovery and the subsequent follow up of radio emission from SDSS J130402.36 + 293840.6 (J1304+2938), the candidate host galaxy of the gamma-ray burst (GRB) GRB 200716C. The galaxy is detected in the RACS (0.89 GHz), the NVSS, the Apertif imaging survey, and the FIRST (1.4 GHz), the VLASS (3 GHz), and in public LOFAR (130-170 MHz), WISE (3.4-22 m), and SDSS ( z, i, , g, u filters) data. The luminosity inferred at 1.4 GHz is (5.1 0.2 ) 10^ 30 erg s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 .

To characterise the emission and distinguish between different components within the galaxy, we performed dedicated, high-sensitivity and high-resolution observations with the European VLBI Network (EVN) + e MERLIN at 1.6 and 5 GHz. We did not detect any emission from a compact core, suggesting that the presence of a radio-loud active galactic nucleus (AGN) is unlikely, and therefore we ascribe the emission observed in the public surveys to star-forming regions within the galaxy. We confirm and refine the redshift estimate, z = 0.341 0.004 , with a dedicated Telescopio Nazionale Galileo (TNG) spectroscopic observation. Finally, we compiled a list of all the known hosts of GRB afterglows detected in radio and computed the corresponding radio luminosity: if GRB 200716C belongs to J1304+2938, this is the third most radio-luminous host of a GRB, implying one of the highest star-formation rates (SFRs) currently known, namely SFR 324 61 M _ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00008828.name: UGC 3463
gmw_00008828.overview: UGC 3463 is classified as an SABbc spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0311030]. It exhibits asymmetric H II regions located primarily south of its nucleus, which may be caused by differential extinction effects within its spiral arms [arXiv:astro-ph/0311030].

sources.summary: UGC 3463 is described as an SABbc galaxy with asymmetric H II regions south of the nucleus, possibly caused by extinction offsets in spiral arms.

quotes.quote: A less marked asymmetry is shown by the SABbc galaxy UGC 3463 (Fig. Fige ), where most of the bright H ii regions lie south of the nucleus. However, in this case the line of asymmetry coincides with the major axis of this inclined spiral, and it is tempting to ascribe the asymmetry to differential extinction effects, possibly due to systematic offsets between the dust lanes and the star formation centres in spiral arms. The underlying galaxy appears highly symmetric, as shown by the near-IR K-band image of UGC 3463 presented by Peletier et al. ( ), with no obvious differences between the upper and lower spiral arms in the light emitted by the old stellar population.

gmw_00008829.name: UGC 3847
gmw_00008829.overview: UGC 3847 is an irregular galaxy that is potentially interacting with the neighboring irregular galaxy UGC 3851 [arXiv:astro-ph/0311030]. It exhibits an extremely high H-alpha equivalent width and contains a exceptionally high surface brightness H II region [arXiv:astro-ph/0311030].

sources.summary: UGC 3847 is discussed as an irregular in a possible interaction with UGC 3851, having one of the survey highest H-alpha equivalent widths and extreme H II surface brightness.

quotes.quote: Another possible interaction which is worthy of comment is UGC 3847 and UGC 3851 (NGC 2363 and NGC 2366, shown in Fig. Figf ), both irregular galaxies, with the 2 highest H + [ N ii ] EWs of all 334 galaxies observed in this study. Drissen et al. ( ) consider UGC 3847 to be an outlying H ii region of UGC 3851, and note that UGC 3847 is the highest surface brightness H ii region in the entire sky. UGC 3851 is not actually part of the statistical sample, since it is too large to satisfy the selection criteria, but is included in this discussion of individual objects due to its proximity to UGC 3847.

gmw_00008830.name: NVSS J212912 153841
gmw_00008830.overview: NVSS J212912 153841 is a high-redshift quasar and a luminous blazar with a hard X-ray spectrum [arXiv:1702.04006]. The galaxy hosts an extremely luminous accretion disk and one of the most massive black holes ever found in a gamma-ray emitting blazar [arXiv:1702.04006].

sources.summary: NVSS J212912 153841 is described as a hard-X-ray luminous blazar whose SED modeling and spectroscopy indicate one of the most massive gamma-ray-blazar black holes.

quotes.quote: The quasar NVSS J212912 153841 is a hard X-ray spectrum luminous blazar and included in the 70 month Burst Alert Telescope catalog 2013ApJS..207...19B .

We generate broadband SEDs of the three objects that have archival X-ray observations and model them using a simple one zone synchrotron-IC emission approach prescribed in 2009MNRAS.397..985G . In brief, the model assumes a spherical emission region located at a distance R_ diss from the central engine and filled with a population of highly energetic electrons that follow a broken power-law distribution. In the presence of a tangled magnetic field, the electrons lose energy via synchrotron, synchrotron self Compton (SSC), and external Compton (EC) processes. For the latter, the seed photons originate from several external AGN components: photons directly emitted from the accretion disk e.g., 1993ApJ...416..458D , from BLR 1994ApJ...421..153S and from the infrared torus e.g., 2000ApJ...545..107B . The calculated jet powers and SED parameters are given in Table tab:sed_par and the modeled SEDs are shown in Figure fig:sed . In each of the three objects, the IR-UV emission is found to be dominated by an extremely luminous accretion disk ( L_ disk >10^ 46 ). The X-ray spectra, on the other hand, are hard and the entire X-ray to ray band of the SED can be explained by the IC scattering off the photons originating from the BLR.

In particular, the object NVSS J212912 153841 hosts one of the most massive black holes, 7 10^9 M _ , ever found in ray emitting blazars, confirmed both from optical spectroscopic and disk modeling approaches.

gmw_00008831.name: NGC 0337
gmw_00008831.overview: NGC 0337 is a bulgeless barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:0806.1020, arXiv:1304.0447] characterized by patchy, asymmetric stellar and hydrogen distributions that suggest a weakly interacting state [arXiv:1606.05713]. This star-forming galaxy hosts a large population of ultraluminous X-ray sources [arXiv:1304.0447] and was the host of supernova SN 2014cx [arXiv:2103.09166].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 337 as a spiral SINGS case study with broad photometric coverage, using its SED fit in a sequence of increasing star formation and dust temperature.

quotes.quote: To investigate the implications of these fits for the determination of physical parameters, we focus on three galaxies spanning different dust properties along the / versus / colour-colour relation in Fig. irx_beta a, for which a wide set of observations are available: NGC 3521, a spiral (SABbc) galaxy at a distance of 9 Mpc; NGC 337, a spiral (SBd) galaxy at a distance of 24.7 Mpc; and Mrk 33, a dwarf irregular (Im) starburst galaxy at a distance of 21.7 Mpc. For all three galaxies, observations from GALEX ( FUV and NUV ), 2MASS ( JHKs ), Spitzer (IRAC and MIPS), IRAS and SCUBA are available, along with integrated \ and \ spectroscopy from . For NGC 3521 and NGC 337, additional constraints are available from RC3 ( UBV ) and ISO (ISOCAM 6.75 and 15 ) photometry. Fig. seds2 shows the models (in black) providing the best fits to the observed ultraviolet, optical, \ and , and infrared spectral energy distributions of these three galaxies (in red). The quality of the fits is remarkable, as quantified by the residuals shown at the bottom of each spectrum.

We find that, as anticipated from Fig. seds2 , the transition from NGC 3521 to NGC 337 to Mrk 33 corresponds to a combined rise in specific star formation rate ( ) and dust temperature ( \ and ), a larger contribution to the total infrared luminosity by warm dust ( ) relative to cold dust ( ), and by stellar birth clouds relative to the ambient ISM ( ), and a weakening of PAH features ( ). These results are consistent with the finding by , on the basis of a more sophisticated physical dust model, that the intensity of the dust-heating starlight increases, and the fraction of dust mass contributed by PAHs decreases, from NGC 3521 to NGC 337 to Mrk 33.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 337 as a star-forming bulgeless SBd galaxy hosting a large ULX population, an HMXB luminosity function consistent with its star formation rate, and no detected supernova X-rays.

quotes.quote: NGC 337 is an SBd galaxy at a distance of 20.7 Mpc (see Table 2). This object is part of the SINGS sample of galaxies (Kennicutt 2003), and has been extensively studied in the literature. Our exposure uncovered ten sources within the D25 ellipse, including eight whose fluxes are consistent with ULXs, as seen in Figure 1. M03 predict 0.6 soft and 1.3 hard background sources above our flux limit within this area, which implies the existence of a large population of ULXs associated with NGC 337.

There are 8 sources within the inner 1 ' of NGC 337 that have fluxes comparable to ULXs at a distance of 20.7 Mpc (see Figure 1). These include all NGC 337 sources listed in Table 7, except for X-2 and X-10. Based on M03, we expect 0.35 background sources in the central arcminute above the ULX threshold.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 0337 as weakly interacting, with patchy asymmetric stellar and H distributions, a flat low-q central profile, and low relative metallicity.

quotes.quote: In the case of NGC 0337, which NED characterizes as non-interacting, The Carnegie Atlas of Galaxies sandage1994 classifies NGC 0337 as Sc(s)II.2 pec due to the patchiness and asymmetry in the stellar distribution. Our own data also shows the same patchiness and asymmetry in both the stellar and H distributions (see Figure fig:vengamaps1 ), prompting us to classify this galaxy as weakly interacting.

We also note that the azimuthally averaged profile of q in the weakly interacting late-type galaxy NGC 0337 is markedly different from that of the other six galaxies. In NGC 0337, _ SFR rises by over an order of magnitude from a radius of 8 kpc to the centre of the galaxy, but the q profile remains flat at a value of 7.1 dex. The value of q in the central 2 kpc radius of NGC 0337 does not rise to the typical values of 7.4 dex shown by other galaxies although _ SFR in this region is as high as in NGC 2903, NGC 4254, NGC 5194, and almost a factor of 10 above that of NGC 3938. This effect is not due to distance or resolution effects as NGC 0337 is at a similar distance as NGC 4254 and NGC 3938.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 337 as SN 2014cx host, uses its SN-site spectrum to estimate local oxygen abundance, and finds that environment sub-solar relative to the comparison sample.

quotes.quote: SN 2014cx was discovered in the galaxy NGC 337 on 2014 September 2 UT (JD 2456902.9) independently by and .

To estimate the local oxygen abundance of SN 2014cx, we have used the dereddened emission line fluxes given in which is computed from the integrated optical spectrum of a radial strip covering the SN site of NGC 337. The dereddened fluxes of the emission lines from NGC 7742 are measured from the spectrum of H II region in NGC 7742 closest to SN site from and that from NGC 582 using the spectrum of the host galaxy centre. The oxygen abundance of NGC 337 (SN 2014cx) is 8.27 0.02 dex , NGC 7742 (SN 2014cy) is 8.90 0.02 dex and NGC 582 (SN 2015cz) is 8.81 0.02 dex . The host oxygen abundance of SN 2014cx is 0.22 dex lower while that of SNe 2014cy and 2015cz are 0.36 dex and 0.27 dex higher than the average oxygen abundance of the PISCO SN II sample.

gmw_00008832.name: LSBG-285
gmw_00008832.overview: LSBG-285 is an isolated, star-forming low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy located at a comoving distance of approximately 25 Mpc [arXiv:1805.04118]. It has an effective radius of 1.2 kpc, a low stellar metallicity, and no massive neighboring galaxies within 1.5 Mpc [arXiv:1805.04118].

sources.summary: The paper presents LSBG-285 as an isolated, star-forming low-surface-brightness dwarf with a 25 Mpc distance, 1.2 kpc radius, and low metallicity.

quotes.quote: We present optical long-slit spectroscopy and far-ultraviolet to mid-infrared spectral energy distribution fitting of two diffuse dwarf galaxies, LSBG-285 and LSBG-750, which were recently discovered by the Hyper Suprime-Cam Subaru Strategic Program (HSC-SSP). We measure redshifts using H line emission, and find that these galaxies are at comoving distances of 25 and 41 Mpc, respectively, after correcting for the local velocity field. They have effective radii of r_ eff =1.2 and 1.8 kpc and stellar masses of M_ 2 3 10^ 7 M_ .

There are no massive galaxies ( M_ >10^ 10 M_ ) within a comoving separation of at least 1.5 Mpc from LSBG-285 and 2 Mpc from LSBG-750. These sources are similar in size and surface brightness to ultra-diffuse galaxies, except they are isolated, star-forming objects that were optically selected in an environmentally blind survey. Both galaxies likely have low stellar metallicities [ Z_ /Z_ ] < -1.0 and are consistent with the stellar mass--metallicity relation for dwarf galaxies.
Based on the NSA, LSBG-285's nearest neighbor is a dwarf galaxy with M_ = 4 10^7 M_ at a comoving separation of 1.2 Mpc, and the nearest galaxy with M_ > 10^ 10 M_ is at a distance of 3.4 Mpc.

gmw_00008833.name: LSBG-750
gmw_00008833.overview: LSBG-750 is an isolated, star-forming low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy located at a comoving distance of 41 Mpc [arXiv:1805.04118]. It has an effective radius of 1.8 kpc, low stellar metallicity, and a rotational velocity limited to 50 km s^-1 [arXiv:1805.04118].

sources.summary: The paper presents LSBG-750 as an isolated, star-forming low-surface-brightness dwarf at 41 Mpc, with 1.8 kpc radius, low metallicity, and limited rotation.

quotes.quote: We present optical long-slit spectroscopy and far-ultraviolet to mid-infrared spectral energy distribution fitting of two diffuse dwarf galaxies, LSBG-285 and LSBG-750, which were recently discovered by the Hyper Suprime-Cam Subaru Strategic Program (HSC-SSP). We measure redshifts using H line emission, and find that these galaxies are at comoving distances of 25 and 41 Mpc, respectively, after correcting for the local velocity field. They have effective radii of r_ eff =1.2 and 1.8 kpc and stellar masses of M_ 2 3 10^ 7 M_ .

There are no massive galaxies ( M_ >10^ 10 M_ ) within a comoving separation of at least 1.5 Mpc from LSBG-285 and 2 Mpc from LSBG-750. These sources are similar in size and surface brightness to ultra-diffuse galaxies, except they are isolated, star-forming objects that were optically selected in an environmentally blind survey. Both galaxies likely have low stellar metallicities [ Z_ /Z_ ] < -1.0 and are consistent with the stellar mass--metallicity relation for dwarf galaxies.

In the case of LSBG-750, we set an upper limit on its rotational velocity of 50 km s ^ 1 , which is comparable to dwarf galaxies of similar stellar mass with estimated halo masses of <10^ 11 M_ (Figure fig:rotation ).

gmw_00008834.name: KLAMA
gmw_00008834.overview: KLAMA is a massive, optically-dark galaxy at a redshift of approximately 5.7 [arXiv:2405.04572]. It is the most massive and dusty component of an ongoing merger, exhibiting tidal features and a star-forming nature that places it on the main sequence [arXiv:2405.04572].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID 2 as an extremely red, massive quenched z~5 galaxy candidate whose SED favors high dust extinction over less dusty or nebular-emission alternatives.

quotes.quote: We identify the most extreme red object in our sample (F200W-F440W 7mag), that can be explained only by templates of a massive (log(M _* /M _ ) 11.8) quenched galaxy at z 5 with anomalous abundant dust extinction, A_V=5.5 mag (ID 2). This object is even redder that the JWST source with SCUBA2 analysed by zavala , which is at a similar redshift but it is starbursting. We verify the presence of a possible less dusty solution by running BAGPIPES limiting the reddening to A_V<2 . However, the best solution results in a ^2 that is three times larger than the solution with A_V=5.2 .

We also varied the ionization parameter (U) to test if the red colors of this galaxy could be due to the presence of nebular emission lines. However, even with log(U)=-2 and -4, the best solution remains a galaxies a galaxy around z=5 and with A_V 5 . While passive sources at 3<z<5 are already emerging in JWST early observations Carnall_QG , the existence of a dusty, massive and quenched source at z=5 could represent the precursor of dust rich quiescent galaxies at z 2 Gobat .

sources.summary: The paper identifies KLAMA as the massive dusty A component of a z~5.7 merging system, with strong F444W emission, high extinction, and nearby companions.

quotes.quote: We have identified KLAMA, an optically-dark source showing a very close companion (angular distance < 0.5 ). The spatially resolved SED fitting procedure indicates that all components lying within 1.5 from the dark source are indeed at z 5.7 . Tidal features (leading to a whale shaped morphology) corroborate the hypothesis that KLAMA is the most massive ( (M_ / M_ ) > 10.3 ) and dusty (A _V 3 at the core) system of an ongoing merger with a mass ratio of 10 .
The primary object of our selection, KLAMA (A), is clearly the most luminous component in the F444W band, and, thus the most massive.

With (M_ / M_ ) = 10.3 0.51 and ( SFR / M_ yr ^ 1 ) = 1.91 0.49 , KLAMA is a galaxy at z=5.7 sitting on the main sequence of star-forming galaxies e.g. Popesso23 . According to the best-fit model, its extremely red color is due to the dusty nature of this object, reaching values of A_V>3 towards the central core.

gmw_00008835.name: HT
gmw_00008835.overview: HT is a head-tail radio galaxy located on the west side of the Abell 1914 cluster [arXiv:1811.08430]. The galaxy features a diffuse radio tail that extends 630 kiloparsecs in the northwest direction and exhibits spectral steepening along its length [arXiv:1811.08430].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HT as a head-tail radio galaxy in Abell 1914, with an optical counterpart, a long northwest tail, and spectral steepening along the tail.

quotes.quote: HT: A head-tail galaxy We identify the source HT as a head-tail galaxy. In Figure fig:sdss-wl-radio the CFHT band optical image is overlaid with the LOFAR 150 MHz map contours shows the optical counterpart of the head of the HT (marked as A). Another optical counterpart (B) is not a member of Abell 1914 sifon15 . The extent of the tail is 630 kpc in the NW direction. From the high-resolution spectral index map (Figure fig:specindex left panel) we observe the typical steepening of the radio spectrum (-0.89 1.15) along the length of the tail.

Head-tail galaxy The head of the head-tail galaxy on the west side of Abell 1914 was reported as a discrete source by B03, while the diffuse tail was unresolved and blended together with the other diffuse sources. In this paper, with the help of detailed spectral index maps, we classify this source unambiguously as a head-tail radio galaxy. From the spectral index maps (Figure fig:specindex ) a clear spectral steepening can be observed towards its tail due to the synchrotron ageing.

gmw_00008836.name: TXS 0506+056
gmw_00008836.overview: TXS 0506+056 is a bright blazar located at a redshift of z = 0.3365 [arXiv:1812.06338]. It is associated with high-energy neutrino events and has exhibited multiwavelength flaring, particularly in the high-energy and very-high-energy gamma-ray bands [arXiv:1812.06338].

sources.summary: The paper describes TXS 0506+056 as the leading blazar-neutrino association, with IceCube events, multiwavelength flaring, hard gamma-ray emission, and preferred status over PKS 0502+049.

quotes.quote: The most promising candidate so far is the blazar TXS 0506+056 (with the coordinates of RA= 77.36 and Dec=+5.69) which can be associated with the neutrino event IceCube-170922A, detected on 22 September 2017 IceCubeFermi . TXS 0506+056 is a bright blazar in the MeV/GeV band at the redshift of z= 0.3365 0.001 2018ApJ...854L..32P . The multiwavelength observation campaign started after the neutrino alert showed that the source was in an active sate almost in all electromagnetic bands, most interestingly, flaring in the HE and VHE bands IceCubeFermi . Moreover, IceCube has reported an independently observed 3.5 excess of neutrinos from the direction of TXS 0506+056 between September 2014 and March 2015 IceCube1 , strengthening the association between the neutrino events and TXS 0506+056.

Further, dissection in space, time, and energy of the region around the IceCube-170922A showed that in the band the emission from the nearby flaring blazar dominates at low energies, but TXS 0506+056 dominates the sky above energies of a few GeV sah . Also, during the period of the neutrino excess in 2014-2015, the emission from TXS 0506+056 hardened with an excess of hard radiation at the highest energies observable by Fermi Large Area Telescope ( ) sah . All these make TXS 0506+056 the most probable source of the observed VHE neutrinos and many different scenarios have been already proposed to explain the observed neutrinos 2018ApJ...863L..10A,2018arXiv180704275G, 2018arXiv180704335C, 2018ApJ...864...84K, 2018ApJ...865..124M, 2018arXiv180705210L,2018arXiv180900601W,2018ApJ...866..109S .

gmw_00008837.name: LEDA 96373
gmw_00008837.overview: LEDA 96373 is a Seyfert 2 active galaxy [arXiv:2210.15376, arXiv:0912.1519] located at a redshift of z=0.0294 [arXiv:0912.1519]. It is a variable X-ray source [arXiv:2210.15376, arXiv:0912.1519] that has been classified as Compton-thick [arXiv:2210.15376], though some modeling suggests it is consistent with a Compton-thin torus [arXiv:2210.15376].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LEDA 96373 as a Seyfert 2 and Compton-thick galaxy with variable X-ray flux, while current reflection fitting favors a Compton-thin torus.

quotes.quote: LEDA 96373* LEDA 96373 (also 2MASX J07262635–3554214 or IGR J07264–3553) was first observed at high energies during the all-sky hard X-ray IBIS survey 2007krivonos and reported in the Palermo /BAT survey. This galaxy is classified in NED as a Seyfert 2 and as a Compton thick galaxy 2016koss . 2009landi observed this galaxy with XRT data and reports an excess emission below 2 keV and found that a double power law model is a good fit.

They found an intrinsic column density of 7 10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 and a photon index 2.5. The source seems to vary by a factor of 2 within a timescale of few days, with an average 2–10 keV flux of 4.2 10 ^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 .

Using PEXMON we found R _ f >9.38. Using borus02 we found that the data is consistent with a Compton thin torus ( (N_ H,refl ) =23.40 _ 23.33 ^ 23.69 ) covering 59 of the sky. Then we fitted XILLVER and found =2.08 _ 2.03 ^ 2.17 and R _ f >9.37.

sources.summary: The paper confirms LEDA 96373 as IGR J07264--3553, a Seyfert 2 active galaxy with radio/IR counterparts, intrinsic absorption, and variable X-ray flux.

quotes.quote: LEDA 96373 This source, known also as IGR J07264--3553, was first observed at high energies during the all-sky hard X-ray IBIS survey (Krivonos et al. 2007) and recently reported in the Palermo Swift /BAT survey (Cusumano et al. (2009); both works associated it to LEDA 96373 (also 2MASX J07262635--3554214) a galaxy classified in NED as a Seyfert 2 at z=0.0294 . However, X-ray follow-up observations with XRT indicate the presence of more possible counterparts in the IBIS error circle as listed in Table tab2 and shown in Figure fig2 ): four X-ray sources are visible of which two ( 1 and 4) are located within the 99 error radius and the other two ( 2 and 3) are instead found within the 90 positional uncertainty. Sources 1 and 2 are weak and soft being detected mostly below 3 keV, but the other two objects are brighter and of comparable intensity; object 4 is mostly emitting soft X-rays. Source 3 is indeed the galaxy LEDA 96373 proposed as the likely counterpart of the hard X-ray emitter. The combined use of both IBIS and BAT error circles (see Figure fig2 ) clearly indicates that only this source is detected by both instruments, thus confirming previous associations.
The X-ray spectral characteristics, reported in this work for the first time, are compatible with the AGN type 2 nature of this source.

gmw_00008838.name: IGRJ 11366-6002
gmw_00008838.overview: IGRJ 11366-6002 is an active galactic nucleus with no prior information available in the literature [arXiv:2210.15376]. X-ray data fits for the galaxy are consistent with a Compton-thick reflector torus covering 93 percent of the sky [arXiv:2210.15376].

sources.summary: The paper reports no prior literature information for IGRJ 11366-6002, then fits XMM/BAT/XRT data with models consistent with a Compton-thick reflector torus.

quotes.quote: IGRJ 11366-6002: We did not find previous information about this galaxy in the literature. Here we perform the fit of the and /BAT and /XRT data.

When using PEXMON reflection model, we find R _ f =0.72 _ 0.49 ^ 1.57 . We also fit the data using borus02 model, and we found that the data is consistent with a Compton thick reflector torus ( (N_ H,refl ) >24.47) covering 93 of the sky. Using XILLVER , the fit was consistent with =3.14 _ 3.03 ^ 3.32 and R _ f >1.12. All the models are indistinguishable, and they are consistent with a Compton-thin absorber in the line of sight to the primary coronal emission with (N_ H, los )< 21.2 .

gmw_00008839.name: NGC 1129
gmw_00008839.overview: NGC 1129 is a giant early-type galaxy located at the center of the poor group AWM7 [arXiv:2212.06174, arXiv:1604.01764]. It is an X-ray outlier featuring two distinct X-ray peaks, a strongly peaked surface brightness profile with core disturbances, and evidence of a cooling flow [arXiv:1604.01764].

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGC 1129 has a compact GC system, possible contamination, and an anomalously metal-rich transformed metallicity distribution requiring further investigation.

quotes.quote: For HST-based photometry of the young cluster population, see, for example, holtzman+1992,carlson+1998,canning+2014,lim+2020,lim+2022 . IC 4051: GC photometry in this Coma giant ETG drawn from HST/WFPC2 imaging was presented in baum+1997 and woodworth_harris2000 . This galaxy and its GC system have an unusually compact spatial central concentration. NGC 4874, NGC 4889: These are the two centrally dominant giants in the Coma cluster.

These plot the mode separation parameter DD and the blue fraction f_1 versus [ Fe/H ] _ mid , the metallicity at which the blue and red Gaussian components give equal probabilities. Both show well defined trends that arise from the MDF shape. If f_1 is low, the fact that the component dispersions _1, _2 stay almost constant means that as [ Fe/H ] _ mid shifts to lower metallicity, the sharper red mode stands out more clearly, and the mode separation DD increases.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1129 as AWM7’s central galaxy and an X-ray outlier, with two X-ray peaks, disturbed core surface brightness, bulk motions, and cooling-flow evidence.

quotes.quote: NGC 1129 is a distinct outlier from the observed L_ X,gas - T_ gas ^ 4.5 relation. NGC 1129 is the central galaxy of the poor group AWM7. There are two clear X-ray peaks within AWM7, one centered on the 2MASS position of NGC 1129, and the other offset 1  kpc eastwards Furusho:2003aa .

The X-ray surface brightness (SB) profile is strongly peaked, with small scale disturbances in the core, with evidence for bulk motions and a cooling flow Neumann:1995aa,Furusho:2003aa . Additionally, there are small SB depressions indicative of gas density fluctuations Sanders:2012aa .

gmw_00008840.name: J0817+0709
gmw_00008840.overview: J0817+0709 is a Z-shaped radio galaxy with a redshift of z = 0.27 [arXiv:2006.07219]. It has a linear size of 0.38 megaparsecs and is identified as the most luminous Z-shaped radio galaxy in its sample [arXiv:2006.07219].

sources.summary: The survey describes J0817+0709 as the most luminous ZRG, reports a 0.38 Mpc linear size, and gives it the largest ZRG redshift in the sample, z=0.27.

quotes.quote: J0929+5156 is the most luminous XRG in our sample with L_ rad =1.34 10^ 28 watt Hz ^ 1 ( z 2.23 ) and J0817+0709 is the most luminous ZRG in our sample with L_ rad =3.24 10^ 26 watt Hz ^ 1 ( z=0.27 ).
J0817+0709 and J2125+3738 have a linear size of 0.38 and 0.33 Mpc respectively.
J0817+0709 has the largest redshift among the ZRGs, at z = 0.27 Ri09 .

gmw_00008841.name: J0020+2351
gmw_00008841.overview: J0020+2351 is classified as an X-shaped radio galaxy [arXiv:2006.07219]. It is distinguished as the only such galaxy in its sample to exhibit an inverted radio spectrum, possessing a spectral index of -0.37 [arXiv:2006.07219].

sources.summary: The survey singles out J0020+2351 as the only inverted-spectrum XRG and reports it as having the lowest XRG spectral index, -0.37, among the sample.

quotes.quote: Out of 62 XRGs with spectral index information, 10 (16 ) are showing flat spectrum ( _ 150 ^ 1400 <0.5 ) only J0020+2351 is having inverted spectrum ( _ 150 ^ 1400 <0 ) with _ 150 ^ 1400 =-0.37 .
J0020+2351 is the only source that shows ( <0) inverted spectra.
Among XRGs, J1020+4830 has the highest spectral index (with ^ 1400 _ 150 =2.97 ) and J0020+2351 has the lowest spectral index (with ^ 1400 _ 150 =-0.37 ).

gmw_00008842.name: RBS1423
gmw_00008842.overview: RBS1423 is a radio-quiet quasar and broad-line active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.208 [arXiv:0705.1267]. It exhibits high X-ray luminosity and optical variability, with an X-ray spectrum that displays a broad iron emission feature [arXiv:0705.1267].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RBS1423 as a z=0.208 broad-line QSO/AGN with high X-ray luminosity, optical variability, and a broad iron feature favoring relativistic disk reflection over partial covering.

quotes.quote: We analysed an XMM-Newton observation (PN net time exposure 14.1 ksec) of RBS1423, a suspected high-luminosity QSO. We showed that the corresponding optical counterpart is a broad-line AGN at z=0.208 , thus correcting the identification in . The absolute magnitude in the B band of M_ B 23.0 and the X-ray luminosity of L_ X 5.7 10^ 44 erg s^ 1 (0.5-12 keV) classify the objects as a QSO.

In the X-ray spectrum of RBS1423 we detected a broad emission feature around 5 keV, which is well described by iron K fluorescence in the innermost parts of the accretion disk. This makes RBS1423 one of the most luminous radio-quiet AGN with a detection of relativistic disk reflection. Alternatively, the X-ray spectrum can be modelled equally well with a partially covered power law model.

gmw_00008843.name: Andromeda XVI
gmw_00008843.overview: Andromeda XVI is a dwarf spheroidal satellite galaxy of M31 [arXiv:1603.00625]. It experienced prolonged star formation until it was quenched six billion years ago, showing a mild radial gradient where star formation was stronger in its central regions [arXiv:1603.00625].

sources.summary: The paper presents Andromeda XVI as an M31 dSph satellite with prolonged star formation until 6 Gyr ago, late stellar mass assembly, mild radial SFH gradient, RR Lyrae distance, and unlikely reionization quenching.

quotes.quote: Based on data aquired in 13 orbits of HST time, we present a detailed evolutionary history of the M31 dSph satellite Andromeda XVI , including its life-time star formation history, the spatial distribution of its stellar populations, and the properties of its variable stars. And XVI is characterized by prolonged star formation activity from the oldest epochs until star formation was quenched 6 Gyr ago, and, notably, only half of the mass in stars of And XVI was in place 10 Gyr ago. And XVI appears to be a low mass galaxy for which the early quenching by either reionization or starburst feedback seems highly unlikely, and thus, is most likely due to an environmental effect (e.g., an interaction), possibly connected to a late infall in the densest regions of the Local Group.

Studying the star formation history as a function of galactocentric radius, we detect a mild gradient in the star formation history: the star formation activity between 6 and 8 Gyr ago is significantly stronger in the central regions than in the external regions, although the quenching age appears to be the same, within 1 Gyr. We also report the discovery of 9 RR Lyrae stars, 8 of which belong to And XVI . The RR Lyrae stars allow a new estimate of the distance, (m-M)_0 = 23.72 0.09 mag, which is marginally larger than previous estimates based on the tip of the red giant branch.

gmw_00008844.name: 091127 host
gmw_00008844.overview: The 091127 host is a faint, low-redshift emission-line galaxy [arXiv:1505.06743, arXiv:1605.02438] that hosted the gamma-ray burst GRB 091127 [arXiv:1505.06743]. Its spectrum reveals narrow emission lines [arXiv:1605.02438], an unusually high [OII]/H-beta ratio [arXiv:1605.02438], and a broad metallicity estimate near the R23 turnover point [arXiv:1505.06743].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 091127 host as a faint low-redshift galaxy whose strong lines place it near the R23 metallicity turnover, yielding a broad metallicity estimate.

quotes.quote: GRB 091127 ( z=0.490 ) is a typical low-redshift GRB with a bright afterglow and faint host 2011A&A...535A.127V,2011A&A...526A.113F . It is an example of a galaxy having R_ 23 in the overlap region between the two metallicity solutions. Even though the forbidden oxygen and Balmer lines are detected at good S/N, the lack of detection of either or limits the number of useful line-diagnostics to R_ 23 . Given that the line ratios put the host of GRB 091127 at around the turn-over point of the two branches, the probability distribution is not double valued, but rather broad because is not very sensitive to variations in R_ 23 in this region. We derive =8.07_ 0.20 ^ +0.18 for the host of GRB 091127.

sources.summary: The paper describes the 091127 host spectrum as having narrow FWHM, an unusually high [OII]/H ratio, low model velocity and density, very thin clouds, near-solar O/H, and low N/H/S/H.

quotes.quote: The FWHM of 091127 host observed by Vergani et al are rather narrow (50 ). The [ OII ] / line ratio is unusually high. The modelling shows low and 0 (60 and 60 3, respectively), very thin clouds (0.03 pc) and O/H 1.12 solar, while N/H and S/H are low.

gmw_00008845.name: SGRB 051221a
gmw_00008845.overview: The host galaxy of the short gamma-ray burst SGRB 051221a is located at a redshift of z=0.546 [arXiv:1605.02438] and is best modeled as an exponential disk [arXiv:0909.1804]. It exhibits a low ionization parameter, indicating that the observed positions are far from active star-forming regions [arXiv:1605.02438].

sources.summary: The paper reports a modeled spectrum for SGRB 051221a at z=0.546, with reddening-corrected line ratios, O/H matching Soderberg et al., and low ionization parameter implying host regions away from star formation.

quotes.quote: We report in Table 12, last two columns, the spectrum observed by Soderberg et al (2006) for SGRB 051221a at z=0.546 and model mS6. The line ratios were reddening corrected adopting H / =0.46. The best fitting model shows 12+log(O/H)=8.8, in agreement with Soderberg et al who calculated 8.7 by the R32 method (upper branch). We have found =8.2 10 ^4 K and U =0.007. are higher than for SGRB 130603B, where is rather low ( 3.5 10 ^4 K). U , however, are similar. For both SGRB 130603B and SGRB 051221a the ionization parameters are relatively low, roughly indicating that the host observed positions are far from the star-forming region.

sources.summary: The host of GRB 051221a is modeled as an exponential disk, has smaller host-centroid uncertainty than astrometric or afterglow tie errors, and has a 2.0 kpc physical offset.

quotes.quote: We find that for GRBs 050724, 051221, and 061006 the host centroid uncertainty is smaller than both _ GB HST and _ ,GRB , while for GRBs 060121 and 060313 _ ,gal is comparable to _ ,GRB .

From the galfit analysis we find that three hosts (GRBs 050709, 051221a, and 061006) are best modeled with n 1 , corresponding to an exponential disk profile, while two hosts (GRBs 050509b and 050724) are best modeled with n 3 and 5.6 , respectively, typical of elliptical galaxies. We note that GRB 050724 possibly exhibits weak spiral structure, which may explain the resulting value of n 3 (see Figure  fig:050724 and mcd+07 ), but this putative spiral structure is clearly sub-dominant relative to the elliptical structure.

Our HST sample includes three short GRBs with strong extended emission (050709, 050724, and 061006), and one GRB (060121) with possible extended emission ( 4.5 significance; dls+06 ). The physical offsets of these bursts are about 3.7, 2.7, 1.3, and 1 kpc, respectively, leading to a mean offset of about 2.2 kpc. The physical offsets of the bursts without extended emission, but with precise afterglow positions (051221, 060313, and 061201) are 2.0, 2.3, and 14.2 or 32.5 kpc, respectively. The two events with no extended emission and with XRT positions (050509b and 051210) have offsets of about 54 12 and 28 23 kpc, respectively.

gmw_00008846.name: ID 526
gmw_00008846.overview: ID 526 is the brightest cluster galaxy of a high-redshift cluster [arXiv:2206.10063, arXiv:2310.06120]. It exhibits an old stellar population with no signs of recent star formation [arXiv:2206.10063].

sources.summary: The paper models possible BCG contamination in XLSSC 122, finds no ALMA/ACA continuum or line detection, and rejects a point source at the BCG location.

quotes.quote: From the X-ray imaging (see Fig. fig:multiwavelength ), there is a small hint of possible AGN activity as a slight increase in emissions is found at the location of the BCG. However, we do not detect the BCG or any of the central galaxies in the ALMA and ACA data in either the continuum or any of the spectral lines (because of the spectral tuning of the observations). However, Figure fig:multiwavelength shows a decrease in the decrement around the central four galaxies, indicating a possible presence of continuum emission from the BCG. To test this, we reran the best-fitting gNFW model (row five in Table table:sigle ) with an additional point source component frozen at the location of the BCG (based on the HST imaging). We modeled (including the Band 4 data) both the amplitude and spectral slope of the central galaxy.

We find that the amplitude of any point source at the location of the BCG is consistent with noise. Then, by re-sampling the parameter space through the nested sampling routine, we find no significant difference in the derived parameters of the gNFW profile, including the inner slope value, , when including a point source component. Through the Bayes factor, we can reject the presence of a point source at the location of the BCG in the ALMA+ACA data by 4.1 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID 526 as the high-redshift cluster BCG, finding no recent star formation, an old stellar population, and star formation beginning before halo virialization.

quotes.quote: The BCG Unlike some other high-redshift BCGs e.g. webb_star_2015,bonaventura_red_2017,cooke_stellar_2019 , ID 526 does not exhibit any signs of recent star formation. Its age distribution (see Figure fig_very_old_members ) is typical of a very old galaxy, albeit with slightly more probability toward 1 to 2 Gyrs than others, and its short characteristic time is inconsistent with the presence on a small population of young stars.

The left panel of Figure fig_assembly_526 presents a comparison between the age posterior distribution of the BCG ( a_0 ) with the time at which each simulated halo becomes at least 10 per cent assembled. The 10 per cent assembly times for the simulated haloes correspond roughly to the younger half of the posterior distribution, showing that the onset of star formation occurred long before virialization.
The BCG acquires 90 per cent of its stellar mass on approximately the same time-scale for the simulated haloes to become 10 per cent assembled.

gmw_00008847.name: ID 726
gmw_00008847.overview: ID 726 is a member of the XLSSC 122 cluster with an irregular shape and a very old stellar age distribution [arXiv:2206.10063]. The galaxy exhibits a high dust content, which suggests it may have undergone or is currently experiencing a highly obscured star-forming episode [arXiv:2206.10063].

sources.summary: The paper treats ID 726 as an unusual XLSSC 122 member with a very old age distribution, high dust content, irregular shape, and possible obscured star formation.

quotes.quote: ID 726 ID 726 (presented in Figure fig_dusty_members ) is the only reliable fit that does not enter into any of the four main age categories. Although its age distribution corresponds to a very old member, its characteristic time is poorly constrained and its dust content is markedly higher than in any other fit. Together with its irregular shape (see the top panel of Figure fig_XLSSC122_members ), this might suggest that ID 726 has undergone (or is still experiencing) an highly obscured star-forming episode.

gmw_00008848.name: ID 522
gmw_00008848.overview: ID 522 is classified as a young galaxy member that is bluer than other evolved members in its cluster [arXiv:2206.10063]. It has a very short, well-constrained characteristic timescale, which suggests the galaxy recently transitioned from a star-forming state to a quenched state [arXiv:2206.10063].

sources.summary: The paper places ID 522 between young and star-forming cluster members, describing it as unusually blue, with a short timescale suggesting recent transition from star-forming to quenched.

quotes.quote: ID 522 ID 522 characteristics place it between the young and the star-forming members: it is younger and bluer than any other evolved member (see Figure fig_young_members and the bottom panel of Figure fig_XLSSC122_members ). However, unlike the other blue cloud members its characteristic time is very short and well-constrained, which could mean that ID 522 recently transitioned from star-forming to quenched. We thus classified it as a young members.

gmw_00008849.name: NGC 470
gmw_00008849.overview: NGC 470 is a spiral galaxy [arXiv:2111.14886, arXiv:1411.0643] hosting a circumnuclear disk inside a large-scale bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0311271]. It is tidally interacting with its companion NGC 474 [arXiv:2111.14886, arXiv:1411.0643], which has displaced its dynamical center [arXiv:astro-ph/0211105] and created tidal-arm-like gas structures [arXiv:2111.14886].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 470 as NGC 474's more massive spiral neighbor, embedded in a shared HI cloud with tidal-arm-like structures, and a possible initiator of the shell-forming encounter.

quotes.quote: NGC 474 forms a pair with the spiral galaxy NGC 470. With a stellar mass of 10^ 10.76 M_ sheth10 , NGC 470 is more massive than NGC 474. The galaxies lie in projection 5.46 = 49 kpc apart and their radial velocities are different by 172 km s ^ 1 . The real separation of the two galaxies is likely substantially larger than the projected separation because shell B , with a radius of 31 kpc, is circular without any convincing sign of a tidal deformation. The distribution of HI gas around the two galaxies suggests that they tidally interacted in the past because they seem to lie in a common gas cloud and the gas forms structures resembling tidal arms in NGC 470 that point to NGC 474 schiminovich97,rampazzo06 .

MOND indicates that NGC 470 initiated the formation of the shells in NGC 474 In this section, we point out that there are indications that the merger that formed the shells in NGC 474 was started by a three-body encounter of NGC 474, the accreted galaxy, and the neighboring galaxy NGC 470.

Galaxies NGC 474 and NGC 470 are embedded in the same HI cloud, and NGC 470 shows structures resembling tidal arms pointing to NGC 474 schiminovich97, rampazzo06 , as if they had a past encounter and exchanged some material cullen06,bil18,young20 .

sources.summary: The paper interprets NGC 470 as having a circumnuclear disk inside a large-scale bar, with disk-like kinematics, molecular and ionized gas concentration, and possible ILR-driven decoupling.

quotes.quote: Thus we think that there is a circumnuclear disk within the large-scale bar (see Sect. disks for additional arguments). In the velocity field the turn of occurs in the same direction with respect to the PA of isophotes at <5-7'' (Fig. 470ifp ). Such a dynamical feature is also in agreement with circular motions in the decoupled disk which may be slightly inclined to the galactic plane (Sect. method ).

An asymmetrical m=1 harmonic is developed in this disk, which also has a large concentration of molecular CO (Sofue et al., ), as well as of ionized hydrogen (see the image on Fig. 470ifp ). The decoupling of the central disk may be caused by its location at the ILR of the large-scale bar.

sources.summary: NGC 470 is described as having a displaced dynamical center and asymmetric m=1 gas mode, plausibly related to interaction with its nearby companion NGC 474.

quotes.quote: In NGC 470, the dynamical center, defined as the symmetry point of the velocity field, is displaced by 4-5'' ( 0.6-0.8 kpc) from the photometric nucleus of the galaxy, which may be due to the peculiar development of the azimuthal m=1 harmonic in the gas velocity field (Emsellem, 2002). This asymmetric harmonic can be generated by the tidal interaction with the nearby companion NGC 474 studied by Turnbull et al. (1999). The observed displacement of the center can also be explained as resulting from the development of an asymmetric harmonic in the surface brightness distribution.

Although the asymmetric m = 1 mode developed within the bar of NGC 470 and the polar gaseous disk in NGC 5850 are rare structures, they, nevertheless, were observed by several authors in other objects as well (see Subsect. 3.2). It was also noted in this subsection that the two galaxies have close massive companions. Therefore, the assumption that the features of their gas kinematics result from the interaction with the companions appears reasonable enough.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 470 as an SA(rs)b spiral hosting the ULX 2XMM J011942.7+032421 in a spiral-arm star-forming region, paired with NGC 474 and showing early interaction signatures.

quotes.quote: Here, we present a study on 2XMM J011942.7+032421 set12,gla13 , one of the most luminous ULXs located at 33 \ (or 5.3 kpc at 33.5 Mpc) from the center of the SA(rs)b galaxy NGC 470, in the optical waveband.
The host galaxy NGC 470 forms a pair with NGC 474 at 5 \ away, and the pair shows clear signs for early interactions between them ret06 .

hk83 identified 51 \ regions in spiral arms of NGC 470, 18 of them within 1 \ distance from \ including the \ region 46 inside the positional uncertainty of the ULX. It is, therefore, highly likely that all the four sources in Figure fig:disp are \ regions or at least associated with \ regions in spiral arms of the host galaxy NGC 470.

gmw_00008850.name: NGC 5566
gmw_00008850.overview: NGC 5566 is classified as an ordinary barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0311271]. It features a large-scale bar with two strong spiral arms, as well as a circumnuclear disk containing an inner mini-spiral [arXiv:astro-ph/0311271].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5566 as an ordinary barred galaxy with a large-scale bar, lacking stellar non-circular motions from a nuclear bar, but hosting a circumnuclear disk and mini-spiral.

quotes.quote: The large-scale bar of 25'' in size is elongated at PA 155^ . Two strong spiral arms start from the bar ends. The velocity field of stars lacks significant non-circular motions as coincides with PA_0 within the errors. The stellar rotation velocity at =10-14'' reaches 250 , which corresponds to the HI rotation curve of Helou et al.

For all these reasons we suggest that the mini-spiral is not connected with a possible secondary bar. Therefore, NGC 5566 is an ordinary barred galaxy. It has a circumnuclear disk ( =5''-8'' , or 500-750 pc in linear radius) with a mini-spiral nesting inside this bar. The properties of the inner isophotes ( PA=205-210^ , _ max =0.55 ) indicate that this kiloparsec-size inner disk lies in the galactic plane.

gmw_00008851.name: Fermi J1049.7+0435
gmw_00008851.overview: Fermi J1049.7+0435 is a GeV gamma-ray source discovered near the galaxy NGC 3628 [arXiv:1401.4511]. Its flux variability and spectral index are compatible with an active galactic nucleus, and it is associated with the radio source GB6 J1050+0432 [arXiv:1401.4511].

sources.summary: The paper reports Fermi J1049.7+0435 as a new GeV source, gives its sky coordinates, and argues its variability and radio counterpart are AGN-like.

quotes.quote: Edwards CSIRO Astronomy and Space Science, PO Box 76, Epping NSW 1710, Australia Jamie Stevens CSIRO Astronomy and Space Science, Locked Bag 194, Narrabri NSW 2390, Australia During a search for gamma-ray emission from NGC 3628 (Arp 317), two new unidentified gamma-ray sources, Fermi J1049.7+0435 and J1103.2+1145 have been discovered ATel .

Their flux variability and spectral indices are compatible with those of gamma-ray detected AGN. Based on angular separation, radio flux density and spectral index, we associate J1049.7+0435 with GB6 J1050+0432, and J1103.2+1145 with the quasar GB6 J1103+1158.

gmw_00008852.name: Fermi J1103.2+1145
gmw_00008852.overview: Fermi J1103.2+1145 is a variable gamma-ray source detected at GeV energies [arXiv:1401.4511]. This active galactic nucleus is associated with the quasar GB6 J1103+1158 [arXiv:1401.4511].

sources.summary: The paper reports Fermi J1103.2+1145 as a new variable GeV source, gives its coordinates, and associates it with the quasar GB6 J1103+1158.

quotes.quote: Edwards CSIRO Astronomy and Space Science, PO Box 76, Epping NSW 1710, Australia Jamie Stevens CSIRO Astronomy and Space Science, Locked Bag 194, Narrabri NSW 2390, Australia During a search for gamma-ray emission from NGC 3628 (Arp 317), two new unidentified gamma-ray sources, Fermi J1049.7+0435 and J1103.2+1145 have been discovered ATel .

Their flux variability and spectral indices are compatible with those of gamma-ray detected AGN. Based on angular separation, radio flux density and spectral index, we associate J1049.7+0435 with GB6 J1050+0432, and J1103.2+1145 with the quasar GB6 J1103+1158.

gmw_00008853.name: NGC257
gmw_00008853.overview: NGC257 is classified as an Sc spiral galaxy [arXiv:1807.09833]. Its stellar velocity anisotropy profile is consistent with isotropic orbits in the inner disk, transitioning to more tangential stellar orbits in its outer regions [arXiv:1807.09833].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC257 as a CALIFA example, describing its Sc type, useful spaxels, isotropic inner-disk anisotropy, and more tangential outer stellar orbits.

quotes.quote: The shown galaxy is NGC257 ( i=51 ), which is a Sc galaxy from the \ subsample that has 206 useful spaxels spread over 12 independent adaptive radial bins.
The anisotropy profile of NGC257 is consistent with isotropy in the inner disk ( _ 0 ) and then with more tangential stellar orbits in outer regions.
The radial range shown for NGC257 corresponds to the isotropic orbits observed in the four innermost radial bins in Fig. fig:n257 .

gmw_00008854.name: TKRS4387
gmw_00008854.overview: TKRS4387 is an inflow-sample galaxy that exhibits gas accretion [arXiv:1110.0837]. It has a measured inflow velocity of 125 km/s and a line-of-sight terminal rotation velocity of 176 km/s [arXiv:1110.0837].

sources.summary: The paper discusses TKRS4387 as an inflow-sample galaxy, comparing its terminal rotation velocity and measured inflow velocity with possible artificial offsets from slit placement and disk rotation.

quotes.quote: Weiner2006 analyzed line emission in two-dimensional spectra of two of these objects, finding line-of-sight terminal rotation velocities of 64 and 176 for TKRS4045 and TKRS4387, respectively.
While most of our measured inflow velocities are much larger than 90 , TKRS4387 has a v_2 of only 125 .

However, most of the galaxies have diameters 1.6 , and a 0.9 slit width was used in 1 seeing conditions; in addition, this scenario would yield an artificial inflow velocity of only 30 and 90 in the cases of TKRS4045 and TKRS4387.

gmw_00008855.name: b1
gmw_00008855.overview: The source b1 is a luminous and moderately obscured active galactic nucleus located at the center of Complex B [arXiv:2008.12129]. It hosts a supermassive black hole accreting at a high rate, with cold dust emission detected in its host galaxy [arXiv:2008.12129].

sources.summary: The paper describes b1 as the Complex B central AGN, luminous and moderately obscured in X-rays, with a 7.2e7 solar-mass black hole, high accretion, and ALMA-detected cold dust.

quotes.quote: Source b1 , at the center of Complex B, is an X-ray point like source. From the analysis of its Chandra X-ray spectrum, we found that it is a luminous ( L_ [ 2-10keV ] 10^ 44 erg/s ) and moderately obscured ( log(N_H/cm^2)=22.7 ) AGN. From the broad (FWHM 2200 km/s) H line, we estimated its BH mass using the virial formula by GreeneHo , finding M_ BH = 7.2 10^7 M_ .

Combining the derived parameters, we found that b1 is accreting at a high rate, i.e. _ Edd =0.46 . Source b1 has also been detected in ALMA continuum at 230 GHz which revealed cold dust emission from the host galaxy, detected at 5 significance.

gmw_00008856.name: ESO 473-G024
gmw_00008856.overview: ESO 473-G024 is a well-studied dwarf irregular galaxy located in the Sculptor group [arXiv:astro-ph/0601321]. In optical wavelengths, the galaxy is extended in a north-south direction and contains several potential star-forming regions [arXiv:astro-ph/0601321].

sources.summary: The paper finds ESO 473-G024 has photometry and H I flux consistent with earlier work, a moderate mass-to-light ratio, and a dwarf-irregular, north-south optical structure with possible star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: Of the nine sample galaxies, ESO 473-G024 had the best agreement with the B band apparent magnitude quoted by LEDA, also agreeing with the results in lau89 and mad90 . Our flux density is in agreement with both the BGC and fou90 . Consequently, remained at a moderate ratio of 2.8 0.6 , one of the higher ratios of this sample after accurate measurements. It is a well studied dwarf irregular from the Sculptor group, and the has been noted before ski03a,ski03b . In the optical the galaxy is extended in the North-South direction, and has a number of potential star formation regions (see Fig. fig:starsub b ), some of which were studied by see also sec:dis-implicat ski03a,ski03b .

gmw_00008857.name: ESO 121-G020
gmw_00008857.overview: ESO 121-G020 is an optical galaxy with a companion galaxy located to its southeast [arXiv:astro-ph/0601321]. Down to sensitivity limits, there is no detected gas or stellar bridge connecting these two galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0601321].

sources.summary: The paper revises ESO 121-G020's optical brightness upward, separates its flux from a newly found southeastern companion, and finds no detected gas or stellar bridge between the two galaxies.

quotes.quote: The unexpected discovery of the companion galaxy to the South East of ESO 121-G020 must affect the previously calculated for this galaxy, as the BGC flux density measurement for HIPASS J0615--57 includes the emission from both galaxies. But, more importantly, our optical B magnitude determined here is 1.7 mag brighter than the value in LEDA, although it is closer to the lau89 total magnitude ( 15.85 0.09 mag). The new ratio for the combined system is 1.62 0.18 , and for the individual galaxies it is 1.50 0.13 for ESO 121-G020 and 2.2 0.3 for . Down to our sensitivity limits there is neither an nor a stellar bridge between the two galaxies detected.

gmw_00008858.name: ESO 428-G033
gmw_00008858.overview: ESO 428-G033 is a galaxy located near the Galactic Plane that suffers from high Galactic extinction and foreground star contamination [arXiv:astro-ph/0601321]. Its optical morphology is difficult to determine, but it displays a nearly circular distribution and an elongated stellar feature that may represent a central bar in a close to face-on disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0601321].

sources.summary: The paper keeps ESO 428-G033 as a high mass-to-light-ratio galaxy despite brighter photometry, noting heavy foreground obscuration and uncertain morphology, with an elongated optical feature favored as a bar in a near face-on disk.

quotes.quote: At only 6 9 from the Galactic Plane, ESO 428-G033 suffers from relatively high Galactic extinction 1.10 0.18 mag in B , sch98 and foreground star contamination (see Fig. fig:optimage d ). Our B band magnitude of 16.90 0.10 mag is 1 mag brighter than the LEDA value (which has a high uncertainty of 0.5 mag), but agrees with the lau89 measurement ( 16.83 0.09 mag). Despite this, with our new data the galaxy still has a high mass-to-light ratio of 4.5 0.9 , which is in good agreement with the results of kra92 . The optical morphology of the galaxy is difficult to determine due to the high foreground star obscuration. We can only discern an elongated stellar feature (see Fig. fig:starsub d ), which could be a galaxy disk.

Alternatively, this could be a central bar, while the fainter spiral arms remain undetected. The distribution of ESO 428-G033 is nearly circular, indicating a disk that is close to face on, which favors the optical feature being a bar rather than a disk. The position angle of velocity field is aligned with the optical emission.

gmw_00008859.name: ESO 572-G009
gmw_00008859.overview: ESO 572-G009 is a faint low-surface-brightness galaxy with a high mass-to-light ratio [arXiv:astro-ph/0601321]. It features two stellar components, consisting of a brighter cigar-shaped central bar surrounded by a fainter disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0601321].

sources.summary: The paper finds ESO 572-G009 remains a high mass-to-light-ratio object after revised photometry, with BGC-consistent H I flux and a faint low-surface-brightness morphology including a likely central bar and fainter disk.

quotes.quote: Although our flux density was in good agreement with the BGC and fou90 , our apparent B magnitude is about half a magnitude brighter than that given in LEDA and that of lau89 . Despite this correction ESO 572-G009 remains one of the few galaxies in our sample to retain a high at 4.8 1.1 . Morphologically it is a faint low surface brightness galaxy and appears to have two stellar components, a brighter cigar shaped central region (likely to be a central bar) surrounded by a fainter disk that extends only a short distance (see Fig. fig:starsub f ).

gmw_00008860.name: ESO 505-G007
gmw_00008860.overview: ESO 505-G007 is an irregular galaxy that exhibits several large clumpy structures and ragged spiral arm-like features [arXiv:astro-ph/0601321]. This morphology suggests the galaxy has undergone recent disruption and star formation triggered by an interaction with ESO 505-G008 [arXiv:astro-ph/0601321].

sources.summary: The paper revises ESO 505-G007 much brighter than LEDA, lowering its mass-to-light ratio from extreme to typical, and describes irregular, clumpy morphology suggestive of interaction-triggered star formation with ESO 505-G008.

quotes.quote: However, literature measurements vary wildly both 17.64 0.09 mag and 15.95 0.09 mag from dependent on the isophotal level it is measured to lau89 . Our measurement is 3.5 mag brighter than that listed in LEDA (corresponding to a 25 times more luminous). The severe underestimate of the B band apparent magnitude quoted by LEDA for this galaxy has a dramatic effect on the mass-to-light ratio. From the highest ratio of the 789 BGC galaxies with B magnitudes in LEDA, for ESO 505-G007 has dropped down to a more typical ratio of 1.18 0.12 , the lowest of our sample galaxies. We note that optically ESO 505-G007 has an unusual irregular morphology (see Fig. fig:starsub g ), with several large clumpy structures and some ragged spiral arm-like features, which could suggest recent disruption and star formation triggered by interaction with ESO 505-G008.

gmw_00008861.name: ESO 505-G008
gmw_00008861.overview: ESO 505-G008 is an edge-on galaxy that has a cataloged companion galaxy, ESO 505-G007, located at a projected distance of 7 arcseconds [arXiv:astro-ph/0601321]. The two galaxies are not fully resolved spatially or spectrally, and ESO 505-G008 exhibits a broad spectral line [arXiv:astro-ph/0601321].

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO 505-G008 as the catalogued neighbor of ESO 505-G007, notes the pair is not fully resolved, and identifies ESO 505-G008 as edge-on with a broad line and measurable flux.

quotes.quote: The galaxy ESO 505-G007 likewise has a neighbor, the catalogued galaxy ESO 505-G008, which lies at a projected distance of 7 . However, the two galaxies are not entirely spatially or spectrally resolved in our observations.

In contrast, the galaxy ESO 505-G008 is seen close to edge-on and shows a broad line, as seen in Fig. fig:hispectra g . By fitting two point sources to the low-resolution distribution we determine an flux density of 21 3 for ESO 505-G007, in excellent agreement with the BGC value for HIPASS J1203--25 despite the confusion, and 8 3 for ESO 505-G008.

gmw_00008862.name: IC 4212
gmw_00008862.overview: IC 4212 is a face-on spiral galaxy characterized by a small, bright central bar, two bright, loosely wound arms, and several fainter arms [arXiv:astro-ph/0601321]. It exhibits a relatively high mass-to-light ratio for a late-type spiral, with an extended gas diameter that may reach significantly beyond its optical disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0601321].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies IC 4212 as a face-on spiral with bright arms and a small bar, revises its luminosity upward, and still finds a relatively high mass-to-light ratio and extended gas diameter.

quotes.quote: IC 4212 is an unusual galaxy to have in our sample since its optical morphology is that of a face on spiral rather than the dwarf irregular we might expect for the magnitude given in LEDA. It has two bright, loosely wound arms, several fainter arms and a small bright central bar (see Fig. fig:starsub h ). The uncertainty in the apparent magnitude given in LEDA of 1.1 mag is quite large. Our optical measurements find that IC 4212 is a much brighter galaxy than suggested by the value LEDA lists (over two magnitudes). This means that the is not the extreme value initally suggested (the third highest ratio of the 789 BGC galaxies).

However, IC 4212 retains at a ratio of 2.55 0.17 , which relatively high for a galaxy with such a distinct spiral structure, typical values for late type spirals being less than 1 rob94 . We measure a deconvolved diameter of 200 , suggesting it may extend significantly beyond the optical disk.

gmw_00008863.name: ESO 348-G009
gmw_00008863.overview: ESO 348-G009 is an edge-on disk galaxy that extends in an east-west direction [arXiv:astro-ph/0601321]. Its stellar disk features clumpy structures along its length and exhibits a closely aligned velocity field [arXiv:astro-ph/0601321].

sources.summary: The paper revises ESO 348-G009's brightness upward and mass-to-light ratio downward, while describing it as an edge-on disk with clumpy structure and a velocity field aligned with the stellar disk.

quotes.quote: The apparent B magnitude we measured for ESO 348-G009 was again brighter than the value listed in LEDA, while the flux density is consistent with the BGC result. This means we end up with an mass-to-light ratio of 1.56 0.16 , down from the preliminary value of 9 7 . The optical image shows an edge on disk galaxy extending East-West, with some clumpy structures visible along the length of the disk (see Fig. fig:starsub i ). The position angle of the velocity field is aligned closely with the stellar disk.

gmw_00008864.name: dumbbell BCG in A3532
gmw_00008864.overview: The dumbbell BCG in A3532 is a brightest cluster galaxy system with a projected separation of only 25 h^-1_70 kpc between its member galaxies [arXiv:1212.0328]. Its brighter nucleus hosts a wide-angle tail radio source with projected radio emission aligned in the northwest-southeast direction [arXiv:1212.0328].

sources.summary: The paper describes the dumbbell BCG in A3532 as hosting a WAT radio source in its brighter nucleus, with radio emission aligned NW-SE and complicated by projection and possible merger activity.

quotes.quote: Specially, the dumbbell galaxy systems, as seen in A3532, are very often seen to host radio sources in one or both of their members. A Wide-Angle Tail (WAT) radio source is indeed associated with the brighter nucleus of the dumbbell BCG in A3532 in the ATCA 13-cm and 22-cm images (Venturi et al. 2001). Close proximity of the galaxies ( 10-30 h ^ 1 _ 70 kpc) in the dumbbell systems can dynamically affect the radio jets through gravitational interaction with the confining gas cloud (Wirth et al. 1982). Here, it should be noted that the separation between the dumbbell galaxies in A3532 is only 25 h ^ 1 _ 70 kpc in projection.

The 20 cm radio contours (red) in Figure fig:A3532_A3530_SuperCOSMOS_optical_image show a C shaped WAT source at the center of A3532, coincident with the position of the brighter nucleus of its dumbbell BCG. In Figure fig:A3532_WAT_source_images , the WAT source is seen at all the radio wavelengths with sufficient angular resolution. The projected radio emission of the WAT galaxy is aligned in the NW-SE direction, i.e., approximately orthogonal to the major axis of the X-ray emission. The structure of the WAT is complicated somewhat by our viewing angle.

gmw_00008865.name: AM 2020-504
gmw_00008865.overview: AM 2020-504 is a polar-ring galaxy featuring an elliptical host galaxy surrounded by a narrow, well-defined ring [arXiv:1208.3421, arXiv:1402.7325]. The galaxy's nucleus exhibits active galactic nucleus characteristics, while its bluer ring is dominated by star-forming regions ionized by massive stars [arXiv:1208.3421].

sources.summary: The paper studies AM 2020-504 as a polar-ring galaxy whose blue, star-forming ring, oxygen gradient, and metallicity-luminosity behavior support formation by accretion rather than cold gas accretion.

quotes.quote: The present work aims to study AM 2020-504 , a PRG with an elliptical host galaxy, and a narrow and well defined ring, probably formed by accretion of material from a donor galaxy, collected by the host galaxy. Our observational study was based on BVRI broad band imagery as well as longslit spectroscopy in the wavelenght range 4100--8600 , performed at the 1.6 m telescope at the Observat orio do Pico dos Dias (OPD), Brazil. We estimated a redshift of z = 0.01683, corresponding a heliocentric radial velocity of 5045 23 km/s. The (B-R) color map shows that the ring is bluer than the host galaxy, indicating that the ring is a younger structure. Standard diagnostic diagrams were used to classify the main ionizing source of selected emission-line regions (nucleus, host galaxy and ring).

It turns out that the ring regions are mainly ionized by massive stars while the nucleus presents AGN characteristics. Using two empirical methods, we found oxygen abundances for the H II regions located in the ring in the range 12+log(O/H)=8.3-8.8 dex, the presence of an oxygen gradient across the ring, and that AM 2020-504 follows the metallicity-luminosity relation of spiral galaxies. These results support the accretion scenario for this object and rules out cold accretion as source for the HI gas in the polar ring.

sources.summary: The review describes AM 2020-504 as a narrow polar-ring prototype whose host and ring have comparable baryonic mass, distinctive spheroidal kinematics, low-metallicity ring gas, and a likely tidal-accretion origin.

quotes.quote: By analyzing the available data for the narrow PRG AM 2020-504 (see Sect.  obs ), the same analysis done for NGC 4650A can be performed. Observations show that the HG and ring have comparable baryonic mass (Tab.  tab1 ); the HG has a high central velocity dispersion along the major axis that decreases outwards, while the rotation velocity increases out to 100-130  km s ^ 1 ; the ring has similar oxygen abundances to those of low- Z spiral galaxies see also Fig.  fig3 Freitas . Thus, taking into account the above observed properties, and the HG morphology, colors, and age which are very similar to those of an ETG, the ring in AM 2020-504 could be reasonably formed through tidal accretion of material from outside by a pre-existing ETG.

gmw_00008866.name: 4FGL J0545.0+0613c
gmw_00008866.overview: 4FGL J0545.0+0613c is a low-ionization broad absorption line (LoBAL) quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.645 [arXiv:2302.10080]. Its optical spectrum exhibits broad carbon III] and magnesium II emission lines, alongside a broad absorption feature on the blue side of the carbon III] line [arXiv:2302.10080].

sources.summary: 4FGL J0545.0+0613c shows broad C III] and Mg II emission at z = 1.645 plus broad absorption, leading the paper to classify its spectrum as a LoBAL quasar.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J0545.0+0613c : The optical spectrum exhibits two prominent and broad emission lines at 5030  (EW 70  ) and 7400  (EW 17.5  ) and a weaker emission line at 5485  . We identify these lines as C III ] 1909 (FWHM = 125  ) and Mg II 2796,2803 (FWHM = 60  ) at z = 1.645. In addition, a broad absorption feature (EW 20  ) is present on the blue side of C III ] emission line. The spectral characteristics are typical of low ionization broad absorption lines (LoBAL) quasars chen2022 .

gmw_00008867.name: 4FGL J0658.6+0636
gmw_00008867.overview: 4FGL J0658.6+0636 is a blazar candidate of uncertain type [arXiv:2302.10080] and a possible counterpart to the IceCube 201114A neutrino event [arXiv:2302.10080]. Its featureless optical spectrum supports a BL Lacertae object classification [arXiv:2302.10080] with a redshift lower limit of z > 0.5 [arXiv:2302.10080].

sources.summary: 4FGL J0658.6+0636 is treated as a very-high-energy BCU and possible IceCube counterpart whose reddened, featureless power-law spectrum supports a BLL classification and only a redshift lower limit.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J0658.6+0636 : This source is classified as blazar candidate of uncertain type (BCU) in the 4FGL-DR3 catalogue and with a possible detection up to very high energy (E > 100 GeV) Atel 14200, ATel14200 . It was proposed as a plausible association to the neutrino event IceCube 201114A GCN28887 . This object is significatly reddened (E(B-V) = 0.22). The optical spectrum is featureless and well described by a power law ( 1.3) typical of a BLL.

We set an EW limit < 0.45  for any emission/absorption lines. From the absorption line limit we can set a redshift lower limit z  >  0.5 assuming a standard elliptical host galaxy. No previous optical spectra are found in literature.

gmw_00008868.name: 4FGL J0946.2+0104
gmw_00008868.overview: 4FGL J0946.2+0104 is a blazar classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2302.10080]. It is located at a redshift of z = 0.5768, and its spectrum is dominated by a non-thermal component [arXiv:2302.10080].

sources.summary: The paper improves the redshift of 4FGL J0946.2+0104 to z = 0.5768 from Ca II and G-band absorption and finds a non-thermal-dominated spectrum.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J0946.2+0104 : A noisy spectrum of the source is obtained by the SDSS survey where the Ca II doublet is detected at z = 0.577. In our better quality spectrum (S/N 80), in addition to Ca II, we clearly detect the G-band absorption line yielding a redshift of z = 0.5768 (see Fig.  fig:spectra ). The spectrum is dominated by a non-thermal component (N/H = 11: see Fig.  fig:decomposition ).

gmw_00008869.name: 4FGL J1003.4+0205
gmw_00008869.overview: 4FGL J1003.4+0205 is a blazar classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2302.10080]. Its high-quality optical spectrum is featureless and well described by a power law, establishing a conservative spectroscopic redshift lower limit of 0.469 from an intervening Mg II absorption system [arXiv:2302.10080].

sources.summary: The paper finds 4FGL J1003.4+0205 remains featureless in high-quality spectra, is well described by a power law, and has a conservative spectroscopic redshift lower limit from Mg II absorption.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J1003.4+0205 : The optical spectrum secured by SDSS is featureless, although the automatic procedure proposed z = 1.4. Our high quality (S/N 190) spectrum obtained at the VLT appears featureless and well described by a power law with 1.4. No emission/absorption features are apparent at level of EW = 0.20  and we can set a redshift lower limit > 0.75 from the non-detection of the absorption lines of the host galaxy.

We also obtained another spectrum at GTC that covers better the blue part of the spectral range down to 3900 . At 4115 we can detect an intervening absorption system due to Mg II 2796,2803 (see Fig.  fig:closeup ) allowing us to set a spectroscopic redshift lower limit 0.469 .

gmw_00008870.name: 4FGL J1055.7 1807
gmw_00008870.overview: 4FGL J1055.7 1807 is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2302.10080]. Its optical spectrum features a power-law shape and contains intervening iron and magnesium absorption systems that establish a spectroscopic redshift lower limit of 0.8465 [arXiv:2302.10080].

sources.summary: 4FGL J1055.7 1807 has intervening Fe II and Mg II absorption systems that set a robust redshift lower limit, and its power-law spectral shape confirms a BLL classification.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J1055.7 1807 : In the optical spectrum (S/N 80) there are two absorption doublets at 4774, 4801  and 5163, 5176  , which are attributed to intervening Fe II 2586,2600 and Mg II 2796,2803 systems, respectively, yielding a robust spectroscopic lower limit  0.8465. The spectral shape is well described by a power law (  = 0.4) confirming the BLL classification. No other spectra are found in the literature.

gmw_00008871.name: 4FGL J1124.9+2143
gmw_00008871.overview: 4FGL J1124.9+2143 is a BL Lacertae object with a featureless, power-law-like spectrum [arXiv:2302.10080]. Because its host galaxy has not been detected, its redshift is constrained to a lower limit of greater than 0.6 [arXiv:2302.10080].

sources.summary: The spectrum of 4FGL J1124.9+2143 is featureless but power-law-like, consistent with a BLL, and the host-galaxy non-detection sets a redshift lower limit.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J1124.9+2143 : Neither absorption nor emission features are present in our good spectrum (S/N 120) that is well described by a power law ( 1.25) typical of BLL. From the minimum EW = 0.20  of absorption lines of the host galaxy, we set a redshift lower limit >  0.6. There is an SDSS spectrum of the source that appears featureless.

gmw_00008872.name: 4FGL J1321.9+3219
gmw_00008872.overview: 4FGL J1321.9+3219 is a blazar and BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2302.10080]. High-quality spectroscopy reveals an intervening magnesium II absorption system at a redshift of 0.5175 and an oxygen II emission line that establishes the source redshift at 0.8126 [arXiv:2302.10080].

sources.summary: 4FGL J1321.9+3219 has Mg II intervening absorption at z = 0.5175 and an [O II] emission line giving z = 0.8126, revising the earlier featureless-spectrum estimate.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J1321.9+3219 : We obtained a high quality S/N 180 spectrum from which we clearly detect an intervening absorption system at 4243,4254  that is identified with Mg II 2796,2803 at z = 0.5175. In addition an emission line at 6755  identified as [ O II ]  3727 yields z = 0.8126. Note that based on a lower S/N featureless SDSS spectrum and the overall spectral energy distribution (SED) we proposed z 0.4 in Paper II.

gmw_00008873.name: 4FGL J1439.5 2525
gmw_00008873.overview: 4FGL J1439.5 2525 is a BL Lacertae object and elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of 0.161 [arXiv:2302.10080]. Its spectrum shows host-galaxy absorption lines but no emission lines, indicating a very marginal contribution from the nucleus [arXiv:2302.10080].

sources.summary: For 4FGL J1439.5 2525, the paper detects host-galaxy absorption, confirms z = 0.161, finds no emission lines, and shows the nucleus contributes only marginally.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J1439.5 2525 : No emission lines are found in our spectrum. We detect absorption lines due to the host galaxy, confirming the redshift of 0.161 reported in desai2019 . From the spectral decomposition in Fig.  fig:decomposition , the contribution of the nucleus is very marginal with N/H 0.25.

gmw_00008874.name: 4FGL J1507.3 3710
gmw_00008874.overview: 4FGL J1507.3 3710 is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 0.239 [arXiv:2302.10080]. Its spectrum exhibits host-galaxy absorption lines alongside emission lines of [O III], [N II], [S II], and edge-truncated [O II] [arXiv:2302.10080].

sources.summary: The spectrum of 4FGL J1507.3 3710 shows host-galaxy absorption, [O III], [N II], [S II], and edge-truncated [O II] emission, yielding z = 0.239.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J1507.3 3710 : We obtain a spectrum with S/N 100 which exhibits clear absorption lines attributed to the host galaxy (Ca II 3934,3968, G-band 4305, Mg I 5175, and Na I 5892) and emission lines at 6144,6203  attributed to [ O III ]  4959,5007, yielding z = 0.239. In addition we detect very faint emission lines due to [ N II ] and [ S II ] . The [ O II ]   emission line is detected at the very blue edge of the spectral range and a fraction of the line is lost. No previous spectra are available in literature.

gmw_00008875.name: 4FGL J1528.4+2004
gmw_00008875.overview: 4FGL J1528.4+2004 is a gamma-ray source associated with the blazar 3HSP J152835.8+200420 [arXiv:2302.10080]. The object is classified as a BL Lacertae object at a redshift of z = 0.64, and its optical spectrum shows a contribution from its host galaxy [arXiv:2302.10080].
