sources.summary: 4FGL J1528.4+2004 is linked to 3HSP J152835.8+200420, classified as a BLL, and measured at z = 0.64 from [O II] and host-galaxy absorption.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J1528.4+2004 : This ray source is unassociated with a lower energy counterpart and unclassified in the 4FGL-DR3 catalogue. However, the source 3HSP J152835.8+200420 3HSP , located well within the 0.005 square degrees error ellipse of the Fermi LAT source, is the likely counterpart since its ray flux and spectral shape are perfectly consistent with the SED of an HSP blazar see also Paiano2017sedtemp . Our LBT and GTC spectra are the first ones obtained for the optical counterpart.

Our LBT spectrum is characterized by a power-law continuum with =1 superimposed on a significant contribution of the host galaxy in the redder part of the spectrum (see Fig.  fig:decomposition ), yielding N/H 11. This confirms the BLL classification for the source. We clearly detect an emission line (EW 2.80  ) at 6110  attributed to [ O II ]  3727.
The redshift of the source is z = 0.64.

gmw_00008876.name: 4FGL J1702.2+2642
gmw_00008876.overview: 4FGL J1702.2+2642 is an active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z = 0.3197 [arXiv:2302.10080]. It has been identified as a plausible counterpart to the neutrino event IceCube 200530A, and its optical spectrum displays both faint emission lines and stellar absorption lines from its host galaxy [arXiv:2302.10080].

sources.summary: 4FGL J1702.2+2642 is a possible IceCube counterpart whose spectrum shows [O II], [O III], and host-galaxy absorption at z = 0.3197 with strong host contribution.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J1702.2+2642 : This source was proposed as a plausible association to the neutrino event IceCube 200530A ( GCN27865 and GCN27879 ). Our high quality (S/N 190) optical spectrum exhibits faint emission lines attributed to [ O II ]  3727 (at 4918  , EW = 0.55  ) and [ OIII ]  5007 (at 6608  , EW = 0.40  ), together with stellar absorption lines of its host galaxy (CaII (3934,3968) at 5191,5237  and marginally the G-band 4304 at 5681  ), yielding z = 0.3197. From the spectral decomposition (see Fig.  fig:decomposition ) of the two components (nucleus and host galaxy), we get N/H = 11. No previous spectra are available in literature.

gmw_00008877.name: 4FGL J1808.2+3500
gmw_00008877.overview: 4FGL J1808.2+3500 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2302.10080]. Located at a redshift of z = 0.282, its optical spectrum displays weak Ca II absorption features alongside emission lines of [O II], [O III], and [N II] [arXiv:2302.10080].

sources.summary: 4FGL J1808.2+3500 has a new LBT spectrum with [O II], [O III], [N II], weak Ca II absorption, and a measured redshift z = 0.282.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J1808.2+3500 : No spectra are present in the literature for the source. We obtained a S/N 250 optical spectrum at the LBT that exhibits emission lines at 4778, 6419, and 8440   attributed to [ O II ]  3727, [ O III ]  5007, and [ N II ]  6583 respectively (see Fig.  fig:spectra and Table  tab:emission ). In addition we found weak absorption lines of the Ca II doublet at 5043,5088  . The redshift of the source is z=0.282.

gmw_00008878.name: 4FGL J2030.5+2235
gmw_00008878.overview: The gamma-ray source 4FGL J2030.5+2235 is associated with 3HSP J203031.6+223439, which exhibits a broad-band spectral energy distribution typical of a high-synchrotron-peaked blazar [arXiv:2302.10080]. Its featureless optical spectrum is described by a power-law continuum, establishing a redshift lower limit of greater than 0.5 [arXiv:2302.10080].

sources.summary: 4FGL J2030.5+2235 is associated with 3HSP J203031.6+223439 as an HSP blazar candidate; its featureless LBT spectrum sets a redshift lower limit.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J2030.5+2235 : This ray source is unassociated in the 4FGL-DR3 catalogue. The likely association with the ray detection is the source 3HSP J203031.6+223439, well located inside the Fermi LAT error ellipse and with a broad-band SED typical of an HSP blazar. The optical spectrum (S/N 40), obtained at LBT, is featureless and well described by a power law continuum ( 1.35). Based on the absence of host galaxy lines we can set a redshift lower limit > 0.5.

gmw_00008879.name: 4FGL J2133.1+2529c
gmw_00008879.overview: The active galaxy 4FGL J2133.1+2529c is located at a redshift of z = 0.295 [arXiv:2302.10080]. Its optical spectrum displays absorption lines characteristic of an elliptical galaxy's stellar population, alongside a modest nuclear power-law component [arXiv:2302.10080].

sources.summary: 4FGL J2133.1+2529c shows elliptical-galaxy absorption features at z = 0.295, consistent with previous work, and a modest nuclear power-law component.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J2133.1+2529c : Our optical spectrum clearly shows absorption lines due to the elliptical galaxy stellar population, in particular the Ca II 3934,3968 doublet, G-band 4305, and Mg I 5157 at z = 0.295. This value is in agreement with that reported by massaro2015 and adopted by . From our decomposition analysis (see Fig.  fig:decomposition ), we get N/H 2 and  = 0.8 for the nuclear power-law component.

gmw_00008880.name: 4FGL J2358.1 2853
gmw_00008880.overview: 4FGL J2358.1 2853 is a blazar and BL Lacertae object that exhibits a non-thermal continuum [arXiv:2302.10080]. Its spectrum reveals multiple intervening absorption systems, including a strong Mg II and Fe II system at z = 1.5415 [arXiv:2302.10080].

sources.summary: 4FGL J2358.1 2853 has a non-thermal continuum and multiple intervening Mg II and Fe II absorption systems, including a strong system at z = 1.5415.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J2358.1 2853 : The spectrum exhibits a non-thermal continuum described by a power law ( 0.20). We detect a strong absorption doublet (EW 10  ) at 7106, 7124  that corresponds to a Mg II 2796,2803 intervening absorption systems at z = 1.5415 (see Fig.  fig:spectra ), with other two absorption features found at 6054 and 6608  due to Fe II at the same redshift. Moreover, we find three additional absorption systems at 5270  , 5730  , and 6200  corresponding to Fe II and Mg II intervening at z = 1.2123.

gmw_00008881.name: NGC 4561
gmw_00008881.overview: NGC 4561 is a bulgeless disk galaxy [arXiv:1010.2497] that hosts an active galactic nucleus at its center [arXiv:1209.1354]. It features two distinct H I kinematic components [arXiv:1010.2497], with a misaligned outer component that may indicate a warp or a recent interaction [arXiv:1010.2497].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4561 as a bulgeless disk galaxy with separate main and outer H I kinematic components, whose misaligned outer component may reflect a warp or recent interaction.

quotes.quote: Three of our objects have two kinematic components: NGC 4561, NGC 4713, and NGC 6509. The main component covers most of or more than the optical disk and the outer component is characterized by low surface brightness emission in the outer disk. We carried out the velocity field modeling separately for the two components. Table tab:final_params quotes only the kinematic parameters determined for the main component, as it corresponds to the optical disk.

The left panel of the top, middle, and bottom rows in Figure fig:outer_disk shows the main and outer components in the first-moment map of NGC 4561, NGC 4713, and NGC 6509, respectively. The position angle of the major axis of the outer component differs by between about 30 and 90 from that of the main component.

Although much of the emission in the first-moment maps is of low significance, the position-velocity diagrams show that there is significant emission in the outer-disk components. These outer components are likely due to a warp, which may indicate that these galaxies have undergone a recent interaction.

sources.summary: The paper presents XMM-Newton observations identifying NGC 4561's nuclear source as an AGN, showing a supermassive black hole can exist in a bulge-less galaxy.

quotes.quote: We present XMM-Newton observations of the detected nuclear X-ray source in NGC 4561. The hard X-ray spectrum can be described by a model composed of an absorbed power-law with = 2.5 ^ +0.4 _ 0.3 , and column density N_H=1.9^ +0.1 _ 0.2 10^ 22 atoms cm ^ 2 . The absorption corrected luminosity of the source is L(0.2 - 10.0 keV) = 2.5 10^ 41 ergs s ^ 1 , with bolometric luminosity over 3 10^ 42 ergs s ^ 1 . Based on the spectrum and the luminosity, we identify the nuclear X-ray source in NGC 4561 to be an AGN, with a black hole of mass > 2 10^4 . The presence of a supermassive black hole at the center of this bulge-less galaxy shows that black hole masses are not necessarily related to bulge properties, contrary to the general belief.

gmw_00008882.name: NGC 6509
gmw_00008882.overview: NGC 6509 is a disk galaxy that features two distinct H I kinematic components [arXiv:1010.2497]. The main component spans most of the optical disk, while the outer component consists of low surface brightness emission [arXiv:1010.2497].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 6509 has two H I kinematic components and foreground absorption from a radio source, making its H I mass a lower limit while leaving the rotation model acceptable.

quotes.quote: Three of our objects have two kinematic components: NGC 4561, NGC 4713, and NGC 6509. The main component covers most of or more than the optical disk and the outer component is characterized by low surface brightness emission in the outer disk. We carried out the velocity field modeling separately for the two components.

NGC 6509 shows H 1 in absorption on the east side of the galaxy because it is in the foreground of the radio source 4C +06.63, which is the northwest component of a pair of radio lobes, the southeast component being VLSS J1759.5+0615.

Because of this absorption, the H 1 mass in NGC 6509 is a lower limit. We made no adjustments to the rotation curve analysis for this object. Especially note that we still used both the approaching and receding side of the galaxy, including about 25 where absorption is evident in position velocity diagrams. Even so, the rotation curve model is acceptable.

gmw_00008883.name: WK0912 13R
gmw_00008883.overview: WK0912 13R is a Herschel-detected WiggleZ galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1.3 [arXiv:1308.4299]. It is classified as a large disk galaxy that contains resolved star-forming regions [arXiv:1308.4299].

sources.summary: The paper treats WK0912 13R as a z~1.3 Herschel-detected WiggleZ source with well-constrained cold-dust graybody properties and a large disc morphology containing resolved star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: For the WiggleZ detections at z 1.3 , the three SPIRE bands constrain the turnover of the graybody providing a reliable estimate of the dust temperature. The best-fit graybody is shown in Fig. fig.seds for the three detected WiggleZ sources, with values obtained for = [ 21.6, 29.8, 27.5 ] K, and ( ) = [ 11.8, 12.1, 12.3 ] for WK0912 13R, WK0909 02R, and WK0909 06R respectively. Far-infrared SEDs were fit to the WISE and Herschel photometry to show the overall shape of IR SED using the templates, which are shown in Fig. fig.seds with the UV-NIR photometry and SED fits. A Monte-Carlo analysis is performed to measure errors on and by varying the SPIRE fluxes within a Normal distribution around the measured value. The derived values for WK0912 13R and WK0909 06R are well determined as the three SPIRE points probe the full turn over.

From morphologies, the three detected objects are classified as `multiple emission' galaxies with two, WK0909 06R and WK0912 13R, being the largest disc galaxies in the sample containing resolved star-forming regions, reminiscent of local regions 2012MNRAS.422.3339W .

gmw_00008884.name: WK0909 02R
gmw_00008884.overview: WK0909 02R is a high-redshift infrared galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1.3 [arXiv:1308.4299]. Optical imaging reveals that the system consists of multiple components spanning four arcseconds, indicating it is a galaxy merger [arXiv:1308.4299].

sources.summary: The paper gives WK0909 02R Herschel graybody dust properties, flags its temperature as relatively uncertain, and explains its discrepant infrared-to-H-alpha SFR ratio as an OSIRIS aperture effect.

quotes.quote: For the WiggleZ detections at z 1.3 , the three SPIRE bands constrain the turnover of the graybody providing a reliable estimate of the dust temperature. The best-fit graybody is shown in Fig. fig.seds for the three detected WiggleZ sources, with values obtained for = [ 21.6, 29.8, 27.5 ] K, and ( ) = [ 11.8, 12.1, 12.3 ] for WK0912 13R, WK0909 02R, and WK0909 06R respectively.
The derived values for WK0909 02R are much more uncertain, ( ) 9 K, as the measured 250 m flux less clearly probes the Wein side of the blackbody curve.

However it is noted, the galaxy with the most discrepant and L_ H is WK0909 02R. Optical imaging of WK0909 02R indicates that the OSIRIS luminosity is underestimated due to an aperture effect as multiple components of the system cover 4 arc seconds, comparatively larger than the field of view of OSIRIS. In contrast, the SPIRE beam would encompass the whole system, accounting for the difference in SFR indicators.

gmw_00008885.name: WK0909 06R
gmw_00008885.overview: WK0909 06R is a Herschel-detected WiggleZ galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1.3 [arXiv:1308.4299]. It is classified as a large disk galaxy with resolved star-forming regions and an infrared luminosity of 10^12 solar luminosities [arXiv:1308.4299].

sources.summary: The paper treats WK0909 06R as a z~1.3 Herschel-detected WiggleZ source with well-determined cold-dust properties and a large disc morphology containing resolved star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: For the WiggleZ detections at z 1.3 , the three SPIRE bands constrain the turnover of the graybody providing a reliable estimate of the dust temperature. The best-fit graybody is shown in Fig. fig.seds for the three detected WiggleZ sources, with values obtained for = [ 21.6, 29.8, 27.5 ] K, and ( ) = [ 11.8, 12.1, 12.3 ] for WK0912 13R, WK0909 02R, and WK0909 06R respectively. Far-infrared SEDs were fit to the WISE and Herschel photometry to show the overall shape of IR SED using the templates, which are shown in Fig. fig.seds with the UV-NIR photometry and SED fits. A Monte-Carlo analysis is performed to measure errors on and by varying the SPIRE fluxes within a Normal distribution around the measured value. The derived values for WK0912 13R and WK0909 06R are well determined as the three SPIRE points probe the full turn over.

From morphologies, the three detected objects are classified as `multiple emission' galaxies with two, WK0909 06R and WK0912 13R, being the largest disc galaxies in the sample containing resolved star-forming regions, reminiscent of local regions 2012MNRAS.422.3339W .
However, WK0909 06R and WK0912 13R have infrared luminosities of 1 10^ 12 L _ , an order of magnitude lower than most typical z 2 SMGs that are known to be mergers.

gmw_00008886.name: Segue 1
gmw_00008886.overview: Segue 1 is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy located approximately 23 kiloparsecs from the Sun [arXiv:1103.0477, arXiv:2205.07055]. It is a satellite of the Milky Way and is considered an extremely dark-matter-dominated system [arXiv:1103.0477, arXiv:1110.6775].

sources.summary: The paper treats Segue 1 as a dwarf-spheroidal dark-matter target, gives distance and coordinates, and reports MAGIC observations with no >100 GeV detection and gamma-ray upper limits.

quotes.quote: In this paper we will discuss the possibility of detecting signatures of DM annihilation in the very high energy band (i.e. above 100 GeV) of the electromagnetic spectrum using as particularly convenient target the Segue 1 dwarf Spheroidal galaxy (dSph).

To this class belongs Segue 1, a satellite galaxy discovered by Belokurov et al. Belokurov:2007a in the Sloan Extension for Galactic Understanding and Exploration SEGUE, Yanny:2009a . It is located at a distance of 23 2 kpc from the Sun ( 28 kpc from the Galactic Center) at (RA, Dec) = ( 10.12^h , 16.08^ ). Despite the fact that its nature has been debated after discovery Belokurov:2007a,NiedersteOstholt:2009a,Geha:2009a,XiangGruess:2009a , it has now been interpreted more clearly as an ultrafaint MW satellite galaxy in Ref. Simon:2010a through the identification of several new member stars (66 instead of the previous 24).

We present here the results of the observation of the Segue 1 satellite performed by the Major Atmospheric Gamma-ray Imaging Cherenkov (MAGIC) telescope. The observation took place between November 2008 and March 2009, for a total of 43.2 hours ( 29.4 hours in good observational conditions after quality cuts). No significant detection above the background was found for energies above 100 GeV. In this paper we describe the data analysis and determine upper limits (ULs) for the gamma-ray emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes Segue 1 as an extremely dark-matter-dominated dwarf spheroidal observed by MAGIC-I, with no significant gamma-ray detection and upper limits on dark-matter models.

quotes.quote: Here, we present the results of the survey of Segue 1 by the MAGIC-I telescope performed in 2008 and 2009. This source is considered by many as the most DM dominated Milky Way satellite galaxy known so far. The nearly 43 hours of data taken constitute the deepest observation ever made on a single dwarf galaxy by Cherenkov telescopes. No significant gamma-ray emission was found above an energy threshold of 100  GeV.

Segue 1, discovered in 2006 by the SDSS  , is located at 28 kpc from the Galactic Center, at (RA,DEC)=(10.12 ^h , 16.08 ^ ). Kinematics studies applied to 66 member stars allowed to estimate its mass--to--light ratio to be in the range 1320-3400 M _ /L _   , highlighting Segue 1 as the most DM dominated dSph known so far.

sources.summary: Segue 1 is the ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal target for a MAGIC branon dark-matter search; the paper uses its J factor and deep observations to set annihilation limits after no gamma-ray excess.

quotes.quote: In this work, we are focusing on the ultra-faint dSph Segue 1 located in the Northern Hemisphere and outside of the Galactic plane ( RA = 10.12 h , DEC = 16.08^ ) only 23 2 kpc away from us . The Segue 1 observational campaign was carried out by the ground-based gamma-ray telescope MAGIC between 2011 and 2013 and is with 157.9 hours the deepest observation of any dSph by a Cherenkov telescope to date .

In our analysis, we are using the J factor and its statistical uncertainty for Segue 1 from with the value of log _ 10 J GeV^ 2 cm^ 5 = 19.02_ 0.35 ^ +0.32 . The differential gamma-ray yields per annihilation d N_ i / d E (see Eq. eq:Branon_Flux ) are taken from the PPPC 4 DM ID distribution . The data reduction of the Segue 1 observation have been published by the MAGIC Collaboration . We re-analyse those high-level data in the context of brane-world extra-dimensional theories using the likelihood analysis described in Aleksi , Rico and Martinez .

gmw_00008887.name: UDF1
gmw_00008887.overview: UDF1 is an X-ray-luminous active galactic nucleus that exhibits mild obscuration [arXiv:1607.07710]. It is classified among the most compact and intense star-forming galaxies in its sample, showing a relatively small radio enhancement [arXiv:1607.07710].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UDF1 as an X-ray-luminous AGN with small radio enhancement, among the most compact intense star-forming galaxies, and mildly obscured from X-ray counts.

quotes.quote: X-ray-luminous AGN (UDF1 and 8) tend to have relatively small radio enhancement, having S _ 5 cm similar to the levels predicted by the far-infrared/radio correlation given the S _ 1.3 mm , whereas X-ray-weak AGN candidates are among the most radio-luminous.
Consistent with this trend, the two most intense SFGs in our sample (UDF1 and 2) are also the most compact.

In the remaining two sources with sufficient count statistics, UDF1 and UDF 8, which have 434 and 878 full-band counts, respectively, the column densities are 1.5 10^ 21 and 7 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 respectively, based on Web-PIMMS http://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/Tools , provided by HEASARC, assuming an intrinsic photon index of 2 and the redshift of the source, indicating mild obscuration.

gmw_00008888.name: UDF7
gmw_00008888.overview: UDF7 is an interacting galaxy that hosts a radio-enhanced active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1607.07710]. Its star-formation activity is located 6 kpc away from the dominant stellar-mass concentration, which is a likely consequence of galaxy interactions [arXiv:1607.07710].

sources.summary: The paper highlights UDF7 as interacting, with star formation far from the dominant stellar mass concentration while its radio-enhanced AGN lies near the ALMA star-formation barycenter.

quotes.quote: The separations between the star-formation and stellar-mass barycenters (Table 3) are smaller than this radius in all cases except UDF7, which appears to be interacting.

For UDF7, 11, and 13 where the radio flux enhancement allows pinpointing the AGN location using the VLA images, we find that the AGN are within 0.5 kpc of the barycenter of star formation of their host galaxies as measured from the ALMA images (an upper limit of given by our ability to pinpoint AGN and star formation given their S/N).

For UDF11 and 13, the star-formation concentrations are 1 kpc from the stellar-mass barycenters, whereas UDF7 is notable for these activities being 6 kpc away from the dominant stellar-mass concentration, a possible consequence of galaxy interactions.

gmw_00008889.name: UDF8
gmw_00008889.overview: UDF8 is a galaxy that hosts an X-ray luminous active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1607.07710]. This active nucleus exhibits mild X-ray obscuration and lacks enhanced radio emission [arXiv:1607.07710].

sources.summary: The paper treats UDF8 as an X-ray AGN without enhanced radio emission, uses non-AGN size measurements for it, and reports mild X-ray obscuration.

quotes.quote: X-ray-luminous AGN (UDF1 and 8) tend to have relatively small radio enhancement, having S _ 5 cm similar to the levels predicted by the far-infrared/radio correlation given the S _ 1.3 mm , whereas X-ray-weak AGN candidates are among the most radio-luminous.

For AGN, we adopt the ALMA _ 1/2 (or the _ 1/2 limit when the ALMA image is not spatially resolved) with the exception of UDF8 that harbors an X-ray AGN but does not appear to have enhanced radio emission, which we treat as non-AGN for the purpose of _ SF measurement.

In the remaining two sources with sufficient count statistics, UDF1 and UDF 8, which have 434 and 878 full-band counts, respectively, the column densities are 1.5 10^ 21 and 7 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 respectively, based on Web-PIMMS http://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/Tools , provided by HEASARC, assuming an intrinsic photon index of 2 and the redshift of the source, indicating mild obscuration.

gmw_00008890.name: PKS B1144 379
gmw_00008890.overview: PKS B1144 379 is a BL Lacertae object that exhibits rapid variability in its radio flux density [arXiv:1206.6914]. This variability is best explained by interstellar scintillation, which indicates a compact source component and a high brightness temperature [arXiv:1206.6914].

sources.summary: PKS B1144 379 is a BL Lac whose rapid radio variability is best explained by interstellar scintillation, implying a compact component, high brightness temperature, and extreme scintillator status.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: 11^ h 47^ m 01^ s .4 , 38^ 12^ 11^ in J2000 coordinates

quotes.quote: Rapid variability in the radio flux density of the BL Lac object PKS B1144 379 has been observed at four frequencies, ranging from 1.5 to 15 GHz, with the VLA and the University of Tasmania's Ceduna antenna. Intrinsic and line of sight effects were examined as possible causes of this variability, with interstellar scintillation best explaining the frequency dependence of the variability timescales and modulation indices. This scintillation is consistent with a compact source 20--40   as, or 0.15--0.3 pc in size. The inferred brightness temperature for PKS B1144 379 (assuming that the observed variations are due to scintillation) is 6.2 10^ 12  K at 4.9 GHz, with approximately 10 percent of the total flux in the scintillating component.
PKS B1144 379 is a member of a small class of extreme scintillators which are strong ( > 1 Jy), have high modulation index ( m > 0.05 ) and vary on timescales longer than a day.

gmw_00008891.name: VCC 178
gmw_00008891.overview: VCC 178 is a flattened nucleated dwarf galaxy that exhibits rotation and a flat velocity dispersion profile inside 1 r_e [arXiv:0812.3272]. It has a metallicity gradient and a stellar population that is younger in its center than in its periphery [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 178 is described as a flattened nucleated dwarf with significant rotation, no embedded substructures, a flat inner dispersion profile, a metallicity gradient, and a center younger than its outskirts.

quotes.quote: VCC 178 = IC 3081 (Fig figvcc0178 ). This flattened nucleated dwarf galaxy exhibits significant rotation and quite flat velocity dispersion profile inside 1 r_ e . No embedded substructures were revealed by : VCC 178 is listed in Appendix E there. There is a significant metallicity gradient. Age distribution also demonstrates smooth changes along the radius, being younger in the centre (7 Gyr) than in the periphery (11.5 Gyr).

gmw_00008892.name: VCC 437
gmw_00008892.overview: VCC 437 is a dwarf galaxy that exhibits a steep metallicity gradient beyond 4 arcsec from its center [arXiv:0812.3272]. Drastic changes in ellipticity and color gradient at 3 arcsec suggest the presence of a nearly face-on embedded central structure [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 437 shows a steep metallicity gradient, changing color and ellipticity near 3 arcsec, and evidence for a nearly face-on embedded central structure.

quotes.quote: VCC 437 = UGC 7399A (Fig figvcc0437 ). A very steep metallicity gradient is observed beyond 4 arcsec from the centre. Metallicity at 1 r_e is almost 1 dex lower than at 4 arcsec. Interestingly, at 3 arcsec the g'-z' colour profile of the galaxy presented in changes its gradient, and the ellipticity changes drastically. This suggests the presence of an embedded structure in the central part, however, not clearly detected on the unsharp-masked SDSS images, which can be explained if it has nearly face-on orientation.

gmw_00008893.name: VCC 490
gmw_00008893.overview: VCC 490 is a nucleated dwarf galaxy that exhibits spiral arms in its stellar disc [arXiv:0812.3272]. Its nuclear region hosts a young stellar population with a luminosity-weighted age of approximately 3 Gyr, whereas its main galactic body is older than 10 Gyr [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 490 is a nucleated dwarf with spiral arms, low-SNR outer data, deep MPFS IFU observations, and a young nuclear population compared with its older main body.

quotes.quote: VCC 490 = IC 783 (Fig figvcc0490 ). This nucleated dwarf galaxy exhibits prominent spiral arms in the stellar disc BBJ02,LGB06 . The HFA data available for this object have very low signal-to-noise ratio outside the nuclear region, therefore the stellar population properties cannot be derived in the periphery of the galaxy. Deep IFU observations were carried out with the MPFS spectrograph in 2004 and presented in and . The luminosity-weighted age in the nuclear region of VCC 490 is as young as 3 Gyr, whereas the main galactic body is older than 10 Gyr.

gmw_00008894.name: VCC 545
gmw_00008894.overview: VCC 545 is a faint, nucleated dwarf elliptical galaxy that lacks any detected substructures [arXiv:0812.3272]. It is located close in projection to the galaxies VCC 490 and M 100, sharing a very similar radial velocity with VCC 490 [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 545 is a faint nucleated dE galaxy with no detected substructures; only integrated stellar-population and dispersion properties were measurable, and it lies near VCC 490.

quotes.quote: VCC 545 = IC 783A No substructures were detected by in this faint nucleated dE galaxy. The signal-to-noise ratio of the HFA data was very low so we were able to determine only integrated properties of the stellar populations and mean velocity dispersion, which agree well with the values derived from fitting the SDSS DR6 spectrum. The galaxy is located close in projection to VCC 490 and M 100 and its radial velocity is quite close to that of VCC 490.

gmw_00008895.name: VCC 634
gmw_00008895.overview: VCC 634, also known as NGC 4328, is a nucleated dwarf galaxy that is rotationally supported and lacks embedded structures [arXiv:0812.3272]. It is located approximately 14.84 Mpc away and is likely a satellite of the nearby galaxy NGC 4321 [arXiv:2012.00757].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4328 as a dwarf near NGC 4321, measures a TRGB distance, and confirms it is likely NGC 4321's satellite.

quotes.quote: NGC 4328 is a dwarf galaxy that lies 6 ' away from NGC 4321. Unlike NGC 4321's other satellite (NGC 4323), it is not clearly connected to NGC 4321, and thus could be physically unrelated. One of the two parallel fields for NGC 4321 fully covers this dwarf galaxy, and we are able to use the entire field (which lacks young stars) to measure m_ TRGB = 26.86 0.22 mag. From this we find D = 14.84 1.61 Mpc. With this distance, we confirm that NGC 4328 is likely a satellite of NGC 4321. Note that NGC 4328 is not a member of the PHANGS sample, and is included here only because one of the two parallels for NGC 4321 falls directly onto this satellite.

sources.summary: VCC 634 is a relatively bright, rotationally supported nucleated dwarf without embedded structures, showing an intermediate-age, metal-rich stellar population within one effective radius.

quotes.quote: VCC 634 = NGC 4328 (Fig figvcc0634 ). This relatively bright rotationally supported nucleated dwarf galaxy does not exhibit embedded structures LGB06 . We see the intermediate-age (3 Gyr) quite metal-rich ( 0.2 dex) stellar population without significant changes of the properties within 1 r_ e . Low signal-to-noise ratio of the data did not allow us to measure the metallicity behaviour at larger radii.

gmw_00008896.name: VCC 965
gmw_00008896.overview: VCC 965 is a faint, flattened nucleated dwarf galaxy with an internal velocity dispersion that is too low to be measured [arXiv:0812.3272]. Its nuclear region contains a younger and more metal-rich stellar population than its periphery, and it exhibits a central velocity dispersion drop [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 965 is a faint flattened nucleated galaxy with too-low internal dispersion to measure well, but its nucleus is younger, more metal-rich, and has a dispersion drop.

quotes.quote: VCC 965 = IC 3363 (Fig figvcc0965 ). No substructures are reported by in this faint flattened nucleated galaxy. This is one of a few cases, where the internal velocity dispersion is too low to be measured. The stellar population in the nuclear region turns to be very different from the periphery of the galaxy both in terms of young age and high metallicity. The velocity dispersion profile of this object derived from the red arm DS spectra and presented in demonstrates a central velocity dispersion drop.

gmw_00008897.name: VCC 1075
gmw_00008897.overview: VCC 1075 is a faint, flattened nucleated dwarf galaxy that contains no embedded substructures [arXiv:0812.3272]. Although low signal-to-noise ratio data makes radial variations difficult to assess, no gradients in age or metallicity are detected [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 1075 is a faint flattened nucleated dwarf without embedded substructures; low-SNR data make radial population trends difficult, but no significant age or metallicity gradients are detected.

quotes.quote: VCC 1075 = IC 3383 (Fig figvcc1075 ). This faint flattened nucleated dwarf galaxy harbours no embedded substructures according to . The signal-to-noise ratio of the data is quite low, making difficult to assess the variations of the stellar population parameters along the radius. However, at the present level of precision we do not detect any statistically significant gradients of age neither metallicity.

gmw_00008898.name: VCC 1308
gmw_00008898.overview: VCC 1308 is a dwarf galaxy with disputed rotation classifications [arXiv:0812.3272]. Although some rotation is observed, the galaxy exhibits a peculiar radial velocity profile in its center, a metallicity gradient, and a younger central stellar population [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 1308 has disputed rotation classifications; the authors see some central rotation, a peculiar velocity profile, a metallicity gradient, and a younger central population.

quotes.quote: VCC 1308 = IC 3437 (Fig figvcc1308 ). This galaxy was classified as ``non-rotating'' by but as ``rotating'' by . No substructures were reported in it by . We indeed see some rotation, although the radial velocity profile is quite peculiar in the centre, as well as a remarkable metallicity gradient. The stellar population in the central region of the galaxy looks younger then in the outer regions along the radius.

gmw_00008899.name: VCC 1407
gmw_00008899.overview: VCC 1407 is a dwarf galaxy with no reported embedded structures [arXiv:0812.3272]. Due to a very low signal-to-noise ratio in its data, radial variations in its stellar population properties cannot be studied [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 1407 has low-SNR data that prevent radial stellar-population analysis, but HFA and SDSS estimates agree well and no embedded structures are reported.

quotes.quote: VCC 1407 = IC 3461 Very low signal-to-noise ratio of the data does not allow to study the variations of the stellar population properties along the radius. However, we stress a very good agreement of the age and metallicity estimates for this object obtained from the fitting of the HFA and SDSS DR6 spectra. No embedded structures were reported in this galaxy by .

gmw_00008900.name: VCC 1514
gmw_00008900.overview: VCC 1514 is a strongly flattened dwarf galaxy that exhibits some rotation and may contain an inclined disc [arXiv:0812.3272]. Its stellar age and metallicity estimates do not show radial variations in low signal-to-noise ratio data [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 1514 is a strongly flattened galaxy with a possible inclined disc, some rotation, and no significant radial age or metallicity variations in low-SNR data.

quotes.quote: VCC 1514 = PGC 41726 (Fig figvcc1514 ). Possible inclined disc was reported by in this strongly flattened galaxy. We observe some rotation. The age and metallicity estimates do not exhibit statistically significant variations along the radius, although the signal-to-noise ratio of the data was rather low.

gmw_00008901.name: VCC 1545
gmw_00008901.overview: VCC 1545 is a dwarf galaxy that hosts a young stellar population in its central region [arXiv:0812.3272]. The galaxy exhibits complex kinematics, including large-scale major-axis rotation and a decoupled minor-axis rotation in its circumnuclear region [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 1545 has a young central stellar population, complex minor-axis and major-axis kinematics, a circumnuclear dispersion depression, and a younger metal-rich nucleus.

quotes.quote: VCC 1545 = IC 3509 (Fig figvcc1545 ). This galaxy was among the first, where the young stellar population in the central region was reported by . It demonstrates very complex kinematics: we see a minor-axis rotation (i.e. kinematical decoupling) in the circumnuclear region of the galaxy with the depression in the velocity dispersion distribution there, while there is a large-scale major-axis rotation as well. The stellar population in the nuclear region is younger and considerably more metal-rich than in the main galactic body.

gmw_00008902.name: VCC 1743
gmw_00008902.overview: VCC 1743 is a faint, flattened dwarf galaxy that features an extended nucleus [arXiv:0812.3272]. Outside of this nuclear region, the stellar population appears to be old and quite metal-poor [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 1743 is a faint flattened dwarf with an extended nucleus; low surface brightness limits profile measurements, but outside the nucleus it appears old and metal poor.

quotes.quote: VCC 1743 = IC 3602 This is a faint flattened dwarf galaxy with the faint extended nucleus seen on the HST ACS images but not on the ground-based data. The galaxy is listed in Appendix E of of as an object ``in which substructure other than a disc was found''. The data available to us have very low signal-to-noise ratio due to low surface brightness of the galaxy, therefore we were not able to derive detailed profiles of the stellar population properties. However, outside the nuclear region the age looks to be old ( 10 Gyr) and the metallicity quite low ( 0.6 dex).

gmw_00008903.name: VCC 2050
gmw_00008903.overview: VCC 2050 is a dwarf galaxy that contains a probable inclined disc and exhibits a very low velocity dispersion [arXiv:0812.3272]. The galaxy features a metallicity gradient, faint emission lines, and a young central luminosity-weighted age of approximately 2.5 billion years [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 2050 has a probable inclined disc, very low velocity dispersion, a significant metallicity gradient, a young central luminosity-weighted age, and faint emission lines.

quotes.quote: VCC 2050 = IC 3779 (Fig figvcc2050 ). Probable inclined disc is reported in this galaxy by . The observed velocity dispersion in this object is too low to be precisely measured. There is a significant metallicity gradient, whereas the signal is not sufficient to detect any changes in the age distribution. The luminosity-weighted age in the central part is quite young ( 2.5 Gyr) and we see faint emission lines in the fitting residuals.

gmw_00008904.name: host of FRB 20211127I
gmw_00008904.overview: The host galaxy of FRB 20211127I, also known as WALLABY J131913--185018, is a barred spiral galaxy with a low inclination angle [arXiv:2305.14863, arXiv:2312.01578]. It possesses a high neutral hydrogen gas content [arXiv:2305.14863] and exhibits a clear bar structure [arXiv:2312.01578].

sources.summary: The paper reports H I emission from FRB host WALLABY J131913--185018, finding high gas content, modest disturbance, possible velocity-field warp, and the need for deeper observations.

quotes.quote: We report on the commensal ASKAP detection of a fast radio burst (FRB), FRB 20211127 I , and the detection of neutral hydrogen ( ) emission in the FRB host galaxy, WALLABY J131913--185018 (hereafter W13--18). This collaboration between the CRAFT and WALLABY survey teams marks the fifth, and most distant, FRB host galaxy detected in , not including the Milky Way. We find that W13--18 has a \ mass of = 6.5 10 ^ 9 M _ , a to-stellar mass ratio of 2.17, and coincides with a continuum radio source of flux density at 1.4 GHz of 1.3 mJy. The \ global spectrum of W13--18 appears to be asymmetric, albeit the \ observation has a low S/N, and the galaxy itself appears modestly undisturbed.

We present the commensal detection of \ alongside the localisation for FRB 20211127 I to W13--18 through a collaboration between the CRAFT and WALLABY surveys. W13--18 has an \ mass of 6.5 1.3 10 ^ 9 M _ . Despite the high to-stellar mass ratio of 2.17, and a possible warp in the \ velocity field, we see no strong disturbances in the \ intensity map, with the only notable feature possibly attributed to noise.

sources.summary: The host of FRB 20211127I is fit with disk and bulge components, has a clear bar structure, low inclination, and a possible leading or trailing spiral-arm association.

quotes.quote: The hosts of FRB 20180924B, FRB 20211127I, and FRB 20211212A were fit using two Sérsic components, equating to a disc and a bulge.

The host of FRB 20211127I is now one of only four reported FRBs with a clear bar structure observed in the host galaxy along with the hosts of FRB 20190608B, FRB 20201124A and FRB 20220319D; chittidi2021,Dong2023,Ravi2023 .
The host galaxies are at a range of inclination angles from 33 \ (FRB20211127I) to 59 \ (FRB20210807D).

gmw_00008905.name: host of FRB 20211212A
gmw_00008905.overview: The host of FRB 20211212A is a spiral galaxy [arXiv:2312.01578]. Its structure is characterized by a disk and a bulge, which were fit using two Sérsic components [arXiv:2312.01578].

sources.summary: The host of FRB 20211212A is newly presented, fit with disk and bulge Sérsic components, and has newly reported spiral structure.

quotes.quote: Many of these FRBs have been presented in previous works e.g. Prochaska19,Heintz20, Mannings2021, Bhandari23, Glowacki23 , but the hosts of FRBs 20210807D and 20211212A are newly presented here. In a companion paper, we describe VLT imaging of the hosts of FRBs 20210807D, and 20211212A, including the FRB localization (Deller et al., in prep).
The hosts of FRB 20180924B, FRB 20211127I, and FRB 20211212A were fit using two Sérsic components, equating to a disc and a bulge.
The spiral structure for 20211212A is newly presented here with further discussion in Deller et al. (in prep).

gmw_00008906.name: host of FRB 20191001A
gmw_00008906.overview: The host of FRB 20191001A is a spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.2340 [arXiv:2008.12488]. It forms a possibly interacting galaxy pair with a nearby companion [arXiv:2008.12488] and exhibits diffuse radio emission driven by ongoing star formation [arXiv:2008.12488].

sources.summary: The paper identifies DES J213324.44 544454.18 as the FRB 191001 host, a z=0.2340 spiral in a possibly interacting pair with diffuse star-formation-powered radio emission.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of FRB 191001 was identified as DES J213324.44 544454.18, a galaxy catalogued in the Dark Energy Survey DES DES . Spectroscopic observations conducted on 2019 October 4 UT using the Gemini Multi-Object Spectrograph (GMOS) on the Gemini-South telescope established the redshift of the host to be z=0.2340(1) using the H spectral line see for further details heintz2020 . On 2019 October 05 UT deep optical imaging observations were performed with the FOcal Reducer and low dispersion Spectrograph 2 (FORS2) instrument on the Very Large Telescope (VLT). The left panel of Figure fig:radio shows the g band image of the host, which is clearly a spiral galaxy. The neighbouring galaxy, DES J213323.65 544453.6, is also at a similar redshift ( z=0.2339(2) ), hence the system is a double and possibly interacting pair of galaxies.
However, we detect diffuse synchrotron radio emission from the disk of the host galaxy that we ascribe to ongoing star formation.

sources.summary: The host of FRB 20191001A is fit with three Sérsic components, has the largest effective radius in the sample, and may lie on a leading or trailing spiral arm.

quotes.quote: FRBs 20191001A and 20210807D were fit utilizing three Sérsic components.
The physical effective radii range from 2.07 to 6.67 kpc, those values belonging to the host galaxies for FRB20180924B and FRB20191001A, respectively.
FRBs 20191001A and 20211127I could potentially be leading one spiral arm or trailing another arm.

gmw_00008907.name: host of FRB 20210807D
gmw_00008907.overview: The host of FRB 20210807D is newly presented and was fit utilizing three Sérsic components [arXiv:2312.01578]. Furthermore, this host potentially places the burst on a spiral arm [arXiv:2312.01578].

sources.summary: The host of FRB 20210807D is newly presented, fit with three Sérsic components, and potentially places its burst on a spiral arm.

quotes.quote: Many of these FRBs have been presented in previous works e.g. Prochaska19,Heintz20, Mannings2021, Bhandari23, Glowacki23 , but the hosts of FRBs 20210807D and 20211212A are newly presented here. In a companion paper, we describe VLT imaging of the hosts of FRBs 20210807D, and 20211212A, including the FRB localization (Deller et al., in prep).
FRBs 20191001A and 20210807D were fit utilizing three Sérsic components.
FRBs 20210807D and 20211212A, within their respective uncertainties, are potentially coincident with a spiral arm of their host.

gmw_00008908.overview: 0801+303 is a quasar located at a redshift of z=1.446 [arXiv:astro-ph/0112511]. Radio observations reveal it is a compact steep-spectrum source with a bright central core, a jet, and unequal lobes [arXiv:astro-ph/0112511].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 0801+303 as a z=1.446 QSO CSS source whose MERLIN maps show a bright core, unequal lobes, and central emission split into core and jet.

quotes.quote: This radio source is identified with a QSO of redshift z=1.446 hb89 . The 1.6-GHz observation reveals a triple structure. The central, brightest component C is a core and the two structures B1 and B2 straddling it are lobes. The B1 lobe is much brighter than B2 (Fig. 0801+303_maps , left panel). The 5-GHz map shows a double structure of the source: the B1 lobe and more complex structure of the central component.

The element C seen on the 1.6-GHz map is split here into two components: C1, which is probably the actual core, and C2, which is a part of the jet (Fig. 0801+303_maps , right panel). The spectrum of the component C is the flattest in the part identified with C1 and becoming steeper towards C2. The spectrum of component B1 is steep and the component B2 has presumably even steeper spectrum; it does not appear on the 5-GHz map at all.

gmw_00008909.overview: The source 0805+406 is an unconfirmed quasar identified as a blue object [arXiv:astro-ph/0112511]. Radio observations reveal a core-jet-lobe structure, which includes a flat-spectrum core, a jet component, a steep-spectrum lobe, and a possible counter-jet [arXiv:astro-ph/0112511].

sources.summary: The paper treats 0805+406 as an unconfirmed quasar whose MERLIN maps show a core-jet-lobe radio structure, flat-spectrum compact components, and a possible counter-jet.

quotes.quote: This source is an unconfirmed quasar (a ``blue object'') of unmeasured redshift greg98,vig99 . The map resulting from the 1.6-GHz observation shows a triple structure of the source. The brightest component C is a core, the A component is a part of the jet structure directed towards us and the B component is a lobe (Fig. 0805+406_maps , left panel). A triple structure of the source appears also on the 5-GHz map.

The spectrum of component C is flat and its spectral index amounts to =0.26 . Also the component A has a rather flat spectrum ( =0.46 ) and only the component B is featured by a truly steep spectrum ( =0.71 ). That means the source is clearly a core-jet-lobe structure. Another confirmation of this comes from F2001; their maps additionally show a diffuse western component which may be related to the (hidden) counter-jet.

gmw_00008910.overview: The radio source 0850+331 lacks an optical identification, leaving its magnitude and redshift unknown [arXiv:astro-ph/0112511]. Radio observations reveal a complex structure featuring a likely core and fragmented, wiggling jet components [arXiv:astro-ph/0112511].

sources.summary: The paper reports that 0850+331 lacks an optical identification but shows a complex MERLIN radio structure with a likely core and fragmented, wiggling jet components.

quotes.quote: There is no optical identification of this source mwhb01 so we do not know either its magnitude or redshift. The 1.6-GHz map shows the very complex structure of the source. The brightest component C is a core and structures A1 and A2 are fragments of a jet but the A1 component has lower radio emission than component A2. The brighter elements of A2 structure presumably represent the part of the jet seen at a smaller angle to the line of sight and so more Doppler-boosted (Fig. 0850+331_maps , left panel).

The 5-GHz map also shows a complex structure of the source. The component C seen on the 1.6-GHz map, here falls apart into small elements which, again, seem to be fragments of the wiggling jet, except component C1 which is probably the actual core. We can hardly see the component A1 and the structure A2 also consists of a few small elements (Fig. 0850+331_maps , right panel).

gmw_00008911.overview: 1201+394 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.445 [arXiv:astro-ph/0112511]. Radio maps of the source reveal a symmetric, double structure consisting of two steep-spectrum lobes with no visible core [arXiv:astro-ph/0112511].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1201+394 as a z=0.445 radio galaxy whose MERLIN maps show a symmetric double source with two steep-spectrum lobes and no visible core.

quotes.quote: This source is identified with a radio galaxy of redshift z=0.445 wandaly96 . The 1.6-GHz map shows a symmetric, double structure of the source. The two extended components B1 and B2 are lobes but the lobe B1 has stronger radio emission (Fig. 1201+394_maps , left panel). The 5-GHz map shows the same symmetric, double structure with two lobes B1 (also brighter) and B2 (Fig. 1201+394_maps , right panel). The spectra of the lobes are steep and their spectral indices amount to =0.71 for B1 and =0.66 for B2.

gmw_00008912.overview: 1233+418 is a compact steep-spectrum radio galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.25 [arXiv:astro-ph/0112511, arXiv:astro-ph/0701619]. Radio observations reveal a core-jet morphology with a triple structure at 5 GHz, where all components exhibit steep spectral indices [arXiv:astro-ph/0112511].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1233+418 as a z=0.25 radio galaxy with a core-jet MERLIN morphology, fading one-sided jet emission, triple 5 GHz structure, and steep component spectra.

quotes.quote: This source is identified with a radio galaxy redshifted to z=0.25 murgia99 . The 1.6-GHz map shows a core-jet structure; the source consists of two components: the core (component C) and an elongated jet structure (component A). Emission from the jet fragment lying closer to the core is high and it fades along the jet structure (Fig. 1233+418_maps , left panel). The 5-GHz observation shows a triple structure of the source. The brightest component C is probably a core.

The jet component A is split here into two elements A1 and A2 (Fig. 1233+418_maps , right panel). All the components have steep or very steep spectra: the spectral index of the component C is =0.64 and the spectral index of the component A amounts to =0.89 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 1233+418 as a CSS galaxy with a photometric redshift, marginal near-UV point-source detection, and possible but uncertain faint extended emission to the northeast.

quotes.quote: 1233+418 : A CSS galaxy with a photometric R-band redshift z _ R = 0.25 Fanti04 . We have a marginal detection of a point source with magnitude 24.93. Our image (see Figure galfigs , first panel) suggests faint extended emission towards Northeast but the errors in the GALFIT model for this component are too large to be sure of its existence. Checking the individual pre-drizzle images, it is not clear if these components are real or artifacts.

gmw_00008913.name: PKS 0027-426
gmw_00008913.overview: PKS 0027-426 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of z = 0.495 [arXiv:2108.13386]. It is a highly variable active galactic nucleus that exhibits mostly simultaneous optical and near-infrared emission [arXiv:2108.13386].

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 0027-426, a z=0.495 FSRQ and highly variable VEILS AGN, finding mostly simultaneous optical/NIR emission and color trends that change across filters and seasons.

quotes.quote: PKS 0027-426 RA = 00h30m17.584s, DEC = -42d24m46.02s (J2000) is classified as a FSRQ with z = 0.495 ( Hook2002 ) . It has been observed in the optical griz bands with DES (Dark Energy Survey) from 2013-2018 and VOILETTE (VEILS OptIcal Light curves of Extragalactic TransienT Events) from 2018 onwards, with concurrent observations in the NIR J and Ks bands with VEILS (VISTA Extragalactic Infrared Legacy Survey) from 2017 onward. PKS 0027-426 was found to be the most variable AGN detected in the VEILS fields thus far.

We studied the multi-wavelength temporal and spectral variability of the FSRQ PKS 0027-426, using optical observations from DES (2013-2018) and VOILETTE (2018-2019) in the griz bands, and NIR observations from VEILS (2017-2019) in the JKs bands. The results are summarised below: The temporal variability was studied using cross-correlation analysis of the optical and NIR light curves, and the most consistent correlation over all combination of light curves was found at 0 days, which implies that the emission is simultaneous or any delay between light curves occurs on timescales smaller that the cadence of observations. The spectral variability was studied for each combination of optical DES griz light curves using the slopes of the colour vs magnitude plots. The overall colour trends are shown to vary when different combinations of filters are used, from RWB trends in the g- , g-i , g-z and i to BWB in the z and i-z . The spectral variability was also shown to vary over each observation season, for example, in the g-z , the colour behaviour follows a BWB trend in the 2013 season, a SWB trend in the 2014 and 2015 seasons and a RWB trend in the 2016 and 2017 seasons.

gmw_00008914.name: VLA 512
gmw_00008914.overview: VLA 512 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy candidate [arXiv:0810.3108]. The source is undetected in shallow SCUBA maps but is weakly detected in AzTEC observations and lacks any counterpart in ACS bands [arXiv:0810.3108].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VLA 512 as a conservative z>3.5 starburst candidate: undetected in the shallow SCUBA region, weakly seen by AzTEC, absent from ACS images, and judged a good high-redshift starburst candidate.

quotes.quote: VLA 512: this object is undetected in a relatively shallow region of the SCUBA supermap. However, some signal is found at its position with S_ =3.2 mJy ( S/N=3.4 ) in an AzTEC map of GOODS-N (G. Wilson, private communication). This source has no counterpart at any ACS band, and is a good high redshift starburst candidate.

gmw_00008915.name: VLA 812
gmw_00008915.overview: The galaxy VLA 812 is a reliable candidate for a very high-redshift starburst [arXiv:0810.3108]. It is also identified as SMM J123553.26, which is bright in the mid-infrared and likely hosts a luminous, Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0903.4017].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SMM J123553.26 as a likely Compton-thick AGN, bright and continuum-dominated in the mid-infrared, but notes blending with a nearby radio source complicates interpretation.

quotes.quote: In particular, there is evidence that SMM J123553.26 is a Compton-thick AGN. This SMG is undetected in the X-ray but is bright and dominated by continuum emission in the mid-IR, thus displaying no mid-IR signatures of star formation (see Fig. spectra ). Extrapolating from its mid-IR spectrum, we estimate a hot dust luminosity L _ 6 m = 4 D_L^2 S_ 6 m _ 6 m / (1+z) , L_ 6 m 2.0 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 . Following the approach described by alexander08 we use the 0.5--2 keV observed luminosity of SMM J123553.26 alexander05 to derive a rest-frame 1.6--24.8 keV luminosity and convert it to an intrinsic X-ray luminosity, L_ 2-10 keV < 2 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 , assuming an X-ray spectral index, = 1.4 .

On the basis of the X-ray-- 6 m luminosity of this SMG, we find that it lies significantly below the X-ray-- 6 m luminosity relationship found for nearby AGN, suggesting that this source hosts a luminous Compton-thick AGN (e.g., alexander08 ). However, we note that this SMG lies very close to a second radio source ( 5 ), which complicates the deblending of the individual flux contributions in MIPS 24 m-imaging hainline09 . We detect a single continuum trace in the IRS 2D spectrum that, even though it coincides with the position of SMM J123553.26, likely corresponds to the blending of the mid-IR emission of both sources.

sources.summary: The paper argues VLA 812 is the true faint radio/IRAC counterpart of a previously listed SCUBA source, rejecting the brighter z=2.098 identification and classifying it as a reliable very high-redshift starburst candidate.

quotes.quote: VLA 812: this galaxy is very close to the edge of the SCUBA supermap, where the noise is fairly high. However, this source is listed in Chapman et al. (2005) as a SCUBA detection in photometry mode ( S_ 850 m =8.8 2.1 mJy), with a redshift of z=2.098 (the source is also detected by Perera et al. 2008 and Greve et al. 2008 at and with MAMBO and AzTEC, respectively). We find that a relatively bright z=2.098 galaxy is present in the area, but 2 '' away from the IRAC and radio position, which corresponds instead to a faint galaxy with ACS photometry of i_ AB =27.6 and z_ AB =27.3 , undetected at B and v band. We suggest that the Chapman et al. (2005) redshift identification does not correspond to the actual counterpart of the radio and submm emission. This radio source is a reliable candidate for a very high redshift starburst ( z 4 ).

gmw_00008916.name: VLA 966
gmw_00008916.overview: VLA 966 is a reliable starburst galaxy candidate at a redshift of approximately 4 [arXiv:0810.3108]. Its optical counterpart is classified as a B-band dropout with a red infrared SED and a radio flux density comparable to GN20 [arXiv:0810.3108].

sources.summary: The paper classifies VLA 966 as a reliable z 4 starburst candidate with a B-band-dropout counterpart, red IRAC SED, GN20-like radio flux density, and possible warm SED explaining its submillimeter nondetection.

quotes.quote: VLA 966: we place a 3 upper limit of S_ 850 m <8.1 mJy from the SCUBA supermap at the position of this source. Its optical counterpart is a B band dropout with z=26.6 and red IRAC SED, confirming that this is a reliable z 4 candidate. The VLA radio flux density of this source is comparable to that of GN20. The lack of submm emission could signal a warmer SED (see, e.g., Chapman et al. 2008b).

gmw_00008917.name: Arp 105S
gmw_00008917.overview: Arp 105S is a blue compact tidal dwarf galaxy that formed from collision debris and exhibits star-forming regions with a young burst of star formation [arXiv:1010.2201]. The galaxy also hosts a variable, inverted-spectrum radio source that may be a blazar [arXiv:2111.06141].

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 105S as a blue compact tidal dwarf galaxy with prominent star-forming regions, a young H-alpha burst, and SED fits favoring a 4 Myr collision-debris population with substantial attenuation.

quotes.quote: Arp 105S has the color and morphology of a blue compact dwarf galaxy. have shown that it is in fact a Tidal Dwarf Galaxy exhibiting prominent star--forming regions. The measured H equivalent width of 111 \ suggests a fairly young burst. Arp 105S is located towards the stellar halo of an elliptical galaxy Fig. 1 of boquien2009a .

The best fit (presented in Figure fig:fit-Arp-105S , with a probability of 67 to reproduce the observations) corresponds to a burst strength of =2 , an age of t=4 10^6 years, a very short time scale of =10^6 years (i.e. an instantaneous burst) and an attenuation of 1.6 magnitudes for the population formed in the collision debris. The H flux is however overestimated and the attenuation is larger than the one determined by . The ultraviolet flux is not perfectly adjusted by the model, which may be due to a slight remaining contamination by the elliptical galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1111+2841 as a variable, inverted-spectrum source with a peak above 8 GHz, needing further monitoring and cited as a possible blazar.

quotes.quote: The source J1111+2841 is quite variable at 22.3 GHz. The peak frequency is not less than 8 GHz, the current estimate is 10.7 GHz. This source has an inverted spectrum over a wide frequency range and needs further monitoring.
Some objects show variability of up to 25 and can be blazars (for example, J0906+4124 and J1111+2841).

gmw_00008918.name: R.A._ 2000 =12^ h 30^ m 26.1^ s , Dec_ 2000 =+41^ 42' 58''
gmw_00008918.overview: The distant galaxy at R.A. 2000 = 12h 30m 26.1s, Dec 2000 = +41d 42m 58s is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 0.124880 [arXiv:1606.01699]. It belongs to a galaxy system with a companion located approximately 140 kpc away and may host a gigahertz-peaked or compact steep radio source [arXiv:1606.01699].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a distant point-source galaxy near Arp 269 as an AGN-like steep-spectrum object, possibly a compact steep or gigahertz-peaked radio source.

quotes.quote: A distant galaxy at R.A._ 2000 =12^ h 30^ m 26.1^ s , Dec_ 2000 =+41^ 42' 58'' (z = 0.124880, based on the SDSS data), not related to the Arp 269 pair can be seen north from NGC 4485 in our 0.61, 1.49 and 4.86 GHz maps (as a point source). This object also belongs to a galaxy system, as there is another galaxy at virtually the same redshift (z=0.124696), located app. 140 kpc away. Unfortunately, neither emission from the second component, nor from any intergalactic structure was detected.
As this object is identified as an AGN, much flatter spectrum might be expected. This distant galaxy may host a Gigahertz-Peaked Source (GPS), or a Compact Steep Source (CSS, CSS ).

gmw_00008919.name: Mrk 374
gmw_00008919.overview: Mrk 374 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy that contains neutral hydrogen regions [arXiv:1906.07062]. Observations of its relatively faint H I emission line reveal a double-horn profile shape [arXiv:1906.07062].

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 374 as a faint H I detection whose BusyFit model required fixed parameters to recover a double-horn profile.

quotes.quote: Mrk 374: Mrk 374 has a relatively faint emission line (see Fig. \ batmen2 ) and we were only able to achieve a S/N of 4.7. The initial BusyFit profile converged into a box-shaped best fit with a value of 0 for the central trough amplitude. In order to generate a trough feature while keeping the slopes of the flanks accurate, we held fixed the initial best fits to the flank slopes ( b_ 1 and b_ 2 ) at 0.8, 0.5, respectively. We then fixed the central trough amplitude ( ) at 0.01 and polynomial degree ( n ) at 1.5, which allowed a double horned profile shape to converge. Mrk 374 was previously observed by with the 512 channel autocorrelator on the Nan cay telescope.

gmw_00008920.name: WKK 6801
gmw_00008920.overview: WKK 6801 is a very late-type spiral galaxy located east of the ACO 3627 cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0102200]. It features a highly inclined disk with a symmetric outer neutral hydrogen distribution and an asymmetric inner structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0102200].

sources.summary: The paper describes WKK 6801 as a very late-type spiral east of ACO 3627, with measured optical properties and a largely symmetric, unperturbed outer H i distribution.

quotes.quote: This H i detection in the field East coincides with the optical identification WKK 6801 (see Fig. fig:wkk6801_all a). It is located at a projected distance of 2.6 ^ o =3.5 Mpc from the cluster center. This spiral galaxy has an extinction-corrected B magnitude of B _ J ^ 0 =15.7, an extinction-corrected diameter of D _ 0 =47'' , and a radial velocity of 3519 7 km s ^ 1 (Woudt 1998).

The galaxy has a symmetric H i distribution in the outer parts of its highly inclined disk, whereas the inner part shows two asymmetric maxima with respect to the optical galaxy center. The main maximum is located in the south-west. The maximum surface density in the center is 38 M _ /pc ^ 2 . The profile corresponds to those of field spirals which do not show signs of perturbation in their H i content (Cayatte et al. 1994).

gmw_00008921.name: WKK 6489
gmw_00008921.overview: WKK 6489 is a spiral galaxy that exhibits a very weak detection in near-infrared bands [arXiv:astro-ph/0102200]. The galaxy displays an asymmetric neutral hydrogen gas distribution, which is likely caused by ram pressure stripping rather than gravitational interaction [arXiv:astro-ph/0102200].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WKK 6489 as a spiral with weak near-infrared detection and asymmetric H i morphology, arguing ram pressure is the most probable cause of its observed asymmetry.

quotes.quote: This H i detection in the south-eastern field matches with the optical identification of WKK 6489 (Fig. fig:wkk6489_all a). This spiral galaxy has an extinction-corrected B magnitude of B _ J ^ 0 =15.1, an extinction-corrected diameter of D _ 0 =61'' , and a velocity of 3794 58 km s ^ 1 (Woudt 1998). A very weak detection is seen in co-added 2MASS near-infrared (J,H,K-band) images, whose center lies only 2 '' NW of Woudt's optical position.

Since there is no optical companion, a gravitational interaction is unlikely. Moreover, its asymmetric H i gas distribution reminds that of NGC 4848 located in Coma (Bravo-Alfaro et al. 2000), a cluster which is very similar to ACO 3627 (see Sect. sec:coma ). N-body models simulating the gas disk of spiral galaxies falling into the Virgo cluster (Vollmer et al. 2001) show the importance of ram pressure stripping in this cluster. We expect this effect to be still more pronounced in bigger clusters as ACO 3627 or Coma. We therefore think that ram pressure is the most probable mechanism which caused the observed asymmetry.

gmw_00008922.overview: The object 0748+291 is a radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0505625]. Optical monitoring of the source has revealed clear intra-night micro-variability, including a brightness variation of approximately four percent and a peak-to-peak change near 0.05 magnitudes [arXiv:astro-ph/0505625].

sources.summary: The paper reports optical monitoring of 0748+291 and finds clear intra-night micro-variability, including about four percent brightness variation and a peak-to-peak change near 0.05 mag.

quotes.quote: Both the DLCs (QSO - star1 and QSO - star2) for the source 0748+291 are plotted in Fig. 1 which clearly indicate brightness variation of about 4 during the time UT 18.553 - 22.569 hr. As is clear from Fig. 1, the DLC for the comparison stars (star1 - star2) is quite steady whereas the DLCs for the source show appreciable variation during the period.

During the period UT 18.553 - 22.569 hr, rms variation of qso-comparisons are respectively 0.014 and 0.013 for qso - s1 and qso - s2. Thus the variance for DLCs of qso is more than 6 . This indicates detection of genuine micro variation in the source. If we consider peak-to-peak variation ( 0.05 mag) in the DLC of qso - star1, the variation is at a level of 10 , which further support the existence of micro-variability in the source.

gmw_00008923.overview: The object 0945+438 is a radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0505625]. Optical monitoring of the source has shown no intra-night variation, and historical data from 1978 to 1981 also revealed no variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0505625].

sources.summary: The paper reviews earlier mixed variability reports for 0945+438, then finds its February 2000 optical monitoring noisy and without significant intra-night variation.

quotes.quote: 0945+438 (US 995) Huang et al. (1990) studied the variability of this source using the data taken from Palomar plates for the period 1978 to 1981 ( 3.5 year) and did not find any variability in the source.

Standard variation of qso-comparisons are respectively 0.011 and 0.018 for qso - s1 and qso - s2, and hence the mean variance is less than 2 . The data appear a bit noisy. Hence, the source has not shown any significant variation during the night.

gmw_00008924.overview: The quasar 1029+329 is classified as a radio-quiet source that has been monitored for micro-variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0505625]. Although some earlier studies reported micro-variability, subsequent observations detected no micro-variability and no long-term variation over a span of two years [arXiv:astro-ph/0505625].

sources.summary: The paper notes prior micro-variability reports for 1029+329, but its DLC comparison found source variance near two and no micro-variability.

quotes.quote: 1029+329 (CSO 50) This source was observed earlier by other groups in six nights (Gopal-Krishna et al. 2003; Stalin et al. 2004, 2005) and the existence of micro-variability has been reported in two nights out of the six nights. However, no significant long term variation in the span of two years are reported.

The value for the DLC of the comparisons is estimated 0.011 which gets scaled to the same value as is close to 1; values for qso - s1 and qso - s2 are respectively 0.017 and 0.018. As the observed variance for the source is close to 2 . Hence, no micro variability is detected.

gmw_00008925.overview: The quasar 1101+319 is a radio-quiet source that exhibits long-term variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0505625]. While some observations have reported the presence of micro-variability on a single night, other monitoring data show no evidence of micro-variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0505625].

sources.summary: The paper says previous work found one micro-variable night for 1101+319, while its own noisy six-hour monitoring indicated no micro-variability in the source.

quotes.quote: 1101+319 (Ton 52) Stalin et al. (2004) observed this source in four nights for searching micro-variability and have reported the existence of micro-variability in one night, long term variation is also seen in the source.

From the plot it appears that the quality of data is relatively poor and data are a bit noisy. The scaled value of the comparison stars is estimated at 0.013. The values for DLCs of qso are respectively 0.019 and 0.020 for qso - s1 and qso - s2. The variance for the DLCs of qso is close to 2 , indicating the non existence of micro-variability in the source.

gmw_00008926.overview: The object 1225+317 is a radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0505625]. It exhibits possible micro-variability, showing brightness changes of approximately 0.08 to 0.09 magnitudes over a short monitoring interval [arXiv:astro-ph/0505625].

sources.summary: The paper presents new monitoring of 1225+317, finding possible micro-variation and a short interval with roughly 0.08 to 0.09 mag brightness changes.

quotes.quote: 1225+317 (b2 1225+317) There has been no systematic attempt earlier to study this source for micro-variability.

The value based on DLC of comparison stars is estimated at 0.011 which scales to 0.008 for source DLCs. DLCs for the source show standard deviation 0.016 and 0.012 for qso - s1 and qso - s2 respectively. The variance for the DLC of qso is thus close to 3 times the value, indicating the possible existence of micro variation. During the period UT 22.175 - 22.575 hr DLCs of qso - s1 and qso - s2 show brightness variation 0.081 mag and 0.094 mag respectively which is close to 10 level.

gmw_00008927.overview: The radio-quiet quasar 1252+020 has historically exhibited both micro-variability and long-term variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0505625]. However, observations conducted over a 3.5-hour period in March 2000 revealed an absence of micro-variation in the source [arXiv:astro-ph/0505625].

sources.summary: The paper notes earlier micro- and long-term variability for 1252+020, but its March 2000 observations found no micro-variation in the source.

quotes.quote: 1252+020 (q 1252+020) This source was observed for five nights during March 22, 1999 to March 18, 2002 by Stalin et al. (2004). Source has shown micro-variability in two nights and significant long term variability is also reported.

This source was observed by us for about 3.5 hours in the night of March 09, 2000. The DLCs are plotted in Fig. 9. The standard deviation based on DLC of comparison stars scaled to source DLC is estimated at 0.014. Standard deviations for the DLCs of qso are respectively 0.014 and 0.014 for qso-s1 and qso-s2, indicating the absence of micro variation in the source.

gmw_00008928.name: J162122.54+075808.4
gmw_00008928.overview: J162122.54+075808.4 is a broad-absorption line quasar located at a redshift of approximately 2.14 [arXiv:2105.02249]. Multi-epoch observations spanning nearly 15 years reveal highly correlated variability between an existing lower-velocity red absorption component and an emerging high-velocity blue absorption component [arXiv:2105.02249].

sources.summary: The paper studies BAL quasar J162122.54+075808.4, called 16, finding correlated variability between a high-velocity emerging blue BAL and a lower-velocity red BAL across seven epochs over about 15 years.

quotes.quote: Here, we present a detailed analysis of an interesting BAL quasar, J162122.54+075808.4 ( z_ em = 2.1394 ), hereafter refer to as 16, from our sample. This quasar shows emergence of a BAL component with UFO (and EHVO) velocities followed by a kinematic shift in the BAL profile implying acceleration along our line of sight. In addition to the emerged component, an already existing BAL at comparatively lower velocities (while still satisfying the definition of UFOs) also shows high variability. Interestingly, the variability of these two distinct components are highly correlated.

We have presented a detailed variability analysis of BAL quasar J162122.54+075808.4 ( = 2.1395) spectra obtained during 7 epochs spanning almost 15 years in the observer's time scale. This has allowed us to probe the absorption and emission line variability over time-scales of 3 months to 4.8 yrs in the quasar's rest frame. 16 was identified in the SDSS survey as a BAL-QSO with \ absorption at an ejection velocity of v_ e 15,400 km s ^ 1 , referred to as red-BAL in this work. We report the emergence of a new BAL component with associated absorption from , \ and Al iii ) with v_ e 37,500 km s ^ 1 in the second epoch spectrum, referred to as blue-BAL.

gmw_00008929.name: 3C 99
gmw_00008929.overview: The galaxy 3C 99 is an extended emission-line region source that features a compact radio structure [arXiv:1406.5528]. It exhibits high velocity-dispersion gradients, with regions of high velocity dispersion located along the paths of its radio jets [arXiv:1406.5528].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 99 as an EELR source with high velocity-dispersion gradients and possible jet disturbance along a compact radio structure.

quotes.quote: The few exceptions, such as 3C 99 and 4C 31.38, appear to have clear velocity dispersion gradients and are also the objects that reach the highest velocity dispersions.

The only objects for which there is sign of possible disturbance from the jets in our sample are 3C 99 and 4C 31.38, where there are regions of high velocity dispersion ( FWHM 1000 km s ^ 1 ) along the radio jet paths. These two objects have the smallest corresponding radio structures, 6 and 2 3 respectively, out of all objects with EELRs.
Studies of CSS objects have shown that most of them possess high velocity dispersion (FWHM 1000 km s ^ 1 ) components, similar to 3C 99 and 4C 31.38 e.g. 2009MNRAS.400..589H, 2013ApJ...772..138S .

gmw_00008930.name: 4C 31.38
gmw_00008930.overview: 4C 31.38 is a compact steep-spectrum radio source [arXiv:1406.5528]. Its extended emission-line region shows high velocity dispersions and signs of possible disturbance along the paths of its radio jets [arXiv:1406.5528].

sources.summary: The paper treats 4C 31.38 as a compact steep-spectrum radio source whose EELR shows high velocity dispersion and possible disturbance from radio jets.

quotes.quote: The few exceptions, such as 3C 99 and 4C 31.38, appear to have clear velocity dispersion gradients and are also the objects that reach the highest velocity dispersions.

The only objects for which there is sign of possible disturbance from the jets in our sample are 3C 99 and 4C 31.38, where there are regions of high velocity dispersion ( FWHM 1000 km s ^ 1 ) along the radio jet paths. These two objects have the smallest corresponding radio structures, 6 and 2 3 respectively, out of all objects with EELRs.

4C 31.38 is considered a compact-steep-spectrum (CSS) source, which is a young 10^ 3 10^ 5 yr; 1998PASP..110..493O,1999ASPC..193...79D radio source where the radio structure does not extend beyond the optical scale of the galaxy. Studies of CSS objects have shown that most of them possess high velocity dispersion (FWHM 1000 km s ^ 1 ) components, similar to 3C 99 and 4C 31.38 e.g. 2009MNRAS.400..589H, 2013ApJ...772..138S .

gmw_00008931.name: Arp 256
gmw_00008931.overview: Arp 256 is an interacting system of galaxies [arXiv:1601.02664]. It contains a highly active star-forming hinge clump that hosts an extremely luminous central star cluster or cluster complex [arXiv:1601.02664].

sources.summary: Arp 256 contains one of the interacting sample’s highest-SFR hinge clumps, with an unusually luminous HST-resolved central star cluster or cluster complex.

quotes.quote: As discussed below, the distance-limited 1.0 kpc sample lacks some of the most extreme regions (i.e., the hinge regions in Arp 240 and Arp 256). The Highest SFR Clumps A comparison of the top and bottom panels in Figures 7 and 8 shows that the most luminous clumps are found in the interacting galaxies. On a 2.5 kpc radius scale (Figure 7), no clumps are found in the spiral sample with SFR 2 M _  yr ^ 1 , while five such clumps are found in the interacting sample. These include three hinge clumps in Arp 240 (3.3, 6.2, and 9.0 M _  yr ^ 1 ), the `overlap region' between the two disks of the Antennae galaxies Arp 244 (4.8 M _  yr ^ 1 ), and the hinge clump in Arp 256 (2.0 M _  yr ^ 1 ).
Hubble Telescope images reveal extremely luminous star clusters (M _ I between 12.2 and 16.5) at the centers of the Arp 256, Arp 240, and NGC 2207 clumps smith14 .

If smoothed to the effective resolution of Arp 240 and Arp 256, these clusters would be blended together to produce a source similar in size and luminosity to the sources seen in those galaxies smith14 .

gmw_00008932.name: NGC 3646
gmw_00008932.overview: NGC 3646 is an interacting spiral galaxy with a small companion, NGC 3649 [arXiv:1601.02664]. It features two luminous star-forming clumps in its outer spiral arms near where the arms appear to cross [arXiv:1601.02664].

sources.summary: NGC 3646 has two luminous outer-arm clumps, a small companion, and crossing spiral-arm morphology suggesting that some control-sample star formation was interaction-triggered.

quotes.quote: These both lie in the outer spiral arms of NGC 3646, and have inferred SFRs of 1.2 and 1.5 M _  yr ^ 1 (see Figure 9). Although it is in our control sample of `normal' spiral galaxies, NGC 3646 does have a companion, NGC 3649, which has a blue luminosity of 1/24 ^ th that of NGC 3646 (from NED). This is just below the cut-off used by smith07, smith10 to eliminate interacting galaxies from the control sample. Thus NGC 3646 is interacting, though not as strongly as the systems in the SB T sample. Interestingly, the most luminous clumps in NGC 3646 lie near where spiral arms appear to cross, with a morphology suggestive of intersecting caustics.

gmw_00008933.overview: The source 3042 is a complex, blended object that is detected in both ACS and NIRCam imaging [arXiv:2208.07467]. It likely consists of two overlapping sources, including an elongated galaxy at a redshift of z=1.9938 [arXiv:2208.07467].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3042 as a complex blended NIRSpec source with changing ACS/NIRCam morphology, likely containing overlapping sources including a z=1.9938 elongated galaxy.
