sources.summary: The paper presents XID 403 as a CDF-S luminous X-ray source and TDE candidate, with coordinates, z=1.608 luminosity, and likely SMBH accretion indicating an AGN or TDE association.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The J2000 \ position is RA = 53.094719 , DEC = -27.694609 with a 1 positional uncertainty of 0.38 .

quotes.quote: Basic source properties and classification XID 403 was first reported by as a TDE candidate due to its \ brightening and previous non-detection. Its basic \ properties were then presented in the 7 Ms CDF-S source catalog Luo2017 . The J2000 \ position is RA = 53.094719 , DEC = -27.694609 with a 1 positional uncertainty of 0.38 .

Nevertheless, \ appears to be a luminous \ source. At z=1.608 , its rest-frame 0.5--7 keV luminosity reaches 2.1 10^ 43 erg s^ 1 over the entire 7 Ms exposure ( Luo2017 ; updated to the spectroscopic redshift). Such a large amount of power should originate from SMBH accretion. Thus \ is either an AGN or associated with TDEs.

gmw_00008997.name: UDS 24437 - UDS 23110 pair
gmw_00008997.overview: The UDS 24437 - UDS 23110 pair is a close galaxy pair located at a redshift of approximately 1.8 [arXiv:1903.11099]. This system exhibits two tidal arms, overlapping light between the galaxies, and a possible stellar bridge [arXiv:1903.11099].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the UDS 24437 - UDS 23110 pair as a z 1.8 close pair with two tidal arms, overlapping light, and possible bridge-like residual substructure.

quotes.quote: We note that all three pairs have | z|/(1+z) 0.025 and residual substructure consistent with theoretical predictions for tidal features produced by major mergers Duc2013 . We find a striking structural similarity between the features exhibited by UDS 24437 - UDS 23110 pair ( z 1.8 ) and the GDS 4608 - GDS 4529 pair ( z 1 ), such that each close pair hosts two tidal arms and an overlapping region between the host and companion galaxy. These broad and extended features are similar to those found in observations and simulations of major mergers between spheroid-dominated galaxies bell_dry_2006 . The residual substructure midway between each galaxy core in these two pairs may be a stellar bridge or may be an artifact of the simultaneous S ersic fitting of two galaxies in very close proximity.

gmw_00008998.name: GDS 4608 - GDS 4529 pair
gmw_00008998.overview: The GDS 4608 - GDS 4529 pair is a close galaxy pair located at a redshift of approximately z ~ 1 [arXiv:1903.11099]. The system hosts two tidal arms, an overlapping light region, and a potential stellar bridge between the galaxy cores [arXiv:1903.11099].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GDS 4608 - GDS 4529 pair as a z 1 close pair with two tidal arms, overlapping light, and a possible bridge or fitting artifact between cores.

quotes.quote: We note that all three pairs have | z|/(1+z) 0.025 and residual substructure consistent with theoretical predictions for tidal features produced by major mergers Duc2013 . We find a striking structural similarity between the features exhibited by UDS 24437 - UDS 23110 pair ( z 1.8 ) and the GDS 4608 - GDS 4529 pair ( z 1 ), such that each close pair hosts two tidal arms and an overlapping region between the host and companion galaxy. These broad and extended features are similar to those found in observations and simulations of major mergers between spheroid-dominated galaxies bell_dry_2006 . The residual substructure midway between each galaxy core in these two pairs may be a stellar bridge or may be an artifact of the simultaneous S ersic fitting of two galaxies in very close proximity.

gmw_00008999.name: GDN 14758 -GDN 14516
gmw_00008999.overview: GDN 14758 -GDN 14516 is an interacting close galaxy pair [arXiv:1903.11099]. Each galaxy in the pair displays two thin, sometimes clumpy tidal arms that are characteristic of disk-dominated galaxy interactions [arXiv:1903.11099].

sources.summary: The paper treats GDN 14758 -GDN 14516 as an interacting close pair whose galaxies show thin, sometimes clumpy tidal arms consistent with disk-dominated interaction signatures.

quotes.quote: We note that all three pairs have | z|/(1+z) 0.025 and residual substructure consistent with theoretical predictions for tidal features produced by major mergers Duc2013 .

In contrast, each galaxy in pair GDN 14758 -GDN 14516 appears to have two tidal arms which are thin and clumpy in some places. The characteristics of the latter tidal arms are likely the result of interacting disk-dominated galaxies.

gmw_00009000.name: GDS 14637
gmw_00009000.overview: GDS 14637 is a close-pair galaxy system located in close proximity to another galaxy [arXiv:1903.11099]. The system exhibits a single broad, arm-like residual structure that is consistent with a tidal arm from a post-merger event [arXiv:1903.11099].

sources.summary: The paper describes GDS 14637 as a close-pair system with one plausible interaction feature: a single broad arm-like residual consistent with a nearby post-merger.

quotes.quote: GDS 14637 has a single arm-like residual that is similar in appearance to the broad and extended tidal arms discussed above.

This feature is similar in appearance to the single arm-like structure we extract from one of the two example CANDELS pairs in which only one galaxy has a plausible interaction signature (GDS 14637 ; Fig. collage_ptf ). As such, GDS 14637 is also consistent with a post-merger in close proximity to another galaxy.

gmw_00009001.name: GDS 16440
gmw_00009001.overview: GDS 16440 is a galaxy featuring an inner spiral pattern that extends to a radius of 5 to 10 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1903.11099]. It displays two diffuse, arm-like features extending beyond 25 kiloparsecs, which are consistent with tidal signatures from an interaction with a nearby compact companion galaxy [arXiv:1903.11099].

sources.summary: The paper describes GDS 16440 as having two diffuse arm-like features, an inner spiral pattern, and a nearby compact companion, favoring tidal-interaction signatures over spiral extensions.

quotes.quote: In contrast, GDS 16440 shows two arm-like features, where one extends outward away from the host and is aligned with a nearby (compact) galaxy (at right) with a spectroscopic redshift difference of only | cz| 100 km s^ 1 from GDS 16440 . This picture is consistent with theoretical merger simulations e.g., Figure 6 from Duc2013 of a disk-dominated galaxy experiencing a major merger with a nearby compact companion. We note that GDS 16440 has a clear inner spiral pattern that extends to a radius of R 5 -10 kpc. Despite its point-like appearance suggesting a plausibly interaction-fueld AGN, the companion has no known X-ray detection to corroborate this suggestion. Finally, we acknowledge the possibility that the extracted arm-lke features of GDS 16440 may be extensions of the inner spiral structure, yet, their diffuse appearance and physical extent out to R>25 kpc is much more consistent with tidal interaction signatures.

gmw_00009002.name: GDN 19863
gmw_00009002.overview: GDN 19863 is a galaxy identified as a plausible post-merger system [arXiv:1903.11099]. It features two narrow, clumpy tidal-tail residuals that each extend approximately 10 kpc from the galactic center, with one tail being much fainter than the other [arXiv:1903.11099].

sources.summary: The paper treats GDN 19863 as a plausible post-merger, extracting two tidal-tail residuals about 10 kpc long, one fainter than the other, and comparing them to simulated clumpy tails.

quotes.quote: Second, GDN 19863 appears to have two tidal-tail residuals, each extending 10 kpc from the galaxy center. One is much fainter than the other. Multiple faint tidal tails are found briefly after coalescence in simulations of major mergers.
At t=0.47 Gyr , we extract two narrow tail features with clumpy visual appearance in the deep image, which resemble the plausible post-merger signature we extract for GDN 19863 .

gmw_00009003.name: MMJ1540+6605
gmw_00009003.overview: MMJ1540+6605 is a flat-spectrum source that lacks a spectroscopic redshift [arXiv:astro-ph/0511117]. Millimeter flux density monitoring shows that the source exhibits considerable variability on timescales of months, and X-ray observations hint at intrinsic absorption [arXiv:astro-ph/0511117].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MMJ1540+6605 as one of three variable flat-spectrum sources, detected across Spitzer bands and lacking a spectroscopic redshift, with X-ray hardness suggesting intrinsic absorption.

quotes.quote: Their mm flux densities were monitored with MAMBO pointed observations, showing considerable variability on timescales of months for the three brighter sources MMJ1540+6605, MMJ1541+6622 and MMJ1543+6621 (Fig. FluxTime ). Those were also observed simultaneously at 1 mm and 3 mm with the Plateau de Bure interferometer (PdBI), showing them to have spectra declining with frequency (Fig. SEDs ).
MMJ1540+6605, MMJ1541+6622 and MMJ1543+6621 were detected in all bands, while MMJ1541+6630 was not detected in any band. Spectroscopic redshifts for MMJ1540+6605 and MMJ1541+6630 are not available.

Three of the sources show absorption by N_ H 1-4 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 , comparable to the galactic value of 2.4 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 toward the Abell 2125 field estimated from the Leiden/Dwingeloo survey Hartmann97 . For MMJ1540+6605 we estimate N_ H (2 0.4) 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 , which hints at intrinsic absorption.

gmw_00009004.name: MMJ1541+6630
gmw_00009004.overview: MMJ1541+6630 is a high-redshift submillimeter galaxy that hosts a radio-quiet active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0511117]. Its far-infrared emission is dominated by extreme starburst activity rather than heating from the central quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0511117].

sources.summary: The paper classifies MMJ1541+6630 as a high-redshift submillimeter galaxy hosting a radio-quiet quasar, with FIR emission dominated by extreme star formation rather than AGN heating.

quotes.quote: MMJ1541+6630 has a faint 1.4 GHz radio counterpart with a flux density typical of the MAMBO or SCUBA galaxies. The 1.4 GHz flux density measurement and the upper limits at 4.9 and 8 GHz indicate a falling radio spectrum, consistent with a typical synchrotron spectrum with 0.7 ( S_ ^ ). The ratio between the 1.4 and 250 GHz flux densities is consistent with a typical starburst galaxy SED at z 3 .

The inferred X-ray luminosity is 80 times higher than that expected from the correlations between X-ray, radio and mm luminosities for starforming galaxies. This X-ray excess reveals the presence of a radio-quiet AGN, which is also not unusual for (sub)mm galaxies: suggest that 40 of the (sub)mm galaxies host AGN, which however contribute less then 20 to the IR bolometric luminosity.

The Spitzer measurements of MMJ1541+6630 rule out a significant contribution to the dust heating by an AGN, except if this source were a heavily obscured z 5 quasar with moderate star forming activity. With these uncertainties in mind, we find the properties of MMJ1541+6630 consistent with typical X-ray luminous (sub-)mm galaxies such as those studied by Alexander et al. (2004). In the following we shall assume that MMJ1541+6630 is a quasar with its FIR emission dominated by star formation.

gmw_00009005.name: MMJ1541+6622
gmw_00009005.overview: MMJ1541+6622 is a radio-loud quasar located at a redshift of 1.382 [arXiv:astro-ph/0511117]. It is a variable source that exhibits considerable millimeter flux density variations on timescales of months [arXiv:astro-ph/0511117].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MMJ1541+6622 as a variable flat-spectrum radio-loud quasar, detected in Spitzer and ROSAT data, with a spectroscopic redshift from broad C III] and Mg II lines.

quotes.quote: Their mm flux densities were monitored with MAMBO pointed observations, showing considerable variability on timescales of months for the three brighter sources MMJ1540+6605, MMJ1541+6622 and MMJ1543+6621 (Fig. FluxTime ). Those were also observed simultaneously at 1 mm and 3 mm with the Plateau de Bure interferometer (PdBI), showing them to have spectra declining with frequency (Fig. SEDs ).

MMJ1540+6605, MMJ1541+6622 and MMJ1543+6621 were detected in all bands, while MMJ1541+6630 was not detected in any band. Spectroscopic redshifts for MMJ1540+6605 and MMJ1541+6630 are not available. report that MMJ1541+6622 has a redshift of 1.382 based on the C III ] and Mg II lines. These broad lines indicate that the object is a quasar.
We found X-ray counterparts for MMJ1540+6605, MMJ1541+6622 and MMJ1543+6621 in the ROSAT 1WGA catalog WGAcat .

gmw_00009006.name: MMJ1543+6621
gmw_00009006.overview: MMJ1543+6621 is a bright, radio-loud quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0511117]. It exhibits a non-thermal millimeter spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0511117] and displays considerable flux density variability on timescales of months [arXiv:astro-ph/0511117].

sources.summary: The paper treats MMJ1543+6621 as one of three bright variable flat-spectrum radio-loud quasars, detected in Spitzer bands with a non-thermal millimeter spectrum.

quotes.quote: Their mm flux densities were monitored with MAMBO pointed observations, showing considerable variability on timescales of months for the three brighter sources MMJ1540+6605, MMJ1541+6622 and MMJ1543+6621 (Fig. FluxTime ). Those were also observed simultaneously at 1 mm and 3 mm with the Plateau de Bure interferometer (PdBI), showing them to have spectra declining with frequency (Fig. SEDs ).
MMJ1540+6605, MMJ1541+6622 and MMJ1543+6621 were detected in all bands, while MMJ1541+6630 was not detected in any band.

The Spitzer SEDs of MMJ1541+6622 and MMJ1543+6621 can well be fit with the average SEDs of radio loud quasars Elvis94 shifted to the spectroscopically measured redshifts. The 95 to 230 GHz spectral index measured with the PdBI also suggests a non-thermal spectrum, which is however steeper by 0.5 -1 than the radio spectrum between 1 and 95 GHz.

gmw_00009007.name: A2744-ID02
gmw_00009007.overview: A2744-ID02 is a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:1607.03808] that appears as a large, edge-on disk [arXiv:2310.02500]. It exhibits highly dust-obscured star formation [arXiv:1607.03808] and may be in the early phases of a merger with a compact companion to its west [arXiv:2310.02500].

sources.summary: The paper identifies A2744-ID02 as a high-redshift ALMA source with faint, red, clumpy NIR emission and a large mm-to-NIR offset, making it useful for resolving obscured star formation.

quotes.quote: The only source where a counterpart match is challenging is A2744-ID02, as the ALMA detection sits in a region where emission is quite faint even in the F160W filter and undetected in the bluer bands, exhibiting a highly dust extincted spectral energy distribution. As seen in Figure fig:counterparts , the counterpart of A2744-ID02 appears to be extended in the East-West axis, with 2--3 faint NIR objects ("clumps") lying on either side of the extended 1.1 mm emission and fainter "bridge" emission extending both in between these clumps as well as toward a bright compact peak of NIR emission 1 5 to the west of the ALMA source. All of the emission is red in NIR colors, similar to the other ALMA-FFs counterparts. The resolved ALMA emission appears to be elongated roughly coincident with a suppression in the F160W emission, suggesting that the ALMA source may arise from a dusty region that divides the faint F160W clumps, which may represent less-obscured regions from a single extended object with strong and variable extinction.

For A2744-ID02, the demagnified offset to the brightest peak, assuming z=2 , is 0 48 0 04 including only the uncertainties in the position of both NIR and ALMA emissions. This offsets corresponds to 4.1 0.3 kpc at z=2 similar than the spatial offset of 4 kpc measured in GN20 at z=4.05 , one of the best studied SMGs at high redshift Hodge2015 .

For A2744-ID02, the offset between peak mm and NIR emission is substantially larger, 0 5 or 4 kpc in the source plane. The latter source provides an intriguing case to study spatially resolved highly obscured star-formation activity at high redshift.

sources.summary: The paper treats 9018* as an ALMA-detected galaxy needing custom deblending, appearing as an edge-on disk and possibly an early-stage merger with heavy dust.

quotes.quote: NIR counterparts for all ALCS sources are identified. However, in two cases (UNCOVER IDs 9018, 24143; denoted throughout with an asterisk), visual inspection reveals the galaxies are shredded into multiple sources in the UNCOVER catalog as a result of the detection deblending thresholds, which were optimized to detect faint, high-redshift sources. As the photometry and segmentation maps are used for simultaneous modeling or masking of neighboring objects in the structural modeling (see Sec. sec:methods ), for these two objects we instead perform an alternative detection (using SEP; Barbary18 ) with less aggressive deblending parameters ( KERNEL=3.5 pixel FWHM Gaussian, MINAREA=3 pixels, THRESH=0.8 , DEBLEND NTHRESH=8 , DEBLEND CONT=0.0001 , CLEAN=Y , CLEAN PARAM=1.0 ), and use the resulting segmentation maps and photometric parameters (derived using the same photometric pipeline as except for the parameter changes) for structural modeling of these two galaxies.

Visual inspection (i.e., Figs. fig:sampleRGB fig:SW_LW_fits ) reveals galaxies that appear to be large disks, both edge-on (9018*, 16790, 26131, 43148; see also Kokorev23 for other analysis of 9018*/ID319, which Kokorev23 report may be in the early phases of a merger with the compact companion to the West given its undisturbed disk morphology) and more face-on (24143*; see also Wu23 ).

The -brightest galaxy (9018*; grey) does not follow this trend. However, its high total dust content and edge-on geometry render it optically thick even in the rest-frame NIR, as evidenced by the noticeable dust patches and lanes in the F444W imaging and the extremely high A_ V 4 across the galaxy reported by Kokorev23 . The extremely thick dust may conceal an even more compact stellar distribution than the F444W light, and thus the observed size ratio may be milder than the intrinsic ratio between the stellar mass distribution and rest-frame optical light. If the LW size is higher relative to the stellar size in this galaxy compared to the others, this could explain why its size ratio is higher than we might expect for its dust content.

gmw_00009008.overview: The galaxy 15422 is a confirmed gravitational lens system located at a spectroscopic redshift of 0.62 [arXiv:1104.0931]. Mass modeling of the lens galaxy indicates a velocity dispersion of 305 km/s and an Einstein radius of 1.20 arcsec [arXiv:1104.0931].

sources.summary: The paper treats 15422 as the confirmed lens system, giving the lens-galaxy redshift, B04 velocity dispersion, and an SIE model with a 1.20 arcsec Einstein radius.

quotes.quote: 15422 : This is the only system from our sample which is known to be a confirmed lens system hereafter, B04 blakeslee04 . The lens galaxy has a spectroscopic redshift of 0.62 and a photometric redhift of 2.4 is estimated for the background source. The mass modelling of B04 suggests that the velocity dispersion of the lens galaxy is 305 km s ^ 1 .

A SIE model fits reasonably to this lens system with a reduced ^2 of 3. The Einstein radius is found to be 1.20 arcsec. The mass model suggests an ellipticity of 0.60 with a position angle of 64 deg. This is consistent with the ellipticity and the position angle measured from the distribution of light from the lens galaxy.

gmw_00009009.overview: The system 34244 is modeled as a cusp gravitational lens candidate with a clearly detected counter-image [arXiv:1104.0931]. It is classified as a luminous infrared galaxy with blue, arc-like features that may represent star-forming shells [arXiv:1104.0931].

sources.summary: The paper models 34244 as a cusp lens candidate with a counter-image, but radio and infrared detections imply LIRG-level emission and possible star-forming shells.

quotes.quote: 34244 : In this system, a cusp configuration is likely and a counter-image is clearly detected. As before, we test three possible mass models namely, SIE, SIS+shear and SIE+shear. The SIE model suggests an ellipticity of 0.52 with a position angle of 31 deg. The Einstein radius is found to be 0.40 arcsec.

We inferred the total infrared (TIR) luminosity of 34244 both from the radio flux using the radio-IR correlation , and from the 24 m flux using the templates of char01 to convert it to TIR. Hence, the TIR inferred from either the 1.4 GHz radio flux of 80 Jy kellermann08 or the 24 m flux of 315.76 Jy papo04 , is 1--5 10^ 11 L_ . This is in the range of the TIR luminosity of the luminous infrared galaxies (LIRGs).
Thus, in accordance with their fluffy and not very sharp appearance the blue arc-like features in 34244 could well be star-forming shells instead of being lensed images of a background galaxy.

gmw_00009010.overview: The galaxy 40173 is a candidate gravitational lens system that exhibits a blue, arc-like feature [arXiv:1104.0931]. This structure may represent a partial Einstein ring, a double lens, or a site of star formation within the galaxy [arXiv:1104.0931].

sources.summary: The paper describes 40173 as a possible partial Einstein ring or double lens, with an SIS model reproducing the two-image separation but spectroscopy needed.

quotes.quote: 40173 : Owing to the circular symmetry of the surface brightness distribution, this candidate could be an example of a partial Einstein ring or a double lens system. Since the lens-galaxy light subtraction may not be accurate, the residual arm-like structure could be misleading. Instead, we choose a two-image configuration. An SIS model, centered at the position of the lens galaxy, can be tested using the peak positions of two images as constraints. An Einstein radius of 0.59 arcsec with a symmetric isothermal density profile reproduces the image separation.

However, fitting the image separation alone can not, necessarily, indicate gravitational lensing in the system. Spectra of the lensed images will be crucial in concluding whether blue arc-like feature is a site of star-formation at the lens redshift or a lensed arc. Note that, if this indeed is a lens system then size of the Einstein radius can give a very accurate measure of the mass within the Einstein radius, irrespective of the assumed density profile.

gmw_00009011.overview: The galaxy 43242 is a spiral galaxy that acts as a strong gravitational lens system [arXiv:1104.0931]. It appears to be in a fold configuration with an Einstein radius of approximately 0.57 arcseconds [arXiv:1104.0931].

sources.summary: The paper models 43242 as a fold-configuration lens candidate whose SIE/SIS+shear fits give a 0.57 arcsec Einstein radius and consistent bulge ellipticity.

quotes.quote: 43242 : This lens system appears to be in a fold configuration. We assumed that the two southernmost images are merging to form an arc-like feature. With the positions of four images, following models are tested.

Both the models fit equally well with a reduced ^2 2 . The Einstein radius of the lens galaxy is about 0.57 arcsec with an ellipticity of 0.4 at a position angle of 20 deg or alternatively, a shear with 13 per cent strength at the same position angle. The panels of D in Fig. fig:modres show the critical curves for an SIE mass model on the right whereas the observed and model-predicted image positions (green circles) on the left. The ellipticity of the bulge of the lens galaxy is 0.32 with a position angle of 25 deg which is consistent with the ellipticity of the SIE model. Using this ellipticity as a constraint in a SIE+shear model results in equally good fit.

gmw_00009012.overview: The galaxy 46446 has been modeled as a fold gravitational lens candidate with a small Einstein radius [arXiv:1104.0931]. However, its central light concentration and morphology are inconsistent with the high mass concentration and ellipticity required for it to act as a lens [arXiv:1104.0931].

sources.summary: The paper models 46446 as a fold lens candidate with small Einstein radii, but the extreme shear or ellipticity and light distribution make lensing doubtful.

quotes.quote: 46446 : This candidate indicates a fold configuration, more obvious in the bottom-right residual image of Fig. fig:sample . We model the lens system with standard isothermal models. The SIS+shear and SIE models for this system suggest Einstein radii of 0.42 arcsec and 0.31 arcsec for the lens galaxy, respectively. The reduced ^2 for these models is 2 . The models need either high shear of 30 per cent at a position angle of 76 deg or a high ellipticity of 0.85 at a position angle of 79 deg.

The high ellipticity of the lens from the mass model is inconsistent with the morphology and optical properties of the central galaxy. The concentration of light in the central regions of the lens galaxy does not suggest a high concentration of mass which is required for it to be a lens. In addition, statistically, we expect more red galaxies with star-forming shells than red lens galaxies.

gmw_00009013.name: Draco
gmw_00009013.overview: Draco is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located at a distance of approximately 76 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2305.13415]. The galaxy's dark matter density profile favors a cusped inner slope [arXiv:2305.13415].

sources.summary: The paper targets Draco for Subaru-IRCS ALP dark-matter decay searches, gives its central coordinates and distance, finds a cusped DM profile, and predicts high differential D-factor sensitivity.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Draco  (central sky coordinates _ 2000 = 17:20:12, _ 2000 =+ 57:54:55, distance  D_ =76 6  kpc)

quotes.quote: Here our main targets are Draco  (central sky coordinates _ 2000 = 17:20:12, _ 2000 =+ 57:54:55, distance  D_ =76 6  kpc) and Ursa Major II  ( _ 2000 = 08:51:30, _ 2000 =+ 63:07:48, D_ =32 4  kpc) .

In the analysis of the differential D factor in this paper following Hayashi:2020jze,Hayashi:2022wnw , both Draco and Ursa Major II favor a cusped inner slope of their DM density profiles. Using their resultant DM density profiles, we calculate _ D distributions for Draco and Ursa Major II. Focusing on the center of the dSphs, the values of _ 10 ( _ D/( GeV cm^ 2 sr^ 1 )= 22.60^ +0.27 _ 0.26 for Draco and = 22.50^ +1.02 _ 0.52 for Ursa Major II, respectively, at arcsec .

By concentrating on the decay of dark matter into two particles, one of which is a photon, in the Draco and Ursa Major II dwarf spheroidal galaxies, we demonstrated that an (1) nights observation by IRCS in Subaru Telescope yields sensitivity for the decay rate at a world-record level. In terms of axion-like particles, this surpasses the star cooling bounds in the mass range of 1 eV to 2 eV, aligning with various hints related to the cosmic-infrared background and theoretical predictions of some well-motivated models. Our study strongly advocates for the actual observations of the corresponding dSphs and offers an applicable approach for the indirect detection of dark matter using high-energy and high-angular-resolution detectors.

gmw_00009014.name: QSO B0254-3327B
gmw_00009014.overview: QSO B0254-3327B is a broad-absorption line quasar located at a redshift of z=1.863 [arXiv:2310.03588]. It hosts a highly ionized outflow system with a velocity of -3200 km/s, which may contribute to active galactic nucleus feedback [arXiv:2310.03588].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes QSO B0254-3327B/Q0254-334 as a z=1.863 quasar with a BAL outflow, detecting highly ionized absorption and finding possible AGN-feedback relevance.

quotes.quote: We have identified a broad absorption line (BAL) outflow in the HST/STIS spectrum of the quasar QSO B0254-3327B at velocity v=-3200 km s ^ 1 . The outflow has absorption troughs from ions such as Ne viii , Na ix , Si xii , and Ne v . We also report the first detection of S xiv absorption troughs, implying very high ionization. Via measurement of the ionic column densities, photoionization analysis, and determination of the electron number density of the outflow, we found the kinetic luminosity of the outflow system to be up to 1 of the quasar's Eddington luminosity, or 2 of the bolometric luminosity, making it a potential contributor to AGN feedback.

Q0254-334 (J2000; RA=02:56:47.84, DEC=-33:15:26.16, z=1.863) was observed with HST/STIS on 17 February, 2001 as part of the program SNAP 8681, and on 4 March, 2001 as part of the program GO 8569, with the G230L and G140L gratings respectively. After retrieving the data from the Mikulski Archive for Space Telescopes, we have co-added the two spectra, and corrected the combined spectrum for galactic reddening and extinction with E(B-V)=0.0205 2011ApJ...737..103S , and the extinction model by 1999PASP..111...63F . The co-added and dereddened spectrum of the two observations, covering observed wavelengths 1138.6--3156.6 , is shown in Figure  fig:fluxplot . We have identified a broad absorption line (BAL) outflow system at v=-3200 km s ^ 1 , with its ionic absorption troughs marked by red vertical lines in Figure  fig:fluxplot .

gmw_00009015.name: NGC4325
gmw_00009015.overview: NGC 4325 is a galaxy group with a disturbed core containing two small X-ray cavities [arXiv:1411.6040]. The core features optical filaments aligned with X-ray emission [arXiv:1411.6040], and the central galaxy has experienced a prolonged initial episode of star formation followed by more recent events [arXiv:1410.2294].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC4325's chemical evolution and galactic wind, finding oxygen-rich winds but concluding the central galaxy alone cannot explain the group's metal enrichment.

quotes.quote: We adopted the code of hereafter LM03 and LM04 LM03,LM04 to simulate the chemical evolution of the galaxy NGC4325. It assumes nucleosynthesis and takes the role played by stars of all mass ranges and by supernovae (SNe) of different types (II, Ia) into account for the energetics and for the chemical enrichment of the galaxy. This allows us to follow the evolution of several chemical elements (H, D, He, C, N, O, Mg, Si, S, Ca and Fe) in detail inside and outside the galaxy. The model was adopted for NGC4325, following the formulations of hereafter PM02 PM02 and hereafter PM04 and PM06 PM04, PM06 .

In NGC 4325, the wind starts at a galactic age between 0.22 and 0.40 Gyr (depending on the model), but unlike PM models the SF is not halted after the onset of the wind since the stellar population analysis indicate a very prolonged initial episode of SF followed by other ones until very recent times.

Comparing these values to the ones derived by a chemical evolution model presented in Table tab:evmod2 , and assuming that all the iron and oxygen synthesized in the central galaxy of the group NGC4325 during its lifetime were released into the IGM (these mass values are then upper limits), the winds from the central galaxy alone play a minor role in the metal enrichment of the IGM.

sources.summary: NGC 4325 is described as a disturbed group core with two small cavities, optical filaments aligned with X-ray emission, a central iron abundance drop, and missing iron mass.

quotes.quote: NGC 4325 NGC 4325 is a group with a disturbed core, and has two small X-ray cavities Russell07 . There are also optical filaments in the core of NGC 4325, which appear to be extended in the same direction as the X-ray emission and are contained within the innermost 15 kpc McDonald12 . An iron abundance drop is evident in the central spectral bin of this group (Fig. fig:abundprofspossible ). We calculate the mass of ``missing'' iron to be 9.8 10 ^ 4 M _ , with respect to 0.8 times the Solar abundance.
Unfortunately, measurements for the dust mass contained in the core of this group appear to not exist in the literature to date.

gmw_00009016.name: LEDA 753342
gmw_00009016.overview: LEDA 753342 is an optically faint, low-mass ring galaxy [arXiv:2209.05605]. It is a gas-rich system that either does not appear to experience ram pressure stripping or is only beginning to feel its effects [arXiv:2209.05605].

sources.summary: The paper treats LEDA 753342 as a gas-rich ring galaxy near the Hydra substructure, probably not strongly stripped and possibly only beginning to feel the cluster environment.

quotes.quote: Instead, it was suggested that NGC 3312, NGC 3314a, and a gas-rich ring galaxy, LEDA 753342 (first noted by Wirth80 ) may be part of a foreground cluster substructure: NGC 3312 and NGC 3314a have almost the same systemic velocity, and tidal interactions could be responsible for their disturbed outer \ morphology.

LEDA 753342 : based on the , this galaxy is either just beginning to experience ram pressure, or does not appear to at all (Figure fig:fdpL ). The galaxy is optically faint, and relatively narrow in \ and thus expected to be very low mass.

Given the strength of the ram pressure felt by NGC 3312 and NGC 3314a, the similar velocity of LEDA 753342 and its spatial proximity is likely a coincidence, rather than the three galaxies belonging to the same cluster substructure or being at the same distance. However, future X-ray observations, combined with simulations, are needed to say for certain whether bulk motions of substructure within the cluster could protect LEDA 753342 from the harshest effects of RPS.

gmw_00009017.name: J0644 + 1043
gmw_00009017.overview: J0644 + 1043 is a giant radio galaxy with a projected linear size of over 0.7 Mpc, hosted by an elliptical galaxy at a redshift of 0.0488 [arXiv:2405.14668]. It features an S-shaped radio morphology with 100 kpc long twin jets, though its structure does not strictly conform to traditional FR I or FR II categories [arXiv:2405.14668].

sources.summary: The paper reports J0644 + 1043 as a 0.71 Mpc S-shaped giant radio galaxy with a redshifted elliptical host, massive black hole, rare twin jets, and ambiguous FR type.

quotes.quote: Orla 171, 30-244 Krakow, Poland Lyuba Slavcheva-Mihova Institute of Astronomy and NAO, Bulgarian Academy of Sciences, 72 Tsarigradsko Chaussee Blvd., 1784 Sofia, Bulgaria We report the discovery of an S-shaped morphology of the radio galaxy J0644 + 1043 imaged with a 30 sensitive 525 MHz broadband (band 3 + 4) uGMRT map. Dedicated spectroscopic observations of the host galaxy carried out with the 2-meter Rozhen telescope yielded a redshift of 0.0488, giving a projected linear size of the peculiar radio structure of over 0.7 Mpc. This giant radio galaxy is powered by a black hole of mass 4.1 ^ +9.39 _ 2.87 10^8 , from which vicinity emanate well-collimated and knotty jets, each 100 kpc long. The entire radio structure, presumably due to the effective jet precession, is less than 50 Myr old, has a power of 6 10^ 24 at 1.4 GHz and the observed morphological characteristics do not strictly conform to the traditional FR I or FR II categories.

This RG is hosted by an elliptical galaxy, WISEA J064408.04 + 104341.5 (RA: 06 44 08 04 Dec: 10 43 41 40). It is also listed as a ``zone of avoidance" galaxy by galactic latitude b= 3 2 zone.of.avoid and is located behind the dark region of the Cone Nebula, where the Gunn -band galactic extinction is 0.66 magnitude Galactic.Extinction.S .

Our dedicated uGMRT observations of the RG J0644 + 1043 and the 2-m Rozhen optical telescope detection of its host galaxy revealed previously unknown properties of this source. (i) Our spectroscopic observations revealed the redshift of the host galaxy at z=0.0488, which is five times smaller than the photometric redshift previously used in the literature.

gmw_00009018.name: Dwingeloo 1
gmw_00009018.overview: Dwingeloo 1 is a massive barred spiral galaxy located at a distance of approximately 3 Mpc [arXiv:astro-ph/0006199]. It is a member of the IC342/Maffei group of galaxies and suffers from heavy foreground obscuration in the optical spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0006199].

sources.summary: The paper presents Dwingeloo 1 as a newly found, heavily obscured barred spiral detected in the Dwingeloo survey, confirmed optically, and characterized as a massive nearby galaxy in the IC342/Maffei group.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The optimized position of ( ,b)=(138 5,-0 1) coincided with a very low surface brightness feature on the Palomar Sky Survey plate of 2 2 , detected earlier by Hau in his optical galaxy search of the northern Galactic/SuperGalactic Plane crossing ( Sect. optsear ).

quotes.quote: The most exciting discovery was the barred spiral galaxy Dwingeloo 1 . This galaxy candidate was detected early on in the survey through a strong signal (peak intensity of 1.4 Jy) at the very low redshift of v = 110 \ in the spectra of four neighboring pointings, suggestive of a galaxy of large angular extent. The optimized position of ( ,b)=(138 5,-0 1) coincided with a very low surface brightness feature on the Palomar Sky Survey plate of 2 2 , detected earlier by Hau in his optical galaxy search of the northern Galactic/SuperGalactic Plane crossing ( Sect. optsear ). Despite foreground obscuration of about 6 ^ m in the optical, follow-up observations in the V , R and I band at the INT (La Palma) confirmed this galaxy candidate as a barred, possibly grand-design spiral galaxy of type SBb of 4.2 x 4.2 arcmin ( Fig. Dw1 ). Dwingeloo 1 has been the subject of much follow-up observations (optical: Loan , Buta McCall ; HI-synthesis: Burton ; CO observations: Kuno , Li , Tilanus Burton ; X-ray: Reynolds ).

To summarize, it is a massive barred spiral, with rotation velocity of 130 , implying a dynamical mass of roughly one-third the mass of the Milky Way. Its approximate distance of 3 Mpc and angular location place it within the IC342/Maffei group of galaxies.

gmw_00009019.name: SDSS J023011.28+005913.6
gmw_00009019.overview: SDSS J023011.28+005913.6 is a broad-absorption line quasar at a redshift of z=2.47 [arXiv:1509.02842]. The galaxy exhibits two emerging, high-velocity broad absorption troughs that show rapid variability in its rest-frame [arXiv:1509.02842].

sources.summary: The paper reports rapidly emerging broad absorption in the z=2.47 quasar SDSS J023011.28+005913.6, identifying two high-velocity troughs and favoring structured absorber motion after ruling out simple bulk-motion models.

quotes.quote: We present the discovery of the highest velocity \ broad absorption line to date in the z =2.47 quasar SDSS J023011.28+005913.6, hereafter J0230. In comparing the public DR7 and DR9 spectra of J0230, we discovered an emerging broad absorption trough outflowing at 60,000 , which we refer to as trough A. In pursuing follow up observations of trough A, we discovered a second emergent \ broad absorption trough outflowing at 40,000 , namely trough B. In total, we collected seven spectral epochs of J0230 that demonstrate emergent and rapidly ( 10 days in the rest-frame) varying broad absorption. We investigate two possible scenarios that could cause these rapid changes: bulk motion and ionization variability.

Given our multi-epoch data, we were able to rule out some simple models of bulk motion, but have proposed two more realistic models to explain the variability of both troughs. Trough A is likely an augmented `crossing disk' scenario with the absorber moving at 10,000 < v ( ) < 18,000 . Trough B can be explained by a flow-tube feature travelling across the emitting region at 8,000 < v ( ) < 56,000 .

gmw_00009020.name: J0100 0200
gmw_00009020.overview: J0100 0200 is a Seyfert galaxy that was previously classified as a flat-spectrum object [arXiv:1801.03519]. However, its in-band spectral index suggests it may instead be a steep-spectrum, low-power compact steep spectrum candidate with an unresolved core [arXiv:1801.03519].

sources.summary: The source is reassessed from a bona-fide flat-spectrum object to a possible steep-spectrum, compact low-power CSS candidate with an unresolved core and uncertain western emission.

quotes.quote: J0100 0200 FBQS J0100-0200 was one of the sources included bona-fide (i.e. without a measured spectral index) in the F sample by Foschini15 . While the broad-band index is consistent with a flat-spectrum object, the in-band spectral indexes reveal that it might be a steep-spectrum source. The map shown in Fig. fig:J0100m0200 seems to show an unresolved core with a size of 307 238 pc. It is not clear whether the faint emission located on the west side of the core is real or just noise. Given its uncertain nature, this source would likely require a follow-up at higher resolution.

Because of its compact size and steep spectrum it might be classified as a low-power CSS. Its luminosity is indeed lower than classical CSS sources Odea98 , but close to another example of NLS1 classified as CSS, J1432+3014 Caccianiga14, Caccianiga17 .

gmw_00009021.name: J0146 0040
gmw_00009021.overview: J0146 0040 is a radio-quiet narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1801.03519]. Radio observations reveal only an unresolved core with a very steep broad-band spectral index, which may become inverted at frequencies above 5 GHz [arXiv:1801.03519].

sources.summary: The paper classifies this radio-quiet NLS1 as very steep in broad-band index, faint near 5 GHz, possibly inverted at higher frequency, and undetected at 37 GHz.

quotes.quote: J0146 0040 2MASX J01464481-0040426 is a radio-quiet NLS1, that in our map (Fig. fig:J0146m0040 ) shows only an unresolved core whose size we could not determine. Its broad-band spectral index is very steep, since the source is clearly visible in the NVSS survey with a flux of 8.7 mJy, while it is barely detected at 6 in our survey (total flux 0.07 mJy). Moreover, J0146 0040 is not detected at 4.7 GHz, while it is clearly visible at 5.7 GHz. This could be a sign of a minimum in the spectrum around 5 GHz, and of an inverted spectrum going to higher frequencies. Despite the inverted spectrum, the source has not detection at high frequency (37 GHz).

gmw_00009022.name: J0744+5149
gmw_00009022.overview: J0744+5149 is a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1801.03519] that exhibits a steep radio spectrum and contains an unresolved kiloparsec-scale core [arXiv:1801.03519]. Although the galaxy hosts strong ongoing star formation [arXiv:1801.03519], its q22 ratio suggests that star formation is not the primary driver of its radio emission [arXiv:1801.03519].

sources.summary: The source has a steep radio spectrum, unresolved kiloparsec-scale core, high luminosity, strong star formation, and a q22 ratio implying star formation is not dominant.

quotes.quote: J0744+5149 Little is known about NVSS J074402+514917, which was included bona-fide in Foschini15 F sample. However, all of its spectral index are in agreement within the error bars, and indicate a rather steep radio spectrum, _ 1.2, consistent with the non-detection at 22 GHz. The map (Fig. fig:J0744p5149 ) shows only an unresolved core with a size of 0.7 0.3 kpc. Its total luminosity is approximately 10 ^ 41 , which is higher with respect to other radio-loud NLS1s, and comparable to that of the CSS-NLS1 J1432+3014 Caccianiga15 . A strong ongoing star formation (300 M _ yr ^ 1 ) was found investigating the WISE colors of this object Caccianiga15 .
The q22 ratio in this source is 0.47, possibly indicating that the contribution of star formation is not the main component of the radio emission.

gmw_00009023.name: J0850+4626
gmw_00009023.overview: J0850+4626 is a Seyfert galaxy that was previously classified as a steep-spectrum source [arXiv:1801.03519]. However, its spectral index is consistent with a flat-spectrum source, and it exhibits a compact morphology that may indicate small-scale variability [arXiv:1801.03519].

sources.summary: The paper finds this formerly steep-spectrum source consistent with a flat-spectrum object, unresolved in the JVLA map, compact on VLBA scales, and possibly mildly variable.

quotes.quote: J0850+4626 SDSS J085001.16+462600.5 was classified as S source according to low frequency ( < 1.4 GHz) data. Our spectral indexes however seem to be consistent with a flat-spectrum source. The source, however, is detected neither at 22 GHz, nor at 37 GHz. The object appears as an unresolved core (Fig. fig:J0850p4626 ), with a deconvolved size of 375 350 pc. This source was studied on VLBA scales by Gu15 , who found a compact morphology.
The flux they measured at 5 GHz (11 mJy) is slightly larger than our value, possibly indicating a relatively small scale variability.

gmw_00009024.name: J1031+4234
gmw_00009024.overview: J1031+4234 is a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1801.03519] that exhibits a compact morphology with a deconvolved size under 0.6 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1801.03519]. It has a steep broad-band spectral index that may indicate a curved spectrum peaking at low frequencies, potentially qualifying the object as a classical compact steep-spectrum source [arXiv:1801.03519].

sources.summary: The source is reinterpreted from flat-spectrum toward a steeper, possibly curved spectrum, with compact morphology, high luminosity, and possible classical CSS classification.

quotes.quote: J1031+4234 SDSS J103123.73+423439.3 is classified as an F source, with a spectral index below 1.4 GHz of -0.40 0.06 Yuan08, Foschini15 . Our spectral index measurements point in a different direction, showing a much steeper broad-band index, and relatively steep in-band indexes (although close to the 0.5 threshold). This might be an indication of a curved spectrum peaking at low frequencies, possibly in the MHz range, or of source variability. The map (Fig. fig:J1031p4234 ) clearly shows a compact source, with a deconvolved size lower than 0.6 0.1 kpc and a luminosity higher than 10 ^ 41 . If the source spectrum is actually peaked at low frequencies, these properties might indicate that this object is classifiable as a classical CSS.

gmw_00009025.name: s01143
gmw_00009025.overview: The high-redshift galaxy s01143 has been analyzed using JWST NIRSpec observations [arXiv:2304.11178]. Its inferred dust attenuation curve is similar to a Calzetti curve but is slightly flatter at short wavelengths below 0.2 microns [arXiv:2304.11178].

sources.summary: The s01143 source has a Calzetti-like, slightly flatter attenuation curve without a strong 2175 bump, and its inferred mass, SFR, and attenuation depend strongly on the assumed curve.

quotes.quote: Fig. spec depicts the JWST NIRSpec spectra of the three galaxies (s00717, s01143, and s01149) analyzed in this work, along with the best-fit posterior spectra (left panels, blue and orange spectra, respectively).
The inferred dust attenuation curves of the remaining two sources, s01143 and s01149, are similar to the Calzetti, but slightly flatter at short wavelengths ( 0.2 microns).

For s01143, these three parameters are lower by 7 (0.13 dex), 8 (0.14 dex), and 10 (0.12 dex), respectively. For s01149, M_* , SFR and A_V are lower by 4 (0.16 dex), 6 (0.16 dex), and 7.5 (0.14 dex), respectively. Finally, when assuming the SMC curve, most of the properties of s00717 resemble their fiducial values apart from A_V which is lower by > 10 (0.33 dex). For the remaining two sources, M_* , SFR and A_V deviate substantially ( > 10 ) from their fiducial values, since their fiducial attenuation curve is much flatter than the SMC curve (Fig. curves , green and orange solid lines for s01143 and s01149, respectively). For example, for the s01143 and s01149 sources, M_* , SFR and A_V are lower by by 0.32-0.35 dex, 0.31-0.34 dex, and 0.32-0.38 dex, respectively.

gmw_00009026.name: s01149
gmw_00009026.overview: The high-redshift galaxy s01149 is a star-forming system observed with the JWST NIRSpec instrument [arXiv:2304.11178]. Its inferred dust attenuation curve is similar to the Calzetti curve, though it is slightly flatter at short wavelengths below 0.2 microns [arXiv:2304.11178].

sources.summary: The s01149 source likewise has a Calzetti-like, slightly flatter attenuation curve, lacks a significant 2175 bump, and shows substantial parameter shifts under standard dust models.

quotes.quote: Fig. spec depicts the JWST NIRSpec spectra of the three galaxies (s00717, s01143, and s01149) analyzed in this work, along with the best-fit posterior spectra (left panels, blue and orange spectra, respectively).
The inferred dust attenuation curves of the remaining two sources, s01143 and s01149, are similar to the Calzetti, but slightly flatter at short wavelengths ( 0.2 microns).

For s01149, M_* , SFR and A_V are lower by 4 (0.16 dex), 6 (0.16 dex), and 7.5 (0.14 dex), respectively. Finally, when assuming the SMC curve, most of the properties of s00717 resemble their fiducial values apart from A_V which is lower by > 10 (0.33 dex). For the remaining two sources, M_* , SFR and A_V deviate substantially ( > 10 ) from their fiducial values, since their fiducial attenuation curve is much flatter than the SMC curve (Fig. curves , green and orange solid lines for s01143 and s01149, respectively). For example, for the s01143 and s01149 sources, M_* , SFR and A_V are lower by by 0.32-0.35 dex, 0.31-0.34 dex, and 0.32-0.38 dex, respectively.

gmw_00009027.name: GAMA 106717
gmw_00009027.overview: GAMA 106717 is an emission-line galaxy that hosts regions of shocked gas [arXiv:2307.11831]. The galaxy exhibits low-velocity dispersion star-forming gas alongside a distinct population of high-velocity dispersion shocked gas that displays spatially consistent LINER ionization [arXiv:2307.11831].

sources.summary: GAMA 106717 is used as a shocked-gas example, with spatially consistent LINER ionization, high-dispersion spaxels, mixing sequences, and shock signatures missed by line ratios alone.

quotes.quote: Figure 106717_2d shows the BPT/VO87 diagnostics as well as a spatial map for each diagram for GAMA 106717, which contains regions of shocked gas. It is clear that this galaxy hosts LINER ionization that is spatially consistent between multiple emission line ratio pairs. Figure 106717_panels shows the results of our new classification scheme. The first row is the BPT/VO87 diagrams with spaxels labeled according to our new method, with black points having only star-forming ionization and the colored points are the high velocity dispersion spaxels colored according to increasing radius, which for this galaxy contains shock ionization.

From the projections in velocity dispersion space, two distinct populations of gas can be seen that start at low velocity dispersion and increase with emission line ratios. Two mixing sequences can be drawn from these plots, one for the low velocity disperion star-forming gas, and another for the high velocity dispersion shocked gas that both begin at a single point at the base of the star-forming sequence in the log( [ N 2 ] /H ) diagram. The last row contains a histogram and a map of the velocity dispersion, a map of the shock fraction which is described in more detail in Section ionization_maps , and an optical image of the galaxy for reference. The combination of kinematic information as well as emission lines are what allow us to detect fainter signatures of shock ionization in this galaxy that would be missed with emission line ratios alone.

gmw_00009028.name: GAMA 376478
gmw_00009028.overview: GAMA 376478 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy [arXiv:2307.11831]. Emission-line diagnostics and spatial maps of the galaxy reveal clear signatures of AGN ionization and excitation [arXiv:2307.11831].

sources.summary: GAMA 376478 is the AGN-excitation example; emission-line diagnostics and maps show AGN ionization, while the revised method traces more spaxels farther from the nucleus.

quotes.quote: We describe two examples of a galaxy hosing either shock or AGN ionization here, for GAMA galaxies 106717 and 376478 respectively.

Figures 376478_2d 376478_panels shows a similar result as Figures 106717_2d 106717_panels , but for GAMA 376478, which contains AGN excitation. Clear signatures of AGN ionization can be seen in the emission line diagnostics and spatial maps in Figure 376478_2d , but more spaxels are detected that are traced further away from the nucleus of the galaxy with our revised method.

For galaxies identified as hosting kinematically disturbed AGN or shocks, we also see an increase in the number of spaxels identified as being ionized by shocks or AGN on a galaxy by galaxy basis due to being able to trace the mixing sequence further into the star-forming region of the emission line diagnostic diagram. This can be most easily seen by comparing the emission line ratio only classification in Figures 106717_2d and 376478_2d with their new classification counterpart in the ionization fraction map in Figures 106717_panels and 376478_panels respectively.

gmw_00009029.name: GAMA 273514
gmw_00009029.overview: GAMA 273514 is a low-mass, late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:2307.11831]. Although the galaxy exhibits AGN-like ionization, it lacks the spatial structure and elevated velocity dispersion required to be classified as a true active galactic nucleus host [arXiv:2307.11831].

sources.summary: GAMA 273514 illustrates AGN-like ionization without spatial structure; the paper says such low-mass, late-spiral systems are not classified as true AGN hosts.

quotes.quote: Figure AGN_like_figure shows GAMA 273514, which contains this AGN-like ionization. There is no noticeable structure in the spatial map for any of the AGN-like spaxels, and the [ N 2 ] diagram does not show any AGN-like spaxels at all. Nearly all of the galaxies in this population follow the same emission line diagnostic and spatial trends as GAMA 273514. Despite the often times large number of AGN-like spaxels in these galaxies, given the combination of an on average low galaxy mass, single morphology type, no trace of any elevated velocity dispersion, and lack of spatial structure in the emission line maps, we do not classify these galaxies as true AGN hosts but rather as hosting AGN-like emission. While it is certainly possible that a number of these galaxies do indeed host an AGN, to determine if they are true AGN hosts would require a more in depth look at each galaxy individually, which is beyond the scope of this survey wide study.

gmw_00009030.name: VCC 390
gmw_00009030.overview: VCC 390 is a compact dwarf elliptical galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185]. It is characterized by a low concentration index, an exponential profile, and an unusually broad HI velocity width [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185].

sources.summary: The paper describes VCC 390 as a compact Virgo dwarf elliptical with detected HI, an early-type optical spectrum, an unusually broad HI line, and possible gas-stripping or contamination issues.

quotes.quote: In the DSS and WIYN images (Figures 2 and 3) VCC 390 appears as a small compact galaxy. Using the WIYN image of this galaxy (Figure 3) its scale length is fit as 0.29 0.003 kpc (Table 4). This object is classified as a dE3 in the VCC and has an absolute magnitude of M _ B = -14.4 , has low asymmetry, low concentration, and low clumpiness values (Table 4), all consistent with it being a dwarf elliptical (Conselice 2003). It is also fairly well fit by an exponential profile with a S e rsic index, n = 0.95 0.01 .

The HI line of VCC 390 presents an interesting puzzle due to its large velocity width (Table 3). The 20 and 50 FWHM velocities are 286 5 and 285 7 with a total integrated HI flux of 0.8 0.01 . It is only one of three galaxies in our sample that has a velocity width > 200 .

gmw_00009031.name: VCC 1713
gmw_00009031.overview: VCC 1713 is a low-mass dwarf elliptical-like galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185]. It contains neutral hydrogen gas and exhibits a moderately high clumpiness index, which suggests the presence of low-level star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185].

sources.summary: The paper reports VCC 1713 as a Virgo low-mass galaxy with a new HI detection, narrow HI profile, bright absolute magnitude, dwarf-elliptical morphology, and clumpiness suggesting residual star formation.

quotes.quote: The other low-mass galaxy with a new HI detection is VCC 1713. Compared to VCC 390, VCC 1713 has a narrower velocity HI profile, with W_ 50 = 46 8 and W_ 20 = 60 6 , more commonly expected for a low-mass galaxy. The total HI flux is slightly higher than that of VCC 390, with an integrated value of 1.1 , giving a total HI gas mass of 8 10 ^ 7 . Despite its narrow HI line width, this galaxy has a rather bright absolute magnitude of M _ B = -16.2 .

The morphological appearance (Figure 2 3) and quantitative morphology of this galaxy suggests that it is a dwarf elliptical-like object (Table 4), with low asymmetry and concentration values. The clumpiness index S is moderately high suggesting the presence of some low level of star formation. There are however no measured colors for VCC 1713, which if it were very blue would qualify as a dwarf irregular object. This object is our best candidate for being an object in an ongoing early transition phase between a star-forming and quiescent LMCG.

gmw_00009032.name: VCC 31
gmw_00009032.overview: VCC 31 is a compact dwarf galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185]. It has a high ratio of neutral hydrogen mass to blue luminosity and is suspected to possess an extended distribution of neutral hydrogen gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185].

sources.summary: The paper reviews VCC 31 as an HI-detected Virgo dwarf with high M_HI/L_B, likely extended gas, symmetric dwarf-elliptical morphology, and dynamical ratios depending on the HI measurement.

quotes.quote: VCC 31 has a magnitude of M _ B = - 16.4 and was detected in HI using the Arecibo telescope by van Zee, Haynes Giovanneli (1995) and at Nan ay by van Driel et al. (2000). Van Zee et al. (1995) determined that this galaxy does not have an extended HI distribution, and has a M _ HI /L _ B value high enough to be considered a compact dwarf. Van Driel et al. (2000) also detected this object in HI, although they find a much higher W_ 20 value (312 ) than van Zee et al. (1995) (132 ). This indicates that VCC 31 may have extended gas, as the velocity width and flux of the Nan ay observations are much larger than the Arecibo results, which used a smaller 3.5 beam. There are also no nearby galaxies that would have confused the Nan ay results, further suggesting that there is extended HI gas around VCC 31.

gmw_00009033.name: VCC 168
gmw_00009033.overview: VCC 168 is a gas-rich dwarf elliptical galaxy with a quantitative morphology consistent with this classification [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185]. It is a quiescent system that lacks H-alpha emission and contains an older stellar population, suggesting that star formation ceased more than 2 Gyr ago [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185].

sources.summary: The paper argues VCC 168 is a quiescent gas-rich dwarf elliptical: WIYN imaging and symmetry support the classification, H emission is absent, and red colors imply an older stellar population.

quotes.quote: VCC 168 was first detected in HI using Arecibo by Hoffman et al. (1987), who classified this object as a dE2 or ImIV based on the VCC. This object was later classified by Binggeli Cameron (1993) as a true dE2. It is not visible on the DSS images (Figure 2), but clearly appears as a dwarf elliptical-like object on the WIYN image, with a quantitative morphology consistent with this interpretation (Table 4) including a light profile well fit by an exponential (Table 4; Figure 3). Heller et al. (1999) searched for H in VCC 168, but found none.

Almoznino Brosch (1998) observed VCC 168 in BVR, finding a color (B-V) = 0.76 0.17, the reddest (B-V) color in their sample. This suggests an older stellar population, > 2 Gyr since the cessation of significant star formation. This is the case even if the stars are metal rich (Worthey 1994); VCC 168 thus appears to be a quiescent LMCG with gas. Its HI mass of 3 10^ 7 is unusually high for such a faint dwarf elliptical.

gmw_00009034.name: VCC 405
gmw_00009034.overview: VCC 405 is a very faint cataloged object with a blue magnitude of approximately 20 [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185]. It does not appear on Digitized Sky Survey images, and its single reported 21-cm neutral hydrogen detection is likely spurious [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185].

sources.summary: The paper treats VCC 405 as a very faint cataloged object whose single Arecibo HI detection is probably spurious because its derived HI parameters are implausible.

quotes.quote: VCC 405 is the faintest object in our HI detected sample with B 20 (VCC) or M _ B 11.5 , and it does not appear on DSS images (Figure 2). This object was marginally detected with Arecibo at 21-cm (Duprie Schneider 1996) with a single observation, and thus the detection could be spurious. Based on their HI spectrum and the very unlikely physical parameters derived from its HI profile (Table 3), we conclude that this object is indeed probably a false HI detection, and do not consider it in our subsequent analysis.

gmw_00009035.name: VCC 797
gmw_00009035.overview: VCC 797 is a dwarf elliptical galaxy located approximately 3 arcminutes south of the giant galaxy M85 [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185]. Although classified as a dwarf elliptical, it has a marginal 21-cm detection that led some researchers to consider it a dwarf irregular [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185].

sources.summary: The paper describes VCC 797 as the sample's narrowest-HI-line dwarf, near M85, with a marginal HI detection and dwarf-elliptical classification needing deeper 21-cm confirmation.

quotes.quote: VCC 797 with W_ 50 = 28 has the narrowest HI line of any galaxy in our sample, and the smallest optical scale length of 0.24 kpc. The internal velocity dispersion is low, indicating that its observed HI velocity width may be dominated by turbulence. It is also the only low-mass galaxy that is near a giant galaxy, in this case about 3 south of M85 (Figure 2). HI in VCC 797 was discovered by Burstein, Krumm Salpeter (1987), who detected it during 21-cm observations of M85.

Due to the presence of HI, Burstein et al. (1987) considered VCC 797 to be a dwarf irregular, although it is classified as a dE3,N in the VCC. The VCC classification is based on higher resolution images than those available to Burstein et al. (1987). Burstein et al. (1987) however only marginally detect the object at a 4 level of significance. We tentatively consider this to be a HI detected dE, and not a spurious signal, although this should be confirmed by observing it again at 21-cm with higher sensitivity.

gmw_00009036.name: VCC 1949
gmw_00009036.overview: VCC 1949 is an early-type dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185] whose optical structure is well fit by an exponential profile [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185]. It has a high dynamical mass-to-light ratio [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185] and a very large inferred neutral hydrogen velocity width [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VCC 1949 as the brightest early-type dwarf in the sample, with exponential optical structure, prior HI detections, a very large inferred HI width, and high mass-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: VCC 1949 is the brightest galaxy (M _ B = -17.1 ) in our sample, and it appears as an early-type dwarf on DSS images (Figure 2). It was classified as a dSBO(4),N or dE(6,4),N in the VCC Bingelli Cameron (1993) list VCC 1949 as a dS0. . It was first detected in HI by Huchtmeier Richter (1986), and later re-observed at 21-cm by Haynes Giovanelli (1986).

The optical image of this galaxy is well fit by an exponential profile (Binggeli Cameron 1993). VCC 1949 is one of three galaxies in the present sample with very large ( > 250 ) inferred HI velocity widths (Table 3), giving it a high measured dynamical mass to light ratio of 20 in solar units. There are no obvious companions which could produce this large line width from confusion.

gmw_00009037.name: FDS11 LSB2
gmw_00009037.overview: FDS11 LSB2 is a low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:1901.09648]. It is a large, diffuse galaxy situated in a reasonably crowded field with a varying sky background [arXiv:1901.09648].

sources.summary: The paper identifies FDS11 LSB2 as the largest sample galaxy, requiring rebinned fitting and modified sky modelling; the authors measure it 1.5 times larger with a lower Sersic index than V17.

quotes.quote: In the case of FDS11 LSB2, the largest galaxy in our sample (with ^ _ e,V17 =76 ), we re-binned the data by a factor of 5 to make the fit easier (the original fitting region was 3000 3000 pixels). Over this region the sky background level varies significantly, so we modified the \ sky modelling to use mesh sizes of 200 and median filtered in 3 3 meshes. We note that an image of FDS11 LSB2 is displayed in figure 20 of V17. Overall our results are consistent with V17 (figure figure:imfits ), with a few exceptions. These include FDS11 LSB2, which we measure to be 1.5 times larger than originally reported.

This result was robust against changes in the size of the background mesh. We also note that we measure a slightly lower S ersic index n for this object, and n is generally anti-correlated with r_ e . This discrepancy likely arises from the difficulty involved in measuring such a large, diffuse galaxy in a reasonably crowded field with a varying sky; we use DeepScan whereas V17 fit a 2D sky plane in their \ Peng2002 modelling.

gmw_00009038.name: RXJ1720.1+2638 BCG
gmw_00009038.overview: RXJ1720.1+2638 BCG is an active central galaxy that hosts an actively accreting supermassive black hole [arXiv:2211.09948]. This active galactic nucleus produces sub-kiloparsec radio jets extending approximately 0.8 kiloparsecs to the east and west [arXiv:2211.09948].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the RXJ1720.1+2638 BCG as an active central galaxy whose AGN produces sub-kpc radio jets, potentially supplying electrons that affect the cluster minihalo interpretation.

quotes.quote: A complicating factor, however, was the lack of knowledge of the state of the BCG supermassive black hole. The 2013ApJ...762...78Z simulations assumed a single AGN outburst in the past; the relativistic electrons were then allowed to age for some time before being re-accelerated by the turbulence induced by the minor merger. If the BCG in fact has an AGN which is actively accreting and producing jets, the continuous injection of new relativistic electrons could explain the lack of spectral steepening and slightly flatter spectral index, while still requiring the turbulence to spread the electrons from the jets out to the observed extent of the minihalo. Previous radio observations had only been able to put an upper limit on the size of any prospective jets ( <1.4 kpc; Giacintucci ) although various optical indicators suggested AGN activity (e.g. \ 2012MNRAS.423..422L , 2016MNRAS.461..560G ).

There is a clear detection of a central, compact object plus resolved jets extending to the east and west. The jets extend roughly 0.3 arcsec (corresponding to 0.8 kpc) from the centre in both the east and west directions. The eastern jet may bend abruptly to the north-west, however this feature only appears at 2 significance. In contrast, the minihalo emission extends to at least 80 kpc from the cluster centre, so these jets are around three orders of magnitude smaller.

gmw_00009039.name: NGC 1395
gmw_00009039.overview: NGC 1395 is an optically bright early-type galaxy located in the Eridanus region [arXiv:astro-ph/0503536]. It is a known X-ray source that exhibits diffuse X-ray emission extending approximately 20 arcminutes around the galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0503536].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1395 as an optically bright early-type Eridanus X-ray source whose field shows diffuse emission extending about 20 arcminutes around the galaxy.

quotes.quote: The three sub-groups namely NGC 1407, NGC 1332, and NGC 1395 have their brightest members as an elliptical or an S0.

The optically bright early type galaxies NGC 1400, NGC 1407, NGC 1395, and NGC 1332 are known X-ray sources in this group. Trinchieri et al. (2000) reported the presence of diffuse X-ray emission around NGC 1407. The processed and calibrated X-ray images (0.1 keV - 2.0 keV) centered at NGC 1407, NGC 1395, and NGC 1332 were obtained from the ROSAT PSPC (Roentgen Satellite Position Sensitive Proportional Counter) archival data.

Apart from the X-ray emission associated with NGC 1400, NGC 1407 and a few other unresolved sources in the field, diffuse emission centered at NGC 1407 and NGC 1395 can be seen in Fig. fig:xray . The extent of the diffuse emission is 30' ( 200 kpc) around NGC 1407 and 20' ( 135 Kpc) around NGC 1395.

gmw_00009040.name: MACS0451-arc
gmw_00009040.overview: MACS0451-arc is a strongly-lensed galaxy at a redshift of 2.013 that appears as an elongated arc [arXiv:1408.0816]. Its southern region contains a molecular gas reservoir detected via CO(3-2) emission and shows infrared evidence of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1408.0816].

sources.summary: The paper describes MACS0451-arc as a lensed z=2.013 arc with southern CO(3-2) emission, a reliable total molecular-gas estimate, and infrared SED evidence for an AGN.

quotes.quote: MACS0451-arc The HST image reveals that this z_ H = 2.013 galaxy is strongly stretched by gravitational lensing as testified by the striking, elongated arc extending over 20'' and composed of multiple images of the source. We detect the CO(3-2) emission at 4-5 . It does not cover the entire arc, but is confined to the southern part of the arc (see Fig. fig:COmaps ). Such a confinement may either indicate that the lensing magnification is differential along the arc, and/or that the molecular gas is located in specific regions of the galaxy.
We may conclude with confidence that the detected CO emission provides a good estimate of the total molecular gas content of this strongly-lensed galaxy.

There is evidence that the MACS0451-arc contains an AGN component, as determined from the analysis of its IR SED. In particular, the southern part of the arc, which contains the most magnified image of the center of the source (as said above), shows a much hotter IR SED than the northern part of the arc. Indeed, while the SED of the northern part peaks at 250 m, that of the southern part rises down to 100 m (corresponding to a _ rest < 33 m).

gmw_00009041.name: A2218-Mult
gmw_00009041.overview: A2218-Mult is a low-infrared galaxy located at a high redshift of z = 3.104 [arXiv:1408.0816]. Hubble Space Telescope imaging reveals that the galaxy has two counterpart images, and its CO(3-2) emission remains undetected despite a high gravitational lensing magnification factor of 14 [arXiv:1408.0816].

sources.summary: The paper identifies A2218-Mult as the sample's highest-redshift low-IR galaxy, with two HST counterparts, no detected CO(3-2), and reported upper limits on gas properties.

quotes.quote: A2218-Mult This z_ H = 3.104 galaxy is the highest redshift galaxy from our sample of low- L_ IR selected galaxies. HST images reveal two counterpart images richard11 . We searched for CO in the western counterpart image. Comparatively we achieved similarly deep PdBI observations with an rms of 0.77 mJy for this object, but the CO(3--2) emission remains undetected despite a high magnification factor = 14 . We provide 4 upper limits on the CO(3--2) integrated line flux and its respective derivatives CO(1--0) luminosity and molecular gas mass in Table tab:COresults .

gmw_00009042.name: host galaxy of GRB120327A
gmw_00009042.overview: The host galaxy of GRB120327A is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.81 [arXiv:1407.6905]. It is classified as an irregular galaxy with relatively low dust content [arXiv:1903.01353], though some models suggest it could be a spiral or proto-spheroid system [arXiv:1407.6905].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB 120327A host as a not-so-dusty irregular galaxy, with mixed abundance indicators and a host age of 0.8 Gyr.

quotes.quote: GRB 120327A The comparison of the patterns for the three reference models with the observed abundances in Figure f:120327 shows quite different results depending on the elements. Looking at the elements we have that [ Si/Fe ] is perfectly coherent with the spiral disk model. On the other hand, [ Mg/Fe ] stays between spiral and irregular models, whereas [ S/Fe ] indicates an irregular galaxy.

By means of our test, in fact, we found that the irregular model with decreased dust production by CC-SNe (I ) is the best in explaining this GRB host. In conclusion, we identified this host galaxy as a not so dusty (with respect to the average of this morphological type) irregular galaxy. In this way, we found that the age for this host at the time of the GRB event is 0.8 Gyr .

sources.summary: The paper compares the z=2.81 GRB 120327A host with dust-inclusive chemical models, estimating a 50 Myr age and finding no firm morphology beyond possible spiral or proto-spheroid solutions.

quotes.quote: In Figure 120327A are shown the predictions and the data for the host of GRB 120327A at z=2.81 studied by D'Elia et al. (2014). In this case we show only the models with dust for several elements.

Applying the method to the three GRBs analyzed in this paper, we find an age of 50 Myr for the galaxy hosting the GRB 120327A, 0.32 Gyr for the GRB081008 host and 15Myr for the GRB120815 host. These young ages indicate that we find GRBs to be "young" galaxies, namely galaxies in which the star formation is very active, probably suffering their first major star formation episode, but they are not necessary typical of the main population of galaxies that could have started forming stars already much before.

Our results suggest that one of the galaxies hosting GRB120815 is probably a massive proto-spheroid catched during the active star formation phase, whereas for the other two galaxies hosting GRB120327A and GRB081008, no firm conclusion can be drawn, although some of the data seem to suggest spiral or proto-spheroid systems with high mass and star formation rate.

sources.summary: The paper predicts JWST could measure strong emission lines in the GRB120327A host; IllustrisTNG analogues give Z_emiss about -0.25, making it a strong model test.

quotes.quote: With an exposure time of 1 hour, several strong emission lines of the host galaxy of GRB120327A are predicted to be measurable with S/N 4 , including the lines for [ O ii ] , [ N ii ] , H and H . This would allow Z_ emiss to be computed for this host via the O 3 N 2 diagnostic.

We see that, under this model, the most likely emission metallicity for the host of GRB120327A is approximately (Z_ emiss /Z_ ) = -0.25 , substantially higher than the measured Z_ abs value. This object would thus represent an excellent target for Z_ emiss observations, since it would have a strong power to falsify/validate our model framework.

gmw_00009043.name: host galaxy of GRB120119A
gmw_00009043.overview: The host galaxy of GRB120119A is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.73 [arXiv:2103.15269]. Although the galaxy is optically bright, it is also highly dust-obscured [arXiv:2103.15269].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB120119A host as bright but highly dust-obscured; JWST could detect O3N2 lines in 4.5 hours, though Z_abs is too uncertain for strong model tests.

quotes.quote: While the host galaxy of GRB120119A at z=1.73 is very bright ( m_ AB = 22.89 0.07 ; comparable to the host galaxy of GRB121014A for which Z_ emiss is known), it is also highly dust-obscured, with A_V=1.06 0.02 . This dust increases the difficulty of measuring strong line ratios for this galaxy -- however, with an exposure time of 4.5 hours, this metallicity can likely be detected using the O 3 N 2 diagnostic (S/N = 2.78 for H , 4.13 for [ O iii ] , 7.95 for H , 3.03 for [ N ii ] ).

For other galaxies which are bright enough for JWST observation (see Section subsec:jwst_opportunities ), either the measured value of Z_ abs for these galaxies is too close to the predicted value of Z_ emiss (GRB000926A, GRB090323A, GRB120909A) or too poorly constrained (GRB070802A, GRB120119A) to falsify the hypothesis that Z_ abs and Z_ emiss are drawn from the same distribution.

gmw_00009044.name: A1703-zD1
gmw_00009044.overview: A1703-zD1 is a gravitationally lensed Lyman-break galaxy located behind the strong lensing cluster Abell 1703 [arXiv:2203.00018]. Spectroscopically confirmed at a redshift of z = 6.8269, the galaxy exhibits a velocity gradient that may indicate rotation or an ongoing merger [arXiv:2203.00018].

sources.summary: The paper spectroscopically confirms lensed Lyman-break galaxy A1703-zD1 at z=6.8269 using [C II], finding luminosity consistent with local relations and a possible rotational velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: We present the spectroscopic confirmation of the brightest known gravitationally lensed Lyman break galaxy in the Epoch of Reionisation, A1703-zD1, through the detection of [ C ii ] 158 m at a redshift of z = 6.8269 0.0004. This source was selected behind the strong lensing cluster Abell 1703, with an intrinsic L_ UV L^*_ z=7 luminosity and a very blue Spitzer /IRAC [ 3.6 ] - [ 4.5 ] colour, implying high equivalent width line emission of [ O iii ] +H . [ C ii ] is reliably detected at 6.1 co-spatial with the rest-frame UV counterpart, showing similar spatial extent. Correcting for the lensing magnification, the [ C ii ] luminosity in A1703-zD1 is broadly consistent with the local L_ CII - SFR relation. We find a clear velocity gradient of 103 22 km s^ 1 across the source which possibly indicates rotation or an ongoing merger.

gmw_00009045.name: EGS-5711
gmw_00009045.overview: EGS-5711 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy [arXiv:2203.00018]. It is characterized as a normal star-forming galaxy with a blue Spitzer/IRAC color [arXiv:2203.00018], and it exhibits tentative carbon emission signals [arXiv:2203.00018].

sources.summary: The paper identifies EGS-5711 as a Lyman-break target galaxy and reports tentative [C II] signals, including one 0.5 arcsec from the target and a second co-spatial signal.

quotes.quote: Target selection and properties We obtained NOEMA observations of three sources, A1703-zD1 Bradley12, Smit14 , EGS-5711424617 and EGS-1952445714 hereafter EGS-5711 and EGS-1952 respectively; Smit15 .

These galaxies were initially selected with the Lyman break technique as HST /F814W drop-out galaxies and subsequently identified as sources with blue Spitzer /IRAC [ 3.6 ] - [ 4.5 ] colours, implying high equivalent width [ O iii ] +H emission Smit14,Smit15 . The spectral energy distribution (SED) for each galaxy is shown in Appendix app:A . We selected these targets due to their observed brightness ( m_ UV 24 -- 25), but also as they are representative of `normal' star-forming galaxies (SFR < 100 M _ yr ^ 1 ) at z 7, with intrinsic (corrected for lensing magnification, A1703-zD1 is magnified 9 ) UV SFRs of 5 -- 38 M _ yr ^ 1 .

EGS-5711 EGS-1952 When scanning for [ C ii ] in EGS-5711 we find a tentative signal with S/N = 4.6 at 246.16 0.02 GHz, 0.5 arcsec away from the centre of the target. This line, if real, would put the source at z = 6.7263 0.0006. A second signal at 242.10 0.03 GHz with S/N = 3.4 is also found co-spatial with EGS-5711.

gmw_00009046.name: EGS-1952
gmw_00009046.overview: EGS-1952 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy selected as an HST/F814W drop-out source [arXiv:2203.00018]. It is representative of normal star-forming galaxies at z~7, though observations show no co-spatial [C II] line emission or continuum signal [arXiv:2203.00018].

sources.summary: The paper identifies EGS-1952 as a Lyman-break target galaxy representative of z~7 star-forming systems, then reports no co-spatial [C II] detection and no continuum signal.
