gmw_00009154.overview: WGA J2239 0631 is classified as a quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0509718]. Radio observations reveal a two-lobed structure with asymmetric hot spots on C-array scales, whereas only its nucleus is detected on A-array scales [arXiv:astro-ph/0509718].

sources.summary: WGA J2239 0631 is classified as a quasar and shows C-array two-lobed radio structure with asymmetric hot spots, while only its nucleus is detected in A array.

quotes.quote: WGA J2239 0631. L01 published an optical spectrum and classified this source as a quasar. The source is extended on the C array image (Fig. 1(w)) with two lobes to each side of the nucleus, one of them having a stronger hot spot than the other. Only the nucleus is detected on A array scales.

gmw_00009155.name: WGA J2320 + 0032
gmw_00009155.overview: WGA J2320 + 0032 is classified as a quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0509718]. The source remains unresolved in radio images and is detected as an unresolved core in the FIRST 20 cm map [arXiv:astro-ph/0509718].

sources.summary: WGA J2320 + 0032 is classified as a quasar and remains unresolved in the new radio images and in the FIRST 20 cm map.

quotes.quote: WGA J2320 + 0032. An optical spectrum of this source can be found in L01 who classify it as a quasar. This source is also part of the Sloan Digital Sky Survey Schn03 . The source remains unresolved in the radio on the scales imaged (Fig. 1(x)). Similarly, the VLA B array map from the FIRST survey at 20 cm shows only an unresolved core.

gmw_00009156.name: PKS 0256 005
gmw_00009156.overview: PKS 0256 005 is a quasar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0509718]. Its optical spectra display several strong broad emission lines, and the source remains unresolved in radio observations [arXiv:astro-ph/0509718].

sources.summary: PKS 0256 005 shows strong broad emission lines and remains unresolved in both the new radio images and the FIRST 20 cm map.

quotes.quote: PKS 0256 005. The optical spectra of this source presented by Rich80b and Osm94 show several strong broad emission lines. This source is also part of the FIRST Bright Quasar Survey Beck01 and the Sloan Digital Sky Survey Schn03 . The source remains unresolved in the radio on the scales imaged (Fig. 1(ab)). Similarly, the VLA B array map from the FIRST survey at 20 cm shows only an unresolved core.

gmw_00009157.name: 0959 + 68W1
gmw_00009157.overview: The active galaxy 0959 + 68W1 is classified as a blazar [arXiv:astro-ph/0509718]. Its optical spectrum displays several broad emission lines, and its radio structure features a pronounced core with jet-like components extending over several tens of arcseconds on both sides [arXiv:astro-ph/0509718].

sources.summary: The paper reports broad emission lines for 0959 + 68W1 and A-array jet-like features extending several tens of arcseconds from the core.

quotes.quote: 0959 + 68W1. Optical spectra of this source are available from the Hubble Space Telescope archive. These show several broad emission lines. This source is also part of the CLASS blazar survey Marcha01, Cac02 . The A array map (Fig. 1(ac)) shows jet-like features and jet blobs extending over several tens of arcseconds on both sides of the pronounced core.

gmw_00009158.name: SDSS J080430.99+360718.1
gmw_00009158.overview: SDSS J080430.99+360718.1 is a type 2 quasar located at a redshift of approximately 0.66 [arXiv:1702.06545, arXiv:0901.0567]. It hosts a highly luminous water megamaser [arXiv:0901.0567] that is coincident with its radio continuum emission, making the galaxy a candidate for hosting an inclined maser disk [arXiv:1702.06545].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0804+3607 as a z = 0.66 type 2 quasar with luminous, non-varying water maser emission coincident with continuum emission, making it an inclined disk candidate.

quotes.quote: This is a type 2 quasar showing luminous water maser emission at z 0.66 zakamska2003,barvainis2005 . The isotropic maser luminosity was 2.31 0.46 10^4 \ L_ in 2005 error from the quoted 20 calibration uncertainty barvainis2005 and 1.8 0.1 10^4 \ L_ in 2015 (this work), consistent with no variation. The maser and 20 GHz continuum emission are coincident to within 40 pc (1 ; Table tab:positions and Figure fig:SDSSJ0804 ), which is less precise than the other objects due to the much larger distance and lower observing frequency. The 12--16 GHz spectral index is 1.5 0.3 (Table tab:continuum ). This object remains an inclined maser disk candidate.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J080430.99+360718.1 as a z=0.66 type-2 QSO hosting the most intrinsically powerful known water megamaser, supporting high-redshift gigamaser detectability.

quotes.quote: The Gigamaser J0804+3607 As already reported in bar05 , water maser emission was detected from the type-2 QSO SDSS J080430.99+360718.1 (hereafter J0804+3607; z = 0.66). With L_ H_2O 21,000 L_ Using H_0 = 75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _ = 0.73, and _ M = 0.27. Note that the value given by bar05 , L_ H_2O = 23,000 L_ , is higher due to a smaller value of H_0 . , it is the intrinsically most powerful maser known.

While the detection of a water vapor maser in J0804+3607 shows that H _2 O masers are indeed detectable at high redshift, and thus, that such a project is in principle feasible, no obvious maser emission was discovered in any of the remaining objects (Table table1 ).
Indeed, a scenario in which the high QSO luminosities result in gigamasers is consistent with the detection of J0804+3607, by far the most powerful maser known today.

gmw_00009159.name: Horologium II
gmw_00009159.overview: Horologium II is a faint satellite system located at a heliocentric distance of approximately 79.4 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1508.02381]. While its classification remains ambiguous between a globular cluster and a dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1805.07350], structural analyses and a lack of mass segregation suggest it is likely a dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2402.08731].

sources.summary: The paper confirms Horologium II as a real stellar system and dwarf-galaxy candidate, detecting it as a significant DES Y1A1 overdensity and deriving distance, size, ellipticity, luminosity, age, and metallicity parameters.

quotes.quote: As mentioned earlier, report on the identification of an extra MW satellite galaxy candidate besides those found by and . Our reanalysis of Y1A1 presented here confirms this object, Horologium II, as a real stellar system. In fact, once we allow for a deeper magnitude threshold, as explained in sec:data , we detect it not only with the method described in sec:method but also with the maximum-likelihood satellite search method detailed out in , Figure HorIImap shows the same information as Figure J0034map , but now for Horologium II.

A clear overdensity of stars is seen in the density map on the sky. The statistical significance of this overdensity is close to 8 times the expected Poisson fluctuation in the background. Using the same maximum-likelihood fit method to the on-sky and CMD distributions as in the previous section, we obtain the following structural and isochrone parameters: (m-M)_0 19.5 (heliocentric distance D 79.4 kpc), (Age) 9.9 , [ Fe/H ] 1.2 , A_V 0.01 , r_h 2.2 ( 51.2 pc), position angle 106 , elipticity 0.6 , and absolute magnitude M_V 3.1 .

sources.summary: Horologium II remains ambiguous between globular cluster and galaxy, but the paper derives membership, metallicity limits, and LMC-association constraints from its motion.

quotes.quote: Here we aim at deriving the bulk motions and estimates of the internal velocity dispersion and metallicity properties in four recently discovered distant southern dwarf galaxy candidates, Columba I, Reticulum III, Phoenix II and Horologium II. We combine Gaia DR2 astrometric measurements, photometry and new FLAMES/GIRAFFE intermediate resolution spectroscopic data in the region of the near-IR Ca II triplet lines; such combination is essential for finding potential member stars in these low luminosity systems. We find very likely member stars in all four satellites and are able to determine (or place limits on) the systems bulk motions and average internal properties. The systems are found to be very metal-poor, in agreement with dwarf galaxies and dwarf galaxy candidates of similar luminosity.
For the case of Horo II our data are equally well consistent with a nature as globular cluster or galaxy, because our limits on intrinsic spreads are unconstraining.

An association of Horo II to the LMC becomes significantly more or less likely depending on whether the brightest (most distant in R _ half ) candidate member is truly a member or not. When this star is included, the most likely pole is formally determined with a higher accuracy, l=156 17 and b=-5 12 degree, and it is at only 16 degree distance of the LMC. When excluding it, the most likely pole direction changes very significantly (to l =258 and b =37 ) and the 1- region spans nearly two thirds of the sky, making a prior association with the LMC less likely (although not completely excluded).

sources.summary: The paper treats Horologium II as an LMC-associated UFD candidate, deriving HST structural parameters, integrated magnitude, and noting evidence favoring a likely dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: Horologium II discovered Hor II in the DES Y1A1 images, measuring an a_ h, exp =2 09^ +0.44 _ 0.41 ( r_ h in their paper). The only other structural parameter measurement is from ; using DES DR1, they fit a_ h, p 2 5 , which agreed within 1 . Hor II is one of our targets that had two telescope pointings, giving us approximately a combined FOV of 3 3 6 7 .

Our integrated M_V=-2.1 0.2 mag is 0.5 magnitudes fainter than the M_V=-2.6 presented by , but still within 1.8 agreement. There has not yet been a spectroscopic study of Hor II, but found no evidence for mass segregation and suggested that Hor II is likely a dwarf galaxy.

gmw_00009160.name: AS2COS 0001.2
gmw_00009160.overview: AS2COS 0001.2 is a high-redshift submillimeter galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 4.63 [arXiv:2003.05484, arXiv:2112.07430]. It is gravitationally bound to its companion, AS2COS 0001.1, forming a physically associated pair that is likely undergoing a rapid, highly efficient merger [arXiv:2003.05484, arXiv:2112.07430].

sources.summary: AS2COS 0001.2 is the fainter z=4.635 SMG companion to AS2COS 0001.1; their offsets and velocity fields suggest a physically associated efficient merger.

quotes.quote: We note that these sources have also been discussed in a very recent paper by , where they are named AzTEC2-A and AzTEC2-B respectively, and we compare our results to those from their analysis in the following.

AS2COS 0001.1 0001.2 were identified in an archival ALMA observation of S2COSMOS 0001, the highest significance source in the S2COSMOS survey (SNR = 28, S_ 850 m =, 16.8 mJy). The ALMA-detected SMGs have 870- m flux densities of 13.5 0.3 and 3.6 0.2 mJy -- where these fluxes are based on the line-free continuum in the ALMA cubes. As discussed by and , the ALMA observations of both these sources show strong lines at 337 GHz with apparently high equivalent widths (see Figure fig:cii ). The ^2 P _ 3 / 2 ^2 P _ 1 / 2 fine structure line of atomic carbon (C ^+ ) at 157.7 m, hereafter [ C ii ] , is typically the strongest far-infrared emission line in the spectra of star-forming galaxies (e.g. \ Brauher08,Santos13 ). The [ C ii ] emission can comprise 2 per cent of the total bolometric luminosity of a source, and is typically an order of magnitude brighter than other atomic or molecular emission (e.g. \ [ N ii ] 122 m, [ O i ] 145 m, [ N ii ] 205 m, or mid- J ^ 12 CO). At the depth of our observations, [ C ii ] is the most likely identification for the line emission from AS2COS 0001.1 0001.2, placing these sources at a redshift of z = 4.624 0.001 and 4.635 0.001, respectively.

Hence the observed spatial and velocity offsets between AS2COS 0001.1 0001.2 are consistent with them occupying a dark matter halo which a mass of 10 ^ 13 , suggesting that it is likely these SMGs are physically-associated within a single dark matter halo (see also the discussion of S2COSMOS 0003 in Wang16 ). Based on the orientation of their velocity fields, these two galaxies appear to be co-rotating in a prograde orbit, with a velocity offset comparable to their internal rotation velocities, suggesting the possibility we are witnessing a rapid and highly efficient merger.

sources.summary: The paper describes AS2COS0001.2 as a companion SMG with marginal line detection here, stronger previous detections, z=4.633, and likely bound-pair status.

quotes.quote: We obtain a significant detection from AS2COS0008.2 and a marginal detection from AS2COS0001.2. Although marginal ( 2 over the FWHM) in our data, the same line from AS2COS0001.2 (and AS2COS0001.1) was detected in high significance ( >5 ) by Jimenez-Andrade:2020aa with much deeper data from NOEMA, and Simpson:2020aa and Mitsuhashi:2021aa both reported detection of [ CII ] line from AS2COS0001.2. For the following analyses we therefore adopt the redshift measured by Jimenez-Andrade:2020aa for AS2COS0001.2, which is 4.633 0.001. Together with the previously reported line detection toward AS2COS0001.2, we find that the velocity differences of these two pairs are both within 500 km s ^ 1 , at the projected distances of 20-30 kpc, suggesting that they are gravitationally bound systems therefore confirmed physically associated pairs.

gmw_00009161.name: GRB 190106A
gmw_00009161.overview: GRB 190106A is a star-forming galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 1.8599 [arXiv:2308.14812]. Observations of its afterglow spectrum reveal multiple metal velocity components and a visual extinction of 0.27 mag [arXiv:2308.14812].

sources.summary: The paper uses GRB 190106A as a GRB-selected high-redshift galaxy sightline, measuring neutral hydrogen, metal absorption components, redshift, and visual extinction.

quotes.quote: Here we study the evolution of the gas-phase metallicities, dust-to-gas (DTG), and dust-to-metal (DTM) ratios of 36 star-forming galaxies at 1.7 < z < 6.3 probed by gamma-ray bursts (GRBs). We compile all GRB-selected galaxies with intermediate ( R = 7000 ) to high ( R >40,000 ) resolution spectroscopic data, including three new sources, for which at least one refractory (e.g. Fe) and one volatile (e.g. S or Zn) element have been detected at S/N >3 . GRB 190106A GRB 190106A To determine the metallicity of GRB 190106A, we first measure the \ column density based on the broad Lyman- transition using VoigtFit . The VLT/X-shooter afterglow spectrum and the best-fit model with (N_ HI / cm ^ 2 ) = 21.00 0.04 is shown in Fig. afig:grb190106a_hi . GRB 190106A shows multiple velocity components as displayed in Fig. afig:grb190106a_met , but we report only the sum for the Zn and Fe transitions in Table tab:results for the metallicity and depletion measurements. The spectroscopic redshift of the strongest low-ionization component which we consider as the redshift of the GRB is z_ GRB = 1.8599 . Modelling the observed afterglow spectrum with an SMC extinction curve parametrization from Gordon03 , yields A_V = 0.27 0.03 mag (see Fig. afig:grb190106a_av ).

gmw_00009162.name: GRB 190919B
gmw_00009162.overview: GRB 190919B is a star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z = 3.2241 [arXiv:2308.14812]. Spectroscopic observations of its gas-phase properties reveal a hydrogen column density of 21.49 and a very low visual extinction of less than 0.03 mag [arXiv:2308.14812].

sources.summary: The paper treats GRB 190919B as a GRB-selected galaxy sightline, deriving its hydrogen column, single-component metal abundances at z=3.2241, and low extinction.

quotes.quote: Here we study the evolution of the gas-phase metallicities, dust-to-gas (DTG), and dust-to-metal (DTM) ratios of 36 star-forming galaxies at 1.7 < z < 6.3 probed by gamma-ray bursts (GRBs). We compile all GRB-selected galaxies with intermediate ( R = 7000 ) to high ( R >40,000 ) resolution spectroscopic data, including three new sources, for which at least one refractory (e.g. Fe) and one volatile (e.g. S or Zn) element have been detected at S/N >3 . GRB 190919B GRB 190919B Following the same procedure as in Sect. ssec:a1 but for GRB 190919B, we derive a best-fit \ column density of (N_ HI / cm ^ 2 ) = 21.49 0.03 as shown in Fig. afig:grb190919b_hi . We determine the metal abundances from the single absorption component detected in the afterglow spectrum at z_ GRB = 3.2241 , as reported in Table tab:results and shown in Fig. afig:grb190919b_met . The XRT photon index = 2.10 represents an intrinsic optical/near-infrared power-law slope of = -1.40 , which yields a visual extinction of A_V < 0.03 mag (at 1 ), see Fig. afig:grb190919b_av .

gmw_00009163.name: GRB 191011A
gmw_00009163.overview: GRB 191011A is a star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.7204 [arXiv:2308.14812]. Spectroscopic observations of its gamma-ray burst sightline reveal a neutral hydrogen column density of 21.65 and a visual extinction of less than 0.43 mag [arXiv:2308.14812].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes GRB 191011A as a GRB-selected galaxy sightline, computing neutral hydrogen, summed metal abundances at z=1.7204, and visual extinction.

quotes.quote: Here we study the evolution of the gas-phase metallicities, dust-to-gas (DTG), and dust-to-metal (DTM) ratios of 36 star-forming galaxies at 1.7 < z < 6.3 probed by gamma-ray bursts (GRBs). We compile all GRB-selected galaxies with intermediate ( R = 7000 ) to high ( R >40,000 ) resolution spectroscopic data, including three new sources, for which at least one refractory (e.g. Fe) and one volatile (e.g. S or Zn) element have been detected at S/N >3 . GRB 191011A GRB 191011A For GRB 191011A, we compute an \ column density of (N_ HI / cm ^ 2 ) = 21.65 0.08 as shown in Fig. afig:grb191011a_hi . We determine the metal abundances from the sum of the velocity components detected in the afterglow spectrum, with the redshift of the strongest component being z_ GRB = 1.7204 , as reported in Table tab:results and shown in Fig. afig:grb191011a_met . The XRT photon index = 1.89 represents an intrinsic optical/near-infrared power-law slope of = -1.61 , which yields a visual extinction of A_V < 0.43 0.03 mag, see Fig. afig:grb191011a_av .

gmw_00009164.name: X Comae
gmw_00009164.overview: X Comae is a bright Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of 0.091 [arXiv:astro-ph/0610424, arXiv:astro-ph/0606114]. It is situated behind the Coma cluster and is known to exhibit X-ray flux variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0610424, arXiv:astro-ph/0606114].

sources.summary: The paper uses X Comae, a Seyfert 1 AGN behind Coma, as the XMM-Newton background source, giving its redshift, position, low X-ray flux, and simultaneous EPIC/RGS coverage.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present our XMM-Newton RGS observations of the Seyfert 1 AGN X Comae, which is located behind the Coma cluster to the north but well inside the cluster virial radius. We also describe the EPIC spectra of the Coma cluster gas at the position of X Comae that were obtained simultaneously.
At a redshift of 0.091 0.001 1985MNRAS.216.1043B , X Comae is the brightest X-ray object behind the Coma cluster. Its position is (13 ^ h 00 ^ m 22 ^ s .17, +28 ^ 24 ' 2 '' .6) (J2000.0).

The instrument mode and the filter used are shown in Table tab:instrument , and the gross and net exposure times and flux of X Comae are summarized in Table tab:observation . X Comae had a flux at or below its hitherto historical low during our observations.

sources.summary: The paper identifies X Comae as a bright Seyfert galaxy in Coma's field, considers whether its variable hard emission could contaminate cluster measurements, and reports no excess at its position.

quotes.quote: This uncertainty is substantiated by the presence of other sources present in the FOV like X Comae, a bright Seyfert galaxy discovered by /PSPC 30 northeast of the . Even if X Comae may experience flux variability, Fusco-Femiano (1999) demonstrated that because of its steep spectral index, an unusually strong variability would be necessary in order to have a significant effect on the total detected flux. Hence, its hard emission would be blended into the global flux and not detected as variable. Nonetheless, since / does not detect an excess above 3 at the position of X Comae in either of the four energy bands defined in s:obs , we assume that the spectra obtained by and are truly representative of the emission from Coma and that there really exists a HXR tail, despite the ongoing dispute on this issue .

gmw_00009165.name: [ VV98 ] J010250.2 220929
gmw_00009165.overview: [ VV98 ] J010250.2 220929 is an active galactic nucleus and quasar with a refined redshift of z = 0.7548 [arXiv:1909.10518]. Its sightline intersects an X-ray filament of the Abell 133 galaxy cluster [arXiv:1909.10518].

sources.summary: The quasar sightline intersects an Abell 133 X-ray filament; the paper presents COS and Magellan observations, gives GALEX brightnesses, and refines its redshift to z = 0.7548.

quotes.quote: One of these filaments is intersected by the sightline to quasar [ VV98 ] J010250.2 220929, allowing for a first-ever census of cold, cool, and warm gas in a filament of the cosmic web where hot gas has been seen in X-ray emission. Here, we present UV observations with the Cosmic Origins Spectrograph and optical observations with the Magellan Echellette spectrograph of [ VV98 ] J010250.2 220929.

Included in that survey is a potentially interesting target: \ (also known as XRS 0102 2209, hereafter ), which was identified by 1998MNRAS.299.1047W as an active galactic nucleus behind Abell 133. In archival observations with the Galaxy Evolution Explorer 2007ApJS..173..682M the quasar has an FUV brightness of 20.77 0.26 \ mag and an NUV brightness of 20.42 0.16 \ mag. \ is projected along one of the filaments identified by 2013HEAD...1340101V and at 1100 pkpc ( 0.7R_ vir ) from the center of Abell 133, it is outside R_ 500 , as is shown in Figure fig:QSO_position . \ therefore provides the perfect opportunity to obtain the first comprehensive baryonic census along a filament of the cosmic web.

Using the ASERA toolkit 2013A&C.....3...65Y to identify the peaks of broad-line emission in Mg 2 and H , we compute a quasar redshift of z = 0.7548 , a refinement from the value reported by 1998MNRAS.299.1047W ( 1998MNRAS.299.1047W ; z=0.766 ).

gmw_00009166.name: SMM J 04542-0301
gmw_00009166.overview: SMM J 04542-0301 is a lensed, high-redshift submillimeter system located in the core of the cluster MS 0451.6-0305 [arXiv:astro-ph/0603466, arXiv:0910.1213]. Its radio and submillimeter emission is associated with a multiply imaged merger at z=2.9, which contains a Lyman Break Galaxy and two Extremely Red Objects [arXiv:astro-ph/0603466, arXiv:0910.1213].

sources.summary: The paper describes SMM J04542 0301 as a lensed high-redshift submillimeter system whose radio emission is multiply imaged and modeled as three background star-forming sources.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present VLA 1.4 GHz radio observations of a second case, SMM J04542 0301 C02 , associated with the cluster MS0451.6 0305. MS0451.6 0305 is a cluster of galaxies situated at z=0.55 GL94 that has recently been studied using optical and near-Infrared (NIR) data B04 . They conclude that the sub-mm emission is probably related to an interacting system of three objects lying at z 2.9 : a Lyman Break Galaxy (LBG) and a pair of Extremely Red Objects (EROs). In the optical and NIR images, it is proposed that the LBG is imaged into two visible arcs, and the ERO pair are responsible for 5 additional sources of emission in the field B04, T03 . However, the emission coming from the north-eastern and the central regions of the sub-mm image, cannot be fully reproduced using the LBG and the ERO pair alone .

We have presented deep VLA archive observations at 1.4 GHz of the central region of the cluster MS0451.6 0305, discovering multiply-imaged radio counterparts to the sub-mm emission SMM J04542 0301, originally discovered by and recently studied by . This is the second case of multiply-lensed radio emission coming from an intrinsically faint SMG the first case was SMM J16359+6612 in A2218, see K04, G05 . With a resolution of 7 6 arcseconds, the radio emission associated with SMM J0452-0301 can best be represented by seven discrete Gaussian components. A simple lens model of this system (based on a NFW mass profile) can reproduce the positions of the radio components assuming that they are multiple images of 3 background sources located at z=2.9.

sources.summary: The paper describes SMM J 04542-0301 as an elongated submillimeter source in MS 0451.6-0305, likely linked to a multiply imaged z=2.9 merger traced by radio emission.

quotes.quote: SMM J 04542-0301 is an elongated region of bright sub-mm emission located in the core of the cluster MS 0451.6-0305 . It has been suggested BO04.1 to be partially produced by a multiply-imaged z=2.9 merger which contains a LBG (lensed as ARC1 and ARC1 ci), and two EROs (lensed as B1, B2, C1, C2 and B3/C3). Given the low resolution and poor positional accuracy of the sub-mm map, it is very difficult to confirm the connection between SMM J 04542-0301 and the optical/NIR lensed images. However, high resolution radio interferometric observations can help to establish this connection thanks to the FIR-radio correlation, which has being found to be valid for SMGs KO06.1, VL07.1, IB08.1, MI09.1 .

On the other hand, the other five radio detections constitute a strong observational evidence in favor of the merger scenario proposed by . The evidence that supports this conclusion can be summarized as follows: if the optical arc and the EROs are multiple images produced by three different regions of a merger at z=2.9 , the dust obscured material between these regions is expected to be lensed in the same way, but only visible at radio and sub-mm. In agreement with this scenario, the shape of the radio emission observed at the sub-mm peak follows the distribution of the optical arc and the ERO images remarkably well (Fig. tap30_comparison ).

gmw_00009167.name: RJ
gmw_00009167.overview: RJ is a bright and extended radio source that lacks an identified optical or near-infrared counterpart [arXiv:0910.1213]. Its extended morphology may indicate active galactic nucleus activity with radio jets, or it could represent a star-forming submillimeter galaxy [arXiv:0910.1213].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RJ as the brightest extended radio detection in the submillimeter region, lacking an optical/NIR counterpart and plausibly representing either AGN emission or a star-forming SMG.

quotes.quote: Finally, note that no optical/NIR counterpart has been identified with RJ. Therefore, given the depths of the optical and NIR images, it seems more likely that the extended morphology of RJ corresponds to an AGN rather than a resolved low redshift star forming galaxy. Within the AGN scenario, we expect that the peak of the radio emission corresponds to the position of an undetected optical source, and the extensions in opposite directions are two jets coming from it. Another conceivable scenario would be that the galaxy G14 is an AGN host with a one sided radio extension. However, the mayor axes of RJ does not seem to be aligned with G14, and this kind of AGN is not very common.

The source RJ is the brightest and most extended of these six detections, with no obvious counterpart in the optical/NIR. Although its extended morphology could be interpreted as an AGN signature, the degeneracy between redshift and dust temperature allows the observed S_ 850 m /S_ 1.4 GHz at the position of RJ to be consistent with a starforming SMG that follows the FIR-radio correlation. In any case, the low resolution of the 850 m map makes impossible to determine if RJ is really contributing to the observed sub-mm emission in this region.

gmw_00009168.name: BG1
gmw_00009168.overview: BG1 is one of the two brightest elliptical galaxies in the RX J1548.9+0851 system [arXiv:1202.4470]. It is among the oldest objects in the sample, anchoring the core of the system's old-galaxy velocity substructure [arXiv:1202.4470].

sources.summary: BG1 is one of RX J1548.9+0851's two brightest ellipticals; the paper finds it among the oldest objects, anchoring the core of the old-galaxy velocity substructure.

quotes.quote: The two brightest ellipticals, hereafter BG1 and BG2, are the oldest objects in the sample (with the exception of one faint galaxy, exhibiting a huge error bar in age, however) and build up the centers of two subsamples in the velocity distribution of old galaxies (cross-hatched histograms). While the oldest galaxies around BG1 form the core of the overall velocity distribution, old galaxies around BG2 are found at systematically lower velocities.

Dashed ellipses in Fig. agemetallicitymaps and agevelocity b contain the same objects, indicating that the subpopulations around BG1 and BG2 in radial velocity space are also more or less spatially correlated.

Old galaxies are mainly found at velocities around the two brightest ellipticals BG1 and BG2 which are also the oldest objects in the system. In contrast, young objects show a smooth distribution over all velocities and exhibit a much larger dispersion supporting the highlighted scenario where the old galaxy population assembled first, leaving enough time to virialize around the most massive subhaloes BG1 and BG2.

gmw_00009169.name: BG2
gmw_00009169.overview: BG2 is a bright, old elliptical galaxy located in the RX J1548.9+0851 system [arXiv:1202.4470]. It dominates the luminosity of the system's faint, young galaxies and anchors a lower-velocity subpopulation of old galaxies [arXiv:1202.4470].

sources.summary: BG2 is the other bright old elliptical in RX J1548.9+0851; it dominates the luminosity of faint young galaxies and anchors a lower-velocity old subpopulation.

quotes.quote: The two brightest ellipticals, hereafter BG1 and BG2, are the oldest objects in the sample (with the exception of one faint galaxy, exhibiting a huge error bar in age, however) and build up the centers of two subsamples in the velocity distribution of old galaxies (cross-hatched histograms). While the oldest galaxies around BG1 form the core of the overall velocity distribution, old galaxies around BG2 are found at systematically lower velocities.

This restriction does not lower the measured dispersion as severely as the exclusion of BG2 which dominates the luminosity of all other faint and young galaxies by far. Omitting BG2 leads to much smaller values in the central region for both the whole (321 km s ^ 1 ) and old (266 km s ^ 1 ) galaxy populations.

Old galaxies are mainly found at velocities around the two brightest ellipticals BG1 and BG2 which are also the oldest objects in the system. In contrast, young objects show a smooth distribution over all velocities and exhibit a much larger dispersion supporting the highlighted scenario where the old galaxy population assembled first, leaving enough time to virialize around the most massive subhaloes BG1 and BG2.

gmw_00009170.name: RX J 0439.6-5311
gmw_00009170.overview: RX J 0439.6-5311 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1905.02920]. It hosts a supermassive black hole with a mass of approximately 6 to 9 million solar masses that undergoes super-Eddington accretion [arXiv:1905.02920].

sources.summary: The paper fits RX J 0439.6-5311 with a slim-disc model, finding a super-Eddington accretion rate and reduced spin constraints because advection lowers radiative efficiency.

quotes.quote: We use this to fit the broadband spectrum of one of the most extreme Active Galactic Nuclei, the Narrow Line Seyfert 1 RX J 0439.6-5311 , which has a black hole mass of (6 9) 10^6M_ as derived from the H line width. This cannot be fit with the standard disc emissivity at this mass, as even zero spin models overproduce the observed luminosity. Instead, we show that the spectrum is well reproduced by the slim disc model, giving mass accretion rates around (5 10) Eddington limit. There is no constraint on black hole spin as the efficiency is reduced by advection.

gmw_00009171.name: GMP 2599
gmw_00009171.overview: GMP 2599 is an asymmetric, blue Sa-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1006.4867, arXiv:2207.06624] that is strongly deficient in neutral hydrogen [arXiv:1006.4867]. The galaxy exhibits distorted optical structures and one-sided radio tails extending to the southeast, which are indicative of ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:1006.4867, arXiv:2207.06624].

sources.summary: GMP 2599/KUG 1259+279A is a distorted spiral with GALEX streaks, MegaCam knots, offset radio and X-ray emission, strong HI deficiency, and eastward H emission.

quotes.quote: GMP 2599 = KUG 1259+279A This spiral galaxy appears distorted in the GALEX image, showing broad streaks that point south-east away from the cluster centre. Some faint knots are visible in the MegaCam images. Miller et al. (2009) note the radio emission is offset from the galaxy and suggestive of ram-pressure stripping, while Finoguenov et al. (2004) found that the X-ray source is offset to the east of the galaxy. Gavazzi et al. (2006) detected the galaxy in HI but indicate that it is strongly gas-deficient (Def _ HI =0.76 ). The H data show an offset in the emission to the east of the galaxy centre.

sources.summary: The paper describes KUG 1258 + 279A as an asymmetric blue Sa emission-line disk with southeast RPS radio tails and measured host-tail spectral indices.

quotes.quote: 19 (q): KUG 1258 + 279A The optical host shows emission lines of Sa type and is an asymmetrical blue disk galaxy possibly due to the presence of dust BothunandDressler . The radio emission at both bands, 250--500 MHz and 550--850 MHz, is one-sided showing asymmetric tails to the southeast, extending beyond the optical host galaxy with the 550--850 MHz band image showing smaller extent than the 250--500 MHz band image. The source is also classified as an RPS galaxy see Sec. tab:tab3-rps Chenetal . The largest projected linear extent of the source in the 250--500 MHz band is 1 01 ( 28.3 kpc). The host galaxy (D _ 25 = 39 24) and the tail have spectral index, (400--700 MHz), 0.39 0.07 and 0.56 0.12, respectively.

gmw_00009172.name: GMP 2640
gmw_00009172.overview: GMP 2640 is a massive dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2111.01140] classified as an edge-on S0 or spiral galaxy [arXiv:1006.4867]. It features a disturbed inner morphology [arXiv:1006.4867], a central star-cluster structure [arXiv:2111.01140], and a long, UV-bright stripped tail [arXiv:2111.01140, arXiv:1006.4867].

sources.summary: The paper presents GMP 2640 as the sample's most massive dwarf galaxy, with a 250 kpc UV-bright stripped tail, pronounced truncation-age gradients, kinematic disturbances, and central star-cluster structure.

quotes.quote: Young stars are UV-bright and expose a 250 kpc long tail in the most massive galaxy in our sample, GMP 2640 .

Deriving mass-to-light ratios from our best-fitting parameters, we estimate the total stellar masses of our PSGs to be in the range 5.1 10^8 M_ (GMP 3176) to 2.36 10^9 M_ (GMP 2640). Therefore, despite their large sizes these are all dwarf galaxies. Truncation age profiles (see Extended Data Figure psg_spop ) for most galaxies show weak global gradients across the disc superimposed on radially increasing gradients. The radial gradients are more pronounced for denser and more massive galaxies, e.g. GMP 4348, GMP 2640, GMP 3640.

GMP 4060, GMP 2640 and GMP 4188 have noticeable kinematical disturbances localised in the area of significant morphological irregularities. Detailed analysis of high-resolution HST images for the brightest galaxy in our sample, GMP 2640, revealed a large number of luminous star clusters in its central part whose asymmetric distributions on the sides of the slit causes the non-uniform slit illumination and, hence, affects the radial velocity measurements.

sources.summary: GMP 2640/KUG 1258+277 is described as an edge-on S0 or spiral with a 100 kpc GALEX trail, disturbed inner morphology, HI non-detection, and possible tidal stripping.

quotes.quote: GMP 2640 = KUG 1258+277 This appears to be an edge-on S0 or spiral galaxy, which presents a very long trail in the GALEX imaging, extending 3.5 arcmin (100 kpc) north towards the cluster centre. In the MegaCam data, the trail is faintly visible and the inner morphology is clearly disturbed. The galaxy is an HI non-detection in Bravo-Alfaro et al. (2000), with an upper limit which implies that it is gas deficient (Def _ HI >0.7 ). In the INT data, we do not detect any H emission, either from the galaxy itself or from the trail. Close in projection are an elliptical (GMP 2670 at 0.5 arcmin or 15 kpc, with 360 \ radial velocity difference) and a spiral (GMP 2601 at 1.2 arcmin or 35 kpc, with 1800 \ radial velocity difference); hence this could possibly be a case of tidal stripping by a neighbouring galaxy, rather interaction with the cluster itself.

gmw_00009173.name: GMP 2910
gmw_00009173.overview: GMP 2910 is an irregular or spiral post-starburst galaxy [arXiv:1006.4867] that exhibits an amorphous or tailed radio morphology [arXiv:2207.06624]. The galaxy features ongoing nuclear star formation [arXiv:1006.4867] and a narrow, star-forming ultraviolet and H-alpha tail extending to the northeast [arXiv:1006.4867, arXiv:2207.06624].

sources.summary: GMP 2910/MRK 0060 NED01 is an irregular or spiral post-starburst galaxy with ongoing nuclear star formation and a narrow UV/H tail containing stripped, star-forming material.

quotes.quote: GMP 2910 = MRK 0060 NED01 This irregular or spiral galaxy has a post-starburst spectrum in the disk region, with the burst age estimated at 250 Myr and ongoing star formation in the nucleus (Caldwell, Rose Dendy 1999). In our GALEX image, we observe a narrow tail of length 0.5 arcmin (15 kpc), extending to the north-east. Stripping in this galaxy was first discussed by Yagi et al. (2007), who reported a 60 kpc H tail, which is also seen in our INT H imaging. This remarkably narrow and straight feature is also clearly seen in the deep Adami u band image, and is co-located with the UV trail. The presence of continuum emission suggests that star formation is taking place in the stripped material, not merely ionization of a purely gaseous tail as proposed by Yagi et al.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 60 as an RPS radio source with northeast extension, diffuse northern low-frequency emission, amorphous/tailed morphology, and 11.5 kpc extent.

quotes.quote: (o): Mrk 60 (a.k.a. D100, GMP 2910) Both, the 250--500 MHz and 550-250 MHz band images show extensions in radio emission toward the northeast, beyond the extent of the optical host. The 250--500 MHz band image also shows diffuse extensions toward the north, providing it an amorphous-like radio morphology, though the 550-250 MHz band image suggests it has a tailed radio morphology. The largest projected linear extent of the source in the 250--500 MHz band is 11.5 kpc. The integrated spectral index is (400--700 MHz) = 0.93 0.46, which agrees, within uncertainties with the measurement at 1.4 GHz see Table tab:tab3-rps , Chenetal .

gmw_00009174.name: GMP 4555
gmw_00009174.overview: GMP 4555 is a disturbed Sb galaxy that may consist of two overlapping galaxies [arXiv:2207.06624]. It exhibits asymmetric radio emission, a UV plume, and H-alpha emission extending to the west and southwest, which are indicative of ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:2207.06624, arXiv:1006.4867].

sources.summary: The paper describes KUG 1255 + 283 as an Sb optical host with one-sided western radio tails, smaller high-frequency extent, and RPS classification.

quotes.quote: 7 (): KUG 1255 + 283 (a.k.a. (GMP 4555, MCG + 05 31 035) The elliptical optical host is Sb and there are possibly two overlapping galaxies with their nuclei 3 ^ apart BothunandDressler . The radio emission at both bands, 250--500 MHz and 550--850 MHz, is one-sided, showing asymmetric tails toward the west, extending 19.9 kpc beyond the optical host galaxy. The radio extent in the 550--850 MHz band image is smaller than in the 250--500 MHz band image. The source is also classified as an RPS galaxy see Sec. tab:tab3-rps Chenetal . The largest projected linear extent of the source in the 250--500 MHz is 0 67 ( 18.8 kpc).

sources.summary: GMP 4555/KUG 1255+283 is a disturbed galaxy with a westward UV plume, southwest u-band filaments, asymmetric H emission, and offset radio/X-ray morphology suggestive of stripping.

quotes.quote: GMP 4555 = KUG 1255+283 The GALEX imaging reveals a UV plume to the west of this disturbed galaxy, oriented away from the cluster centre. In the MegaCam u band data, the inner part of the plume is delineated by faint filaments extending to the south-west The H emission is also asymmetric and extended to the south-west. Miller et al. (2009) note that the radio emission is offset from the galaxy and suggestive of ram-pressure stripping. The X-ray morphology also appears extended to the west in figure 3 of Finoguenov et al. (2004), although they do not specifically discuss this source.

gmw_00009175.name: GMP 4570
gmw_00009175.overview: GMP 4570 is a distorted spiral galaxy [arXiv:1006.4867] that exhibits a tailed radio morphology [arXiv:2207.06624]. Its radio emission extends westward beyond the optical host [arXiv:2207.06624], coinciding with a clumpy ultraviolet extension and u-band trails that stretch at least 12 kiloparsecs from the galaxy center [arXiv:1006.4867].

sources.summary: The paper describes GMP 4570 as a tailed RPS radio source with westward emission beyond the optical host, 7.0 kpc extent, and consistent 1.4 GHz spectrum.

quotes.quote: (b): GMP 4570 The radio emission peaks at the location of the host galaxy and extends toward the west, beyond the extent of the optical host, suggesting it is a tailed radio morphology source. The largest projected linear extent of the source in the 250--500 MHz band is 7.0 kpc. The source was not covered in our 550--850 MHz band pointings. It was detected in the measurement at 1.4 GHz see Table tab:tab3-rps , Chenetal . Thus, the integrated spectral index is (400--1400 MHz) = 0.51 0.11, which agrees, within uncertainties with the upper limit, (400--700 MHz) 0.98 0.52.

sources.summary: GMP 4570 is a distorted spiral with a westward clumpy UV extension, no INT H detection because of velocity shift, and u-band trails and knots matching UV peaks.

quotes.quote: GMP 4570 This is a distorted spiral galaxy, for which the GALEX image shows a clumpy UV extension to the west, away from the cluster centre. H is not detected from this object in the INT narrow-band imaging, because its large velocity with respect to the cluster shifts the emission line outside of the filter profile. The u band MegaCam image shows some trails and knots, coincident with the UV peaks, extending at least 0.4 arcmin (12 kpc) from the galaxy centre.

gmw_00009176.name: GMP 4629
gmw_00009176.overview: GMP 4629 is a distorted spiral galaxy that exhibits an asymmetric extension in ultraviolet light [arXiv:1006.4867]. It features multiple blue knots to its northwest and secondary hydrogen-alpha emission peaks to its north and northwest, which may be influenced by a nearby elliptical companion [arXiv:1006.4867].

sources.summary: GMP 4629 is a distorted spiral with asymmetric GALEX emission, northwest blue knots, northern H peaks, and a nearby elliptical companion making tidal interaction possible.

quotes.quote: GMP 4629 This distorted spiral shows an asymmetric extension in the GALEX image. In the MegaCam data, multiple blue knots are seen to the north-west of the galaxy, away from cluster centre. The H imaging shows secondary emission peaks to the north and north-west of the core. GMP 4629 lies close in projection (0.7 arcmin or 20 kpc) to an elliptical galaxy (GMP 4648), with 300 \ radial velocity difference, and hence a tidal interaction is possible in this case.

gmw_00009177.name: GMP 5422
gmw_00009177.overview: GMP 5422, also known as IC 3913, is a spiral galaxy located in the NGC 4839 group [arXiv:astro-ph/0109316, arXiv:1006.4867]. It features a regular gas disk extending beyond its optical radius [arXiv:astro-ph/0109316], opened southwest spiral arms, and enhanced star formation in the northeast [arXiv:1006.4867].

sources.summary: The paper places IC 3913 in southwest Coma near NGC 4839, with a regular extended gas disk, group-like velocity, and no evident strong ICM or tidal disturbance.

quotes.quote: IC 3913, KUG 1255+275, and Mrk 057 These three galaxies were detected in the SW of Coma, between 17 and 25 south of the cD galaxy NGC 4839. They display several common features: they are rich galaxies with regular gas distributions, where disks extend well beyond the optical (Figs. 1, 3 and 4). Systemic velocities for these galaxies are between 7400 and 7650 , close to the value of the NGC 4839 group, 7339 (Colless Dunn, 1996). Because of their position, lying far from the cluster center and outside the X-ray emission, no strong interaction with the ICM is expected. Although these three galaxies are likely to be part of the infalling NGC 4839 group (Neumann 2001), we see no signs of a gravitational interaction either optically or in or the radio emission.

sources.summary: GMP 5422/IC 3913 is a spiral in the NGC 4839 group with opened southwest arms and northeast star-formation enhancement, but prior studies did not suggest ongoing stripping.

quotes.quote: GMP 5422 = IC 3913 This is perhaps the least convincing example of stripping in our sample. The galaxy is a spiral with a distinct opening of the spiral arms to the south-west, and an enhancement of star formation to the north-east. The galaxy is part of the NGC 4839 group to the south-west of the cluster core, and lies beyond the extent of our H observations. Previous studies in HI (Bravo-Alfaro et al. 2001), in H (Gavazzi et al. 1998), and in radio continuum (Miller et al. 2009) have not suggested ongoing stripping in this galaxy.

gmw_00009178.name: SDSS J131122.15-012345.6
gmw_00009178.overview: SDSS J131122.15-012345.6 is a member galaxy of the Abell 1689 cluster [arXiv:1008.4140]. The galaxy hosted a likely tidal disruption flare that varied by a factor of 30 over at least two years [arXiv:1008.4140].

sources.summary: The paper reports a likely tidal disruption flare in SDSS J131122.15-012345.6, an Abell 1689 member whose flare varied by about 30 and had a soft X-ray blackbody spectrum.

quotes.quote: We examine the first results of an ongoing archival survey of galaxy clusters using \ and selected data, and report a likely tidal disruption flare from SDSS J131122.15-012345.6 in Abell 1689 . The flare is observed to vary by a factor of 30 over at least 2 years, to have maximum L_X(0.3-3.0 \ keV ) 5 10^ 42 \ erg \ s ^ 1 and to emit as a blackbody with kT 0.12 \ keV.

With a spectroscopic estimate of z=0.195 for SDSS J131122.15-012345.6, we take the galaxy to be a member of Abell 1689 . A variety of redshift estimates have been published for Abell 1689 ranging from 0.183 Struble99 to 0.203 Estrada07 . The high velocity dispersion of this massive cluster suggests that membership of the SDSS galaxy is plausible even for a low estimate of the cluster redshift.

gmw_00009179.name: 14^ h 29^ m 52.146^ s, +54^ d 47^ m 17.55^ s
gmw_00009179.overview: The object 14^ h 29^ m 52.146^ s, +54^ d 47^ m 17.55^ s is a radio-loud quasar located at a high redshift of z=6.18 [arXiv:2007.04735]. It is the most X-ray luminous quasar observed at z > 6, with a bolometric luminosity estimated at (2 - 3) 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 [arXiv:2007.04735].

sources.summary: The coordinate-identified z=6.18 radio-loud quasar is detected in X-rays, has extreme X-ray luminosity relative to optical/UV emission, and coincides closely with the reported eROSITA position.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: optical position of the quasar ( =14^ h 29^ m 52.146^ s , =+54^ d 47^ m 17.55^ s )

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of X-ray emission from , the most distant known radio-loud quasar at z=6.18 , on Dec. 10--11, 2019 with the \ telescope on board the \ satellite during its ongoing all-sky survey. The object was identified by cross-matching an intermediate / \ source catalog with the Pan-STARRS1 distant quasar sample at 5.6 < z < 6.7 . The measured flux 8 10^ 14 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 in the 0.3--2 keV energy band corresponds to an X-ray luminosity of 2.6^ +1.7 _ 1.0 10^ 46 erg s ^ 1 in the 2--10 keV rest-frame energy band, which renders \ the most X-ray luminous quasar ever observed at z > 6 . Combining our X-ray measurements with archival and new photometric measurements in other wavebands (radio to optical), we estimate the bolometric luminosity of this quasar at (2 - 3) 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 .

A new X-ray source was detected at the position =14^ h 29^ m 52.14^ s , =+54^ d 47^ m 15.7^ s with a 1 positional uncertainty of 4.5 arcsec as calculated by the ermldet task of eSASS. The detection likelihood is 20.1 in the 0.3--2.0 keV band, i.e. the detection is highly significant. The best-fit X-ray source position deviates by 1.9 arcsec from the optical position of the quasar ( =14^ h 29^ m 52.146^ s , =+54^ d 47^ m 17.55^ s ) in CFHTLS Hudelot_CFHTLS .

gmw_00009180.name: Q1549-C25
gmw_00009180.overview: Q1549-C25 is an isolated star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z=3.15 [arXiv:1606.00443]. It exhibits a clean detection of Lyman-continuum radiation, negligible dust extinction, and an estimated stellar mass of 7.9 billion solar masses [arXiv:1606.00443].

sources.summary: The paper presents Q1549-C25 as an isolated z=3.15 star-forming galaxy with uncontaminated LyC detection, negligible dust, and SED-inferred age and stellar mass.

quotes.quote: We present observations of Q1549-C25, an L^* star-forming galaxy at z=3.15 for which Lyman-continuum (LyC) radiation is significantly detected in deep Keck/LRIS spectroscopy. We find no evidence for contamination from a lower-redshift interloper close to the line of sight in the high signal-to-noise spectrum of Q1549-C25. Furthermore, the morphology of Q1549-C25 in V_ 606 , J_ 125 , and H_ 160 Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) imaging reveals that the object consists of a single, isolated component within 1" . In combination, these data indicate Q1549-C25 as a clean spectroscopic detection of LyC radiation, only the second such object discovered to date at z 3 . We model the spectral energy distribution (SED) of Q1549-C25, finding evidence for negligible dust extinction, an age (assuming continuous star formation) of 1 Gyr, and a stellar mass of M_*=7.9 10^ 9 M_ .

gmw_00009181.name: DESJ0053-2012
gmw_00009181.overview: DESJ0053-2012 is a lensed quasar at a redshift of approximately 3.8 [arXiv:1912.09133]. It is a quadruply imaged system whose gravitational lensing model is improved by including a nearby companion galaxy [arXiv:1912.09133].

sources.summary: DESJ0053-2012 is identified as the campaign’s only confirmed quad, a z~3.8 lensed quasar whose modeling improves when companion galaxy G2 is included.

quotes.quote: This system is the only confirmed quad from the 2017/2018 follow-up campaign. It was selected by the Gaia WISE selection of Section gaia1 as a Gaia DR1 double (G=19.21, 19.43) corresponding to a red WISE detection ( W1 W2 =0.55). The redshift of the system is z 3.8. It is not more precisely determined due to the strong absorption of Lyman alpha.

A SIE+shear model is insufficient to reproduce the positions and flux ratios, providing a best-fit ^2 30 for 4 degrees of freedom. The model requires a strong shear of 0.22, 159 degrees East of North, while the companion galaxy G2 is 4.3 arcseconds 131 degrees East of North. We choose to explicitly model G2 as an SIE with shape fixed to that of the light (as measured in the z band SOAR data), and leave the Einstein radius as a free parameter. This new model provides a best-fit ^2 = 2.43 for 3 degrees of freedom, now requiring a shear of 0.13, 20 degrees East of North.

gmw_00009182.name: DESJ0112-1650
gmw_00009182.overview: DESJ0112-1650 is a gravitational lens system featuring two quasars with highly similar spectra at a redshift of z = 0.99 [arXiv:1912.09133]. A massive lensing galaxy at z = 0.52 produces absorption features in both quasar images [arXiv:1912.09133].

sources.summary: DESJ0112-1650 is described as a likely lens with two similar z=0.99 quasars, z=0.52 galaxy absorption, a redder offset object, and unresolved image configuration.

quotes.quote: Resolved ESI spectra show two quasars with very similar spectra at z= 0.99. There is absorption due to a massive galaxy seen in both quasar images at z= 0.52 (from Ca H, K, G, and Na at 6063, 6116, 6635, and 9084 \ respectively), which is a promising sign of a lensing galaxy. While we lack high-resolution imaging of this system, the DES data clearly shows a redder object offset from the line joining the two blue point sources. This is often seen with fold quad configurations, where the faint counterimage is either highly reddened or the data are not deep enough, while one of the observed PSFs is a pair of close images, as was observed in the cases of PSJ0030-1525 lemon2018 or SDSSJ1330+1810 oguri1330 . We model the system as two point sources, and subsequently as three point sources, which both show an extended galaxy in the residuals.

Therefore we adopt three point sources and a galaxy (Sersic profile) as the fiducial pixel model for this system, as this fits the data without any obvious residual structures (Figure fig:J0112 ). We currently cannot determine whether the third point source, C, is a highly reddened quasar image, a foreground star, or structure due to the lensing galaxy. Our mass model given in Table tab:massmodels assumes only A and B are quasar images, with G being the only lensing galaxy.

gmw_00009183.name: DESJ0229+0320
gmw_00009183.overview: DESJ0229+0320 is a bright system consisting of two quasars at a redshift of 1.43, separated by 2.13 arcseconds [arXiv:1912.09133]. Although the system exhibits similar spectra and long-term variability, it is believed to be a binary quasar because no lensing galaxy has been detected in deep imaging [arXiv:1912.09133].

sources.summary: DESJ0229+0320 is treated as a bright two-quasar system with similar spectra and variability, but no detected lensing galaxy even in DES and HSC imaging.

quotes.quote: This system consists of two quasars at a redshift of 1.43, separated by 2.13 arcseconds. The spectra are similar, and the multi-band multi-epoch modelling (Section variability ) shows similar long-term variability. Furthermore, the apparent brightnesses of these quasars ( Gaia magnitudes of 18.15 and 18.79) place them at the bright end of the luminosity function for quasars. These characteristics are all in accordance with strong gravitational lensing --- similar variations, similar spectra, and large apparent brightness due to magnification --- however no lensing galaxy is seen in the deep DES images.

This magnitude is reached in a single-epoch image for a S/N ratio of 10 for an isolated point source, desdr1 ; however, if it lies close to one of the quasar images, or is particularly extended, then it might not be apparent after PSF subtraction. However, modelling 0.5 ^ seeing archival Hyper Suprime Cam z band data of the system also reveals no evidence of a lensing galaxy. We believe this is a binary quasar, but high-resolution imaging is required to fully rule out the lensing hypothesis.

gmw_00009184.name: DESJ0340-2545
gmw_00009184.overview: DESJ0340-2545 is a wide-separation lensed quasar system with two spectroscopically confirmed quasar images at a redshift of 1.68 [arXiv:1912.09133]. High-resolution imaging reveals three lensing galaxies and a third object that is modeled as a predicted third quasar image [arXiv:1912.09133].

sources.summary: DESJ0340-2545 is modeled as a wide-separation lensed quasar system with three lensing galaxies and a predicted third quasar image near component C.

quotes.quote: This system was found through a Gaia and WISE selection following Section gaia1 . A slit positioned at 27 degrees East of North confirms two quasar images separated by 6.8 arcseconds, each at z= 1.68 (see Figure fig:lens_gris ). NIRC2 imaging of the system clearly shows three lensing galaxies, two quasar images, and one further object, north of G1 (as labelled in Figure fig:NIRC2 ). Overlaying the Gaia detections for the system on the NIRC2 data reveals that this further object is exactly centred on a Gaia optical detection, and the DES colour image reveals a blue object blended with G1. We investigate whether this object could be the third image of the system, as these faint central images are often observed in lenses with multiple galaxies, resulting in an image slightly offset from one of the lensing-galaxy centres, for example, PSJ0630-1201 ostrovski2018 .

This model reproduces the two bright confirmed quasar images and their flux ratio well. It also predicts a third image 0.22 arcseconds away from the postulated image C, and with a flux 15 per cent that of A, while C has a measured flux of 10 per cent of A. This prediction is enough for us to consider C as a third quasar image without its spectroscopic confirmation.

gmw_00009185.name: DESJ0407-1931
gmw_00009185.overview: DESJ0407-1931 is a double quasar system at a redshift of z=2.26 [arXiv:1912.09133]. The system is strongly lensed by a foreground galaxy at z=0.288, with potential residuals from the lensed host galaxy detected in stacked modeling [arXiv:1912.09133].

sources.summary: DESJ0407-1931 is described as a double quasar with a blended lensing galaxy, matching z=2.26 emission lines, z=0.288 absorption, and possible host-galaxy residuals.

quotes.quote: The fainter image of this double is significantly blended with the lensing galaxy, but it is clearly detected in the DES stacked data, and the SOAR imaging. Resolved spectra also show identical emission lines of a quasar at z=2.26 . By subtracting off the scaled NTT spectrum of A from B, we are able to see clear signs of the lensing galaxy absorption lines at z= 0.288 as shown in Figure fig:J0407_spec . The stacked modelled residuals show an excess to the East and West of the brighter image, potentially caused by the lensed host galaxy.

gmw_00009186.name: DESJ0508-2748
gmw_00009186.overview: DESJ0508-2748 is a system whose spectra show a blended quasar at a redshift of z=1.14 [arXiv:1912.09133]. High-resolution imaging resolves two point sources separated by 0.65 arcseconds, though no lensing galaxy is apparent [arXiv:1912.09133].

sources.summary: DESJ0508-2748 remains inconclusive: spectra show a blended z=1.14 quasar, NIRC2 resolves two point sources, but no lensing galaxy is apparent.

quotes.quote: The NTT and ESI spectra for this object both show a blended z= 1.14 quasar without any other obvious features. NIRC2 data reveals two point sources, separated by 0.65 arcseconds, with no obvious lensing galaxy after PSF subtraction. We leave this object as inconclusive since the spectra are not resolved and there are no other indicators as to its nature (e.g., high proper motion Gaia detection, additional spectral features, or detection of a single quasar host galaxy).

gmw_00009187.name: DESJ2349-4518
gmw_00009187.overview: DESJ2349-4518 is a radio-detected double quasar lens system with a source redshift of z=2.90 [arXiv:1912.09133]. The system exhibits a high flux ratio, with residuals from a single lens model likely explained by microlensing, variability, or attenuation of the fainter image [arXiv:1912.09133].

sources.summary: DESJ2349-4518 is a high-flux-ratio radio-detected double quasar lens whose SIE residuals are attributed to flux-ratio effects from microlensing, variability, or attenuation.

quotes.quote: This system is a high-flux-ratio double (7.5 to 1), with a SUMSS (Sydney University Molonglo Sky Survey) detection of 13.6 mJy at 843 MHz sumss , with a source redshift of z=2.90 . This lens system lacks SOAR or NIRC2 data, so we show DES gri colour images with the brighter PSF and both PSFs subtracted in Figure fig:J2349 . This system is the only double with a non-zero ^ 2 for the single SIE fit. The largest contribution to the residuals is from the observed flux ratio being higher than the model predicts. This is most likely explained by microlensing, variability, or attenuation of the fainter image due to the lensing galaxy.

gmw_00009188.name: Pegasus III
gmw_00009188.overview: Pegasus III is a Milky Way ultra-faint dwarf galaxy located at a distance of approximately 174 kpc [arXiv:2105.11488]. It is dominated by an old stellar population with a metallicity spread of 0.4 dex [arXiv:2105.11488].

sources.summary: The paper studies Pegasus III variable stars, deriving a 174 kpc distance, old metal-poor stellar population, metallicity spread, and no isodensity evidence for connection to Pisces II.

quotes.quote: Using the intensity-averaged apparent magnitude of the bona-fide RRab star in each galaxy we estimate distance moduli of (m-M)_ 0 = 21.22 0.14 mag (d= 175 11 kpc) and 21.21 0.23 mag (d=174 18 kpc) for Pisces II and Pegasus III, respectively. Tests performed to disentangle the actual nature of variables with an uncertain classification led us to conclude that they most likely are bright, long period and very metal poor RRab members of their respective hosts. This may indicate that Pisces II and Pegasus III contain a dominant old stellar population (t > 12 Gyr) with metallicity < [ Fe/H ] >- 1.8 dex along with, possibly, a minor, more metal-poor component, as supported by the V , B-V color-magnitude diagrams of the two UFDs and their spectroscopically confirmed members. The metallicity spread that we derived from our data sample is 0.4 dex in both systems.

A few years later another new MW UFD, Peg III [ R.A.(J2000) = 22h24m48s, DEC.(J2000) = 5 ^ 24 ^ 18 ^ ; l = 69.85 ^ , b = 41.83 ^ ] was discovered from an analysis of the SDSS Data Release 10 (DR10; ahn14 ) and then confirmed with deeper Dark Energy Camera (DECam) follow-up observations by kim15 . The physical properties derived for Peg III are very similar to those of other previously known MW UFDs, and indeed, Peg III perfectly locates in the UFD region of the M_V r_h diagram. A further study of this galaxy was carried out by kim16 , who using deeper photometry and spectroscopy data from the Magellan/Baade Telescope Inamori-Magellan Areal Camera Spectrograph (IMACS) provided a better definition of the Peg III structural and photometric parameters. By fitting the HB fiducial line of the Galactic GC M15 ( [ Fe/H ] = 2.42 dex; ber14 ) to the blue horizontal branch stars, kim16 derived a distance modulus (m-M)_ 0 = 21.66 0.12 mag corresponding to a heliocentric distance d _ = 215 12 kpc.

gmw_00009189.name: CassII
gmw_00009189.overview: CassII is a dwarf spheroidal satellite galaxy of Andromeda [arXiv:1511.08289]. It forms a subgroup with NGC 147 and NGC 185 [arXiv:1511.08289], with orbital parameters favoring a closer connection to NGC 185 [arXiv:1211.2638].

sources.summary: The paper describes CassII as a recently discovered dwarf spheroidal in the NGC 147-NGC 185 subgroup, with models allowing it to remain bound without strong tidal disruption.

quotes.quote: NGC 147, NGC 185 and CassII measured positions and velocities strongly suggest that these three satellites of the Andromeda galaxy are a subgroup of the Local Group. In this work, the results of a statistical analysis show that it is highly unlikely that the similar positions and velocities are due to a chance alignment of the satellites, which further reinforces the sub-group hypothesis. Nevertheless, differences in their star formation history and interstellar medium, and the recent discovery of a stellar stream in NGC 147 combined with the lack of tidal features in the other two satellites seemed to contradict the sub-group scenario, since one would expect that the members of a sub-group suffer similar tidal forces and interactions. However, that is not necessarily the case: using the genetic algorithm we find sets of orbits for which NGC 147 had a closer encounter (and therefore stronger tidal forces) with M31 than the other two satellites of the subgroup.

This scenario, proposed by Irwin et al. (in preparation) as a possible explanation for the observed differences, was tested in this work using N-body simulations. We found that the closer encounter always resulted in the formation of a clear stellar stream in NGC 147, whereas the other two satellites had no significant tidal features. We showed that it is therefore possible to have contrasting internal properties for satellites within a subgroup, and that the tidal stream in NGC 147 could be the result of an interaction with M31 during which the other members of the subgroup were further away and suffered no significant tidal disruption.

sources.summary: The paper treats Cass II as a recently discovered M31 satellite whose line-of-sight velocity and orbital parameters favor association with NGC 185 rather than NGC 147.

quotes.quote: Recent discovery Cass II ( a = 181 15 kpc, e = 0.21 6 ) lies close to this pair and could be a third member of their subsystem, if such a subsystem exists see e.g., irwin2012 . collins2012 argue that a connection is possible based on the line-of-sight velocity they measure for Cass II. Our results certainly favour a connection between Cass II and NGC 185 as the orbital parameters we find for them are in close agreement, but not for Cass II and NGC 147.

We reject all pairs with median separation larger than 100 kpc, to obtain three candidate pairs and one candidate group, of which the Cass II - NGC 185 pair (separation 46^ +32 _ 29 kpc) has already been discussed.

gmw_00009190.name: ATCA J0536.9 6913
gmw_00009190.overview: ATCA J0536.9 6913 is a point-like radio source that corresponds to an X-ray source [arXiv:astro-ph/0011431]. It exhibits a radio spectral index of 0.73, which is typical for background active galactic nuclei or radio quasars [arXiv:astro-ph/0011431].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ATCA J0536.9 6913 as a point-like radio counterpart to an X-ray source, with multi-frequency fluxes and a radio spectral index typical of background AGN or radio quasars.

quotes.quote: Radio-continuum emission from a point-like source is detected at RA = 05 ^ h 36 ^ m 56 62 and Dec = 69 13 27 7 (J2000), compatible with the X-ray position given the uncertainty in position angle of the X-ray image. The integrated flux density of this source, designated ATCA J0536.9 6913 , at 843, 1420, 2370 and 4800 MHz was determined to 336 30, 211 20, 116 11 and 102 10 mJy, respectively. We derive a spectral index = 0.73 0.16 (defined by S_ ^ ) from the flux densities, S_ , at the frequencies, , of 843, 1420, 2370 and 4800 MHz (Fig. fig-radio ). This spectral index is typical for radio background sources which are mainly AGN or radio quasars (Filipovi et al. ).

gmw_00009191.name: AM 1339-445
gmw_00009191.overview: AM 1339-445 is a dwarf elliptical galaxy located as a distant companion to Centaurus A [arXiv:astro-ph/0512366]. It hosts an intermediate-age upper asymptotic giant branch stellar population [arXiv:astro-ph/0512366], with star formation having continued in the galaxy until approximately 6.5 billion years ago [arXiv:astro-ph/0512366].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes AM 1339-445 as a Cen A dwarf elliptical, measuring its distance, metallicity, intermediate-age upper-AGB population, and star formation continuing until about 6.5 Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: We have presented an analysis of the red giant populations of two dE galaxies in the Cen A group, AM 1339-445 and AM 1343-452, using a combination of near-IR and optical data. Both dEs are distant companions of Cen A, the dominant galaxy of the group. Using the luminosity of the tip of the RGB we have measured distance moduli of both galaxies, ( m M )_0( AM1339-445 )=27.74 0.20 , and ( m M )_0( AM1343-452 )=27.86 0.20 , which are in good agreement with previously published values k+02,JFB00 . The mean colour of the upper RGB stars is used to determine the mean metallicities of [ Fe/H ] =-1.4 0.2 for AM 1339-445 and [ Fe/H ] =-1.6 0.2 for AM 1343-452.

We find evidence for the presence of intermediate-age upper-AGB stars in both galaxies, with the most luminous of these stars being 0.2-0.3 mag brighter in AM 1343-452, than in AM 1339-445. The luminosities of these stars indicate that significant star formation continued in AM 1343-452 until an age of 4 Gyr as against 6.5 Gyr in AM 1339-445. In this respect these Cen A group dEs are similar to the outlying dE satellites of the Milky Way. However, we estimate that the fraction of the total population that is of intermediate-age is perhaps 15 , which is significantly less than the dominant intermediate-age populations found in outlying Milky Way dE satellites such as Fornax and Leo I.

sources.summary: The paper classifies KK211 as inconsistent with CASP membership under default criteria, noting it appears close to the Centaurus A plane but has orbits fundamentally misaligned from the satellite plane.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, relaxing the required orbital pole alignment to 45^ results in NGC5011C, dw1341-43, and KK211's removal from the set of Unlikely satellites, instead being classified as Both . These three satellites demonstrate an initial proximity to Centaurus A and may visually appear to participate in the CASP, but have orbits fundamentally misaligned from the satellite plane.

We identified a set of 5 satellites -- NGC5011C, dw1341-43, KK211, ESO269-037, and KK221 -- as being inconsistent with CASP membership, the latter two of which remain robustly so even when relaxing our criteria.

gmw_00009192.name: AM 1343-452
gmw_00009192.overview: AM 1343-452 is a dwarf companion galaxy of Centaurus A [arXiv:astro-ph/0507168, arXiv:astro-ph/0512366]. It contains a population of intermediate-age upper asymptotic giant branch stars [arXiv:astro-ph/0507168, arXiv:astro-ph/0512366], indicating that star formation continued in the galaxy until approximately 4 Gyr ago [arXiv:astro-ph/0512366].

sources.summary: The paper presents AM1343-452 as a Cen A companion dSph with measured RGB properties, possible carbon stars, upper-AGB stars, and ongoing star formation a few Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: Analysis of this dataset continues but I present here initial results for AM1343-452 (kk217), a dSph with absolute visual magnitude M _ V -11.5 that lies approximately 290 kpc in projection from Cen A . classify it as a Cen A companion. The left panel of Fig. \ CenAfig shows an optical -m diagram derived from the HST snapshot data for this galaxy. The stars plotted are only those that are measured on both the HST and the VLT ISAAC data. The tip of the red giant branch is clearly visible at I 23.9 0.1.

Regardless of the actual status of individual stars, these data suggest that AM1343-452 does contain a population of upper-AGB stars. This interpretation confirmed by an analysis of the field-subtracted J-band luminosity function (see Rejkuba et al., these proceedings), which reveals a clear excess of stars brighter than the RGB tip. The most luminous of these stars have M _ bol values in the range of --4.5 to --4.8, corresponding to ages of a few Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes AM 1343-452 as a Cen A dwarf elliptical, finding a measured distance, low metallicity, brighter upper-AGB stars, and significant star formation until about 4 Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: We have presented an analysis of the red giant populations of two dE galaxies in the Cen A group, AM 1339-445 and AM 1343-452, using a combination of near-IR and optical data. Both dEs are distant companions of Cen A, the dominant galaxy of the group. Using the luminosity of the tip of the RGB we have measured distance moduli of both galaxies, ( m M )_0( AM1339-445 )=27.74 0.20 , and ( m M )_0( AM1343-452 )=27.86 0.20 , which are in good agreement with previously published values k+02,JFB00 . The mean colour of the upper RGB stars is used to determine the mean metallicities of [ Fe/H ] =-1.4 0.2 for AM 1339-445 and [ Fe/H ] =-1.6 0.2 for AM 1343-452.

We find evidence for the presence of intermediate-age upper-AGB stars in both galaxies, with the most luminous of these stars being 0.2-0.3 mag brighter in AM 1343-452, than in AM 1339-445. The luminosities of these stars indicate that significant star formation continued in AM 1343-452 until an age of 4 Gyr as against 6.5 Gyr in AM 1339-445. In this respect these Cen A group dEs are similar to the outlying dE satellites of the Milky Way. However, we estimate that the fraction of the total population that is of intermediate-age is perhaps 15 , which is significantly less than the dominant intermediate-age populations found in outlying Milky Way dE satellites such as Fornax and Leo I.

gmw_00009193.name: UNAM-KIAS 359
gmw_00009193.overview: UNAM-KIAS 359 is an isolated galaxy with no similar-sized neighbors within 727 kpc [arXiv:1511.08809]. Spectroscopically, it is classified as a transition object containing a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus and a circumnuclear star-forming region [arXiv:1511.08809].

sources.summary: The paper finds UNAM-KIAS 359 is isolated, centrally reddened with bluer outer structure, has marginal low-surface-brightness features and H I detection, and is spectroscopically a transition object.

quotes.quote: UNAM-KIAS 359 No similar-sized neighbor galaxy is found within 727 kpc and 1000 km s ^ 1 . The color map in the top-left panel of Fig. mosaico359 shows a conspicuous central region with a uniform reddened cusp but asymmetric reddening distribution, an intermediate bluer region, and an outer diffuse red region. Our 2D residual images show some tiny central residuals consistent with the color index map showing a strong reddened central cusp and an adjacent reddened patch to the right, probably associated with dusty structures. There is marginal evidence of external low surface brightness features.

This galaxy does not show any significant sloshing in the inner kpc region. This galaxy has been reported as detected in H i 21 cm. Our analysis from GALFIT shows three inner components ( r_ eff = 0.15, 0.55, and 0.80 kpc), all with S ersic indices lower than 1 ( n = 0.1 , 0.15, and 0.30, respectively), and one intermediate-outer component ( r_ eff = 1.95 kpc) with n = 1.00 . At the distance of UNAM-KIAS 359, the 3 arcsec SDSS fiber spectrum subtends 1.04 kpc. From our spectroscopic analysis, this galaxy qualifies in the BPT diagram as a transition object (TO) with a mixture of a central, low-luminosity AGN and emission coming from a circumnuclear SF region (see Fig. BPT ).

gmw_00009194.name: UNAM-KIAS 613
gmw_00009194.overview: UNAM-KIAS 613 is an isolated blue star-forming elliptical or lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1511.08809]. It hosts a type 1.8 active galactic nucleus with broad emission lines and features three distinct structural components [arXiv:1511.08809].

sources.summary: The paper describes UNAM-KIAS 613 as an isolated blue star-forming elliptical/S0 with a nuclear region, multiple GALFIT components, broad emission lines, and type 1.8 AGN classification.

quotes.quote: UNAM-KIAS 613 Only one neighbor galaxy fainter than 2.5 mag with respect to UNAM-KIAS 613 is within 712 kpc and 1000 km s ^ 1 . The SDSS images in the top-left panel of Fig. mosaico613 show (i) a conspicuous nuclear region (maybe a lens) surrounded by (ii) an adjacent more diffuse region and also an (iii) outer envelope. The central strongly reddened cusp region contrasts with the more bluish colors of the rest of the galaxy in the color map.

Our analysis from GALFIT, summarized in the top-right panel of Fig. mosaico613 , shows three components; an inner one ( r_ eff = 0.43 kpc) with S ersic index n = 1.47 , an intermediate one ( r_ eff = 2.81 kpc) with n = 0.55, and an outer one ( r_ eff = 4.54 kpc) with n = 6.50 . At the distance of UNAM-KIAS 613, the 3 arcsec fiber from the SDSS spectrum subtends about 1.62 kpc. From our spectroscopic analysis, this galaxy initially qualifies as a TO (see Fig. BPT ) with broad emission lines (see panel d in Fig. mosaico613 ). UNAM-KIAS 613 is the only galaxy in the sample where a broad component in H is clearly detected as can be seen in more detail in Fig. broad613 . Also a fainter broad component is detected in H (see panel d in Fig. mosaico613 where the whole spectrum is plotted) and consequently, according to Osterbrock's classification Osterbrock1989 it corresponds to a type 1.8 AGN.

gmw_00009195.name: UNAM-KIAS 1197
gmw_00009195.overview: UNAM-KIAS 1197 is an isolated galaxy with no similar-sized neighbors within 692 kpc [arXiv:1511.08809]. It is classified as a LINER, featuring a blue central region, localized dust-like residual patches, and outer low surface brightness shell-like structures [arXiv:1511.08809].

sources.summary: The paper finds UNAM-KIAS 1197 isolated with blue central structure, dust-like residual patches, outer shell-like features, and spectroscopic evidence placing it as a LINER.

quotes.quote: UNAM-KIAS 1197 No similar-sized neighbor galaxy is found within 692 kpc and 1000 km s ^ 1 . Our images in the top-left panel of Fig. mosaico1197 distinguishes a conspicuous bright central region with more uniform blue color along the face of this galaxy and an outer diffuse red region. The 2D residual images show some central localized patches, probably associated with dust structures. The sloshing is not significant in the inner kpc. There is evidence of external low surface brightness shell-like structures.

From our spectroscopic analysis, this galaxy qualifies as a LINER (see Fig. BPT ). We note the intensity of N ii with respect to H in the bottom-right panel of Fig. mosaico1197 . This feature is usually associated with Seyfert 2 nuclei.

gmw_00009196.name: UNAM-KIAS 1394
gmw_00009196.overview: UNAM-KIAS 1394 is characterized by a compact nuclear region and a diffuse outer region with blue color gradients [arXiv:1511.08809]. The galaxy features dust-like central filaments and exhibits active star formation within its nuclear region [arXiv:1511.08809].

sources.summary: The paper describes UNAM-KIAS 1394 as compact-nuclear and diffuse-outer, with blue color gradients, dust-like central filaments, no low-surface-brightness features, and nuclear star formation.

quotes.quote: The top-left panel of Fig. mosaico1394 shows a compact nuclear region and a diffuse outer region. The color map shows bluer colors along the face of this galaxy and a clear color gradient in the central region. Our 2D residual images show a set of localized filaments in the central region, probably associated with dust structures.

We do not detect any low surface brightness features in this galaxy. Top-right panel of Fig. mosaico1394 shows two inner components ( r_ eff = 0.60 and 0.72 kpc) with S ersic indices n = 0.63 and 1.41, respectively, and a more extended intermediate component ( r_ eff = 1.71 kpc) with n = 1.89 . In this case, a solution with only one inner component in addition to the intermediate component is also reasonable. At the distance of UNAM-KIAS 1394, the 3 arcsec fiber spectrum subtends 2.1 kpc. The spectroscopic analysis shows definite star formation in the nuclear region (SFN) for this galaxy (see Fig. BPT ).

gmw_00009197.name: SDSS J161434.67+470420.0
