gmw_00009197.overview: SDSS J161434.67+470420.0 is a luminous quasar located at a redshift of z=1.86 [arXiv:1904.04784]. The active galaxy has been detected in X-ray observations [arXiv:1303.3585] and hosts an optically thick, low-temperature corona [arXiv:1904.04784].

sources.summary: 2MASSJ1614346+470420 is a z=1.86 luminous quasar observed with XMM-Newton and NuSTAR; its spectra yield a low-temperature, optically thick corona.

quotes.quote: To fill this observational gap, we selected two luminous (log( ) >47.5 erg/s) quasars, 2MASSJ1614346+470420 (z=1.86) and B1422+231 (z=3.62), and obtained \ and \ deep observations for them. We performed detailed spectral analysis of their quasi-simultaneous soft and hard X-ray data, in order to constrain the parameters of their coronae. Using a phenomenological cut-off power-law model, with the inclusion of a reflection component, we derived rest-frame values of the high energy cut-off of =106^ +102 _ 37 keV and =66^ +17 _ 12 keV, respectively. Comptonization models consistently give as best-fit parameters electron temperatures of 45 keV and 28 keV, respectively, and optically thick coronae ( >1 ).
The brightest one is 2MASSJ1614346+470420 (2MASSJ16 hereafter).
The two \ observations for 2MASSJ16 were taken at 40 days distance, and we verified that no significant variability was detected before merging the two data-sets with standard Heasoft tools.

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J161434.67+470420.0 as a quasar-like low-energy counterpart candidate to 2FGLJ1614.8+4703 and reports both ROSAT and Chandra X-ray detections.

quotes.quote: A quasar-like spectrum is then available for SDSS J161434.67+470420.0 candidate counterpart of 2FGLJ1614.8+4703. The search for the optical counterparts for UGSs with ray analysis flags was less successful. Only one source has an optical, counterpart: WISE J131552.98-073301.9, associated with 2FGLJ1315.6-0730. This source has a counterpart in both the NVSS and in the FIRST radio survey. According to Bauer et al. (2009), this source is also variable in the optical and it was therefore selected as a blazar candidate. In Figure fig:optical2 we show the archival SDSS spectrum of the \ J161434.67+470420.1 candidate as the low energy counterpart of 2FGLJ1614.8+4703.

In the UGS sample without ray analysis flags, only 3 objects have X-ray counterparts in the ROSAT all-sky catalog: the Seyfert 1 galaxy SDSS J015910.05+010514.5, the quasar SDSS J161434.67+470420.0 (both described in Section sec:optical ) and WISE J164619.95+435631.0 associated with 2FGLJ1647.0+4351. In addition, SDSS J161434.67+470420.0 is also detected in the \ source catalog: CXO J161434.7+470419 as occurs NVSS J161543+471126, alias CXO J161541.2+471111 evans10 .

gmw_00009198.name: 1WGA J2229.4-2018
gmw_00009198.overview: 1WGA J2229.4-2018 is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 0.27 [arXiv:2007.10193]. Spectroscopic observations classify the source as a Seyfert type I AGN, displaying a spectral energy distribution trough that indicates the transition between the dusty torus and the disk [arXiv:2007.10193].

sources.summary: The paper follows up 1WGA J2229.4-2018 spectroscopically, deriving z=0.27 and classifying it as a Seyfert type I AGN with a torus-to-disk SED trough.

quotes.quote: Medium-resolution spectroscopy of 1WGA J2229.4-2018 was obtained with the X-shooter spectrograph Vernet2011 of the Very Large Telescope (VLT) UT2 at the ESO Paranal Observatory on 2019 April 26. The three arms of X-shooter, (UV: UVB, optical: VIS and near-infrared: NIR) were used with slit widths of 1 0, 0 9, and 0 9, respectively. These data provide quasi-simultaneous 300--2400 nm spectral coverage with average spectral resolutions / of 5400, 8900, and 5600, respectively, in each arm. Observing conditions were intermediate, with a clear sky, a seeing of 1 5, and an airmass of 1.7. Individual exposure times are 445 s, 352 s, and 200 s for the UBV, VIS, and NIR arms, respectively, which lead to a total integration times of 3560 s, 2816 s, and 3200 s. Standard ABBA nodding observing mode was used to allow for an effective background subtraction. Data were reduced using the ESO X-shooter pipeline Goldoni2006, Modigliani2010 (v.2.9.3), producing a background-subtracted, wavelength-calibrated spectrum.

The X-shooter rest-frame spectrum of 1WGA J2229.4-2018 matches with the optical spectrum of a Seyfert 1.2 AGN with [ O III ] lines weaker than the H one. The F_ optical/near-infrared spectral energy distribution of 1WGA J2229.4-2018 displays a deep trough around 10 ^ 14.8 Hz which probably indicates the transition between the dusty torus and the disk contributions strengthening the classification of the source as a Seyfert 1.2 AGN. Fig. fig:xshooter_spectrum shows our spectrum of 1WGA J2229.4 2018. We derive a source redshift of z = 0.27 using the emission lines of Mg II, Ne V, H , and H and we use this number to calculate the absolute luminosity of the source and its SED. Based on the optical spectrum the source can be identified as Seyfert type I AGN.

gmw_00009199.name: UGC1457
gmw_00009199.overview: UGC1457 is a barred spiral galaxy characterized by a bar-like feature at its center [arXiv:2106.13809]. The galaxy also exhibits broad, asymmetrical spiral arms [arXiv:2106.13809].

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC1457 as a challenging barred, asymmetric spiral case where AUTOPROF and PHOTUTILS fail but XVISTA and GALFIT recover different parts of the light distribution.

quotes.quote: The fourth column shows UGC1457 , which has a bar-like feature at the centre and broad asymmetrical spiral arms. AUTOPROF cannot adequately fit this galaxy, and this case was not identified by the fit checks. XVISTA , however, succeeded in modelling the galaxy at all radii. PHOTUTILS failed to find a solution for this galaxy and returned no result, while GALFIT recovered the global light distribution.

gmw_00009200.name: ESO541-G001
gmw_00009200.overview: ESO541-G001 is a barred spiral galaxy characterized by asymmetrical spiral arms [arXiv:2106.13809]. The galaxy also features a faint, extended outer disk surrounding its bright central structures [arXiv:2106.13809].

sources.summary: The paper discusses ESO541-G001 as a barred spiral with asymmetrical arms and a faint extended disk, contrasting how multiple photometry methods recover or miss those features.

quotes.quote: The sixth column shows ESO541-G001 , which has a bar, asymmetrical spiral arms and a faint extended disc. Here AUTOPROF adequately recovered all features, including the faint extended dist. XVISTA faithfully matched the main spiral structure of the galaxy, but failed to capture the faint extended disc. PHOTUTILS accurately matched the features of this galaxy, but could not extend its solution as far as AUTOPROF ; GALFIT fitted the bright central structures well, but missed the faint extended disc.

gmw_00009201.name: G1
gmw_00009201.overview: G1 is a dwarf galaxy with a stellar mass of approximately 10 to the power of 8.5 solar masses [arXiv:2202.04669]. It exhibits a disturbed optical continuum morphology and a modest star-formation rate of 0.04 solar masses per year, with a faint stellar stream pointing toward a nearby quasar [arXiv:2202.04669].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G1 as the closer dwarf in an interacting pair hosting the z=0.026 DLA, with disturbed morphology, mass estimates, modest star formation, and H I connected to the absorber.

quotes.quote: We refer to these galaxies as G1 and G2, where G1 is the galaxy at smaller impact parameter, d , with respect to the QSO. At d = 6.2 kpc, G1 is projected in close enough proximity to the QSO that the 3 optical continuum light overlaps with the wing of the QSO point spread function (PSF) on the southwest side of the galaxy. G2 is found at the edge of the MUSE footprint at d = 32.5 kpc and 15 of the area within the 3 band contour is found outside of the IFU coverage. We estimate the stellar mass of both galaxies to be log (M_ star / M _ ) = 8.5 0.2 from extinction-corrected, band photometry from DES using the M_ M_ star relation derived from NASA–Sloan Atlas galaxies by Liang2014 .

As shown in Fig. fig:pointing , the optical continuum morphology of the faint outskirts of both dwarfs show disturbances; these irregularities are most prominent on the sides of the galaxies closest to their companion, including a faint stellar stream protruding from G1 that points toward the QSO. The extinction-corrected H luminosity of G1, L( H ) 8 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 , suggests a star-formation rate (SFR) of 0.04 M _ yr ^ 1 following the relation of Calzetti2008 . About two thirds of the star formation is found in the bright, star-forming knot labeled G1R1 in Fig. fig:pointing .

gmw_00009202.name: G2
gmw_00009202.overview: G2 is a dwarf galaxy that forms a pair with another dwarf galaxy, G1 [arXiv:2202.04669]. The two galaxies are connected by a 10-kiloparsec-wide neutral hydrogen tidal bridge and share a gas envelope spanning over 40 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2202.04669].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G2 as the second dwarf in the z=0.026 DLA host pair, with similar stellar and H I mass to G1, disturbed outskirts, modest star formation, and slightly higher metallicity.

quotes.quote: We refer to these galaxies as G1 and G2, where G1 is the galaxy at smaller impact parameter, d , with respect to the QSO. At d = 6.2 kpc, G1 is projected in close enough proximity to the QSO that the 3 optical continuum light overlaps with the wing of the QSO point spread function (PSF) on the southwest side of the galaxy. G2 is found at the edge of the MUSE footprint at d = 32.5 kpc and 15 of the area within the 3 band contour is found outside of the IFU coverage. We estimate the stellar mass of both galaxies to be log (M_ star / M _ ) = 8.5 0.2 from extinction-corrected, band photometry from DES using the M_ M_ star relation derived from NASA–Sloan Atlas galaxies by Liang2014 .

We detect spatially extended \ 21-cm emission that reveals a neutral gas envelope surrounding G1 and G2 on a scale of > 40 kpc, including an apparent tidal bridge between them. We display the \ column density contours in Fig. fig:pointing . The bridge has a width of 20^ , or 10 kpc, at the level of 5 10^ 19 \ and is thus resolved by MeerKAT's synthesized beam (FWHM = 4 kpc \ 3 kpc). The total integrated line flux of the system is 0.44 0.06 Jy , which corresponds to a total \ gas mass of M( ) 1.3 10^ 9 M _ . If we bisect the gas distribution at the midpoint between the galaxies, the two dwarfs are associated with nearly the same \ mass.

gmw_00009203.name: Abell 963 X1
gmw_00009203.overview: Abell 963 X1 is a blue galaxy [arXiv:0908.3158] with an optical spectrum consistent with a Seyfert nucleus [arXiv:0908.3158]. It is classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0908.3158] and has also been described as a type 2 quasar with a nearly Compton-thick X-ray spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0310772].

sources.summary: The paper treats A963 15 as a very hard, nearly Compton-thick type 2 quasar whose photometry, redshift estimate, absorption, Fe K feature, and luminosity support that classification.

quotes.quote: A963 15 Source 15 in the field of Abell 963, whose X-ray spectrum is shown in Fig fig:a963_src15_xray , is a very hard source with S/H=0.08 0.04. Based on UBRiJHK photometry, we estimate =0.56 [ 0.4, 0.7 ] , which is in agreement with a tentative redshift of 0.536 found by lavery93 based on detections of recombination lines of Oxygen. Even if the redshift is fixed at z =0, the absorption implied is high: > 23.

The peak at 4.1 keV prompted us to include a narrow (10 eV fixed width), redshifted Fe K line, and this was found to match the peak energy well, further supporting the redshift estimate. While the rest-frame equivalent width of the line is close to 1 keV, its significance is marginal (f-test gives a null hypothesis probability of 0.1). With the above fixed value of =2, the X-ray absorption implied is N_ H =1.0 [ 0.5, 1.5 ] 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , making this source nearly Compton-thick. The implied absorption-corrected 2--10 keV luminosity is 6 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 , making this a clear type 2 quasar detected in X-rays.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Abell 963 X1 as a blue Seyfert-like cluster AGN whose X-ray spectrum is power-law dominated, includes Fe K-alpha emission, and lacks intrinsic absorption.

quotes.quote: Abell 963 X1 has an optical spectrum consistent with a Seyfert nucleus (with emission-line luminosity of [ OIII ] >> H ). Given the presence of only one Seyfert projected within 1 Mpc of a cluster core and 745 km s ^ 1 relative radial velocity for this Seyfert (Abell 963 cluster velocity dispersion of _v =1350 km s ^ 1 ; 1999ApJS..125...35S ), this source is consistent with being a cluster member either foreground or background to the core at a physical radius > 1 Mpc.

This XPS is the bright (M _r 22.8), blue (almost 1 magnitude bluer than the CRS) galaxy in Figure fig:cmd (Object A) and possesses a typical Seyfert optical spectrum. Using XSPEC , the spectrum of Abell 963 X1 is well-modeled by a power-law with spectral index =2.0 0.1 , emission from the 6.4 keV Fe K- line with an equivalent width of 1.7 keV, and no intrinsic absorption. But this spectrum also can be fit by a Raymond-Smith thermal model with kT = 3.4 ^ +0.5 _ 0.3 keV, but at a higher reduced- ^2 value (1.18 vs. 1.03). Given the clear Seyfert-like optical spectrum and likely power-law X-ray spectrum we identify Abell 963 X1 as a clear AGN.

sources.summary: The paper treats Abell 963 X2 as an unusually blue X-ray point-source host whose optical colors and Ca II break support a low-luminosity BL Lac interpretation.

quotes.quote: A second XPS (Abell 963 X2, object D in Figure fig:cmd ), the least luminous XPS host galaxy in our sample, is several tenths of a magnitude bluer than the red sequence, but, nonetheless, possesses a passive absorption line spectrum. This galaxy will be discussed in detail in subsec:lum_ratio .

The detailed optical properties of Abell 963 X2 (source D in Figures fig:cmd , fig:xray_color , fig:lum_ratio ) also support the HBL classification of the XPSs. Figure fig:cmd shows that this XPS host galaxy is 0.5 mag bluer in (g-) than its associated CRS. Further to the blue, the discrepancy enlarges with Sloan (u-g)=0.64 0.26 while the CRS galaxies in Abell 963 have Sloan (u-g)=1.4-2.4. Deeper (u,g,) imaging of Abell 963 X2, obtained by us at the APO 3.5m telescope, confirms its anomalous colors blueward of the Ca II break.

gmw_00009204.name: NGC 4483
gmw_00009204.overview: NGC 4483 is a lenticular dwarf galaxy located in the virial core of the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2312.14017]. It hosts a nuclear stellar disk and a short, weak bar characterized by an ansae morphology and an associated barlens [arXiv:2312.14017].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 4483 as a Virgo SB0 dwarf with an ansae/barlens bar, nuclear stellar disk, and a short weak bar whose rotation may be slow.

quotes.quote: NGC 4483 is an SB0 galaxy, with a stellar mass of M _ =7.5 10^9 Roediger2017 , is located at an angular distance of 3.3 \ from M 87 Kashibadze2020 . It lives in the virial core of the Virgo Cluster. It is in an intermediate density environment ( _5=0.45 Mpc ^ 2 ; see also deBortoli2022 ) and its closest bright galaxy is IC 3430, located at a projected distance of 0.6 Mpc. NGC 4483 hosts a bar with an ansae morphology, associated with a barlens Buta2019 , and a nuclear stellar disc ledo2010 . It possibly hosts a boxy/peanut (BP)-shaped bulge Bureau1999b .

NGC 4483 hosts a short ( R_ bar =2.1 0.3 kpc) and weak bar ( S_ bar =0.378^ +0.001 _ 0.004 ), according to the classification criteria of . The bar is associated with a clear peak in the PA profile, and a boxy shape of the isophotes (peaking at B_4 0.03 ). The bar is embedded in an intermediate-inclination disc characterised by a constant profile of PA and , and discy isophotes ( B_4 0.01 ).

gmw_00009205.name: NGC 4516
gmw_00009205.overview: NGC 4516 is a barred spiral dwarf galaxy located in the core of the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2312.14017]. It features a short, strong central bar, S-shaped spiral arms, and an X-shaped structure associated with a boxy-peanut bulge [arXiv:2312.14017].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4516 as a Virgo SBa dwarf with an X-shaped central structure, spiral arms, and a short strong bar that is probably slowly rotating.

quotes.quote: NGC 4516 is an SBa galaxy (with M _ =5.7 10^9 Roediger2017 , located in the virial core of the Virgo Cluster at an angular distance of 2.3 \ from M 87 Kashibadze2020 . It lives in a low density environment ( _5=0.15 Mpc ^ 2 ) and its closest bright galaxy is IC 3478, located at a projected distance of 2.1 Mpc. NGC 4516 clearly shows an X-shaped structure in the central region, which is associated with a BP bulge, and S-shaped spiral arms, which emerge from the ends of the bar Buta2019 .

The isophotal analysis (Fig. fig:photometry_n4483 ) shows a peak in the radial profile, produced by the short ( R_ bar =2.3 0.3 kpc) but strong bar ( S_ bar =0.51^ +0.02 _ 0.01 ). The inner isophotes are boxy peaking at B_4 0.03 ( R<20 arcsec), given the presence of the BP bulge. The disc has an exponential surface brightness profile ( R 60-80 arcsec) with constant PA and values, and an increasing blue g-i colour. In the outer region of the galaxy ( R>80 arcsec), the g-i colour steepens, whereas the PA and remain constant.

gmw_00009206.name: borg 0240-1857 129
gmw_00009206.overview: The high-redshift galaxy candidate borg 0240-1857 129 is the brightest source in its sample [arXiv:1606.05955]. It exhibits an extended structure, a very red color, and a photometric redshift solution that peaks at z = 10.1 [arXiv:1606.05955].

sources.summary: The paper identifies this as the brightest candidate, with strong H-band detection, red color, extended structure, and a photometric-redshift solution peaked at z = 10.1.

quotes.quote: borg 0240-1857 129 This candidate is the brightest in the sample, with magnitude H_ 160 = 24.7 . It is robustly detected in at S/N = 14.5 , and marginally detected in at S/N = 2.5 , even though it is close to the edge of the chip. The source has a very red J_ 125 H_ 160 color, with J_ 125 H_ 160 = 2.2 . It also shows extended structure, and has r_e = 0 33 . Its photometric redshift solution is sharply peaked at z = 10.1 , with a broad higher-redshift wing.

gmw_00009207.name: borg 0240-1857 369
gmw_00009207.overview: The high-redshift galaxy candidate borg 0240-1857 369 has a photometric redshift peaked at z = 10.0 [arXiv:1606.05955]. It is the most extended source in its sample, with an effective radius of 0.38 arcseconds and a marginal J-band detection [arXiv:1606.05955].

sources.summary: The paper describes this same-field candidate as the second-brightest and most extended source, with marginal J-band detection and a photometric redshift peaked at z = 10.0.

quotes.quote: borg 0240-1857 369 This candidate, in the same field as the previous one is detected with magnitude H_ 160 = 25.2 , making it the second-brightest source in the sample. It is detected with S/N = 9.6 in , and again marginally detected with S/N = 2.2 in . It is the most extended source in the sample, with r_e = 0 38 . Its photometric redshift, like borg 0240-1857 129, is peaked at z = 10.0 , with a broad higher-redshift wing.

gmw_00009208.name: borg 0240-1857 25
gmw_00009208.overview: The high-redshift galaxy candidate borg 0240-1857 25 is a compact source with a measured half-light radius of 0.13 arcseconds [arXiv:1606.05955]. It is located close to a foreground galaxy, with a projected center-to-center separation of 1.25 arcseconds [arXiv:1606.05955].

sources.summary: The paper treats this as a third bright candidate in the field, compact and single-band detected, with possible point-source contamination and slight foreground-galaxy lensing.

quotes.quote: borg 0240-1857 25 Field borg 0240-1857 includes a third bright candidate with H_ 160 = 26.4 , detected at S/N =8.1 . This source is not detected in the other bands ( , or _ 600 ). Unlike the two brighter candidates, this object is more compact, with a measured half-light radius r_e = 0 13 . This is smaller than the PSF of the image ( 0 15 ), indicating that it could be a point-source-like contaminant such as a cool dwarf star, although the stellarity of this source is 0.71 , which is lower than the value expected for a point source (e.g. schmidt14 uses CLASS STAR < 0.85 and bouwens15a CLASS STAR <0.9 to exclude stars). This candidate is close to a foreground galaxy with H_ 160 = 26.0 , with a centre-to-centre projected separation of 1 25 .

gmw_00009209.name: borg 0456-2203 1091
gmw_00009209.overview: The high-redshift galaxy candidate borg 0456-2203 1091 is identified as an H-band dropout [arXiv:1606.05955]. It exhibits a compact but extended structure with an effective radius of 0.24 arcseconds [arXiv:1606.05955].

sources.summary: The paper presents this object as an H-band dropout candidate with compact extended structure; inspection of image artifacts supports a robust candidate identification.

quotes.quote: borg 0456-2203 1091 This object has a magnitude H_ 160 = 26.1 ( S/N = 8.1 ), and is detected in the only, with an extended but compact structure (effective radius r_e = 0 24 ). The source is located relatively close ( 0 5 separation) to a hot pixel, which appears in the and images. The band image is unaffected since it was acquired in a later orbit than the images in bluer bands. We carefully examined the individual FLT files and conclude that since the separation between the source center and the hot pixel is larger than twice r_e , and there is no sign of a hot pixel in the band, the identification of the candidate as a dropout is robust.

gmw_00009210.name: borg 1153+0056 514
gmw_00009210.overview: The high-redshift galaxy candidate borg 1153+0056 514 is detected in the H-band with an effective radius of 0.23 arcseconds [arXiv:1606.05955]. It is located close to a foreground object, with a projected centre-to-centre separation of 1.46 arcseconds [arXiv:1606.05955].

sources.summary: The paper reports this candidate as an H-band detection with no bluer-band detections, an effective radius of 0.23 arcsec, and robust photometry despite a bad pixel.

quotes.quote: borg 1153+0056 514 This candidate is detected with a magnitude H_ 160 = 26.3 , and has S/N =8.0 . It is not detected in , or . It has an effective radius of r_e = 0 23 . This candidate is close to a foreground object ( 1 46 centre-to-centre projected separation).

Analysis of the FLT images of this field highlighted the presence of a bad pixel, correctly identified and masked by the data reduction pipeline, at the outer edge of the segmentation map of the dropout candidate in one of the two frames. To determine the impact on the final photometry, we measured the source flux in the FLT frames separately, finding that the candidate is detected with S/N = 5.1 in the unaffected image and also S/N = 5.1 in the image affected by the bad pixel.

gmw_00009211.name: borg 1459+7146 785
gmw_00009211.overview: The candidate galaxy borg 1459+7146 785 is confidently detected with a photometric redshift peaked at z = 9.8 [arXiv:1606.05955]. However, its high stellarity and compact half-light radius mean a cool-dwarf stellar nature is also relatively likely [arXiv:1606.05955].

sources.summary: The paper describes this final candidate as confidently detected with a z = 9.8 photometric-redshift peak, but compactness and high stellarity make a cool-dwarf nature plausible.

quotes.quote: borg 1459+7146 785 The sixth and final candidate is confidently detected at S/N =12.8 in ( H_ 160 = 26.0 ), and also in the with S/N = 3.7. Its photometric redshift is sharply peaked at z = 9.8 , with a secondary solution at z 2.5 . This candidate is also very compact, with measured half-light radius r_e = 0 14 , and the highest stellarity of the sample (CLASS STAR = 0.91). Combining compactness with high stellarity from a high S/N source, a stellar nature (cool dwarf) for this source is relatively likely, as we discuss in Section contamination .

gmw_00009212.overview: 0745+241 is a radio quasar that hosts active galactic nuclei activity [arXiv:1212.3423]. Polarization observations reveal that the source exhibits epoch-dependent rotation measure sign changes and a monotonic decrease in rotation measure away from its core [arXiv:1212.3423].

sources.summary: The paper describes 0745+241 as a radio quasar with a monotonic RM decrease away from the core and epoch-dependent RM sign changes.

quotes.quote: Polarization observations of 6 radio--quasars (0133+476, 0420-014, 0745+241, 0906+430, 1633+382 and 1954+513) and 2 BL Lac objects (0256+075 and 1823+568) were carried out in a 24 hour session on November 2, 2008 using the VLBA.
0745+241 shows a behavior similar to these of 0420--014 with higher RM values ( 3000 rad/m ^2 ) closer to the core and a monotonic decrease to 1000 rad/m ^2 at a distance of 1 mas.

In our case, this is particularly true for 0745+241, 0906+430 or 1823+568, sources that show a change in the sign of the RM for the two epochs. Such RM sign changes have been detected before for 0133+382, 0745+241 and 1633+382 when compared with another set of observations Algaba12 .

gmw_00009213.name: NGC 6047
gmw_00009213.overview: NGC 6047 is a brightest cluster galaxy in Abell 2151 classified with an E/S0 morphology [arXiv:1103.0450]. Its optical morphology indicates a recent merger that disrupted the dynamics of a neutral hydrogen disk, while its kiloparsec-scale radio emission exhibits a two-sided Fanaroff-Riley class I jet structure [arXiv:1103.0450].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6047 as a BCG in Abell 2151, links its E/S0 morphology to a recent merger, and reports extended FRI jet emission but no VLBA 5 GHz detection.

quotes.quote: Among the undetected sources (Sect. res ), we found 2 peculiar cases of BGCs, IC 712 and NGC 6047 for which the non detection in our previous VLBA data at 5 GHz was not expected and not at all understood.
NGC 6047 is a BGC of the second condensation of Abell 2151.

NGC 6047 is classified as an E/S0 and has an unusual optical morphology indicative of a recent merger that severely disrupted the dynamics of an HI disk whose gas lost its kinematic coherence . On the kiloparsec scale, NGC 6047 exhibits an extended FRI structure with a two-sided jet emission . The northern jet is brighter (flux density at 5 GHz 13.6 mJy) and more collimated. The total flux density at 1.4 GHz is 728 mJy and the core flux density at 5 GHz is 8.3 mJy. In our VLBA maps at 5 GHz, differently to NGC 6041A (unresolved on parsec scale), NGC 6047 appears undetected above 5 = 0.55 mJy/beam.

gmw_00009214.name: NGC 3313
gmw_00009214.overview: The galaxy NGC 3313 contains a disk structure with bright knots [arXiv:2106.15789]. This disk extends across the full mapped area of the source [arXiv:2106.15789].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3313 to illustrate source-finding performance, showing that smoothing-based masks recover the disc beyond the brightest knots and trace the full disc extent.

quotes.quote: To illustrate this effect, we show in panel B of Fig. fig_hydra an individual channel map from the original data cube in the direction of the galaxy NGC 3313. The source mask produced by 2 in this frequency channel (bold, black contour) extends well beyond the brightest knots in the disc of NGC 3313.
The 2 source mask nicely traces the full extent of the disc after smoothing, as is also shown by the resulting moment 0 map of NGC 3313 across the full source mask in panel C.

gmw_00009215.name: UGC12506
gmw_00009215.overview: UGC12506 is a galaxy characterized by a rotation curve that is unmistakably cuspy in nature [arXiv:2310.20272]. Its dark matter halo exhibits a small or unresolved core size and an elevated concentration parameter [arXiv:2310.20272].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC12506 as one of two clearly cuspy SPARC rotation curves, with a cuspy profile fit, a small or unresolved core size, and elevated c_200.

quotes.quote: Cuspy RCs Among the 21 most reliable SPARC RCs, two galaxies, namely, UGC12506 and UGC02259, are unmistakably characterized as cuspy in nature with values of -1.57 and -1.15, respectively. Notably, the NFW component of the best-fit cored profiles for these two RCs aligns precisely with their cuspy profile fits. In both cases, the core size ( r_c ) is either slightly less than or closely matches the radius at which the first data point is available. Additionally, the best-fit values of c_ 200 for UGC12506 and UGC02259 are 3 and 1.5 higher than the mean MCR, respectively, suggesting that these RCs exhibit an extremely cuspy nature.

gmw_00009216.name: MCG+00-27-005
gmw_00009216.overview: MCG+00-27-005 is a host galaxy with a heliocentric recessional velocity of 5655 km/s [arXiv:0707.2237]. Located at a distance of approximately 75 Mpc, the galaxy contains interstellar dust that causes the majority of the reddening observed in its supernova SN 2005bf [arXiv:0707.2237].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MCG+00-27-005 as the host of SN 2005bf, gives the supernova offset, host velocity and distance, and links observed absorption and reddening to the host.

quotes.quote: Li and Moore determined the position of SN 2005bf as _ 2000 =10^ h 23^ m 57.27^ s and _ 2000 =-3 11'28.6'' , 11.7'' east and 32.6'' south of the nucleus of the host galaxy MCG+00-27-005 (see Fig. 2005bf_pos ). The heliocentric recessional velocity of the host is given by HyperLEDA http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ as v_ =5655 , with the recessional velocity corrected for infall to Virgo, v_ Vir =5623 , yielding a distance of 75Mpc (for H_ 0 =75 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 ).

Weak and narrow interstellar absorptions of Ca II H K and Na I D are observed at the recessional velocity of the host galaxy. There are no comparable absorptions for Milky Way interstellar absorption and, given the resolution of the observations and the high recessional velocity of the host galaxy, such components would be resolved if present.
The majority of the reddening of SN 2005bf arises in the host galaxy, as the only Na I D component observed is at the redshift of the host.

gmw_00009217.name: IC 863
gmw_00009217.overview: IC 863 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2206.00625] located at a distance of approximately 34.8 Mpc [arXiv:2206.00625]. The galaxy has a redshift of z = 0.008386 and exhibits a roughly solar metallicity near the site of the supernova SN 2021foa [arXiv:2206.00625].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 863 as the barred spiral host of SN 2021foa, adopts its redshift and distance, and derives host extinction plus near-solar local metallicity.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy IC 863 is a barred spiral, with a redshift of z =0.008386 pisano . The NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ reports a kinematic distance, corrected for the Virgo Infall, of d=34.8 2.4 Mpc ( =32.71 0.15 mag), that we adopt as the distance to IC 863. The Milky Way reddening towards IC 863 is A_V =0.224 mag schlafly . From spectroscopic considerations (see Appendix appendix ) we infer the presence of additional host galaxy extinction of A_V(host) 0.40 0.05 mag.

The host galaxy metallicity plays an important role on the mass loss history of massive stars, as a metal-poor environment is expected to inhibit mass loss in massive stars, in contrast with what happens with metal rich environments. Indeed, the metallicity near the site of SN 2021foa (see Appendix appendix ) is roughly Solar.

gmw_00009218.name: NGC 619
gmw_00009218.overview: NGC 619 is a gas-rich barred galaxy with an "8-shaped" optical appearance typical of galaxies with a bar [arXiv:0805.3371]. It is connected to its companion, NGC 612, by a 400-kiloparsec tidal bridge and a gas tail that indicate a past interaction [arXiv:0805.3371, arXiv:1811.06266].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 619 as a gas-rich barred galaxy 400 kpc from NGC 612, with a large H I tail and bridge indicating an interaction between the systems.

quotes.quote: The galaxy NGC 619 is located at a projected distance of 400 kpc east of NGC 612. The `8-shaped' optical appearance of NGC 619 is typical for galaxies with a prominent bar e.g. ath99 . NGC 619 contains 8.9 10^ 9 M_ of . Most of this \ gas is located at the position of the host galaxy. A prominent tail of \ gas stretches 219 kpc toward the south-west.

The \ tail south-west of NGC 619 is clearly visible in the third and fourth frame (arrow). This tail connects to a faint stream of \ in the direction of NGC 612 (feature `3', as indicated in the eighth frame). The faint \ stream -- most likely some sort of bridge between both systems -- appears to end at the central region as well as the systemic velocity of NGC 612.

sources.summary: NGC 619 is discussed as the nearby gas-rich companion of NGC 612, connected by a 400 kpc H I tidal bridge attributed to an interaction at least a Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: observations by Emonts2008 reveal a faint bridge extending 400 kpc to nearby galaxy NGC 619 with no detectable optical counterpart related to the bridge or tails. The mass distribution is mainly found in disks around NGC 619 ( M_ HI = 8.9 10^ 9 ) and NGC 612 ( M_ HI = 1.8 10^9 ), with M_ HI = 2.9 10^8 located in the bridge connecting the two galaxies see their Fig. 5 Emonts2008 .

Interestingly, NGC 612 is connected to NGC 619 through a narrow bridge of tidal debris detected in \ Emonts2008 . The \ bridge connects these two galaxies along the Eastern radio lobe. From an in-depth study of both the \ bridge and the regularly rotating \ disk around HGC 612, Emonts2008 argue that the bridge is the result of an interaction or collision between NGC 612 and NGC 619 that occurred at least a Gyr ago.

gmw_00009219.name: T124
gmw_00009219.overview: T124 is a low surface brightness tidal dwarf galaxy candidate located at the end of the Young Tail [arXiv:1201.5149]. It has an estimated stellar population age of 7 Myr and a mass of approximately 10^6 solar masses [arXiv:1201.5149].

sources.summary: The paper identifies T124 as the tidal dwarf galaxy candidate at the Young Tail, deriving a young age, high metallicity, roughly 10^6 solar masses, and transient visibility.

quotes.quote: Concerning the other three sources in the Young Tail: T117 has strong emission lines indicating an age <7 Myr; T122 is absorption line-dominated indicating an age 50 Myr; and T124 (the tidal dwarf galaxy candidate) has an age of 7 Myr and will be discussed in detail in sec:tdg .

In addition to the 21 clusters/complexes discussed above, we have also obtained a spectrum of the Tidal Dwarf Galaxy (TDG) candidate at the end of the Young Tail. A WFPC2 V-band image of this low surface brightness object is shown in Fig. fig:tdg , along with the slit position. Although this is a resolved object, and likely has undergone an extended star formation history, the obtained spectrum does not have the necessary S/N to attempt a multi-component fit of age, extinction and metallicity. Instead, we have adopted the same technique as for the clusters, and assume that it can be described by a single stellar population.

The spectrum of T124 does not show prominent emission lines, but a fit to the spectral continuum shape reveals a young age, 7 Myr, and a metallicity consistent with other clusters in the Young Tail ( 1.4 Z_ 0.4 ). Using the same method as above, we have estimate the mass contained in 1) a 10-pixel aperture of the end clump where our spectrum was obtained and 2) a 35 pixel aperture which covers the full extent of the TDG. We obtain masses of 3.3 and 10.3 10^5 , respectively.

gmw_00009220.name: Leo-TDG
gmw_00009220.overview: Leo-TDG is a tidal dwarf galaxy candidate located at the tip of the gaseous tidal tail of NGC 3628 [arXiv:1404.1744]. It features a faint optical counterpart, a very high neutral gas content, and independent rotation with a small dark matter fraction [arXiv:1404.1744].

sources.summary: The paper presents Leo-TDG as a self-gravitating tidal dwarf candidate with independent H I rotation, a faint optical counterpart, high gas content, low dark matter fraction, and unusual distance from NGC 3628.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: diffuse, low surface brightness patch at R.A._ 2000 = 11^ h 23^ m 15^ s , Dec_ 2000 = 13^ 43 15

quotes.quote: We report discovery of a dwarf galaxy in the Leo Triplet. Analysis of the neutral hydrogen distribution shows that it rotates independently of the tidal tail of NGC 3628, with a radial velocity gradient of 35--40 km s ^ 1 over approximately 13 kpc. The galaxy has a very high neutral gas content, explaining large part of its total dynamic mass -- suggesting a small dark matter content. As it is located at the tip of the gaseous tail, this strongly suggests its tidal origin.

In this paper we use the archive neutral hydrogen and optical data to show that the velocity field of the TDG candidate detected in the Leo Triplet exhibits a velocity gradient and has a faint optical counterpart. These findings strongly support the idea of its independent rotation, thus confirming its identification as a galaxy.

Leo-TDG shares many of its characteristics with the TDGs Kaviraj et al. kaviraj . It is rather bluer than its supposed progenitor 0.65 compared to 0.8 for NGC 3628, Paturel et al. hyperleda , it is located exactly at the tip of the tidal tail, and has mass of some 10^ 8 M_ , typical for such objects.

gmw_00009221.name: IGR J14552-5133
gmw_00009221.overview: IGR J14552-5133 is a gamma-ray-selected active galactic nucleus discovered by INTEGRAL/IBIS and Swift/BAT [arXiv:0806.4824]. It is classified as a candidate narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy that appears to accrete close to its Eddington rate [arXiv:0806.4824].

sources.summary: The paper treats IGR J14552-5133 as a gamma-ray-selected NLS1 candidate, localized by Swift/XRT, with a low-mass black hole and an accretion rate near the Eddington rate.

quotes.quote: In this work we present a sample of 5 NLS1 (3 bona-fide and 2 candidates): IGR J14552-5133, IGR J16185-5928, IGR J16385-2057, IGR J19378-0617 and SWIFT J2127.4+5654. All 5 objects are gamma-ray selected AGNs discovered by INTEGRAL /IBIS and Swift/BAT; all 5 are reported in the last INTEGRAL /IBIS survey catalogue (Bird et al. 2007). The large (arcmin) gamma-ray error box has been reduced to arcsec uncertainty using X-ray follow-up observations performed by the Swift/XRT telescope (Malizia et al. 2007, Rodriguez et al. 2008 and this work)

Overall we can conclude saying that 3 objects (IGR J16385-2057, IGR J19378 and Swift J21247.4+5654) in our sample are bona-fide NLS1 while the remaining 2 can be considered at this stage only NLS1 candidates with IGR J14552-5133 probably a more convincing case than IGR J16185-5928. Clearly more detailed optical spectroscopy is necessary and encouraged to refine and confirm the optical classification of these last two objects.

We obtained 1.3, 0.5, 1.2, 0.9, 0.4 for IGR J14552-5133, IGR J16185-5928, IGR J16385-2057, IGR J19378-0617 and SWIFT J2127.4+5654 respectively; these values support the claim that our NLS1 accrete close to the Eddington rate, as expected (e.g. Boroson Green 1992).

gmw_00009222.name: IGR J16185-5928
gmw_00009222.overview: IGR J16185-5928 is an active galactic nucleus candidate, specifically classified as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 candidate [arXiv:0806.4824]. X-ray observations of the source indicate that it accretes close to its Eddington rate [arXiv:0806.4824].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IGR J16185-5928 as an NLS1 candidate, gives its X-ray coordinates from Swift/XRT, and estimates an accretion rate near the Eddington rate.

quotes.quote: Overall we can conclude saying that 3 objects (IGR J16385-2057, IGR J19378 and Swift J21247.4+5654) in our sample are bona-fide NLS1 while the remaining 2 can be considered at this stage only NLS1 candidates with IGR J14552-5133 probably a more convincing case than IGR J16185-5928. Clearly more detailed optical spectroscopy is necessary and encouraged to refine and confirm the optical classification of these last two objects.

For IGR J16185-5928 the X-ray position provided by the XRT observations presented in this work (RA: 16 ^ h 18 ^ m 26.38 ^ s , Dec: -59 ^ 28 ^ m 45.27 ^ s , 5 arcsec uncertainty) is fully consistent with the ROSAT position.

We obtained 1.3, 0.5, 1.2, 0.9, 0.4 for IGR J14552-5133, IGR J16185-5928, IGR J16385-2057, IGR J19378-0617 and SWIFT J2127.4+5654 respectively; these values support the claim that our NLS1 accrete close to the Eddington rate, as expected (e.g. Boroson Green 1992).

gmw_00009223.name: GRB 070306
gmw_00009223.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 070306 is a well-studied, dusty galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.497 [arXiv:1505.06743]. It has been detected across the electromagnetic spectrum and exhibits a near-solar metallicity [arXiv:1505.06743].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 070306 host as a dusty z=1.497 galaxy detected across the spectrum, with X-Shooter emission lines giving a Balmer decrement and near-solar metallicity.

quotes.quote: GRB 070306 ( z=1.497 ) is a well-studied dusty GRB 2008ApJ...681..453J , with a host galaxy that is detected throughout the electromagnetic spectrum 2014A&A...565A.112H, 2014arXiv1408.5076S, 2015ApJ...801..102P . The X-Shooter spectrum shows numerous emission lines, which allow the Balmer decrement to be reasonably well measured ( E_ B-V = 0.43_ 0.07 ^ +0.08 mag). The / line-flux ratio uniquely puts the R_ 23 solution onto the upper branch. The individual emission line diagnostics have overlapping probability distributions, and a simultaneous fit to all five line ratios results in a metallicity of =8.54 0.09 , which is 0.8 0.2 times the solar value (Fig. fig:zexamples ).

gmw_00009224.name: GRB 111209A
gmw_00009224.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 111209A is a low-luminosity, compact galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.677 [arXiv:1505.06743]. It exhibits low reddening, narrow emission lines, and a low metallicity that shares many similarities with blue compact dwarfs [arXiv:1505.06743].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 111209A host as a low-metallicity, low-reddening, compact galaxy with narrow lines and similarities to blue compact dwarfs, but not extremely metal-poor.

quotes.quote: GRB 111209A ( z=0.677 ) is one of the longest GRBs ever detected. The extreme temporal properties were used to postulate a new class of GRBs 2013ApJ...766...30G, 2014ApJ...781...13L , a result questioned by 2013ApJ...778...54V . The ultra-long duration is possibly indicative of a different progenitor channel 2013ApJ...778...67N , and the associated luminous supernova is well-explained in a magnetar scenario 2015Kannsubm, 2015Greinersubm .

Based on the detection of , and , in addition to the Balmer lines, we measure =7.95_ 0.17 ^ +0.30 or between 10 and 40 of the solar value in the adopted metallicity scale. This puts the galaxy at the low end of metallicities compared to many GRBs of more normal duration at a similar redshift (Fig. fig:zhist ). Together with the low reddening, narrow line width, compact morphology and low luminosity 2014ApJ...781...13L it shares many similarities with Blue Compact Dwarfs e.g., and references therein 2003ApJS..147...29G or hosts of super-luminous supernovae e.g., 2014arXiv1409.8331L . The galaxy hosting GRB 111209A is, however, not extremely metal-poor, and its spectroscopic properties do not provide a stark contrast to long GRBs with a more common duration (see, e.g., GRB 091127 above), a conclusion similar to the one of 2014ApJ...781...13L .

gmw_00009225.name: GRB 130427A
gmw_00009225.overview: GRB 130427A is a low-redshift host galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.340 [arXiv:1505.06743]. The galaxy has a star-formation rate and metallicity that are typical for low-redshift gamma-ray burst hosts [arXiv:1505.06743].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 130427A host as a low-redshift GRB galaxy whose star-formation rate and metallicity are typical for low-redshift GRB hosts.

quotes.quote: GRB 130427A ( z=0.340 ) is one of the brightest GRBs of all time due to its low redshift and is extremely well studied from the radio to GeV energies e.g., 2014ApJ...781...37P, 2014Sci...343...42A, 2014MNRAS.444.3151V . It is a very rare example of a low-redshift GRB with an energy budget comparable to common high-redshift GRBs and therefore elucidates cosmological GRBs and their connection to SNe through a local analogue 2013ApJ...776...98X, 2014ApJ...792..115L, 2014A&A...567A..29M . The host characteristics are relatively typical for the given redshift: With a SFR_ H = 0.3^ +0.2 _ 0.1 and metallicity of =8.57_ 0.13 ^ +0.12 it is well within the distribution of host properties for low-redshift GRBs.

gmw_00009226.name: Mrk 141
gmw_00009226.overview: Mrk 141 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1809.05113, arXiv:1909.01395] that features a structured and asymmetric broad-line region profile [arXiv:1909.01395]. This broad-line region exhibits an exponential radial distribution [arXiv:1809.05113] and shows signatures of outflowing trajectories [arXiv:1909.01395].

sources.summary: The paper finds Mrk 141 has lower-quality monitoring data but well-fit line profiles, an exponential BLR radial distribution, and thick inclined disk geometry.

quotes.quote: Mrk 141 The dataset for \ is of relatively low quality owing to many spectroscopic observing nights lost to poor weather. The integrated \ light curve is relatively short and there are not many strong variability features, but there is one large increase and decrease in flux over the course of the campaign. The models are able to fit this overall feature, but do not fit the smaller fluctuations on scales of a few days. The models are able to fit the shape of the \ profile very well. The posterior PDFs from runs using all three \ templates agree very well for most parameters, with the largest discrepancy coming from the parameter .

The radial distribution of the BLR in \ is roughly exponential, with shape parameter = , and is shifted from the origin by r_ min = \ light days. The mean radius is r_ mean = \ light days, and the radial width of the disribution is _r = \ light days. The mean lag is very similar to r_ mean , with _ mean = \ days.

sources.summary: The paper highlights Mrk 141 as a case where single-spectrum modelling constrains most BLR parameters, likely because its line profile is asymmetric and structured.

quotes.quote: Mrk 141 is a remarkable case since most of its BLR parameters can be constrained using a single spectrum. As discussed in Section sec:discussion this is likely due to the significant asymmetry and structure of the line profile in this AGN, which provides more information for the model to constrain the BLR parameters. We find = mrk141_opening degrees, = mrk141_inc degrees = mrk141_kappa , = mrk141_eps , = mrk141_fellip , \ > 0.5 i.e. signatures of outflowing trajectories, = mrk141_thetae degrees and = mrk141_rmin light-days, all agreeing within the uncertainties with the full light-curve result (Fig. posterior_mrk141 ). The only exception is the parameter , which cannot be constrained. However we note that an unconstrained is a common feature for all our objects.

gmw_00009227.name: Object 277912
gmw_00009227.overview: Object 277912 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with a best-fitting photometric redshift of approximately 6.97 [arXiv:1205.4270]. It exhibits a flat near-infrared spectral energy distribution, and spectral energy distribution fitting suggests a preference for zero Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:1205.4270].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Object 277912 as a robust z>6.5 galaxy candidate with a flat near-infrared SED, high photometric redshift, and an unlikely low-redshift alternative.

quotes.quote: Object 277912 has z'-Y = 2.5 and a flat near-infrared SED through to the IRAC bands, with a best-fitting spectral template corresponding to a galaxy with z_ phot = 6.97^ +0.06 _ 0.07 .

The ^2 distribution for object 277912 indicates that the low-redshift solution is not only extremely unlikely, but would also lie at z 1.7 . Hence it seems clear that this object is at high redshift and not associated with the low-redshift foreground galaxy. Unfortunately the presence of the foreground galaxy means that the IRAC photometry, where the FWHM of the PSF is 1.7 , is confused, and therefore the stellar mass estimate is uncertain. Allowance for a possible contribution from Lyman- emission in the SED fitting does not change the photometric redshift, with zero Lyman- emission remaining the preferred option.

gmw_00009228.name: Object 155880
gmw_00009228.overview: Object 155880 is a high-redshift star-forming galaxy [arXiv:2206.03503] with a photometric redshift estimated around 6.75 to 6.78 [arXiv:2206.03503, arXiv:1205.4270]. Observations of the galaxy have successfully detected [C II] line emission [arXiv:2206.03503], although its C III] emission line remains undetected [arXiv:2206.03503].

sources.summary: The paper includes UVISTA-Z-002 as a z 6.75 REBELS star-forming galaxy with archival X-SHOOTER and ALMA [C II] data, finding no X-SHOOTER lines but measuring [C II] flux.

quotes.quote: UVISTA-Z-002 galaxy at z 6.75 was observed as a part of the REBELS (reionization-era bright emission line survey) ALMA large survey of z>6.5 luminous star-forming galaxies ( 2021arXiv210613719B ). We retrieved and re-reduced (Sect. reduction_arch ) the X-SHOOTER archival data for UVISTA-Z-002 (ESO Program ID: 097.A-0082(A); PI: R. Bowler). For this source, we also used the [ C II ] observations from the ALMA archive in band 6 during Cycle 7 (Program ID: 2019.1.01634.L; PI: R.

In the spectra of UVISTA-Z-002, we did not detect any emission line. We placed a 3 upper limit on the C III ] line flux and estimated the velocity integrated flux upper limit, assuming FWHM_ C III ] = FWHM_ [ C II ] , where FWHM_ [ C II ] = 45.2 7.6 \ km \ s^ 1 was obtained from the Gaussian fit on the observed [ C II ] line from this work.
We derived an integrated flux of 35.5 7.7 \ mJy \ beam^ 1 \ km \ s^ 1 of the [ C II ] line emission for the UVISTA-Z-002 galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats Object 155880 as a robust high-redshift galaxy candidate, using its blue near-infrared slope, Subaru z-band detection, and unacceptable T-dwarf fit.

quotes.quote: Object 155880 also has a blue spectral slope in the near-infrared bands and is clearly detected in the deep Subaru z' band imaging giving a z_ phot = 6.78^ +0.06 _ 0.09 . We stress that the z' band detection ( z' = 26.1 0.1 ) is achieved because the Subaru Suprime-CAM z' band imaging is so deep and the filter extends to 1 m; the measured spectral break is still large z'-Y > 1.5 , although not large enough to warrant inclusion as a high-redshift candidate in some traditional colour-colour cut selection methods (e.g. z' - Y/J 2 ). Fitting with the addition of a line results in a slightly higher-redshift fit with z_ phot = 6.98 (with a Ly line EW_0 = 40 ). The best-fitting stellar template of an earlier type T-dwarf star (T3) can produce the z'-Y colour, but not the other near-infrared colours, with the result that the high-redshift galaxy solution is the only acceptable SED fit.

gmw_00009229.name: Object 218467
gmw_00009229.overview: Object 218467 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with a photometric redshift estimated at approximately 7.04 [arXiv:1205.4270]. It exhibits an extremely blue Y-J color and is formally undetected in K_s and H band imaging [arXiv:1205.4270].

sources.summary: The paper presents Object 218467 as one of the robust candidates, with a very blue Y-J color, unacceptable contaminant fits, and photometry consistent with z around 7.

quotes.quote: Object 218467 has the bluest Y-J colour of all our candidates as shown in the colour-colour plot in Fig. fig:JKz , which results in unacceptable SED fits for both the low-redshift and stellar templates, as neither can reproduce both the magnitude drop between z' and Y and the blue spectral slope at longer wavelengths. The J band detection is below the 5 limit of the data and hence there is a large uncertainty in the extremely blue Y-J = -0.5 colour (bluer than predicted by our range of models; see Fig. fig:JKz ). The candidate is formally undetected in the current K_s and H band imaging which leads to a large uncertainty on these data points in the SED fitting. However, when a smaller 1.2 diameter aperture is used, marginal detections produce a flat spectral shape as seen in the dwarf-star SED fit figure. A faint detection in the z' band is consistent with the z_ phot = 7.04^ +0.10 _ 0.08 within the errors, although the detection here is best fitted with inclusion of a strong line of EW_0 = 110 at the very red end of the Subaru z' band filter at z_ phot = 7.20 .

gmw_00009230.name: object 277880
gmw_00009230.overview: Object 277880 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with a favored photometric redshift of approximately 6.77 [arXiv:1205.4270]. Although alternative low-redshift galaxy and T-dwarf stellar classifications cannot be completely excluded, its flat Y-J color strongly supports the high-redshift solution [arXiv:1205.4270].

sources.summary: The paper keeps object 277880 as a favored z>6.5 galaxy candidate, although low-redshift galaxy and T-dwarf alternatives cannot be fully excluded.

quotes.quote: The best-fitting SED for object 277880 is a galaxy at z_ phot = 6.77^ +0.07 _ 0.08 . However, because of a strong z' band detection in combination with a relatively red spectral-slope through the near-infrared bands, the low-redshift solution at z = 2.0 cannot be completely excluded. A stellar fit of type T3, although not as good as that of the high-redshift galaxy SED fit, cannot be completely excluded either. However, the flat Y-J colour is hard to reproduce with either a low-redshift galaxy or dwarf-star fit, and so the z > 6.5 galaxy solution is still favoured for 277880. Allowance for a possible contribution from Lyman- emission in the SED fitting does not change the photometric redshift, with zero Lyman- emission remaining the preferred option.

gmw_00009231.name: object 268511
gmw_00009231.overview: Object 268511 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with a photometric redshift estimated at 7.07 [arXiv:1205.4270]. This estimate can rise to 7.22 if Lyman-alpha emission is factored into the spectral energy distribution fitting [arXiv:1205.4270].

sources.summary: The paper describes object 268511 as a high-redshift solution favored by SED fitting and a strong z-Y break, while noting possible transient or T8-star contamination.

quotes.quote: For object 268511, the SED fitting again indicates that the high-redshift solution is the best fit, with the strong z'-Y > 2.6 break resulting in z_ phot = 7.07^ +0.12 _ 0.10 and excluding all stellar fits except for the reddest T-dwarfs. Inspection of the postage-stamps for this object, however, shows that it is only clearly seen by eye in the Y band where it is compact, and therefore it could be a transient object or a T8 star. In this case, allowance for a possible contribution from Lyman- emission in the SED fitting raises the photometric redshift to z_ phot = 7.22 , with an implied Lyman- rest-frame equivalent width of EW_0 = 50 .

gmw_00009232.overview: The object 271105 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with a best-fitting photometric redshift of approximately 6.62 [arXiv:1205.4270]. Although a stellar fit as a T3 dwarf is possible, its red J-3.6 m color is inconsistent with a T-dwarf star [arXiv:1205.4270].

sources.summary: The paper says 271105 has a favored high-redshift SED and red J-IRAC color inconsistent with a T-dwarf, despite an allowed stellar-fit ambiguity.

quotes.quote: Finally, 271105 has a best fitting high-redshift SED with z_ phot = 6.62^ +0.13 _ 0.11 , with a higher-redshift solution ( z_ phot = 6.97 ) possible with the introduction of emission with EW_0 = 70 . A clear detection in the z' band does, however, allow the stellar fit for a T3 dwarf to recreate the spectral break. However, the red J- 3.6 m colour of 271105 is inconsistent with a T-dwarf star (the object is well detected and unconfused in all near-infrared bands). Hence, although we cannot rule a stellar solution, it still seems highly likely that 271105 is indeed a high-redshift galaxy.

gmw_00009233.name: LSB J22311-60160
gmw_00009233.overview: LSB J22311-60160 is an extreme low surface brightness galaxy candidate [arXiv:astro-ph/0701797]. It is a very faint object that was identified in a region with a lower signal-to-noise level [arXiv:astro-ph/0701797].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LSB J22311-60160 as an extreme low surface brightness candidate found in lower-sensitivity imaging and included in the final LSB sample despite profile-fit uncertainty.

quotes.quote: Additionally, we find three extreme low surface brightness candidates ( _ 0,B_ W 25.0 mag arcsec ^ 2 ) for which the GSFC multi-color data set is not available ( LSB J22311-60160 , LSB J22320-60381 , LSB J22364-60405 ). Due to the large diameter selection limit used for our sample, the distances of these galaxies are not likely to exceed the 0.5 redshift limit. Correcting their surface brightness assuming they are at z = 0.5 leads to a 1.8 mag arcsec ^ 2 higher surface brightness. Therefore, these galaxies can still be classified as LSB galaxies (e.g., LSB J22320-60381 with _ 0,B = 26.91 mag arcsec ^ 2 could be even at a redshift of z 1.8) and we decided to include these galaxies in our final LSB candidate sample.
However, our sample has just one candidate located in a region with lower sensitivity ( LSB J22311-60160 ). Which is reasonable because LSB objects would not appear in regions of low signal to noise.

LSB J22311-60160 : Very faint object ( _ 0,B_ W = 26.3 mag arcsec ^ 2 ) found only in the deep B_ W image in a region with a lower signal to noise level and it could not be fitted very well by an exponential profile.

gmw_00009234.name: LSB J22320-60381
gmw_00009234.overview: LSB J22320-60381 is a low surface brightness galaxy candidate that was detected only in deep B_W band imaging [arXiv:astro-ph/0701797]. It exhibits an exceptionally low central surface brightness of 26.86 mag arcsec^-2 and features a light profile that is well fitted by an exponential model [arXiv:astro-ph/0701797].

sources.summary: The paper describes LSB J22320-60381 as the faintest LSB candidate, found only in deep B_W imaging, with an exponential light profile and exceptionally low central surface brightness.

quotes.quote: Additionally, we find three extreme low surface brightness candidates ( _ 0,B_ W 25.0 mag arcsec ^ 2 ) for which the GSFC multi-color data set is not available ( LSB J22311-60160 , LSB J22320-60381 , LSB J22364-60405 ). Due to the large diameter selection limit used for our sample, the distances of these galaxies are not likely to exceed the 0.5 redshift limit. Correcting their surface brightness assuming they are at z = 0.5 leads to a 1.8 mag arcsec ^ 2 higher surface brightness. Therefore, these galaxies can still be classified as LSB galaxies (e.g., LSB J22320-60381 with _ 0,B = 26.91 mag arcsec ^ 2 could be even at a redshift of z 1.8) and we decided to include these galaxies in our final LSB candidate sample.

LSB J22320-60381 : Faintest LSB candidate in our sample with central surface brightness of _ 0,B_ W = 26.9 mag arcsec ^ 2 . Only found in the deep B_ W image. The light profile is very well fitted by an exponential profile.
We found three galaxies with very low surface brightness, and especially LSB J22320-60381 has one of the lowest central surface brightness known today ( _ 0,B_ W = 26.86 mag arcsec ^ 2 ).

gmw_00009235.name: LSB J22324-60520
gmw_00009235.overview: LSB J22324-60520 is a low surface brightness disk galaxy characterized by a very bright core and a faint, diffuse outer structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0701797]. It exhibits very red color indices and may be highly inclined [arXiv:astro-ph/0701797].

sources.summary: The paper treats LSB J22324-60520 as a final-sample LSB candidate with HSB-like colors, a bright core, diffuse outer structure, possible high inclination, and very red colors.

quotes.quote: Two galaxies of our final selected LSB candidate sample ( LSB J22311-60503 , LSB J22324-60520 ) have a location in the color-color diagrams consistent with those of HSBs with redshifts z 0.15. Although their photometric redshift estimation has a large distance uncertainty ( z 0.08), their central surface brightnesses assuming z = 0.15 is still below our LSB threshold of _ 0,B_ W 22.0 mag arcsec ^ 2 .

LSB J22324-60520 : Disk galaxy with a very bright core, a central surface brightness at our upper limit ( _ 0,B_ W = 22.2 mag arcsec ^ 2 ), and very faint and diffuse structure in the outer region. Maybe highly inclined.
The sample contains galaxies with moderate color indices but also one galaxy with a very red color index ( LSB J22324-60520 : U B =0.46 mag, B V =1.28 mag, B R =1.99 mag).

gmw_00009236.name: LSB J22364-60405
gmw_00009236.overview: LSB J22364-60405 is an extreme low surface brightness galaxy candidate [arXiv:astro-ph/0701797]. It is a very faint object that was detected only in deep B_W band imaging [arXiv:astro-ph/0701797].

sources.summary: The paper includes LSB J22364-60405 as an extreme low surface brightness candidate found only in deep B_W data, with variable-noise location and uncertain radial-profile values.

quotes.quote: Additionally, we find three extreme low surface brightness candidates ( _ 0,B_ W 25.0 mag arcsec ^ 2 ) for which the GSFC multi-color data set is not available ( LSB J22311-60160 , LSB J22320-60381 , LSB J22364-60405 ). Due to the large diameter selection limit used for our sample, the distances of these galaxies are not likely to exceed the 0.5 redshift limit. Correcting their surface brightness assuming they are at z = 0.5 leads to a 1.8 mag arcsec ^ 2 higher surface brightness. Therefore, these galaxies can still be classified as LSB galaxies (e.g., LSB J22320-60381 with _ 0,B = 26.91 mag arcsec ^ 2 could be even at a redshift of z 1.8) and we decided to include these galaxies in our final LSB candidate sample.
LSB J22364-60405 : Very faint LSB galaxy candidate ( _ 0,B_ W = 25.82 mag arcsec ^ 2 ) found only in the deep B_ W image in a region with variable signal to noise level.

The derived values for the two most extreme low surface brightness candidates ( LSB J22311-60160 _ 0,B_ W = 26.52 0.29 mag arcsec ^ 2 and LSB J22364-60405 _ 0,B_ W = 25.41 0.28 mag arcsec ^ 2 ) have higher uncertainties due to their location in regions with lower signal to noise levels.

gmw_00009237.name: MLS121104
gmw_00009237.overview: MLS121104 is identified as a host galaxy of a Type I superluminous supernova [arXiv:1605.04925]. It is considered one of the most metal-rich hosts of this supernova class, although the supernova itself is offset from the galaxy's center [arXiv:1605.04925].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MLS121104 as a possibly metal-rich SLSN Type I host, but notes the supernova offset and need for better metallicity verification.

quotes.quote: For example, one of the most metal-rich host galaxies of a SLSN Type I is MLS121104 Lu14 , with 12 + log (O/H) = 8.8 ( R_ 23 scale, KK04 ; hereafter KK04). However the SN location is clearly offset from the host centre, and further investigation is required to determine if this is indeed the host, or if the metallicity at the SN position is the same as that of the observed galaxy. Also, the metallicity estimate needs verification with the detection of the 4363 \ line.

gmw_00009238.name: UGC 6923
gmw_00009238.overview: UGC 6923 is a spiral galaxy with a rotation curve consistent with a Navarro-Frenk-White cold dark matter halo [arXiv:astro-ph/0204344]. It is five times more luminous than UGC 6969, yet both galaxies reach the same maximum rotational velocity [arXiv:astro-ph/0204344].

sources.summary: The paper finds UGC 6923 has the same maximum rotation as UGC 6969 despite being much more luminous, with its rotation curve consistent with an NFW-CDM dark halo.

quotes.quote: When comparing the position-velocity diagrams of the two brightest galaxies, UGC 6923 and UGC 6969, it is obvious that the rotation curve of the latter has a shape closer to solid body than the former, yet the same maximum rotational level is reached. This is likely generated by the equal dark matter halos in combination with UGC 6923 being a factor five more luminous than UGC 6969 and so its luminous matter gives a higher contribution to the rotation in the inner regions. An NFW-CDM dark halo is consistent with the observed rotation curve of UGC 6923 but not consistent with the rotation curve of UGC 6969.

gmw_00009239.name: UGC 6940
gmw_00009239.overview: UGC 6940 is a small companion galaxy with its gas distribution broadly coinciding with its optical counterpart [arXiv:astro-ph/0204344]. The galaxy exhibits a near solid-body velocity field and displays slightly skewed line profiles that extend toward higher velocities [arXiv:astro-ph/0204344].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 6940 as the smallest companion, finding its gas broadly coincident with the optical galaxy, a near solid-body velocity field, and skewed line profiles.

quotes.quote: The smallest galaxy (UGC 6940) is so moderately resolved that not much can be said about the gas in relation to the optical, except that in general both coincide.
UGC 6940 The velocity field of UGC 6940 is close to solid body and so small compared to the beam that it was impossible to determine independently the dynamical centre.
Secondly, both UGC 6969 and UGC 6940 exhibit a slight skewness of the line profiles in the sense that the tail extends to higher velocities.

gmw_00009240.name: UGC 6969
gmw_00009240.overview: UGC 6969 is a galaxy with a rotation curve that is closer to a solid body shape than that of UGC 6923 [arXiv:astro-ph/0204344]. It has a lower luminosity than UGC 6923, and its observed rotation curve is inconsistent with an NFW-CDM dark matter halo [arXiv:astro-ph/0204344].

sources.summary: The paper says UGC 6969 shares UGC 6923's maximum rotation but has a more solid-body curve, lower luminosity, and a rotation curve inconsistent with an NFW-CDM halo.

quotes.quote: When comparing the position-velocity diagrams of the two brightest galaxies, UGC 6923 and UGC 6969, it is obvious that the rotation curve of the latter has a shape closer to solid body than the former, yet the same maximum rotational level is reached. This is likely generated by the equal dark matter halos in combination with UGC 6923 being a factor five more luminous than UGC 6969 and so its luminous matter gives a higher contribution to the rotation in the inner regions. An NFW-CDM dark halo is consistent with the observed rotation curve of UGC 6923 but not consistent with the rotation curve of UGC 6969.

gmw_00009241.name: AS2COS0006.1
gmw_00009241.overview: AS2COS0006.1, also identified as AzTEC9, is a high-redshift starburst galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z = 4.5 to 5 [arXiv:1312.1173, arXiv:2011.09917]. It is a luminous submillimeter galaxy with confirmed carbon monoxide and carbon line emissions at z = 4.62 [arXiv:2011.09917].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AzTEC9 as a high-redshift source near z = 4.5-5, rejects a lower-redshift Smolcic et al. counterpart, and favors the higher-redshift galaxy in Table 5.

quotes.quote: AzTEC9. A robust single identification 0.77 arcsec from the SMA position. Like AzTEC4, z_ phot and z_ LW are in good agreement that the source has a redshift in the range z = 4.5 - 5 . The radio identification would have been correctly selected on the basis of the original AzTEC position.

Smolcic et al. (2012) selected a different object 2.8 arcsec from the SMA position with a photometric redshift of z_ phot 1.07 and a spectroscopic redshift z = 1.357 . However, not only is such a large positional offset very unlikely, but AzTEC9 has a large mm/radio flux ratio of 100 , completely inconsistent with such a low redshift (see Fig. 4). We therefore conclude that the counterpart selected by Smolcic et al. (2012) cannot be correct, and that the true identification is the higher redshift galaxy listed in Table 5.

sources.summary: The paper identifies AS2COS0006.1 as an ALMA Band 6 SMG with CO and carbon-line confirmation at z=4.62 and possible foreground cluster lensing.

quotes.quote: While more recently, AS2COS0001.1/1.2, AS2COS0006.1 and AS2COS0034.1/34.2 were identified in ALMA Band6 as AzTEC-C3a/C3b, AzTEC-C14 and AzTEC-C30a/C30b 2017A&A...608A..15B . Among the five line emitters, three have been already confirmed to be at z 4.6 . Recently, 2020ApJ...890..171J observed the two line emitters (AS2COS0001.1/1.2) with NOrthern Extended Millimeter Array (NOEMA) Band 1 and detected ^ 12 CO(5--4) lines at z=4.63 see also 2020MNRAS.495.3409S . 2020arXiv200903341B also observed AS2COS0006.1 as a part of large CO surveys of luminous SMGs and detected ^ 12 CO(5--4) and [ C i ] at z=4.62 .

There is a z=0.73 rich cluster 5-arcmin north-east from AS2COS0006.1 2007ApJS..172..254G , which could amplify the emission from the SMGs either due to cluster-scale gravitational lensing 2011MNRAS.415.3831A , or more realistically given the angular separation to the cluster center, by increasing the projected density of foreground galaxies, which could act as galaxy-scale lenses e.g., 2005ApJ...631..121S .

gmw_00009242.name: LV J1228+4358
gmw_00009242.overview: LV J1228+4358 is a dwarf satellite galaxy of NGC 4449 [arXiv:2105.01082]. The galaxy is currently undergoing tidal disruption [arXiv:2105.01082], though it retains a centrally concentrated stellar component that may still be bound [arXiv:2105.01082].

sources.summary: The paper recovers LV J1228+4358 as a known NGC 4449 dwarf satellite, treats it as a galaxy despite disruption, and reports new photometric measurements.

quotes.quote: We discover no new dwarf satellites, but we recover two previously known dwarfs (DDO 113 and LV J1228+4358) with M_ V <-12 that lie in our survey volume. We preview a new theoretical framework to predict satellite luminosity functions using analytic probability distribution functions and apply it to our sample, finding that we predict one fewer classical dwarf and one more faint dwarf ( M_ V 7.5 ) than we find in our observational sample (i.e., the observational sample is slightly top-heavy). However, the overall number of dwarfs in the observational sample (2) is in good agreement with the theoretical expectations. Interestingly, DDO 113 shows signs of environmental quenching and LV J1228+4358 is tidally disrupting, suggesting that low-mass hosts may affect their satellites more severely than previously believed.

We therefore consider only two satellite dwarf galaxies to have been found in our imaging survey, both of which were previously known: DDO 113, which is a satellite of NGC 4214 and was examined in , and LV J1228+4358, which is a large, disrupting Rich2012,Martinez-Delgado2012,Toloba2016a satellite of NGC 4449. Both of these dwarfs are easily identified by our detection algorithm, with estimated detection efficiencies of 100 . Since LV J1228+4358 is in the process of tidal disruption, we must consider whether it should be characterized as a galaxy or a stream for the purposes of this analysis.

Given that it appears to have a centrally-concentrated stellar component that may still be bound Martinez-Delgado2012 , we choose to treat it as a galaxy and include it in our theoretical analysis. It is worth noting that DDO 113 also shows evidence for interaction with its host, as its star formation was quenched about 1 Gyr ago Weisz2011 , likely because of cessation of cold gas inflows after its infall to NGC 4214 Garling2020 . Thus we may be seeing signatures that low-mass hosts affect their satellites more strongly than previously believed. We adopt the measurements from for DDO 113, namely m_ V =15.17 mag, so that its intrinsic luminosity is M_ V =-12.17 at the TRGB-measured distance of 2.95 Mpc Dalcanton2009 . There is a measurement of m_ B =14.2 for LV J1228+4358 in , while measure m_ g =16.50 and m_ i =15.95 , and and both find M_r=-13.5 , M_g=-13.05 . From our own imaging, we measure m_ B =15.69 ( M_ B =-12.35 ) and m_ V =14.99 ( M_ V =-13.03 ) by fitting S ersic profiles using galfitm Haeusler2013 , which is a multi-band extension of galfit Peng2010 ; we perform this fitting analagously to the procedure described in 3.1 of .

gmw_00009243.name: PKS0552 640
gmw_00009243.overview: PKS0552 640 is a quasar field analyzed as part of the MUSE Quasar-field Blind Emitters Survey [arXiv:2310.18406]. Oxygen-line velocity structure function measurements of its circumgalactic medium are consistent with the Kolmogorov slope [arXiv:2310.18406].

sources.summary: The paper includes PKS0552 640 as a newly analyzed MUSEQuBES QSO field and finds both oxygen-line VSF measurements consistent with the Kolmogorov slope.

quotes.quote: Out of the seven QSO fields, the measurements for four fields (PKS0454 22, J0454 6116, J2135 5316, and TXS0206 048) were presented in Chen2023 . The three newly included fields (PKS0405 123, HE0238 1904, and PKS0552 640) are all part of the MUSE Quasar-field Blind Emitters Survey (MUSEQuBES) program, and we use the MUSE-DEEP datacubes directly downloaded from the ESO phase-3 archive with program IDs 097.A-0089(A) and 094.A-0131(B) PI: J. Schaye; Muzahid2020 .

Nebulae with _2 consistent with 2/3: the VSF measurements for PKS0405 123 E, HE0238 1904, PKS0552 640 and TXS0206 048 lead to a constrained 2nd-order slope in agreement with the value 2/3. For HE0238 1904 and PKS0552 640, the measurements for both and within the 16 ^ th - 84^ th percentiles are consistent with the Kolmogorov slope. For TXS0206 048, only measurements with are available and the result is consistent with _2=2/3 .

gmw_00009244.name: NGC 4656UV
gmw_00009244.overview: NGC 4656UV is a UV-bright tidal dwarf galaxy candidate with an extremely disturbed morphology [arXiv:1203.1319]. It features a blue spectral energy distribution with no dust opacity, low metallicity, and recent star formation that likely began within the last 260 to 290 Myr [arXiv:1203.1319].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4656UV as a UV-bright tidal dwarf candidate with disturbed gas, blue low-dust photometry, recent star formation, low metallicity, and marginally bound dynamics after an interaction.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: NGC 4656UV is located just to the northeast of NGC 4656 at 12^h44^m14.794^s +32^ 16^m48.26^s (J2000)

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a UV-bright tidal dwarf galaxy candidate in the NGC 4631/4656 galaxy group, which we designate NGC 4656UV. Using survey and archival data spanning from 1.4 GHz to the ultraviolet we investigate the gas kinematics and stellar properties of this system. The \ morphologies of NGC 4656UV and its parent galaxy NGC 4656 are extremely disturbed, with significant amounts of counterrotating and extraplanar gas. From UV-FIR photometry, computed using a new method to correct for surface gradients on faint objects, we find that NGC 4656UV has no significant dust opacity and a blue spectral energy distribution. We compute a star formation rate of 0.027 M_ yr ^ 1 from the FUV flux and measure a total \ mass of 3.8 10^ 8 \ M_ for the object.

Evolutionary synthesis modeling indicates that NGC 4656UV is a low metallicity system whose only major burst of star formation occurred within the last 260-290 Myr. The age of the stellar population is consistent with a rough timescale for a recent tidal interaction between NGC 4656 and NGC 4631, although we discuss the true nature of the object--whether it is tidal or pre-existing in origin--in the context of its metallicity being a factor of ten lower than its parent galaxy. We estimate that NGC 4656UV is either marginally bound or unbound.

gmw_00009245.name: SDSS J103143.69+350154.5
gmw_00009245.overview: SDSS J103143.69+350154.5 is an early-type galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.125 [arXiv:1501.00043]. It is a radio source associated with nuclear activity, and its past AGN outburst may have formed a nearby radio phoenix [arXiv:1501.00043].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J103143.69+350154.5 as Source 4, a likely southern-subcluster early-type radio/AGN galaxy whose past outburst may have formed the radio phoenix.

quotes.quote: A fourth radio source is interesting for further discussion. It is a faint ( S_ 1400 = 0.5 mJy) point source (PS) with an optical counterpart (SDSS J103143.69+350154.5, z=0.125 ) located in projection 29 kpc North of the brightest peak of Source 1 . Given its location and redshift the source is probably part of southern sub-cluster of and it is labelled Source 4 (see Fig. fig:sdss4 ).

Given the difficulties to interpret Source 1 as a radio relic, we present an alternative explanation for the formation of this source which is related to the radio emission from another cluster member (SDSS J103143.69+350154.5, Source 4 in Fig. fig:sdss4 ), located just above (29 kpc in projection) the centre of the extended emission. The radio source, probably related to nuclear activity, is coincident with an early-type galaxy whose redshift ( z=0.125 ) and proximity to the southern BCG suggest it is part of the southern sub-cluster (see Fig. fig:cartoon .). The extended radio emission has a brightness and a morphological configuration compatible with a Fanaroff-Riley I radio galaxy. Therefore, it could be a striking example of CRs produced by an old AGN outburst, displaced, and likely compressed by the galaxy cluster merger event through shock waves (i.e. a radio phoenix).

gmw_00009246.name: J2358-00
gmw_00009246.overview: The galaxy J2358-00 hosts a young stellar population with an estimated age of 0.02 Gyr [arXiv:1612.01549]. Its model nebular continuum contributes 28 percent of the total flux below the Balmer edge [arXiv:1612.01549].

sources.summary: The paper models J2358-00 with a young stellar population, subtracts nebular continuum without reddening, and finds Balmer lines constraining YSP age and reddening.

quotes.quote: J2358-00 The initial modelling for J2358-00 was performed using combination 2, and resulted in a best fitting model with = 0.02 Gyr and = 0.5. The model nebular continuum contributes 28 of the total flux below the Balmer edge. The reddening derived from the / \ and / \ ratio is consistent with case B (Table blines ), and therefore, no reddening was applied before subtraction. The resulting spectrum shows no evidence of an unphysical step at the Balmer edge, thus was deemed acceptable.

gmw_00009247.name: J0114+00
gmw_00009247.overview: The galaxy J0114+00 has been studied using Sloan Digital Sky Survey spectral data [arXiv:1612.01549]. Spectral synthesis modelling of this data reveals a young stellar population and a modest nebular continuum contribution [arXiv:1612.01549].

sources.summary: The paper uses an adequate SDSS spectrum for J0114+00, subtracts a young-stellar-population model, and measures a modest nebular continuum contribution.

quotes.quote: J0114+00 Unfortunately, no Gemini \ spectrum is available for J0114+00 and therefore, we model the SDSS spectrum instead. Although this spectrum has a lower S/N than the Gemini GMOS-S data, it is still of adequate quality and covers a sufficient wavelength range to perform spectral synthesis modelling.
