After performing the initial run (combination 2), the stellar model, which included a YSP with = 0.04 Gyr and = 0.8 was subtracted. The model nebular continuum then contributes 15 of the total flux blue-ward of the Balmer edge (Table blines ).

gmw_00009248.name: J0123+00
gmw_00009248.overview: The galaxy J0123+00 requires a power-law component to model its spectrum [arXiv:1612.01549]. Its stellar light is dominated by an old stellar population, though its intermediate-age populations remain poorly constrained [arXiv:1612.01549].

sources.summary: The paper finds J0123+00 requires a power-law component, has poorly constrained intermediate-age populations, and is dominated by old stellar light in many fits.

quotes.quote: J0123+00 The initial modelling for J0123+00 was performed using combination 4, because it was not possible to obtain acceptable fits without the inclusion of a power-law component. However, this resulted in a wide range of ages and reddenings producing acceptable fits ( 2 (Gyr) 5 and 0.4 1.2 ), which, based on age-sensitive stellar features, were impossible to differentiate between. Therefore, the stellar model that produced the lowest \ value was subtracted, which included an ISP with =5.0 Gyr, = 0.6 and a power-law component. The nebular continuum model produces a large contribution to the total flux blue-wards of the Balmer edge ( 48 ), and subtraction of this model without first applying reddening of the value derived from the Balmer line ratios (Table blines ), results in an unphysical step in the spectrum.

gmw_00009249.name: J0142+14
gmw_00009249.overview: J0142+14 is a compact, morphologically undisturbed galaxy classified as a plausible E+A or post-starburst galaxy [arXiv:1612.01549]. Its spectrum displays strong Balmer absorption lines, indicating that an episode of star formation occurred within the last 0.4 Gyr [arXiv:1612.01549].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0142+14 as compact and morphologically undisturbed, yet its Balmer absorption and recent star formation make it a plausible E+A galaxy.

quotes.quote: J0142+14 is classified as undisturbed in terms of its morphology, and appears to be relatively compact in the deep ^ band image presented in bessiere12 . However, the modelling results presented here combined with the clearly detectable Balmer absorption lines in the spectrum (illustrated in Figure spec:q0142_confit ), make it clear that there has been a powerful episode of star formation within the last 0.4 Gyr. This suggests that J0142+14 may be an `E+A' galaxy, which is thought to be a short lived phase in which a galaxy transforms from star-forming to passively evolving tran03,tran04 . These galaxies are characterised by weak nebular lines (associated with star formation) such as [ OII ] 3727 and strong absorption lines, such a , characteristics which are clearly visible in the spectrum of J0142+14 (Figure all_spec ). swinbank12 present integral field unit (IFU) observations for 11 E+A galaxies ( 0.07 < z < 0.12 ) and find that 90 of their sample have residual rotational motion, with the A type stars spread throughout the galaxy, a finding which they attribute to previous minor/major merger activity. It is plausible that this is also the case for J0142+14 implying that, even though no evidence of a recent merger is detected, it is likely that one has occurred fairly recently.

gmw_00009250.name: J0217-00
gmw_00009250.overview: The active galaxy J0217-00 hosts a young stellar population [arXiv:1612.01549]. Modeling of the galaxy indicates stellar ages for this population in the range of 0.06 to 0.1 gigayears [arXiv:1612.01549].

sources.summary: The paper models J0217-00 with a young stellar population, subtracts a weak nebular continuum, and finds fits requiring YSP ages around 0.06-0.1 Gyr.

quotes.quote: J0217-00 After the initial modelling of J0217-00 (using combination 2), a stellar model with a YSP = 0.05 Gyr and = 0.5 was selected for subtraction, resulting in a nebular continuum that contributes 14 of the total flux just below the Balmer edge (3540 - 3640 ). In this case, it was not possible to determine any values for the Balmer decrements because \ falls directly adjacent to a chip gap, and the low equivalent width of \ results in uncertainties which are larger than the measured values. Therefore, no reddening correction was applied to the nebular continuum before subtraction, however this did not result in an unphysical step at the Balmer edge. Tables results8 and results2 show that all combinations which include a YSP are successful, and that for both cases in which the spectrum was modelled with no power-law component (combinations 2 and 3), the acceptable ages are in the range 0.06 (Gyr) 0.1 and the associated values of reddening are in the range 0.5 0.6 .

gmw_00009251.name: J0217-01
gmw_00009251.overview: The galaxy J0217-01 is modeled with a young stellar population and a weak nebular continuum [arXiv:1612.01549]. Its acceptable stellar population fits are dominated in the majority of cases by an old stellar population component [arXiv:1612.01549].

sources.summary: The paper models J0217-01 with a young stellar population, weak nebular continuum, and acceptable fits dominated mostly by the old stellar population.

quotes.quote: J0217-01 The initial modelling of J0217-01 was performed using combination 2, and produced a best fitting model which included a YSP of age = 0.2 and = 0.9 , which resulted in a nebular continuum model that contributes 5 of the total flux just below the Balmer edge. Attempting to derive the Balmer decrements proved unsuccessful because of the large errors associated with the \ and \ measurements, which are ultimately due to this weak nebular continuum. All five combinations of components were applied, and of these, only combinations that include an 8 Gyr unreddened component and a YSP (combinations 2 4) produced acceptable fits (Table results8 ). In the majority of cases where acceptable solutions are found, the flux is dominated by the OSP component.

gmw_00009252.name: J0218-00
gmw_00009252.overview: J0218-00 is an interacting quasar host galaxy system featuring two distinct nuclei with a projected separation of 12 kpc [arXiv:1612.01549]. This ongoing interaction has left the host galaxy in a ring-like configuration [arXiv:1612.01549].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0218-00 as a two-nucleus interacting quasar host with a ring-like configuration and separately modeled nuclear and extended apertures.

quotes.quote: J0218-00 J0218-00 is a complex system, with two distinct nuclei which have a projected separation of 12 kpc bessiere12 . This interaction has left the quasar host galaxy in what appears to be a ring-like configuration. The spectroscopic slit does not pass through both nuclei, yet it was still possible to extract two distinct apertures from the quasar host galaxy. The main aperture is centred on the region containing the active nucleus (with a spatial scale of 8 kpc) and a second, narrower aperture is centred on an extended region of the ring like structure (with a spatial scale of 5 kpc).

gmw_00009253.name: J0227+01
gmw_00009253.overview: The galaxy J0227+01 is modeled with a reddened young stellar population that has an estimated age of 0.04 Gyr [arXiv:1612.01549]. It exhibits a weak nebular continuum and weak emission lines, which prevent a reliable measurement of its nebular reddening [arXiv:1612.01549].

sources.summary: The paper models J0227+01 with a reddened young stellar population and weak nebular continuum, preventing reliable Balmer-decrement reddening estimates.

quotes.quote: J0227+01 The initial modelling for J0227+01 was performed using combination 2, which resulted in a best fitting model with = 0.04 Gyr and = 0.6 . This resulted in a nebular continuum model that contributes 11 of the total flux just below the Balmer edge (Table blines ). Unsurprisingly, with such a comparatively weak nebular continuum, both the \ and \ emission lines are weak, preventing a reliable measurement of the nebular reddening, via the Balmer decrements, being made. In light of this, it was not possible to apply a reddening correction to the nebular component before subtraction.

gmw_00009254.name: J0234-07
gmw_00009254.overview: The galaxy J0234-07 is a quasar host galaxy with ambiguous stellar-population fits [arXiv:1612.01549]. Its stellar model is not strongly dependent on the choice of a young stellar population, as an older stellar population dominates the flux in most modeling solutions [arXiv:1612.01549].

sources.summary: The paper finds J0234-07 has ambiguous stellar-population fits: an old population often dominates, and a power-law-only alternative makes the YSP requirement unclear.

quotes.quote: J0234-07 The initial modelling for J0234-07 was performed using combination 2 and resulted in a wide range of possible solutions that have . An inspection of the overall shape of the continuum and regions around age sensitive stellar absorption lines showed the best fit to have a YSP \ = 0.001 and \ = 0.1. However, Table results8 shows that the OSP dominates the flux for all other possible solutions for combination 2 and therefore, the resulting stellar model is not strongly dependent on the choice of YSP component. A nebular continuum model that contributes 24 of the flux in the 3540--3640 \ range was constructed.

gmw_00009255.name: J0249-00
gmw_00009255.overview: The galaxy J0249-00 is modeled with a young stellar population [arXiv:1612.01549]. Its best-fitting model includes a substantial nebular continuum, though the associated nebular reddening remains poorly constrained [arXiv:1612.01549].

sources.summary: The paper models J0249-00 with a young stellar population and substantial nebular continuum, but the nebular reddening is poorly constrained.

quotes.quote: J0249-00 In order to produce a stellar model, the initial run was performed using combination 2. This resulted in a best fitting model with \ = 0.03 and \ = 0.7 and produced a model nebular continuum which contributes 25 of the flux in the 3540--3640 \ range. No reddening was applied to this continuum because, as is shown in Table blines , the uncertainty associated with measuring the / \ ratio is large in comparison to the measured value, and thus is consistent with reddenings of 0 1.7 . Therefore, because the value of reddening is poorly constrained, and also because subtracting an unreddened nebular continuum provides an acceptable result, a maximum nebular subtraction was performed before proceeding with the fitting.

gmw_00009256.name: J0320+00
gmw_00009256.overview: The galaxy J0320+00 is a quasar host galaxy [arXiv:1612.01549]. Its stellar content is characterized by a young stellar population and a model nebular continuum, which can be modeled alongside an older stellar population [arXiv:1612.01549].

sources.summary: The paper models J0320+00 with a young stellar population and nebular continuum, finding acceptable two-stellar-component fits and stronger YSP contribution with a power law.

quotes.quote: J0320+00 The initial modelling for J0320+00 was performed using combination 2, and the resulting best-fit model includes a YSP of \ = 0.02 Gyr and \ = 0.1 and a model nebular continuum which contributes 14 of the flux between in the 3540 \ - 3640 \ range was generated. The Balmer decrements were measured using \ and \ (Table blines ) but because of the large uncertainty associated with the measured value, which allows for reddenings in the range 0 1.3 , and the fact that the maximum subtraction produces a good result around the Balmer edge, no reddening correction has been applied to the nebular continuum model before subtraction. The results of running combinations 2--5 are presented in Tables results8 and results2 , which show that only combination 3, which includes an underlying 2 Gyr ISP, does not provide acceptable solutions. Columns 5 and 6 of Tables results8 and results2 demonstrate that, when no power-law is included in the model, the YSP contributes a slightly higher proportion of the flux than the OSP, and that when a power-law component is included, this difference becomes more pronounced.

gmw_00009257.name: J0332-00
gmw_00009257.overview: J0332-00 is a type I active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1612.01549] that exhibits broad Balmer emission [arXiv:1612.01549]. The system has a double-nucleus morphology, with its two components separated by a distance of 4 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1612.01549].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies J0332-00 as a type I AGN, confirms broad Balmer emission, and notes its double-nucleus morphology with a companion nucleus.

quotes.quote: J0332-00 Although this object was originally classified by zakamska03 as a type II quasar, the very broad \ component detected in the Gemini \ spectrum presented in Figure all_spec clearly shows that J0332-00 is, in fact, a type I AGN. This finding is confirmed by barth14 , who also find clear evidence for a broad \ component in their data. Despite this, an attempt was made to fit the stellar populations in the same manner as the other objects presented in this section. In bessiere12 , J0332-00 was classified as a double nucleus, with the two components separated by 4 kpc ( 0.9 ).

gmw_00009258.name: J0334+00
gmw_00009258.overview: The galaxy J0334+00 hosts a young stellar population with an estimated age of 0.03 gigayears [arXiv:1612.01549]. Its nebular continuum contributes 22 percent of the flux in the 3540 to 3640 Angstrom spectral region [arXiv:1612.01549].

sources.summary: The paper models J0334+00 with a young stellar population, constructs a nebular continuum, and adopts average reddening from inconsistent Balmer-decrement measurements.

quotes.quote: J0334+00 The initial modelling for J0334+00 was performed using combination 2, and resulted in a best fitting model with \ = 0.03 Gyr and \ = 0.1. The nebular continuum model then contributes 22 of the flux in the 3540 \ - 3640 \ region. The Balmer decrements were measured using both the / \ and / \ ratios (Table blines ). The two measurements result in different ranges of possible reddenings, and therefore a value of \ = 0.36 was assumed because this is the average value of the region in which the values overlap.

gmw_00009259.name: J0848+01
gmw_00009259.overview: The active galaxy J0848+01 contains a young stellar population with estimated ages of 0.2 to 0.5 Gyr [arXiv:1612.01549]. This galaxy is characterized by intrinsically low equivalent width H-beta and [O III] emission lines [arXiv:1612.01549].

sources.summary: The paper models J0848+01 with a young stellar population and weak emission lines, finding successful YSP fits with ages of roughly 0.2-0.5 Gyr.

quotes.quote: J0848+01 The initial modelling for J0848+01 was carried out using combination 2, and the resulting best fitting model comprised a YSP \ = 0.05 with \ = 0.4. The nebular continuum generated for this object contributes 19 of the flux just below the Balmer edge. J0848+01 has intrinsically low equivalent width \ and \ emission lines, and therefore it was not possible to make a reliable measurement of the Balmer decrements in order to estimate the reddening, and an unreddened nebular continuum was subtracted. Tables results8 results2 show that all the models which included a YSP component produced successful fits with fairly consistent ages of 0.2 0.5 Gyr.
